So very true. It is so hard to determine what the effect is going to be on your child. No one wants to be that rare case of reaction to them. I don't know that abandoning all vaccines is the answer, but I would certainly want to improve the quality of them - removing fetal tissues, eliminating preservatives, etc. But it would also be incredibly fantastic if we had more effective newborn screening - not just for ASD, per se - but for the underlying organic issues that can set a neonate up for problems. What if we could test them for leaky gut then and use that to make informed decisions about how we nourish, vaccinate, etc.? Sure no mom is going to want to hear their newborn isn't perfect, but if she hears that her newborn is at high risk for developing an ASD, so we should adopt this plan of - x diet for mom, breast feeding for baby, and delayed vaccination (sensitive to season of birth since some disease is more prevalent in winter). Wow! wouldn't it be great to have a pediatrician that locked on?

### IceBarbie — 2012-05-15T19:59:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vaccines-and-leaky-gut.1356/post-24285>

(NaturalNews) There is only one kind of immunity and that is natural immunity which is achieved by battling the infectious diseases itself. Vaccination is merely the artificial triggering of temporary responses to manmade pathogens. Vaccines are both harmful and dangerous and are leading to generations of humans with no natural defenses to disease.

Vaccines do not provide long-term immunity; only temporary at best. In vaccines, an antigen is injected into the body to produce a reaction and the immune system responds in the form of antibodies, but antibody presence does not confer immunity. People still catch the diseases that they are vaccinated against. Vaccines actually skip the normal immune responses to activate killer cells which can trigger an overproduction of cytokines in response to the toxic vaccine adjuvants and can damage tissues and organs and even stop the heart and block air pathways.

Vaccines should never be called immunizations because that is a misnomer. Immunity and vaccinations are two different subjects altogether. In fact, breast milk is so potent with immune energizing effects in the infant that researchers at the CDC recommend women withhold breastfeeding their children in order to boost the "effectiveness" of childhood vaccines. The paper claims that women should stop breastfeeding long enough for the man-made poison to work on artificial/temporary "immunity."

There is no such thing as a "side effect"

After-effects of vaccines are only followed for a very short time. Effects that are not seen for 30 years will not even be associated with the vaccine. Immunizations are contributing to the lowering of immunity along with the spread of auto-immune diseases such as arthritis and even AIDS throughout the world. Research clearly shows that aluminum mixed into vaccines carries a risk for autoimmunity, long-term brain inflammation, and subsequent neurological complications and may have profound and widespread adverse health complications. Many vaccines contain both aluminum and trace amounts of mercury. When you mix these 2 metals together it causes Extreme Synergistic Toxicity.

The facts:

As vaccinated disease rates go down (but not necessarily as a result of toxic vaccinations), the rate of chronic disease goes up in lock-step. Vaccines, as they are commonly given, destroy the natural immunity process and accelerate the auto-immune disease process.

Deaths from measles in 1900 were 13 per 100,000 people. In 1948: less than one. Measles vaccines introduced in 1963 but took full credit for what they never did - eliminate measles. Japanese health authorities realized that early inoculations were causing crib deaths so they postponed them until the 24th month and SIDS virtually disappeared along with whooping cough (pertussis) during the first two years of babies' lives. Instead of preventing whooping cough the DPT promotes it as well as SIDS. Reuters recently reports that according to the CDC, the number of pertussis cases is growing - in the fully vaccinated population!

The recent Bachmair vaccine study reveals that the allergy rate in vaccinated children is more than double the rate in unvaccinated ones. Vaccinated children are also nearly eight times more prone to develop asthma or chronic bronchitis than unvaccinated. Furthermore, vaccinated kiddos suffer from more neurodermatitis, herpes, otitis media, hay fever, hyperactivity, scoliosis, epilepsy and seizures, migraine headaches, thyroid disease, and SIDS than unvaccinated children. Vaccinated kids historically also have more measles than unvaccinated kids

Doctor after doctor will tell you that vaccinations have reduced the incidence of many infectious diseases but they have no real proof. In fact, all the epidemical evidence shows that disease rates rise after vaccines - in the vaccinated population. Should we trust them just on their word? Make an informed decision about you and your child's health.

### René Borg — 2016-01-18T18:56:01+0200

I haven't yet vaccinated our son (he's just past 8 months) but I am under some pressure to at least discuss the options from my wife's side as she fears the social and governmental implications of not adhering to the schedule. I have done my best to read the relevant books, following this thread and Jack's epic commentary on vaccines on Facebook and accumulating a list of questions for the GP, so I can go down and explain which vaccines I don't see risk-benefit working out for and which I would consider but have concerns about the ingredients. I don't know how this conversation will go but I won't start him on the schedule until I feel we have had a conversation leading to satisfying answers. Currently he's super-healthy and happy child - very robust and I would hate to be the one 'doing' something to change that. I looked back at my own vaccination schedule and I received less than 10 times the vaccinations in the official Irish schedule!

Any advice on how such conversations have gone for you are welcome. We had similar discussions around child-birth where informing ourselves and standing our ground helped us avoid most common interventions so that he had an almost perfect birth. I'd like to apply the same principles here. The most grating thing is that people consider you conspiratorial because I refuse you to trust any authority point blank whereas I see this as the only sensible position given what history teaches us.

### Jasmin — 2012-05-09T11:54:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-im-ls-now.1359/post-22508>

Haemoglobin 7,7 (7,3-9,5)

Potassium 4,1 (3,5-4,6)

Natrium 140 (137-145)

Kreatinin 65 (50-90)

eGFR Kidney 88 (>=60)

HDL 2,3 (1-2,7)

LDL 10,7 (1,4-4,7)

Cholesterol 13,4 (3,3-6,9)

Triglycerid 1,0 (0,45-2,6)

Bloodpressure 120/70

Oestradiol 0,06

Prolaktin 132 (64-432)

Globulin 134 (26-110)

TSH 0,01 (0,3-4)

fT4 10,1 (10-26)

T3 0,74 (1,2-2,8)

Leukocytes 7,9 (7,3-9,5)

Platelets 204 (145-390)

Lymfocyttes 0.9 (1,3-3,5)

Monocyttes 0,4 (0,2-0,7)

ALAT 42 (10-45)

CCP -Ab(IgG) 4,4 (0.0-7.0)

CRP 0,5 (0.0-8)

DNA-Ab(IgG) 0,7 (0.0-10.0)

Is all of this of any use?

### Jasmin — 2012-05-18T10:17:57+0300

### janagram — 2012-05-19T01:25:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-im-ls-now.1359/post-25345>

joints still ache....

### Jennelizabee — 2012-05-24T06:13:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-im-ls-now.1359/post-26963>

Jasmin, I'm new around here & this is my first post. I just had to respond to your Thyroid numbers. Your TSH is REALLY low like suppressed/hyper. Current normal TSH ranges are considered between .3-3.0 by the American Association of Clinical Endocrinology with a caveat that those of us with antibodies for Hashimotos often need a TSH 1.5 or lower. However your t4 & t3 are both quite low as in hypothyroid. What this means is that you are showing signs of being hyper & hypo at the same time. Are you on thyroid medication and if yes are you on just T4 or a t3/t4 combo?

### Jasmin — 2012-05-27T11:15:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-im-ls-now.1359/post-27813>

Jenneelizabee, first wellcome to this amazing forum  And thank you for your answer

I'm on thyroid medication, Erfa thyroid (combo). I have tried a lot of different medications, but they diden't work - I got Erfa in 2011. But I still fell like a zombi and gets stressed too easy. My energy is very, very low and I feel like my life is passing by without me being able to partizipate. I have been diagnosed with osteoporosis and renaud so I really need to get my life back  (I'm 37 years old) I started LR 24 of january but I'm probably one of those that have to keep working with the LR for a long time

Everything was getting better in march and so I tried to cut back on the thyroid medication but in the end of april I had to bring up the dose again because I was so exhausted - it was really bad. SLOWLY I feel the energy coming back but I really have to be carefull about what I am doing - my energy runns out so easy. The bloodtest is from end april and I am going to make a new test in the beginning of june. But since I started medications in 2009, my TSH went down and my T3 and T4 has allways stayed in the lower range. The highest my T3 and T4 went up was T3 1,21 and T4 14, so I have allways been in the low range although I took 3.5 grain Erfa.

How is it possible to treat both hyper and hypo at the same time?

### janagram — 2012-05-27T21:17:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-im-ls-now.1359/post-27902>

I'm post menopausal, yet I feel I am getting benefits from maca caps....

### Momma28 — 2012-05-27T23:36:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-im-ls-now.1359/post-27923>

> **janagram;25428 wrote:** mmm, cravings are under control....

Have you eliminated eggs and nightshades? I was still having a small amount of joint pain as well, until I discontinued these foods. The eggs are usually fed a ratio of grain, hence we still suffer inflammation from eating these. Try this and see what happens...

### janagram — 2012-05-29T00:28:49+0300

# Thread: Thought I was LS but now I am back to the BAB.  Anyone else?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### vwaggs — 2012-05-09T16:10:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-22526>

I was doing great on the post Leptin Rx. I was becoming cold adapted, no cravings, and all the signs were there that I was LS. Then the toxin dump hit and I haven't been the same even a month later so now I am starting again with the Leptin Rx. Not fair I tell you! I had gained back most of what I lost during the dump and felt like crap. And the cravings and hunger kicked in and I found myself eating junk (even WHEAT) after months of no cheats and not even wanting to. I am so upset because I thought I had this craving thing cured. So I am back to the BAB and things leptin Rx. Anyone else have to go back after they thought everything was fixed? So not happy right now. Is this even normal?

### Melinda — 2012-05-09T16:42:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-22532>

I'm not able to leave Leptin Rx. Not sure why since I can't afford testing right now. When I try to move away from the BAB, my day just doesn't go well: weird sweat, increased hunger and too alert in the evening. I'm not hungry for breakfast (never have been) so I'd rather "listen to my body" but it doesn't work for me now. I'm hoping that when I live with the cold next fall and winter that I'll get a great circadian reset and be "most" fixed!

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-05-09T19:24:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-22613>

I had to go back to the BAB too. I started the Rx last October and I'm thinking I moved to the post leptin rx in January. When I started CT I had the cravings, hunger, and mega toxin dump too initially. No weight gain, but I also was continuing a ketogenic paleo diet. I went back to eating the BAB late March / early April and am continuing now. Despite the correlation with the CT, I'm confident that the cause of needing to go back to the leptin RX is my unresolved underlying health issues.

In my case I had lingering fatigue and brain fog and after one of Dr. K's posts over at MDA I had my MSH tested. It was on the low side (as was my ACTH and serum cortisol). It took some work to find a helpful Dr. and figure out what the root cause was. For me this is mycoplasma pneumonia and HHV6 reactivated infections. Also, I've lost over 60lbs so far, but I still have 50 to lose to get back down to my normal weight - so I'm still obese and so I still have all those fat cells working against me. Guess it's a double whammy.

So to wrap it up.... I think the key is a combo of history and symptoms with educated testing. Unlimited financial resources would be helpful too!!! Too bad mine are finite.

### kathylu — 2012-05-09T21:57:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-22650>

Same story here. I had most of the LS signs and was losing weight slowly. Started CT in March thinking it would accelerate my gains and it was like a brick wall fell on me! Weight loss stopped, I get very sick from the toxin dump (muscle soreness like I was a marathoner, nasty headaches, nausea) and I became RAVENOUS! I went back to the BAB and eating lunch. Emailed Jack and he told me to get my hormones tested (3rd time he had said that to me), so I finally did. Everything except cortisol was low (my pregnenolone was 7. Seven!) I started BHRT on 4/3 and I am feeling much better. Hunger is better now and weight loss has re-started. So, maybe you need a biohack too...just sayin'.

Hang in there. There is a solution!

### melrito — 2012-05-09T22:57:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-22659>

same issue for me, did the reset, had all the "wins", but as soon as i started ct, i was hungry and gained 4 pounds in a week. I've been doing Ct almost a month now. sugar cravings that i hadn't had for months are back, although i am not giving in and feel like I'm slowly making progress again with this - some days are better than others. I have gone on and off the BAB since starting CT and in general feel better with the BAB so do that most days. I am not experiencing the toxic dump like others, just this small weight gain and a bit of tummy bloating sometimes. I have no money for labs now - I'm sure my hormones are off - but am planning to do what I can afford next month.

I will just keep going until I become more cold adapted (although I thought it would be here by now) I think I started mid February but I have only been doing it 3 times a week for 30 min and my water with ice only gets down to 58-68 degrees. So it could be a slower process for me but I am not sick and I am almost at goal weight so I didn't think I needed it every day. Plus I HATE it still so there is that! I hope I can embrace the cold soon! Very frustrating to not get results.

CT is supposed to help you detoxify and balance your hormones too, amongst other things (thyroid, adrenals). So CT as much as you can. It took me 2 weeks of CTing to get the detoxifying going along with the ugly side affect of hunger. Then another 8 weeks of CTing to get to a point where I lost 3 of the 8 I gained from CT. However, all along I know I'm getting rid of excess estrogen and now my hunger has really subsided. I CT about 5-6 days a week in 55ish water. I wish I could get it colder but I need more ice right now.

### tweety — 2012-05-10T02:58:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-22706>

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-10T03:26:33+0300

I've been CTing for 12-13 weeks now so you may be turning a corner soon. I'm going to keep at it as my thermostat is definitely coming on. I jump two degrees in temp after 30 min in the bath!

### Petra — 2012-05-10T09:21:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-22784>

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-11T17:23:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-23173>

After reading this thread, I'm a little scared to even do CT!! I wasn't sure if I was going to do it anyway. I'm starting LR Sunday and just hoping that goes well for me. I obviously don't want to gain on it!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-11T17:29:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-23176>

Do the CT - it's like spring cleaning a messy hoarder-like closet. The entire area in and surrounding look horrid and get worse before they get better. You have to take everything out, look at it, throw some away, organize, figure out how to build it back. Some people have a much messier disorganized closet with clothes from the last 20 years. Those are going to get much worse and take longer before they end up with a glossy new Container Store masterpiece!

Substitute closet for fat cells and there you go. If you are not healthy, consider your closet a mess and just enjoy the fact that even though you have a messy disaster waiting for you this weekend, you will work though it and end up with something mucho better.

Too bad we can't get tax write offs for the crap coming out of our bodies like we do our closets.

### janagram — 2012-05-11T18:53:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-23194>

### Jennie747 — 2012-05-12T23:09:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-23500>

I've been on the LR since January 1st and holding steady weight wise, then when I started CT (mostly spot icing on my stomach) I started slowly gaining & my stomach has gotten bigger! Ugh. The hunger and the amount of time it's taking to see any results is frustrating and I'm finding myself cheating more often, I'm sure that doesn't help.

### Dali Dula — 2012-05-14T14:13:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-23866>

I basically never stopped the BAB. It's the way I live now. Some mornings like right now I wake up not hungry at all even though my last meal was over twelve hours ago. If I don't eat a BAB I eat a small amount of protein and put some butter in my coffee just to let my body know it's ok. I also noticed ,although it's hard to tell, that everyone on this thread is female. Don't know if that means anything.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-14T16:20:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-23879>

> **Dali Dula;23948 wrote:** I basically never stopped the BAB. It's the way I live now. Some mornings like right now I wake up not hungry at all even though my last meal was over twelve hours ago. If I don't eat a BAB I eat a small amount of protein and put some butter in my coffee just to let my body know it's ok. I also noticed ,although it's hard to tell, that everyone on this thread is female. Don't know if that means anything.

I've been strict on it since early March, and doing serious CT. Losses have been slow (but I am shrinking - big improvement over previous year of paleo) and I never ever ever want that damn BAB. I tried transitioning to the post RX, but the snacking and cravings came back so I realized I had a ways to go on the RX. I was a fat kid tho, so I am going on the theory there is a LOT to work through in these cells!

Also, if judging from anecdotal evidence of the ladies on this site - the fat cell apoptosis isn't an overnight thing. It's simply going to take awhile. Afterall, I imagine it would NOT be optimal to have a gazillion dead cells floating around trying to be processed out. Probably you lose at what you can effectively process. That's my working theory.

To add to Dali's comment, I've observed a pretty material difference in:

Men/Women

Former athletic folks/always fat folks

Old/young folks.

That my friend, is the way the cookie crumbles. Fortunately those of us that are old and fat are wise and can handle it!

### vlynnb — 2012-05-14T17:11:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thought-i-was-ls-but-now-i-am-back-to-the-bab-anyone-else.1361/post-23899>

Caveman,

Your closet analogy was just what I needed. Maybe have something to do with cleaning out my recently deceased mother's house this past month. The mess is still a mess but gradually getting better.

Because of mom's illness, death, and all that comes after I have put off CT though the LRX seems to be going well, losing about a pound a week (only about 12 pounds to go), increased perspiration, really no cravings from the beginning but had been on hhcg. Also haven't done any testing, just too busy trying to meet deadlines with all of the post death financial stuff. I did get to hate the BAB though and cut back a bit. Then to start reading more about CT problems people were having was really making me more reluctant. I needed the reassurance your closet analogy gives.

We close on the house sale on the 25th of this month so that is my target date for starting CT, though I'll start the face CT right now.

# Thread: Vit D, TSH, T4/T3 Free lab results - please review
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vit-d-tsh-t4-t3-free-lab-results-please-review.1364/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Clabbergirl — 2012-05-09T17:27:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vit-d-tsh-t4-t3-free-lab-results-please-review.1364/post-22565>

Here are the results of labs I had drawn a week ago. I'd love some input on how far from optimal they are and any suggestions on what I might try to move these numbers in the right direction.

Vitamin D, 25 OH total - 95 (30-100 ng/mL range)

Vitamin D, 25 OH, D2 - < 4

Vitamin D, 25 OH, D3 - 95

TSH - 3.98 (.40 - 4.50 range)

T4, Free - 1.0 (.8 - 1.8 ng/dL)

T3, Free - 2.7 (2.3 - 4.2 pg/mL)

I've been supplementing Vit D at least 5,000/day for over 2 years, and upped it to 10,000-15,000/day within the last 2 months. I'm currently taking Armour for low thyroid, so those numbers are with supplementation. Thanks.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-09T17:36:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vit-d-tsh-t4-t3-free-lab-results-please-review.1364/post-22569>

I'm sooo jealous of your Vit D! That is great! However, your thyroid is not so great. Your FT3 (the bio-available hormone) should be at the top of the range and the FT4 should be mid-range to near the top. Not to mention that TSH is way too high. I would guess you still feel yucky, fatigued and maybe even lethargic. That's how I felt with similar numbers. Many docs these days use 3.0 as a top range for TSH. Look to me like you need to up your meds. How much are you taking?

Are you chewing or braking up the Armour before swallowing it? Armour has a filler (can't remember which one) that doesn't release well into the system, if not broken apart. Maybe try that before upping the meds.

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-09T17:39:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vit-d-tsh-t4-t3-free-lab-results-please-review.1364/post-22573>

> **Patty Cakes;22648 wrote:** I'm sooo jealous of your Vit D! That is great! However, your thyroid is not so great. Your FT3 (the bio-available hormone) should be at the top of the range and the FT4 should be mid-range to near the top. Not to mention that TSH is way too high. I would guess you still feel yucky, fatigued and maybe even lethargic. That's how I felt with similar numbers. Many docs these days use 3.0 as a top range for TSH. Look to me like you need to up your meds. How much are you taking?
>
> .

Concur w/this... your ranges are crazy! did you get a rT3 run as well??? if not next time you need to push for that, even if you have topay out pocket for it!

### Clabbergirl — 2012-05-09T17:58:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vit-d-tsh-t4-t3-free-lab-results-please-review.1364/post-22580>

I did not get rT3 even though I asked for it. Frustrating. I had to beg for the Vit D test also. Ugh.

Patty Cakes and Shijin, I do feel lethargic and fatigued. I'm taking 120mg of thryoid so I guess I should up it. I had upped it several months back and felt nothing at all. This is after a year of slowing upping my amount and continuing to feel lousy and gain weight (or maintain) even at 300mg. I never felt 'wired and tired' either. I finally said forget it and just went back to what my doctor prescribed - 120mg.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-09T18:07:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vit-d-tsh-t4-t3-free-lab-results-please-review.1364/post-22582>

Clabbergirl, I liked the old version of Armour but not the new version (post late 2009). I just wonder if you need to change the med first. I've read where some people have challenges with Armour. Your lab tests almost look like you are not on anything at all, certainly not 120 mg. I read somewhere that the average amount most folks take is around 150-300mg/day.

I also tried Naturethroid and it was OK but I still wasn't satisfied. Found Thyroid-S (from Thailand) and liked the results but didn't like the fillers. But I still used it for over two years. Then I found NP Thyroid by Acella. It's the generic version of Armour so very easy to get and taken sublingually under the tongue it just dissolves into nothingness almost immediately. I had a rT3 problem so I'm on T3 only for a few months but when I'm done I'm going back on the NP Thyroid (150 mg)!

# Thread: Please Help With My Labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-my-labs.1373/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### indigogirl — 2012-05-09T22:13:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-my-labs.1373/post-22652>

Story of my life...I'm always tired...been a poor/light sleeper my whole life.

I am a 59 year old female, menopausal, hypothyroid for almost 20yrs (familial...mom and sister also hypo), weight perfect until my mid thirties when I started emotionally overeating...yo-yo'ed for the last 20 years...about 60-70 lbs overweight now. 5' 2", small boned. No uterus...still have my ovaries and cervix.

On 9/28/11 :

Vitamin B12. 617. range 254-1320

Cortisol AM. 14.30. range 3.70-19.40

My doc said these were both in the normal range.

ARE THEY????

Most recent labs from 1/18/12: for blood work

Estradiol. 17.0 (pg/ml). Range 50-150

Testosterone total. 18.53 (14.00-76.00 ng/dl). Range 40-80

Progesterone. 0.3. (0.0-0.2 ng/ml) H Range 1.0-10

T4 Free. 0.72 (0.76-1.46 ng/dL) L.

TSH. 2.610 (0.358-3.740 uIU/ mL)

T3 total. 1.43 (0.70-1.90 ng/mL)

T3 Free. 4.1 (2.0-4.4 pg/mL)

I take 90 mg of Armour.

I take bioedentical estriol,estradiol and progesterone.

I know my Testosterone sucks...won't take bioedentical testosterone...I get facial cysts...am taking 25 mg 7 keto DHEA when I remember.

The above labs were done with my GYN who is CW, but open to discussing things with me.

Today my internist told me I need a sleep study because of my constant exhaustion. I think it's a waste of time.

I told her I wanted a CRP. She said she couldnt really justify it. My GYN will do the CRP at my next visit (August) if I want to her to.

Will someone please hack these labs and tell me what you think??

THANK YOU!!

# Thread: Faulty Fat Sensor Implicated in Obesity and Liver Disease
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faulty-fat-sensor-implicated-in-obesity-and-liver-disease.1378/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-10T06:13:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faulty-fat-sensor-implicated-in-obesity-and-liver-disease.1378/post-22767>

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2012/02/120219143222.htm](http://http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2012/02/120219143222.htm)

ScienceDaily (Feb. 19, 2012) â€” Defects in a protein that functions as a dietary fat sensor may be a cause of obesity and liver disease, according to a study published in the journal Nature, led by researchers at Imperial College London. The findings highlight a promising target for new drugs to treat obesity and metabolic disorders.

The protein GPR120 is found on the surface of cells in the gut, liver and fat tissue and allows cells to detect and respond to unsaturated fatty acids from the diet, especially the omega-3 fatty acids which are believed to have a beneficial impact on health. Scientists found that mice deficient in GPR120 were more prone to developing obesity and liver disease when fed a high-fat diet. They also found that people with a certain mutation in the gene encoding GPR120, which stops the protein from responding to omega-3 fatty acids, were significantly more likely to be obese.

In the gut, when unsaturated fatty acids from food bind to GPR120, this stimulates the release of hormones that suppress appetite and stimulate the pancreas to secrete insulin. When fat cells sense high levels of fat in the blood through GPR120, it stimulates them to divide to produce more fat cells to store all the fat, reducing the risk of fatty liver and furring of the arteries. This mechanism could be an important pathway for bringing about some of the healthy effects of omega-3s.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: The epigenetic switch for t-cell mediated auto immunity is identified
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-epigenetic-switch-for-t-cell-mediated-auto-immunity-is-identified.1379/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-10T06:30:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-epigenetic-switch-for-t-cell-mediated-auto-immunity-is-identified.1379/post-22771>

Histone deacetylase 9 deficiency protects against effector T cell-mediated systemic autoimmunity.

Yan K, Cao Q, Reilly CM, Young NL, Garcia BA, Mishra N.

Source

Section on Rheumatology, Department of Internal Medicine, Wake Forest University School of Medicine, Winston-Salem, North Carolina 27157, UAS.

Abstract

Co-repressor histone deacetylase 9 (HDAC9) plays a key role in the development and differentiation of many types of cells, including regulatory T cells. However, the biological function of HDAC9 in T effector cells is unknown. Systemic autoimmune diseases like lupus, diabetes, and rheumatoid arthritis have dysfunctional effector T cells. To determine the role of HDAC9 in systemic autoimmunity, we created MRL/lpr mice with HDAC9 deficiency that have aberrant effector T cell function. HDAC9 deficiency led to decreased lympho-proliferation, inflammation, autoantibody production, and increased survival in MRL/lpr mice. HDAC9-deficient mice manifested Th2 polarization, decreased T effector follicular cells positive for inducible co-stimulator, and activated T cells in vivo compared with HDAC9-intact MRL/lpr mice. HDAC9 deficiency also resulted in increased GATA3 and roquin and decreased BCL6 gene expression. HDAC9 deficiency was associated with increased site-specific lysine histone acetylation at H3 (H3K9, H3K14, and H3K18) globally that was localized to IL-4, roquin, and peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor-γ promoters with increased gene expression, respectively. In kidney and spleen, HDAC9 deficiency decreased inflammation and cytokine and chemokine production due to peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor γ overexpression. These findings suggest that HDAC9 acts as an epigenetic switch in effector T cell-mediated systemic autoimmunity.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-in-michigan-and-looking-for-grassed-meat-or-pastured-pork.1382/post-22823>

I have a great farm I've been buying from. We purchased 1/4 of a cow and have loved the meat. We have half a piggy coming soon. The guy who runs it is exceptionally helpful particularly with helping you figure out how to have it processed. His processor will do sugar free bacon and sausage. They are also keeping the back fat for me so I can render my own lard. Yay! His prices are the best I've found. Oh, included in his prices for buying part of an animal is hand delivery to your door and all of the meat comes to you vacuum sealed.

### Larry — 2012-05-10T17:29:25+0300

What part of Michigan? I am in South Bend and I would love to find a good supplier. Thank you!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-in-michigan-and-looking-for-grassed-meat-or-pastured-pork.1382/post-22872>

### Larry — 2012-05-10T20:12:22+0300

### joyfuljoyful — 2012-05-10T21:15:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-in-michigan-and-looking-for-grassed-meat-or-pastured-pork.1382/post-22966>

### Guest — 2012-05-10T22:35:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-youre-in-michigan-and-looking-for-grassed-meat-or-pastured-pork.1382/post-22995>

@ Joyfuljoyful, Look into Thomas Creamery. He is at the Royal Oak Market on Saturdays. They always have grass fed ground beef, but hoping soon he will have other cuts as well. He also carries eggs that are to die for. No soy, and they are pastured eggs. Other farmers carry eggs that have no soy and are also pastured, but they just are not the same. Other things he carries are yogurt, colostrum ice cream, and pastured butter (yum yum). I also get veal and beef soup bones from them. John Henry is also at the Royal Oak market and they have grass fed as well, but I trust and love Thomas Creamery.

# Thread: Bleeding ear?  Ct?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bleeding-ear-ct.1383/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-10T17:17:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bleeding-ear-ct.1383/post-22827>

Ok, I've had tinnitus a bit lately - THOUGHT it was in conjunction with my new deeper CT efforts. This a.m. I q-tipped and there was blood in one of my ears.

Yikes! Bleeding from the head doesn't really seem good. Has anybody had anything like this? Tinnitus? Debating on giving it a day or going to the clinic. It's not painful, although I did have a headache this a.m., and off and on this last week (again, attributed to deeper CT and detox).

Thoughts from the very sweet and brilliant peanut gallery here?

omg, the thought of saying "well, i thought the headaches and tinnitus were from my cold baths and detox" is just not something I want to face today! Time to google. . .

### thyme — 2012-05-10T17:30:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bleeding-ear-ct.1383/post-22830>

I'm sorry about your ear!!!

I can't give you any useful advice on the clinic or no front, but I can be somewhat useless by getting onto my hard-won experience soapbox for a moment, which might at least distract you....

I came down with swimmer's ear a few years ago; the pain was far beyond any physical injury I'd ever had before, including migraines and broken limbs. I wasn't swimming (or CTing at the time.) The ER doc and the follow-up general doc \_both\_ looked at my ear infection and stated, as simple fact, "You use Q-tips."

The inner part of your ear is so very delicate that the cotton bud of a q-tip can scratch it, and if an infection then sets in...holy bleep, but I wondered how young children survive repeated ear infections with their mental facilities intact.

You could well have an outer ear infection/swimmer's ear--but blood there, I don't want to speculate.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-10T17:49:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bleeding-ear-ct.1383/post-22833>

I get tinnitus from CTing. Have been wondering about that. It usually gets better or goes away as I warm up.

I'd bin those q-tips. My ear got clogged from them as a child and the doc said to just point the shower head towards the ear when in the shower... I guess you do need a European shower head to accomplish this. I'm not sure even that is necessary...

Not much input on whether or not this needs to be seen by a doctor, sorry.

### MJ* — 2012-05-10T18:06:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bleeding-ear-ct.1383/post-22838>

> **Souldanzer;22914 wrote:**
>
> Not much input on whether or not this needs to be seen by a doctor, sorry.

I agree blood in the ear, unless it's you can tell it's from a cut or like a pimple or something, you should get it checked...

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-10T18:07:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bleeding-ear-ct.1383/post-22839>

Decided to go to urgent care. Here now! Wonder if i can wrangle some scans out of this.

Pulse : 60 bpm

I feel fine - i think i just panicked a little after seeing blood.

# Thread: Blood Oxygen Saturation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-oxygen-saturation.1391/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Souldanzer — 2012-05-11T03:51:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-oxygen-saturation.1391/post-23071>

\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`

### nonchalant — 2012-05-11T06:01:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-oxygen-saturation.1391/post-23099>

Souldanzer, I think it means they don't have anything to prescribe for it.

### Glamazon — 2012-05-11T08:38:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-oxygen-saturation.1391/post-23112>

> **Souldanzer;23153 wrote:** When you go to the doctor, they often take your blood oxygen saturation level with one of those little finger clips.
>
>
>
> Well, mine has been going down for the last couple of years. Today it was jumping between 94/95 percent. I asked the nurse about it who said I shouldn't worry, that it's normal. Anything between 95 and 100 percent is considered normal.
>
> I pointed out that I was sort of below the lower limit of even that "normal". Was told to talk to the doc about it which I subsequently forgot as we had a lot of other stuff to talk about.
>
>
>
> What is a normal/optimal level of blood oxygen saturation? When ever they tell me not to worry, I DO!!

Ask the doctor to explain it to you. It all depends on context which could be multifactoral. If you notice a slow decline over the years ask your doc. Generally, recommendations to wear Oxygen doesn't come till you hit about 88% because that is when insurance will pay for it.

[http://www.mayoclinic.com/health/hypoxemia/MY00219](http://http://www.mayoclinic.com/health/hypoxemia/MY00219)

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-11T12:12:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-oxygen-saturation.1391/post-23122>

\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-11T12:15:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-oxygen-saturation.1391/post-23123>

\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`

### Paradox — 2012-05-12T01:39:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-oxygen-saturation.1391/post-23298>

Hi Souldanzer. I noticed you live in a dark cold cave in the Rockies. My question for you is at what elevation and how long have you lived there. The Rockies can be quite high and at higher elevations there is less oxygen, hence many people may have lower blood 02 saturations.

I speak from firsthand knowledge as I have seriously impaired lung function and cannot go to altitude without supplemental oxygen. I personally wouldnt worry about a 94-95 reading at altitude. What is your heart rate? Is it elevated? The heart will compensate if the body senses a lack of oxygen. If you are worried have a pulmonary function test.

Next time at your doctors ask if many people at your altitude have reduced levels of O2. Would be interesting to know.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-12T02:14:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-oxygen-saturation.1391/post-23310>

Thanks paradox. I've been wondering about the altitude issue but I'm really not that high (5,500ft). I've been here for years now and my oxygen level has been going down while living here. My heart rate has gone up significantly in the past couple of years as well.

I will let my doc know next week that I'm worried about it.... I was supposed to have a stress echo a couple of weeks ago (b/c of my heart symptoms) but had to postpone since the cardiologist wasn't too thrilled about me hopping on the treadmill after telling him that my doc is worried about a brain bleed from my concussion. That has been cleared so maybe I can get it done soon.

I feel like a real mess tonight. Having a discouraged moment

Sounds fun!!

# Thread: Massive Strength Increase Following CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/massive-strength-increase-following-ct.1393/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KA24 — 2012-05-11T06:07:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/massive-strength-increase-following-ct.1393/post-23101>

I've noticed lately that my capacity for strength (total weight) and muscle endurance (more reps) is greatly increased after CT.

Has anyone else noticed this? I was able to do 36 pushups (I can generally do 25-30 quickly before fatigue) and was doing some 40 lb kettlebell one-handed snatches with ease. This is the third or fourth time I've tested this afterward and noticed a difference. Not only do I feel stronger, but mentally I'm convinced as well, which has really allowed me to do some really good strength work for a few minutes after CT.

Do you think there might some possible gains by doing a more intense session immediately post-CT and take advantage of this? This "feeling" seems to wear off after about 30 minutes as I start to really hit the coldest point, but before that, it's pretty cool.

Thoughts?


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Head submerged during CT for long periods

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 11

### Swagger — 2012-05-11T07:27:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/head-submerged-during-ct-for-long-periods.1395/post-23110>

As if sitting in ice water wasn't enough...

I have begun to use a snorkel in an effort to submerge my head for longer periods during CT baths. (I jumped into CT, skipping the face submerging.) At first the shock of the cold caused me to hyperventilate and , however this passes once you adapt to the cold. Once you adapt to using the snorkel, the sensation is wonderful. Has anyone else experimented with this? (I will tell you if you snort 55F water up your nose accidentally, it will clear out your sinuses with a quickness!)

### chocolate — 2012-05-11T08:47:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/head-submerged-during-ct-for-long-periods.1395/post-23115>

### Swagger — 2012-05-14T08:41:59+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/head-submerged-during-ct-for-long-periods.1395/post-24580>

Ct is about lowering skin temps to activate BAT, not lowering core body temp.... Submerging head you will lose a lot of body heat from your scalp. Remember in the beginning you wear hat, socks, gloves to keep core temp up as you lower skin temp. Face dunks are different than whole head submerging...

**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet

### nzgal — 2012-05-11T16:45:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-23166>

Hey guys!!!

I am soo confused!!!!!! There seems to be so many different kinds of coconut oil out there. Am I supposed to buy unrefined, organic??

Does it matter what brand I get???

Will the Walmart brand still work the same???

Thanks!!!!!

### WannaBeOptimal — 2012-05-12T05:18:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-23336>

Trader Joe's now sells "organic virgin coconut oil" in a 16oz jar for $6.99

Coconuts come from the Phillipines. Not as tasty as Nutiva's extra virgin, but serviceable imo.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-12T05:27:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-23339>

> **nzgal;23248 wrote:** Hey guys!!!
>
>
>
> I am soo confused!!!!!! There seems to be so many different kinds of coconut oil out there. Am I supposed to buy unrefined, organic??
>
>
>
> Does it matter what brand I get???
>
>
>
> Will the Walmart brand still work the same???
>
>
>
>
>
> Thanks!!!!!

Definately get unrefined organic cold pressed. The lovely smell is awesome. You will start rubbing it on your skin! I tried the walmart once, not for me. No coconut smell - and i since learned of all e chemicals used to extract oil.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-12T18:34:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-23415>

Slight tangent - what is coconut manna? I know about coconut oil & coconut cream but don't know what manna is?

### JC2K — 2012-05-14T23:14:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-24002>

Tropical Traditions has a good explanation on virgin, etc at:

[http://www.tropicaltraditions.com/what\_is\_virgin\_coconut\_oil.htm](http://http://www.tropicaltraditions.com/what_is_virgin_coconut_oil.htm)

All the research studies I have seen on coconut oil use refined oil. I don't really recall seeing much in the way of studies on the virgin/unrefined stuff. But basic idea is that the unrefined stuff is a better source, so a better starting point. It does have a better taste too.

Coconut Manna = coconut oil with a lot of the coconut fiber blended in. Takes some mixing from time to time because it will separate. Gives it a thick mouth feel like peanut butter, so you can treat it like peanut butter on sandwiches and the like. I think a little goes a long ways though.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-15T00:22:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-24031>

The refined usually doesn't have the coconut taste and aroma. I keep a jar of this around for when I don't want the taste but don't use it often. But for daily use and eating it by the spoonful I love the Nutiva.

### indigogirl — 2012-05-15T06:58:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-24132>

Coconut manna=coconut butter. I've tried Artisinal and Nutiva and like the Nutiva better. JC2K gave an accurate description.

I also use both refined and unrefined in cooking...mostly unrefined, refined when I don't want my food to taste like coconut.

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-16T16:35:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-24589>

On another thread, someone suggested adding some coconut oil to the manna (once its heated and stirred), which will keep it loose so you don't have to restir it all the time. (assuming you don't live Ina cold place that is). Have not tried this yet....

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-16T18:58:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-24612>

Krusing....that's what I do and add some cinnamon. It doesn't stay loose unless it's summer for me. Yesterday our house got to 75 and it still was solid.

PS - For anyone traveling, I have carried CO with me for years when flying but this time since it was solid I decided to pack it in my carry on suitcase and not take it out for the TSA screening. Oooops I was told I have to take it out for them to see but it is fine to carry on. Save yourself an extra screening of the suitcase and the CO!!!

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-17T01:57:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil.1402/post-24730>

So I heard it from you!!! Funny thing I just flew to TX and had CO in my checked baggage...LAX was apparently unconcerned as my baggage was intact upon arrival. Next time I'll carry on! I could see it getting some scrutiny in this day and age.

# Thread: Sleep.....I took Ambien...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-i-took-ambien.1405/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### janagram — 2012-05-11T19:06:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-i-took-ambien.1405/post-23196>

...I am sure taking a sleeping pill is not helping my reset, but when DH and I were traveling a few nights this week, I had to take Ambien to sleep because he snores so loud, whereas at home we have separate bedrooms for said reason. So, maybe I took 2 or so, all tolled...(I cut them in half). Now let me tell you, this makes it hard to get to sleep the night you stop taking them. I've been through this before. Last year this time I was going through extreme anxiety related to my mother's terminal cancer diagnosis, and I took Ambien to sleep for ...weeks? I kind of went through hell getting off of it.

Taking GABA helped. Then I weaned off that. Should I take GABA?

I told my husband, No one can go through that level of anxiety without some serious physical repercussions, which is probably why I have adrenal and thyroid issues. I am just guessing that I have those....but its a good guess.

The anxiety also caused me to lose weight rapidly and I lost about 30 lbs. I have gained those back and am now trying to lose them for real. Consciously.

### janagram — 2012-05-11T21:29:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-i-took-ambien.1405/post-23246>

All the last year stuff was before I heard of leptin reset....tho I did eat paleo, and very sparsely. I gained back the weight and then started looking for a strategy and found this. I am following the reset...for about 6 wks now, with a couple of pecadillos...

I think there should be a whole thread for sleep.

### Pamela — 2012-05-12T07:29:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-i-took-ambien.1405/post-23355>

I used to sleep all the way through the night until probably my early 40's and then gradually it has declined over the past 10 years. I never took sleeping pills but have used a host of other items. Currently I am taking my mag malate and L-Tryptophan (1000mg) before bed. This helps me sleep straight through for 6.5 hours, then I get up, pee, take 2 cal-mag caps and go back to sleep.

### janagram — 2012-05-12T10:36:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-i-took-ambien.1405/post-23361>

Awesome, Pamela. I'd call that a good night's sleep.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-12T15:14:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-i-took-ambien.1405/post-23367>

> **janagram;23278 wrote:**
>
> ...I am sure taking a sleeping pill is not helping my reset, but when DH and I were traveling a few nights this week, I had to take Ambien to sleep because he snores so loud, whereas at home we have separate bedrooms for said reason.

I would say this part is the result of bad planning. This is preventable. Sounds like your DH and you need to have a conversation about how to handle sleeping away from home. If he snores that loud I'd think he needs to be seen by a doctor. There's also mouth appliances that can help. And you need a pair of good ear plugs.

If all that fails, my sleep would be important enough to insist on two rooms even when sleeping away from home. Honestly, I hope I don't sound harsh (not meaning to) but I don't quite understand how you taking Ambien is a solution for your DH's snoring.

### janagram — 2012-05-13T08:40:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-i-took-ambien.1405/post-23621>

well, it's not. It's a p--- poor solution. But, sleep is a touchy thing with me. I get your point though, and should insist hubby gets some kind of treatment. Thanks for the reality check!

SLEEP is the next frontier!!

# Thread: Kid/picky family friendly Optimized recipes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kid-picky-family-friendly-optimized-recipes.1407/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BeingVenus — 2012-05-11T23:36:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kid-picky-family-friendly-optimized-recipes.1407/post-23259>

I thought it would be great to have a thread devoted to all these great recipes you guys are posting so we have it all aggregated. I'll start with this awesome looking "coconut oil disguised as dessert delivery system" recipe

[http://untilthethinladysings.blogspot.com/2012/05/lemon-coconut-energy-bombs.html](http://http://untilthethinladysings.blogspot.com/2012/05/lemon-coconut-energy-bombs.html)

Lemon Coconut Energy Bombs

Makes 9

1/4 cup coconut butter (see below)

1/4 cup virgin coconut oil, high quality

1 Tablespoon of organic lemon zest

1 Tablespoon of organic lemon juice

stevia powder or liquid to taste, I used 1/8 teaspoon NuNaturals powder

toasted coconut to garnish (see below)

mini cupcake wrappers or pan

Combine all ingredients except toasted coconut and mix well. Scoop into cupcake wrappers. Sprinkle toasted coconut on top. Place in freezer for 20 minutes, then place in refrigerator. Lasts a very long time.

How to make coconut butter: Grind unsweetened coconut flakes in a nut/coffee grinder or food processor for at least 20 minutes, or until the consistency of nut butter.

How to make toasted coconut: Preheat oven to Broil. On a cookie sheet, put a single layer of unsweetened coconut flakes and broil for 30 seconds to 1 minute, or until brown. Burns fast! Don't walk away!

Thank you! This is a great recipe. I eliminated the roasted coconut and subbed rushed mac nuts and put on top. No stevia for me as the coconut butter (I used manna) was sweet enough. YUM!

# Thread: Nitrogen Excretion: three end products
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nitrogen-excretion-three-end-products.1410/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-12T01:19:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nitrogen-excretion-three-end-products.1410/post-23281>

[http://jeb.biologists.org/content/198/2/273.short?rss=1&amp%3bssource=mfr](http://http://jeb.biologists.org/content/198/2/273.short?rss=1&amp%3bssource=mfr)

**Nitrogen excretion: three end products, many physiological roles.

**There are diverse physiological functions of nitrogen end products in different animal groups, including excretion, acid-base regulation, osmoregulation and buoyancy.** Animals excrete a variety of nitrogen waste products, but ammonia, urea and uric acid predominate. A major factor in determining the mode of nitrogen excretion is the availability of water in the environment. Generally, aquatic animals excrete mostly ammonia, whereas terrestrial animals excrete either urea or uric acid. Ammonia, urea and uric acid are transported across cell membranes by different mechanisms corresponding to their different chemical properties in solution. Ammonia metabolism and excretion are linked to acid-base regulation in the kidney, but the role of urea and uric acid is less clear. Both invertebrates and vertebrates use nitrogen-containing organic compounds as intracellular osmolytes. In some marine invertebrates, NH4+ is sequestered in specific compartments to increase buoyancy.**


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Decaffeinating Green Tea
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decaffeinating-green-tea.1411/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Souldanzer — 2012-05-12T01:20:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decaffeinating-green-tea.1411/post-23283>

Can I make my own decaffeinated green tea? I'm tired of buying tasteless expensive decaf... but the caffeine can mess with me even though it's little. I was thinking of brewing a whole batch of tea leaves for a minute or so, dumping out the tea, and then drying the leaves for future use.

Would this work to get rid of most of the caffeine but still have a tasty cup of tea in the end? I already brew my leaves about three times.

### vkiernan — 2012-05-17T20:57:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decaffeinating-green-tea.1411/post-24966>

That's an interesting thought. Let us know how it works if you try it. While at work, it is a pain to steep and dump and steep again to be able to drink. Would be nice to just put in cup, steep and drink.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-17T21:13:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decaffeinating-green-tea.1411/post-24969>

Haven't tried yet but am getting ready to as the next box of decaf... I could just steep water as well, lol.

The steeping/throwing out/steeping again doesn't work for me at all b/c my Indian Peaks Mountain Spring Water purchased in glass bottles is way to precious to throw out. So I'd have to have a different water boiler, etc. Too much work. I end up just drinking the caffeine...

### JC2K — 2012-05-17T22:06:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decaffeinating-green-tea.1411/post-24985>

I believe anything you do to remove caffeine will reduce the flavor. For the commercial methods in the US, they use Ethyl Acetate or CO2. The CO2 method is supposed to keep the flavor better than Ethyl Acetate. You'll have to search for companies that advertise that method though. Usually they don't say.

Non commercially, the longer you hot brew tea, the more bitter components come out, so the tea doesn't taste as good. You don't want long hot brew times for green tea.

My recollection when looking at this a while back is that the majority of the caffeine in the tea comes out in the first 30 seconds of hot brewing. So you brew 30 seconds, dump off, then brew normally and it's supposed to be fine. You say you brew 3 times though, so I would think the caffeine would be gone (along with the flavor  ). You're still having problems with that?

The other thing I've seen is cold steeping for 10+ hours in cold water. The caffeine and bitter components don't come out because it's not hot water. Not sure on the taste though.

I'm a bit of a caffeine fiend, so I don't drink decaf.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-17T22:16:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decaffeinating-green-tea.1411/post-24989>

Well, I drink them all  First second and third brew. First is AM after waking, so I figure the caffeine is sort of okay. But really, I'm fixing my adrenals and any caffeine can keep me up at night. Last brew is at night, so I'm happy the caffeine is gone.

I know that a bit of the flavor will go... nothing one can do about that. I'm trying to improve on the store bought version.

I'm going to try the 30secs and dump method. I think it will still be better than the stuff I've been buying. I like the caffeine, especially b/c it helps with my neuro symptoms, but I'm pretty sure, right now, it's not the smartest move. One of the reasons I haven't tried this yet is b/c the caffeine helps me function

### JC2K — 2012-05-17T22:59:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decaffeinating-green-tea.1411/post-25002>

Let us know how it works out. For me, it won't matter. It would take more caffeine than in a cup of green tea to keep me awake at night, but others might find it useful to know.

### Aamia — 2013-03-27T12:18:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decaffeinating-green-tea.1411/post-81944>

I would like to say thank's a lot for sharing this info. Its a useful post for me specially.
I wanna know about Decaffeinating green tea more.

### tweety — 2013-03-27T21:42:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decaffeinating-green-tea.1411/post-81981>

I tried steeping then dumping after 30-60 secs and steeping a second time... I still had issues with the caffiene. I can tell because it will wake me up in the middle of the night. That was the only stimulant in the day. Now it seems I am ok with regular green tea first thing in the morning, but then I have to switch to herbal teas for the rest of the day.

As to cold steeping, I do this often in the summer. In fact its pretty much the only way I make tea. I always wondered if it removed the caffiene. As for bitter, I seem to like my green tea to have steeped longer- I like the grassy and bitterness it has after letting it sit all morning.

# Thread: Lipoic Acid antioxidant and wiked chelator... ummmmmm... fixes ED in rats, peeps anec
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipoic-acid-antioxidant-and-wiked-chelator-ummmmmm-fixes-ed-in-rats-peeps-anec.1414/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-12T03:25:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipoic-acid-antioxidant-and-wiked-chelator-ummmmmm-fixes-ed-in-rats-peeps-anec.1414/post-23323>

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lipoic\_acid](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lipoic_acid)

Metal chelation

Owing to the presence of two thiol groups, dihydrolipoic acid is a chelating agent. **It chelates both intracellular and extracellular mercury in the brain and in the body. Alpha Lipoic Acid (ALA) has a half life of 3 hours. Due to this half life issue it can be dangerous to take chelators unless they are taken on a very strict schedule (every 3â€“4 hours even at night, a few days on, 10â€“14 days off) or they can cause more mercury to enter the brain, especially within 3 months of mercury filling removal.** Lipoic acid administration can significantly enhance biliary excretion of inorganic mercury in rat experiments, although it is not known if this is due to chelation by lipoic acid or some other mechanism.\[97\] Lipoic acid has the potential to cross the blood-brain barrier in humans, unlike DMSA and DMPS; its effectiveness, however, is heavily dependent on the dosage and frequency of application.\[98\]

### chocolate — 2012-05-24T05:44:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipoic-acid-antioxidant-and-wiked-chelator-ummmmmm-fixes-ed-in-rats-peeps-anec.1414/post-26955>

[http://www.elmhurst.edu/~chm/vchembook/623acetylCoAfate.html](http://http://www.elmhurst.edu/~chm/vchembook/623acetylCoAfate.html)

Metabolic Fates of Acetyl CoA:

If you reflect on both the content lipid metabolism and the previous carbohydrate metabolism, you can appreciate that there is a special central role for acetyl CoA. Acetyl CoA acts both as a metabolic "receiving and shipping department" for all classes of biomolecules and as a major source of useful metabolic energy. The diagram on the left summarizes all metabolism and the central role of acetyl CoA. The diagram the next lower panel focuses on other functions as well.

The interactions of amino acids with acetyl CoA and the citric acid cycle will be studied in protein metabolism. Notice that acetyl CoA can react "reversibly" in the degradation or synthesis of lipids and amino acids. This is not the case with carbohydrate metabolism. In mammals, it is impossible to use acetyl CoA to make carbohydrates.

Synthesis of Cholesterol and other Steroids:

**Without going into detail, acetyl CoA forms the basis from which the fairly complicated steroids are synthesized. Some steroids of importance include cholesterol, bile salts, sex hormones, aldosterone, and cortisol.**

The major concern about cholesterol in the diet is muted somewhat by the knowledge that the liver can and does synthesize all of the cholesterol that the body needs. Excess cholesterol, whether from food or synthesized by the liver, ends up in the blood stream where it builds up on the artery walls. It has been determined that cholesterol levels can be controlled by lowering the amount of saturated fat and increasing the unsaturated fats. Unsaturated fats seem to speed the rate at which cholesterol breaks down in the blood. Controlling fats and cholesterol in the diet can significantly affect the levels of these substances in the blood.

My biggest benefit beyond losing more weight on LRX has been my overall mode and sleep patterns. I sleep like a rock and did before CT but it is even better now with CT. I haven't had to take a melatonin since February. If I wake up I always go back to sleep. My mood is much improved, probably because I sleep at night, but could be the reset...not really sure. I only then mentioned, "Garlic is good, right? Get off all grains, Linda. I'm sure you don't have sugar."

She replied, "Yes garlic is good. I dont do grains. I don't eat that much at all, I have slow digestion do to my diabetes and I am never hungry. I have to eat small meals. can't eat red meat because it takes longer to digest."

I could suggest more seafood, but I suspect CT is key. I am looking for something that would quickly grab her attention & give hope with an action plan.

### ColdBren — 2012-05-13T03:33:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-should-i.1430/post-23565>

I would like to lose 15-20 lbs. I have done one hcg round in the past. I am doing well on the leptin Rx, but the scale is not moving. I really want to have some of this weight off by Aug. for some events I am going to. Should I do hcg again? Will I be messing anything up again that I have worked to fix with the leptin Rx? I am really torn. Advice?

### colleencoble — 2012-05-13T04:06:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcg-should-i.1430/post-23575>

Only you can make that decision. I have not lost anything on the leptin reset or CT so I decided to do hcg to lose another 10-15 pounds and give the CT + fat a try for Doc. I firmly believe that hcg helped me get LS quickly. I was already part way there after my rounds. it didn't take much. If you do it, I would continue to eat your breakfast first thing in the morning and then make sure you don't eat more often than every 4-5 hours.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-13T15:57:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gravity-latitude-correction.1433/post-23643>

[http://cires.colorado.edu/people/sheehan.anne/gravhandout.html](http://http://cires.colorado.edu/people/sheehan.anne/gravhandout.html)

**

4\. Latitude correction. Gravity varies with latitude because the Earth is not a perfect sphere and the polar radius is smaller than the equitorial radius, also, the effect of centrifugal acceleration is different at the poles versus the equator. Thus gravitational acceleration is larger at the poles than at the equator.**

### chocolate — 2012-05-13T21:43:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gravity-latitude-correction.1433/post-23731>

Can 50 Hz magnetic fields alter iron metabolism and induce anaemia?

Devevey L, BrugÃ¨re H, Debray M, Bernard M, Pupin F, Patinot C, Jacquemont C, Guillosson JJ, Nafziger J.

Source

Laboratoire d'HÃ©matologie Cellulaire et MolÃ©culaire, UPRES-EA 2509, UFR des Sciences Pharmaceutiques et Biologiques, Paris, France.

Abstract

PURPOSE. Some changes in tissue iron concentration have been reported in animals exposed to electromagnetic fields. In other studies, variations in the haemoglobin level were occasionally observed. In the present experiment, the effects of exposing a rat to a 50 Hz magnetic field (MF) were therefore investigated for the possible induction of anaemia due to changes in iron metabolism.

MATERIALS AND METHODS:

Male Brown Norway rats (n=225) were exposed to a sinusoidal 50 Hz MF of 500 microT for 15 weeks. Haematological parameters, differential bone marrow cell counts and sideroblasts were investigated. Blood parameters of iron metabolism were measured. Iron concentration and total iron content were also determined in the spleen and liver, to assess iron storage in these organs.

**RESULTS:

Significant differences between the exposed and control rat were only detected for iron storage in the spleen, and for the percentage of bone marrow cells of the red cell lineage.

CONCLUSION:

The changes observed were not associated with anaemia during the 15 weeks of MF exposure. However, the decrease in bone marrow cells of the red cell lineage and the changes in iron storage detected at the end of the experiment did not allow the possibility to be ruled out that exposure to 50 Hz MFs may induced delayed biological effects.**

### chocolate — 2012-05-13T21:50:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gravity-latitude-correction.1433/post-23732>

[http://www.unc.edu/depts/oceanweb/turtles/magmethod.html](http://http://www.unc.edu/depts/oceanweb/turtles/magmethod.html)

To a first approximation, the Earth's magnetic field resembles that of an enormous bar magnet. The field lines emerge from the southern half of the earth and re-enter in the northern half.

Several features of the Earth's field vary in a predictable way across the surface of the globe and might, in principle, be used in assessing geographic position. For example, at each location on the Earth, the field lines intersect the Earth's surface at a specific angle of inclination. On the diagram above, note that near the equator, the field lines are approximately parallel to the Earth's surface; the inclination angle in this region is said to be 0Â°. As one travels north from the equator, however, the field lines become progressively steeper. At the magnetic pole, the field lines are directed almost straight down into the Earth and the inclination is said to be 90Â°. Thus, inclination angle varies with latitude. **As a consequence, an animal that has the ability to distinguish between magnetic inclination angles has a mechanism that it might be able to use to approximate its latitude. As we will discuss, hatchling loggerheads have been shown to have this ability.

**A second geomagnetic parameter that varies across the surface of the Earth is the strength or intensity of the field. Although intensity varies somewhat less predictably than inclination, the general trend is that the field is strongest near the magnetic poles and is weakest near the equator. Hatchling loggerheads have also been shown to detect magnetic field intensity.**

Important Features of the Earth's field:

Inclination angle: The angle at which the magnetic field lines intersect the surface of the earth. This angle ranges from 0 degrees at the equator to 90 degrees at the poles.

Intensity: The magnetic field also varies in strength over the earth's surface. It is strongest at the poles and weakest at the equator.**

### chocolate — 2012-05-13T21:58:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gravity-latitude-correction.1433/post-23734>

[http://www-ssc.igpp.ucla.edu/personnel/russell/papers/Fld.Alnd.Currents/](http://http://www-ssc.igpp.ucla.edu/personnel/russell/papers/Fld.Alnd.Currents/)

Field Aligned Currents in the High Latitude, High Altitude Magnetosphere: POLAR Initial Results

C. T. Russell1, X-W Zhou1, Guan Le1, P. H. Reiff 2, J. G. Luhmann3, C. A. Cattell4 and H. Kawano5

1\. Institute of Geophysics and Planetary Physics, University of California, Los Angeles, CA 90095-1567

2\. Center for Space Physics, Rice University, Houston, TX 77251-1892

3\. Space Sciences Laboratory, University of California, Berkeley, CA 94720

4\. School of Physics & Astronomy, University of Minnesota, Minneapolis, MN 55455-0112

5\. Nagoya University, STE Lab., Honahara 3-13, Toyokawa, Aichi 442, Japan

Originally published in:

Geophysical Research Letters, 24, 1455-1458, 1997.

Abstract

Magnetic field measurements obtained by the POLAR spacecraft are used to map out the region 1 and region 2 field-aligned current systems in the dayside hemisphere at radial distances of close to 8 Earth radii above the northern polar cap. The extrapolated invariant latitude of these currents is quite variable. The equatorward edge of the region 1 system ranges from 73 to 83 degrees invariant latitude. **The sense of both systems reverses across noon as expected. The strength of the currents also varies, averaging about 0.03 A/m for the region 1 currents and 0.01 A/m for the region 2 currents. Over the dayside hemisphere this amounts to about 1.5 million amps flowing down and up the field lines in the region 1 system and about 0.5 million amps in the region 2 system. These numbers and the behavior of the current are quite consistent with low-altitude observations.**

the magnetic fields change at noon for day and night....now if could just figure out what a week means... I understand a moonth

### chocolate — 2012-05-13T22:01:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gravity-latitude-correction.1433/post-23735>

"An Impact Basin as Deep as Mount Everest" --Was **Mars'** Magnetic Field Destroyed by a Colossal Asteroid?

This type of changes happened 170 years ago and 370 years ago, both times were followed by ICE AGE within about 10 years since the pattern happened, with reduction of Sun Spots.

HINODE will have a chance to research Sun's Arctic Circle closely in Oct 2012.

### chocolate — 2012-05-14T00:44:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gravity-latitude-correction.1433/post-23758>

Proceedings of the Royal Society of London. Series A, Mathematical and Physical Sciences Â© 1941 The Royal Society

[http://www.jstor.org/discover/10.2307/97621?uid=3739560&uid=2129&uid=2&uid=70&uid=4&uid=3739256&sid=56168768483](http://http://www.jstor.org/discover/10.2307/97621?uid=3739560&uid=2129&uid=2&uid=70&uid=4&uid=3739256&sid=56168768483)

Abstract:

The diurnal and seasonal variations in the vertical cosmic ray intensity, produced by a solar magnetic dipole field, are calculated at latitudes 0 and 45⚬, and compared with 'observed' data. It appears that the diurnal variation at latitude 45circ can be largely accounted for by assuming the existence of a solar dipole of moment 1Â· 1 Ã— 1034 gauss cm.3, a value which is consistent with observational evidence. The **diurnal variation at the equator, however, cannot be explained by the hypothesis of a solar magnetic dipole field. The seasonal variation in intensity inferred by the above value for the solar dipole moment is of the same order of magnitude as the observational variation, _but shows a phase discrepancy of two months_.**

### chocolate — 2012-11-12T19:49:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gravity-latitude-correction.1433/post-63849>

[http://www.drsinatra.com/could-your-home-be-giving-you-high-blood-pressure](http://http://www.drsinatra.com/could-your-home-be-giving-you-high-blood-pressure)

> Could Your Home Be Giving You High Blood Pressure?
>
>
>
> by Dr. Stephen Sinatra
>
>
>
> Filed Under: General Health, Blood Pressure
>
>
>
> Last Reviewed 11/12/2012
>
>
>
> \[Could Your Home Be Giving You High Blood Pressure?\]
>
>
>
> As you know, I spend a lot of my time getting the word out about the dangers of wireless technology. Electropollution is all around us. A recent case study posted on the website hosted by Citizens for Safe Technology really drives home the fact that this is an issue we can’t ignore.
>
>
>
> A German man went to the hospital in a hypertensive crisis. His blood pressure quickly stabilized in the emergency room. Then, after a week in the hospital where his blood pressure normalized, he was allowed to return home—where his blood pressure rose again.
>
>
>
> His indoor wireless transmitter had been turned off, and the DECT cordless phone disabled, so the ambient radiation levels in his home were low. So, the hypertensive fellow looked to some other environmental culprit for raising his blood pressure. What the gentleman finally discovered was that a second handset charging station was continuing to transmit. So, he’d been unknowingly sleeping in an area where the RF exposure was 140 ÂµW/m2—a level well below industry standards of “safe exposure.”
>
>
>
> Once that second charger—which was supposedly deemed “safe”—was disabled, his blood pressure normalized. This documented case adds to the research in the field and also confirms my personal experience of the DECT phone—one of the most commonly used, yet most toxic cordless phones used worldwide.
>
>
>
> In fact, a DECT cordless phone caused a medical setback for my son Step when he returned home from his month-long hospitalization. His weakened immune system started backsliding, and it took quite a while for us to question the cordless phone at his bedside. Removing it got him back on his healing track, made us aware of his electro-hypersensitivity, and got him looking at other areas of exposure.
>
>
>
> If you have high blood pressure, or any chronic illness for that matter, I suggest that you look at your home and work environments for technology health hazards:
>
>
>
> Do you have digital cordless phones and their bases scattered around your house?
>
> Do you have cell phones lurking or charging in your sleeping area?
>
> Do you have WiFi? If so, is it always on, or do you turn it off when not in use?
>
> Do you use video games like Xbox and Wii? Do you know that they emit even when turned off if they are not also unplugged?
>
> Do you live in close proximity to a cell tower?

# Thread: Prep for testing - fasting, supplements, etc.
