### BJK77 — 2012-05-13T16:08:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prep-for-testing-fasting-supplements-etc.1434/post-23645>

My doctor ordered a ton of tests and I quit CTing to prepare for them so I wouldn't be going through any major detoxing that would skew the results. I'm literally craving a cold bath and can't wait to get back on track! I know I'm going to have to split the test up into at least 2 visits because there's just so many, but I'm hoping to go tomorrow and possibly Tuesday to have them done. He did say to fast for them because there were several that required it, but I don't think they all do and he's on vacation this week so I can't ask him to clarify. I was thinking if some don't require fasting I could start out in the morning and then go back in the afternoon to finish up.

Can anyone tell me if any of these DON'T require fasting? I'm also wondering about supplementation and how long before testing we should stop, if at all. I'll make a note of those below too for any input. Thanks!

CBC

CMP

ANA w/ reflex titer

Full Thyroid Panel (TSH, FT4, FT3, antibodies, etc)

DHEA - _\*\*How long before testing should I discontinue supplementation?

Pregnenolone - _\*\*Ditto on supplementation question

Western Blot Lyme

Iodine

Rheumatoid Factor

Insulin

Homocysteine

Vit D / B12 - _\*\*Again questioning when to discontinue supplementation?_

LDH

LH

Mag - I know this is useless, but I'm just going to humor him for now and then ask for the Exatest_

FSH

Full Iron Panel

HCA17135 - not sure what this is - any ideas??_

### Martin — 2012-05-14T14:53:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prep-for-testing-fasting-supplements-etc.1434/post-23869>

Nice line-up! Can you get the good doc to add HS CRP?

I go to the Labcorp site and look up the info on the test and that page will usually tell you about prepping for it.

Supplementation? That's a tougher question. I seem to remember someone posting that a week was sufficient, but considering all the variables, longer may be required. I did 10 days before B-12 and D.

Martin

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-14T19:15:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prep-for-testing-fasting-supplements-etc.1434/post-23929>

I stopped supplementing two weeks (just to be sure) before I did the micronutrient test. I also think for those doing a saliva cortisol test you may want to stop the Bs, C and of course any adaptogens taken specifically for adrenals.

### JC2K — 2012-05-14T22:01:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prep-for-testing-fasting-supplements-etc.1434/post-23982>

I had 3 major groups of blood tests done in one day (probably more than what you have). 13 vials of blood. Mixture of ones that needed fasting vs not. I just fasted for all of them and they drew them all at one time (wasn't a problem. They stuck the needle in one vein and shuffled through the vials). I didn't stop supplementation per se (I just had water during the fasting period, no supplements). But then I'm not taking your stuff, so I don't know.

The heads are kinda hard to chew so I just throw them in some beef stock with a piece of kelp and eat it after it's softened and drink the broth. **Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-14T00:06:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wikipedia-leaky-gut-article.1442/post-23755>

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leaky\_gut](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leaky_gut)

Treatment

Researchers propose intake of natural anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative substances, such as glutamine, N-acetyl cysteine and zinc, in conjunction with a leaky gut diet, prebiotics and the use of probiotics.\[7\]\[14\]\[15\] Berberine is a constituent of several herbs which have been traditionally used to treat gastrointestinal disorders. According to a 2010 study, berberine ameliorated damage to the tight junctions of intestinal epithelial cells induced by pro-inflammatory cytokines (in vitro). Berberine may thus serve as a targeted therapeutic agent for restoring barrier function in intestinal disease states.\[16\]

# Thread: berberine as a metformin alternative?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/berberine-as-a-metformin-alternative.1443/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-14T00:16:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/berberine-as-a-metformin-alternative.1443/post-23756>

[http://www.naturalmedicinejournal.com/article\_content.asp?article=61](http://http://www.naturalmedicinejournal.com/article_content.asp?article=61)

Friday, October 01, 2010

by: By Ronald Steriti, ND, PhD

Section: Peer-Reviewed Articles

About the Author

Ronald Steriti, ND, is a researcher for Dr.

Jonathan V. Wright, director of the Tahoma

Clinic and Meridian Valley Labs. Steriti is a

graduate of the Southwest College of Naturopathic

Medicine.

Abstract

**This brief review focuses on several studies that show the plant alkaloid berberine can lower

blood glucose as effectively as the drug metformin at similar doses (500 mg 3x/day). Berberine

acts on glucose metabolism through several mechanisms, including (AMP-activated protein

kinase) AMPK and incretins.**

### chocolate — 2012-06-08T00:18:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/berberine-as-a-metformin-alternative.1443/post-29988>

[http://www.nature.com/ncomms/journal/v3/n5/full/ncomms1859.html](http://http://www.nature.com/ncomms/journal/v3/n5/full/ncomms1859.html)

Metformin elicits anticancer effects through the sequential modulation of DICER and c-MYC

> Diabetic patients treated with metformin have a reduced incidence of cancer and cancer-related mortality. Here we show that metformin affects engraftment and growth of breast cancer tumours in mice. This correlates with the induction of metabolic changes compatible with clear anticancer effects. We demonstrate that microRNA modulation underlies the anticancer metabolic actions of metformin. In fact, metformin induces DICER expression and its effects are severely impaired in DICER knocked down cells. Conversely, ectopic expression of DICER recapitulates the effects of metformin in vivo and in vitro. The microRNAs upregulated by metformin belong mainly to energy metabolism pathways. Among the messenger RNAs downregulated by metformin, we found c-MYC, IRS-2 and HIF1alpha. Downregulation of c-MYC requires AMP-activated protein kinase-signalling and mir33a upregulation by metformin. Ectopic expression of c-MYC attenuates the anticancer metabolic effects of metformin. We suggest that DICER modulation, mir33a upregulation and c-MYC targeting have an important role in the anticancer metabolic effects of metformin.

I didn't search to compare the pathways of metformine and berberine.

### chocolate — 2012-10-20T17:15:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/berberine-as-a-metformin-alternative.1443/post-59335>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21422199](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21422199)

> Mol Cancer Res. 2011 May;9(5):603-15. Epub 2011 Mar 21.
>
> Metformin induces both caspase-dependent and poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase-dependent cell death in breast cancer cells.
>
> Zhuang Y, Miskimins WK.
>
> Source
>
>
>
> Cancer Biology Research Center, Sanford Research/USD, 2301 East 60th Street-North, Sioux Falls, SD 57104, USA.
>
> Abstract
>
>
>
> There is substantial evidence that metformin, a drug used to treat type 2 diabetics, is potentially useful as a therapeutic agent for cancer. However, a better understanding of the molecular mechanisms through which metformin promotes cell-cycle arrest and cell death of cancer cells is necessary. It will also be important to understand how the response of tumor cells differs from normal cells and why some tumor cells are resistant to the effects of metformin. **We have found that exposure to metformin induces cell death in all but one line, MDA-MB-231, in a panel of breast cancer cell lines.** MCF10A nontransformed breast epithelial cells were resistant to the cytotoxic effects of metformin, even after extended exposure to the drug. In sensitive lines, cell death was mediated by both apoptosis and a caspase-independent mechanism. The caspase-independent pathway involves activation of poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase (PARP) and correlates with enhanced synthesis of PARP and nuclear translocation of apoptosis-inducing factor (AIF), which plays an important role in mediating cell death. Metformin-induced, PARP-dependent cell death is associated with a striking enlargement of mitochondria. Mitochondrial enlargement was observed in all sensitive breast cancer cell lines but not in nontransformed cells or resistant MDA-MB-231. Mitochondrial enlargement was prevented by inhibiting PARP activity or expression. A caspase inhibitor blocked metformin-induced apoptosis but did not affect PARP-dependent cell death or mitochondrial enlargement. Thus, metformin has cytotoxic effects on breast cancer cells through 2 independent pathways. These findings will be pertinent to efforts directed at using metformin or related compounds for cancer therapy.

I hadn't heard about this study.

### chocolate — 2012-10-20T17:20:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/berberine-as-a-metformin-alternative.1443/post-59338>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22574158](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/22574158)

> Berberine induces caspase-independent cell death in colon tumor cells through activation of apoptosis-inducing factor.
>
> Wang L, Liu L, Shi Y, Cao H, Chaturvedi R, Calcutt MW, Hu T, Ren X, Wilson KT, Polk DB, Yan F.
>
> Source
>
>
>
> Department of Pediatrics, Vanderbilt University Medical Center and the Monroe Carell Jr. Children's Hospital at Vanderbilt, Nashville, Tennessee, United States of America.
>
> Abstract
>
>
>
> Berberine, an isoquinoline alkaloid derived from plants, is a traditional medicine for treating bacterial diarrhea and intestinal parasite infections. Although berberine has recently been shown to suppress growth of several tumor cell lines, information regarding the effect of berberine on colon tumor growth is limited. Here, we investigated the mechanisms underlying the effects of berberine on regulating the fate of colon tumor cells, specifically the mouse immorto-Min colonic epithelial (IMCE) cells carrying the Apc(min) mutation, and of normal colon epithelial cells, namely young adult mouse colonic epithelium (YAMC) cells. Berberine decreased colon tumor colony formation in agar, and induced cell death and LDH release in a time- and concentration-dependent manner in IMCE cells. In contrast, YAMC cells were not sensitive to berberine-induced cell death. Berberine did not stimulate caspase activation, and PARP cleavage and berberine-induced cell death were not affected by a caspase inhibitor in IMCE cells. Rather, berberine stimulated a caspase-independent cell death mediator, apoptosis-inducing factor (AIF) release from mitochondria and nuclear translocation in a ROS production-dependent manner. Amelioration of berberine-stimulated ROS production or suppression of AIF expression blocked berberine-induced cell death and LDH release in IMCE cells. Furthermore, two targets of ROS production in cells, cathepsin B release from lysosomes and PARP activation were induced by berberine. Blockage of either of these pathways decreased berberine-induced AIF activation and cell death in IMCE cells. Thus, berberine-stimulated ROS production leads to cathepsin B release and PARP activation-dependent AIF activation, resulting in caspase-independent cell death in colon tumor cells. Notably, normal colon epithelial cells are less susceptible to berberine-induced cell death, which suggests the specific inhibitory effects of berberine on colon tumor cell growth.

or this one

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-21T01:13:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/berberine-as-a-metformin-alternative.1443/post-59398>

I had read some of those, too, Chocolate... as well as studies showing berberine had equal results as metformin re: BG, but even better results in other measurement domains. Looks to me like both are very good. Since metformin is a pharmaceutical product, it will get much more research behind it.

# Thread: Starfish Radiation Belt
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starfish-radiation-belt.1445/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-14T02:54:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starfish-radiation-belt.1445/post-23779>

[http://www-spof.gsfc.nasa.gov/Education/FAQs2.html#q25](http://http://www-spof.gsfc.nasa.gov/Education/FAQs2.html#q25)

[http://www.phy6.org/Education/Intro.html](http://http://www.phy6.org/Education/Intro.html)

[http://www.npr.org/templates/story/story.php?storyId=128170775](http://http://www.npr.org/templates/story/story.php?storyId=128170775)

Code Name: Starfish Prime

The Americans launched their first atomic nuclear tests above the Earth's atmosphere in 1958. Atom bombs had little effect on the magnetosphere, but the hydrogen bomb of July 9, 1962, did. Code-named "Starfish Prime" by the military, it literally created an artificial extension of the Van Allen belts that could be seen across the Pacific Ocean, from Hawaii to New Zealand.

### chocolate — 2012-05-14T03:02:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starfish-radiation-belt.1445/post-23780>

[http://www.sccs.swarthmore.edu/users/08/ajb/tmve/wiki100k/docs/List\_of\_artificial\_radiation\_belts.html](http://http://www.sccs.swarthmore.edu/users/08/ajb/tmve/wiki100k/docs/List_of_artificial_radiation_belts.html)

List of artificial radiation belts

Artificial radiation belts are radiation belts that have been created by high altitude nuclear explosions.\[1\]\[2\]\[3\]\[4\]\[5\]

The table above only lists those high-altitude nuclear explosions for which a reference exists in the open (unclassified) English-language scientific literature to persistent artificial radiation belts resulting from the explosion.

The Starfish Prime radiation belt had, by far, the greatest intensity and duration of any of the artificial radiation belts.\[1\]

The Starfish Prime radiation belt damaged the United States satellites Ariel 1, Traac, Transit 4B, Injun I and Telstar I. It also damaged the Soviet satellite Cosmos V. All of these satellites failed completely within several months of the Starfish detonation.\[1\]

Telstar I lasted the longest of the satellites damaged by the Starfish Prime radiation, with its complete failure occurring on February 21, 1963.\[6\]

In Los Alamos Scientific Laboratory report LA-6405, Herman Hoerlin gave the following explanation of the history of the original Argus experiment and of how the nuclear detonations lead to the development of artificial radiation belts.\[1\]

### chocolate — 2012-05-14T03:06:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starfish-radiation-belt.1445/post-23781>

# Thread: Ear Infections ugghh!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-infections-ugghh.1453/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2012-05-14T16:37:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-infections-ugghh.1453/post-23887>

Ok.. so ds is on the 2nd' round of abx - for a wicked ear infection - the only reason we knew about it was b/c he told us 3 friday's ago - about ear pain. went to our ped - and she told us it was the worst ear infection she's ever seen - head of the practice agreed...so we started w/amoxcillin - and a follow up 10days later...

so 10days later we go back and see the ped - he's still got it...now he's on another abx - cefedir - we go back in another 10days (actually 6 days b/c he's been on the new abx since Thursday)

Ped now thinks ds late talking, problems w/talking etc is all related to his hearing. she thinks he may have been having ear infections w/o showing symptoms since he was little. he always loves to pull/play w/his ears - so we can't rely on his playing w/his ears as an indication of an ear infection. he also doesn't get fevers when he has ear infections!

We see the ENT in 6wks (earliest apt) to check his hearing and see what the proper course of treatment is!

still waiting on our appt w/ Child Find for his speech eval...

we're pushing bone broths, soups, probiotics extra hard considering he's on serious abx .... the life of chasing after optimal health... not only for yourself but for your kids as well....

### Glamorama — 2012-05-15T02:36:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-infections-ugghh.1453/post-24067>

Have you considered chiropractic treatment, and cutting out dairy for him? Both help immensely for kids with reoccurring ear-infections. The chiropractor adjusts the upper neck, and that normally releases the tension that might in turn affect the build-up of germs in his ear canal.

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-15T04:38:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-infections-ugghh.1453/post-24108>

The kids see a Chiro monthly. Ds has only been "out" 2xs in his 2yrs of life . As for dairy we limit it in general at the house & I've considered cutting it back at home but state law says he hast to have milk at Daycare unless he has an allergy. We' ll go to the ent then look at an allergist ugh. He only drink goats milk at dcp. He still nurses at home 2x/day. Ped thinks his etruscan (spelling??) tubes might be a bit faulty. Also thinks this might be why he's a late talker.

### MJ* — 2012-05-15T15:27:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-infections-ugghh.1453/post-24176>

>

> We see the ENT in 6wks (earliest apt) to check his hearing and see what the proper course of treatment is!

I had a feeling it could be the ears, from experience with students of mine...that's why I suggested getting that checked. The good thing is you know now and you can work with that. Once the ear health is cleared the speech may not be an issue, time will tell. It's so annoying to have to wait so long for an appointment...let us know how it goes.

### MJ* — 2012-05-15T15:30:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-infections-ugghh.1453/post-24177>

> **Shijin13;24190 wrote:** The kids see a Chiro monthly. Ds has only been "out" 2xs in his 2yrs of life . As for dairy we limit it in general at the house & I've considered cutting it back at home but state law says he hast to have milk at Daycare unless he has an allergy. We' ll go to the ent then look at an allergist ugh. He only drink goats milk at dcp. He still nurses at home 2x/day. Ped thinks his etruscan (spelling??) tubes might be a bit faulty. Also thinks this might be why he's a late talker.

Eustacian tubes...that's the part of the ear that fills with fluid, and some kids eustacian tubes fill because of how they lay in the ear. Once they get the fluid down, that's when you'll be able to tell about the speech.

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-15T15:40:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-infections-ugghh.1453/post-24184>

> **MJ\*;24259 wrote:** Eustacian tubes...that's the part of the ear that fills with fluid, and some kids eustacian tubes fill because of how they lay in the ear. Once they get the fluid down, that's when you'll be able to tell about the speech.

yeah... that's why the ped thinks his delayed speech is 100% related to his hearing/ears. we'll see what the plan of attack is once we see the ENT ....

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-15T16:31:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-infections-ugghh.1453/post-24204>

> **Shijin13;24266 wrote:** yeah... that's why the ped thinks his delayed speech is 100% related to his hearing/ears. we'll see what the plan of attack is once we see the ENT ....

Ugh, sorry you (and baby shijin) are going through this. We had a ton of probs with baby related to this during months 1-18. We ended up having to sleep with one of us holding him upright. For 18 months. Baby couldn't lay down. Perhaps some mechanical relief for healing like figuring out how their head needs to be positioned with their layout. We got a nice recliner and just went with it.

Good news is one day it just started working right (18 months) and all was good. At first signs of infection after that we dripped a little garlic oil in ear and all was good.

We ended up having to sleep with one of us holding him upright. For 18 months. Baby couldn't lay down. Perhaps some mechanical relief for healing like figuring out how their head needs to be positioned with their layout. We got a nice recliner and just went with it.
>

>
> Good news is one day it just started working right (18 months) and all was good. At first signs of infection after that we dripped a little garlic oil in ear and all was good.

ds had reflux - that he out grew around 13mths... but the ear infections - he doesn't really give clues things are going on - he doesn't present ear infections normally - as far as we know this is his 2nd one - but that may not be the actual case. since birth he's played w/his ears - we figure its a comfort thing - b/c he still does it when he goes to sleep or nurses. he also does when he's upset... so for him playing w/ears isn't an indicator. then there's the fact he only pops fevers when he's teething & bringing in 4 teeth at a time- and then its a whopper of a fever (104 for ~6 days or so!)

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-15T16:54:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-infections-ugghh.1453/post-24214>

we'll get there eventually... so who knows... btwn dcp and dh and I we've really been focusing on working on his letters, numbers and cultivating his interest in reading... so we'll see

### MJ* — 2012-05-15T17:23:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-infections-ugghh.1453/post-24225>

> **Shijin13;24296 wrote:** we'll get there eventually... so who knows... btwn dcp and dh and I we've really been focusing on working on his letters, numbers and cultivating his interest in reading... so we'll see

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-15T17:28:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ear-infections-ugghh.1453/post-24227>

> **MJ\*;24307 wrote:** Focus on the other senses to help him compensate, lots of visuals and tactiles - play with and show him things he can taste, touch and feel, smell, and whole body movement for numbers - like be bunnies and hop the counts...

yup we're doing that... we've got lots of tactile books... we encourage finger painting, playing w/playdough and clay, as well as digging in the dirt! he's got an amazing imagination at 2! so we encourage it. he even likes to do the learning apps on the ipad we got for his sister and he does pretty well w/it! just got to keep working it w/him..

we just heard back from Child Find regarding ds's delayed speech... they think he's in the normal range for now - they want to know how things go after seeing the ENT - then do a re-assessment in 4 mths, to see if there's any positive steps fwd. so we'll get it all figured out.... now if we could just get off the antibiotics for good we'd be golden.... good thing he's still nursing 2x/day - its probably saving his gut from total demolition!

# Thread: Becoming cold adapted?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-cold-adapted.1456/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-05-14T19:13:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-cold-adapted.1456/post-23927>

For a while now I've been ale to easily handle an hour plus in 50 degree water. Just the past week I've started to notice that I'm cold in normal situations like I used to be. For instance yesterday at my moms I felt quite warm and mentioned that it was warm inside to my parents. My mom looked at me strange and said, it's only 70 degrees in here, I'm freezing and you usually are when I am. I was a bit uncomfortably warm though. Does this mean something is finally starting to happen in my body? Or am I just reading too much into this? What sorts of things have you noticed to tip you off that you're becoming cold adapted?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-14T21:40:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-cold-adapted.1456/post-23970>

My temperature, taken orally, goes up approximately two degrees now from when I jump in the tub to about 30 min into it. It took me a long time to get to that point with 55-58 water. Now my water is consistently in the high 50s (tap is in the mid-high 60s) and need more of those reusable ice cubes! They seem to work quite well. I also noticed I like the house temp lower than 68 (66) than I used to. But I also think I still have a ways to go to get totally cold adapted.

### PaleoMom — 2012-05-14T21:50:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-cold-adapted.1456/post-23973>

I'm comfy indoors down to about 62 now and outside down to 50 if it's sunny in a t-shirt, jeans and bare feet. I tend to get really hot at night sometimes. I sleep with the window open (outside temp mid 30's to low 40's at night) and for at least half the night I have only a sheet over me. I don't need that either, but I have a hard time sleeping without something over me (like the sheet is actually protecting from anything )

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-14T21:50:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/becoming-cold-adapted.1456/post-23974>

I think jack has defined it as when your temp goes up after being in the water (or cold air) -core temp- and a couple of degrees. In some folks it will go up \*in anticipation\* of getting In the water (very cold adapted) so if you're curious check your temp before during and after CT and see what your trends are..... Forget which blog or comment it was where I read this!

Also have heard him say in response to a ? That being cold adapted actually makes you tolerate heat better too, as well as cold (which I have already noticed) ....

Other then that could just be hormone changing or something else making you hot? I'm sure I've heard someone else report this and JK commented so check the CT series blog comments and CT threads....

# Thread: MSG...now I think I really get it
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/msg-now-i-think-i-really-get-it.1461/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-14T23:49:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/msg-now-i-think-i-really-get-it.1461/post-24016>

[http://blogs.discovermagazine.com/crux/2012/05/14/is-the-purpose-of-sleep-to-let-our-brains-defragment-like-a-hard-drive/](http://http://blogs.discovermagazine.com/crux/2012/05/14/is-the-purpose-of-sleep-to-let-our-brains-defragment-like-a-hard-drive/)

Is the Purpose of Sleep to Let Our Brains â€œDefragment,â€ Like a Hard Drive?

Yet this poses a problem for the brain. If LTP and synapse formation is constantly strengthening our synapses, and we are learning all our lives, might the synapses eventually reach a limit? Couldnâ€™t they â€œmax out,â€ so that they could never get any stronger?

**Worse, most of the synapses that strengthen during memory are based on glutamate. Glutamate is dangerous. Itâ€™s the most common neurotransmitter in the brain, and itâ€™s also a popular flavouring: â€œMSGâ€, monosodium glutamate. But in the brain, too much of it is toxic.

Glutamate works as a transmitter molecule by opening channels on the cells that receive it. The channels allow calcium into the cells on the receiving end, which activates them, allowing messages to go through. But too much glutamate can cause excess calcium to build up inside the very cells that receive the message, a harmful process called excitotoxicity.**

So, if our brains were constantly forming stronger glutamate synapses, we might eventually run into serious problems. One function of sleep, according to the theory, is to protect the brain against excitotoxicity or other â€œsynaptic overloadâ€ problems by pruning the synapses.

### chocolate — 2012-05-14T23:55:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/msg-now-i-think-i-really-get-it.1461/post-24021>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/20968043](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/20968043)

\[Effect of long-term monosodium glutamate administration on structure and functional state of the stomach and body weight in rats\].

Abstract

The influence of prolonged administration of monosodium glutamate (MSG) on basal gastric acid secretion, body weight and gastric mucosa in rats was studied. **We found that 10-, 20-, 30-days feeding by MSG in doses 15 to 30 mg/kg (equivalent to I and 2 g/person) leads to erosive and ulcerative lesions of the gastric mucosa and an increased secretion of hydrochloric acid and an increased body weight. It is concluded that the stimulating effect of MSG on the basal secretion of hydrochloric acid in the stomach may be implicated in the pathogenesis of a number of acid-dependent diseases. An excessive consumption of MSG can cause a "Chinese Restaurant Syndrome" and gastritis, gastric and duodenal ulcers. Therefore, the maximum dose of MSG should be reconsidered taking into account its influence on the secretory capacity of the stomach.** We also conclude that prolonged, excessive and systemic consumption of MSG causes obesity.

### chocolate — 2012-05-15T00:04:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/msg-now-i-think-i-really-get-it.1461/post-24024>

[http://jn.nutrition.org/content/102/7/835.full.pdf](http://http://jn.nutrition.org/content/102/7/835.full.pdf)

Effects of Dietary Vitamin B6 on the Utilization of

Monosodium Glutamate by Rats '

CHI-PANG WEN AND STANLEY N. GERSHOFF

Department of Nutrition, Harvard School of Public Health

There appeared to be decreased clear

ance of intravenously administered gluta

mate in deficient animals. This might have

been the result of diminished tissue uptake

of the amino acid or of delayed catabolism

of the amino acid. Beaton et al. (13) have

reported that vitamin Be-deficient rats have

a decreased ability to deaminate amino

acids and that their utilization of glutamic

acid is impaired. It has also been shown

that vitamin Be deficiency depresses the

aminotransferase systems most important

to glutamic acid metabolism in rat liver

(14), blood (15), and heart (16). In this

study similar observations were obtained

on the intestinal aminotransferases. These

enzymes are of particular importance be

cause they determine to a considerable ex

tent the amount of glutamic acid which

enters the portal and systemic circulation

from the intestines.

**This article is from 1972**

### indigogirl — 2012-05-15T01:51:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/msg-now-i-think-i-really-get-it.1461/post-24057>

Certain artificial sweeteners are excitotoxins...so are they doing the same thing to the brain that MSG does?

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-15T02:43:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/msg-now-i-think-i-really-get-it.1461/post-24070>

Yes.

[http://www.truthinlabeling.org](http://http://www.truthinlabeling.org)

[http://truthinlabeling.org/II.WhereIsMSG.html](http://http://truthinlabeling.org/II.WhereIsMSG.html)

### angieonthegogo — 2012-05-22T04:47:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/msg-now-i-think-i-really-get-it.1461/post-26184>

> **chocolate;24098 wrote: [http://blogs.discovermagazine.com/crux/2012/05/14/is-the-purpose-of-sleep-to-let-our-brains-defragment-like-a-hard-drive/](http://http://blogs.discovermagazine.com/crux/2012/05/14/is-the-purpose-of-sleep-to-let-our-brains-defragment-like-a-hard-drive/)
>
> Is the Purpose of Sleep to Let Our Brains “Defragment,” Like a Hard Drive?
>
>
>
> Yet this poses a problem for the brain. If LTP and synapse formation is constantly strengthening our synapses, and we are learning all our lives, might the synapses eventually reach a limit? Couldn’t they “max out,” so that they could never get any stronger?
>
>
>
> Worse, most of the synapses that strengthen during memory are based on glutamate. Glutamate is dangerous. It’s the most common neurotransmitter in the brain, and it’s also a popular flavouring: “MSG”, monosodium glutamate. But in the brain, too much of it is toxic.
>
>
>
> Glutamate works as a transmitter molecule by opening channels on the cells that receive it. The channels allow calcium into the cells on the receiving end, which activates them, allowing messages to go through. But too much glutamate can cause excess calcium to build up inside the very cells that receive the message, a harmful process called excitotoxicity.**
>
>
>
> So, if our brains were constantly forming stronger glutamate synapses, we might eventually run into serious problems. One function of sleep, according to the theory, is to protect the brain against excitotoxicity or other “synaptic overload” problems by pruning the synapses.

This helps to explain why when exposed to MSG I get headache within 15 mins that eventually turns into a migraine, stomach cramps, cold sweats, nasuea, increased heart rate, etc. Damned MSG shouldn't be added to ANYTHING but it is indeed in EVERYTHING!

### kjgraffin@yahoo.com — 2012-05-15T04:17:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-iron-kidney-liver-function-tests.1464/post-24098>

Would love for someone to take a look at these results and let me know what they think. I'm a little concerned with the ALT & AST levels but not sure if I should be. I'm wondering if taking Milk Thistle may have raised them.

BUN 26mg/dL H (6-24)

Creatinine, Serum .85mg/dL (.57-1.27)

BUN/Creatinine Ratio 31 H (9-23)

Protein, Serum 6.6 (6.0-8.5)

Globulin 2.7 (1.5-4.5)

Bilirubin .2 (0.0-1.2)

AST 34 (0-40)

ALT 50 H (0-40)

TIBC 291 (250-450)

UIBC 208 (150-375)

Iron, Serum 83 (35-155)

Iron Saturation 29 (15-55)

Ferritin 27 (13-150)

Also, my serum glucose was 93 (65-99) but I haven't eaten sugar in months, so I don't quite get that one.

Please hack away

### kjgraffin@yahoo.com — 2012-05-17T16:54:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-iron-kidney-liver-function-tests.1464/post-24886>

So, I've been doing some research on the iron levels. According to STTM, Ferritin levels below 50 is too low, serum iron should be 110 & iron saturation should be at least 35%. They noted that too much Milk Thistle can lower ferritin and that bacteria or viruses coudl be feeding on iron.

Now I'm really thinking I should either cut back or drop the Milk Thistle supplementation for a while and see what happens. I was taking it to help with the estrogen dumping.

Also, Jack made the following comment on another blog: "lactoferrin helps bind Fe in gut........"

Does that play into my numbers at all? My lactoferrin is 2.91 (1.00-7.24) but I don't know if it supposed to be on the low end or the high end.

Thoughts?

# Thread: How can I put webinar on itunes?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-can-i-put-webinar-on-itunes.1469/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dali Dula — 2012-05-15T15:05:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-can-i-put-webinar-on-itunes.1469/post-24168>

I purchased webinar which is in wmv format. Do you know how I can convert this so I can put the audio on my ipod? thanks

### admin — 2012-05-15T16:38:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-can-i-put-webinar-on-itunes.1469/post-24208>

It's a video file, not an audio file.

### JC2K — 2012-05-15T16:57:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-can-i-put-webinar-on-itunes.1469/post-24215>

What exactly are you looking for Dali? The audio or the video?

I'm assuming the WMV is not DRM protected. There's misc free websites that will convert WMV to MP3 if you just want the audio part. Depending on the size of the WMV file, they may want money. On the other hand, if it is an iPod that can play video, there are sites that will convert WMV to MP4 format to play on the iPod (again for money if it is too large). There is also software you can buy like Anysoft's Any Dvdconverter that will do it as well with tons of options (you have to pay for it).

Drag the resulting file to the correct area in iTunes and then sync it to your device.

I've used them all for Youtube videos for my iPhone and they've worked well enough, just search Google for them.

### Dali Dula — 2012-05-15T17:16:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-can-i-put-webinar-on-itunes.1469/post-24224>

The webinar although in wmv actually has no video. I just want the audio on my ipod. I was hoping there was a way to do this without third party software, but it seems lke that is the only way. A podcast format of the webinar would be awesome.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-15T17:45:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-can-i-put-webinar-on-itunes.1469/post-24234>

> **Dali Dula;24306 wrote:** The webinar although in wmv actually has no video. I just want the audio on my ipod. I was hoping there was a way to do this without third party software, but it seems lke that is the only way. A podcast format of the webinar would be awesome.

I agree with this! I would love to listen on idevice without video. Much smaller audio file would be peachy.

# Thread: Tinnitus r us
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tinnitus-r-us.1470/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 23

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-15T15:45:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tinnitus-r-us.1470/post-24186>

Through the years, I've had little blips of tinnitus that last for 1-3 mins. lately however, I've had a constant and louder ringing. Yesterday it was enough in interfere with sleep. And I love my sleep - very unwelcomed!

I've seen a few things about magnesium deficiency and hearing loss causes. For the record I've been supplementing like a champ for months. I do have some absorption issues - so that COULD be the issue.

I can't start trying to figure this out without at least looking at what I am doing differently NOW that might be triggering it.

I'm a good leptin RX girl, heavy seafood, CT, lots of supps. AND my friends - that is what worries me a little. Instead of "not enough of X", do any of you admirable minds have ideas on "too much of X"

I've got a doc appt tomorrow for lots of tests (yay! finally getting tests!) so I think I won't take any supps today and see if it backs off tonight.

I hate to be an internet whiner - but last night was a different tone in each ear and they were not nice 3rds, it was dischord. Can't have that!

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-15T15:56:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tinnitus-r-us.1470/post-24189>

Cavemam, sorry to hear this. All this talk about tinnitus made me realize that mine has disappeared! Weird. I wish I knew what it was. I take a lot of mag too, but I've been doing that for a while. So, I'm not sure what clear it up! Sorry, wish I could help...how about coffee? Are you doing coffee?

### Melinda — 2012-05-15T16:14:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tinnitus-r-us.1470/post-24197>

Too early in the morning for me to provide links but as I understand it, tinnitis is the result of hearing loss in spots along the frequency range where the brain adds in a "ghost" noise. You can go get brain training (link to follow later) that gets the brain to grab the deaf spots and train them to the neighboring spots that work/hear. That being said, why does it wax and wane? Mine buzzes when I'm hungry and other times. I'm still working on it.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-15T16:22:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tinnitus-r-us.1470/post-24199>

You can go get brain training (link to follow later) that gets the brain to grab the deaf spots and train them to the neighboring spots that work/hear. That being said, why does it wax and wane? Mine buzzes when I'm hungry and other times. I'm still working on it.

Thanks Mel - I'm going to look into this brain training. I know I have hearing loss in one side, and since I spent the better part of 10 years in marching bands surrounded by blasting trombones and trumpets - not a total surprise.

It's something to look at, after only one night of lost sleep I can see how people would go nuts. I was envisioning having to go see Jack to dissconnect something in my brain so I could at least just be deaf and experience sweet relief. I daydream too much.

I take a lot of mag too, but I've been doing that for a while. So, I'm not sure what clear it up! Sorry, wish I could help...how about coffee? Are you doing coffee?

Awesome that yours is gone. It's hopeful. I am going to get the rub-on mag that you guys have been raving about -stat. I am doing coffee. My day always starts with a great big cup of optimal.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-15T16:31:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tinnitus-r-us.1470/post-24205>

> **Cavemam;24283 wrote:** Awesome that yours is gone. It's hopeful. I am going to get the rub-on mag that you guys have been raving about -stat. I am doing coffee. My day always starts with a great big cup of optimal.

It's too bad I didn't realize when it stopped, thenI might have a clue to offer. I was lucky that although it was annoying, it never interrupted my sleep. Thank god. The only radically different things I am doing are the BAB, 5 Htp and L-tyrosine, and I cut out eating O6 (mayo, blue cheese dressing, almonds). Oh and I haven't had coffee except for a few cups here and there. Good luck!

### nonchalant — 2012-05-15T16:57:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tinnitus-r-us.1470/post-24216>

Hi Cavemam, sorry you're in distress. I had tinnitus that was getting fairly bad, but not enough to wake me up, before leaving SAD-land. All my senses were a bit wonky though.

Loud noises would irritate me; my family knew better than to slam the door or pop a balloon near me! I couldn't take bright lights, whether car headlights, a flashlight, or bright sunlight. I was also quite ticklish. Seems all my senses were irritable, except I remember I could barely smell if something was rancid or not. All that has changed for the better now. Not sure if my taste has changed any, but everything else has really calmed down.

CT this year has not aggravated my tinnitus. I have been taking various chelates of magnesium for well over a year, in increasing amounts. I seem to recall that it really helped, at the beginning when tinnitus was bothering me. What a relief! If you are having problems absorbing magnesium I would definitely go the skin route, including CT in the lakes nearby. I wonder if you can absorb Mg Threonate?

### ealachan — 2012-05-15T17:28:11+0300

Do you supplement heavily with B12? I get once-in-a-while tinnitus, but the one thing that made me have it almost constantly was sublingual B-12 supplementation. No idea why. Third or fourth day of taking the supplement, the tinnitus cropped up and I had it for two days straight. By the third morning, I was a wreck. I stopped the B-12, and by that evening the tinnitus had faded considerably. The next morning I was pretty much back to normal.

Not saying B-12 would do that to everyone...just my weird tinnitus experience. I have weird ears anyway, for the record. Lots of ear infections as a kid (because I have enormous tonsils that block my eustacian tubes so that they don't drain properly), I've been able to hear my heart beating in my left ear for like 10 years now, and I have some hearing loss in one ear as well.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-15T19:05:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tinnitus-r-us.1470/post-24259>

I get this from allergies. Particularly supplements that have inactive ingredients that don't agree with me (it comes with heart palpitations, stuffy nose, and a face full of dermatitis).

### vlynnb — 2012-05-15T20:14:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tinnitus-r-us.1470/post-24299>

Souldanzer, Which inactive ingredients bother you? I have been having louder tinnitus at night than usual. I am allergic to a lot of foods, pollen and animals but never connected supplement ingredients to the tinnitus.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-15T20:30:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tinnitus-r-us.1470/post-24309>

At this point I count them all unless it's MCT oil, olive oil. Worst offenders I am sure of:

Cellulose/microcrystalline cellulose/hypoallergenic cellulose

rice flour

magnesium stearate

natural flavors

whatever colors no matter how natural

pretty sure magnesium carbonate

silicon dioxide

titanium dioxide

....

I have not found a pill/tablet that I can tolerate. Glycerine in soft gels appears to be acceptable as long as they have absolutely no other ingredients. A few weeks ago I figured out I am allergic to toothpaste as well. Have switched to toothsoap now. I can't have store bought Kevita either... natural flavors. Easiest way to find out is to stop them all for a week and look for improvement.

### chocolate — 2012-06-24T06:15:22+0300

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-24T07:06:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tinnitus-r-us.1470/post-33257>

I think the underlying cause for tinnitus is reduced blood flow to the ear. For me laying of the allergens helps b/c it makes my headache better... which means increased blood flow. So whatever you need to do to increase blood going to that area might help. I still have tinnitus from my brain injury. Hoping to get that dealt with once I'm ready for HBOT which should make it go away.

### Glamazon — 2012-06-26T04:19:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tinnitus-r-us.1470/post-33573>

I have had tinnitus for years.For awhile acupuncture helped but not anymore. Last year I thought about the vasodilation, that it might help. So I started taking increasingly higher doses of niacin. It did help but, annoyingly one day, I stood up and had lost all control of my bladder. Went all over my feet. I couldn't figure out what happened. Really scared me. The only new thing that I had added to my life at that time was increased niacin supplementation. I did not take the sustained release. I no longer had the flushing because I was taking so much, about three tabs of 600 throughout the day. Reading some side effects on the internet I found that a very small percentage of the population do lose bladder control with niacin supplementation. It was so bad it was like emptying out a cup. I could not feel any muscle contraction in the urethra. I stopped the supplementation immediately and it took almost two months for control to be re-established. It was awful. I had to do kiegels for awhile.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-06-26T04:33:22+0300

# Thread: Use your mind

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Birdy — 2012-05-15T17:57:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/use-your-mind.1474/post-24240>

reading about people who can start warming themselves up before actually getting in the water intrigues me. While I have never done official biofeedback, I have been able to change a few things at will, such as blood pressure, blood flow and pulse rate. Don't have any water? Can you just think your way to thermogenesis? Is there any literature out there on this, and/or have you tried it?

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-05-15T18:34:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/use-your-mind.1474/post-24249>

And, should you try to concentrate/visualize that your body is freezing cold - so it will warm itself up? or should you imagine your furnace taking off and generating heat?

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-15T18:53:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/use-your-mind.1474/post-24257>

It's a habituated response. Your nervous system and brain will do this for you if trained correctly.

> Classical conditioning (also Pavlovian conditioning or respondent conditioning) is a form of learning in which one stimulus, the conditioned stimulus or CS, comes to signal the occurrence of a second stimulus, the unconditioned stimulus or US.

The moment you hit the cold water your body reacts with turning on the furnace. Pair this moment with an unconditioned stimulus of your choice for a few times the moment you enter the cold (could be a mental image if you've got enough control of your mind for this or an actual stimulus... ring a bell, knead a stress ball, look at a particular picture... pick something that you don't encounter too often in daily life when it isn't followed by cold).

Within a few trials of pairing CS with UCS, your body should react with warming up to just the new stimulus (the mental image, the kneading of a stress ball, ...).

Happy classical conditioning. Works with dogs, too

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-17T21:34:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/use-your-mind.1474/post-24976>

it appears my body has gotten accustomed to the time of day I usually hit the tub... if I don't I start to get way too hot

### neaderthal — 2012-05-17T22:17:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/use-your-mind.1474/post-24990>

This is an interesting thread as it somewhat captures something I've wondered about. I was surprised when I was able to sit in a bath of 55 degree water for over an hour the first time I tried CT with no problems--no shivering during or after. After a couple of times at 55 degrees, I got the water down to 50 degrees--again no problem. While I'm CTg, I'm always looking outside; so, my eyes are observing a hot & sunny environment. I believe the visual of "hot & sunny" is contributing to my ability to tolerate the cold bath so well. Although, I wonder if the "hot & sunny" visual could block the full potential of biochemical changes we are aiming for by CTg. Thoughts?

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-17T22:22:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/use-your-mind.1474/post-24992>

I was surprised when I was able to sit in a bath of 55 degree water for over an hour the first time I tried CT with no problems--no shivering during or after. After a couple of times at 55 degrees, I got the water down to 50 degrees--again no problem. While I'm CTg, I'm always looking outside; so, my eyes are observing a hot & sunny environment. I believe the visual of "hot & sunny" is contributing to my ability to tolerate the cold bath so well. Although, I wonder if the "hot & sunny" visual could block the full potential of biochemical changes we are aiming for by CTg. Thoughts?

Nice. I doubt it hurts since your body still goes through the same biological changes to warm you up.

Looking for a nice picture to place right where I look while I'm in the tub....

### neaderthal — 2012-05-17T22:40:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/use-your-mind.1474/post-24997>

Souldanzer, yes, the "visual" seems to work quite well as it's hard to think one is going to get too cold as you are looking out towards beautiful green trees and mountains and sunshine. My roommate doesn't much like the fact that I keep removing the shower doors, but who wants a claustrophobic CT session when with a little tweaking of your bathroom and maybe your mind, you can be enjoying a cool bath out in nature?!

# Thread: How much protein for lunch and supper?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### justjoan060 — 2012-05-15T19:05:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/post-24261>

I get about the BAB, but have only seen sketchy "meat and veggies" information about the other two meals. I'm wondering how much protein I should be shooting for the other two meals to keep hunger away. I'm sure there's a thread somewhere, but can't find it. What do the rest of you do?

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-15T20:22:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/post-24305>

However much you need to eat to end your hunger.

I know that sound like a smart-alec reply but I don't mean it that way. I don't count, weight or measure. I eat. If I'm full I stop. If I'm still hungry I eat some more.

I have a lifetime of trust issues to overcome but I am slowly learning to just trust my body. Listen to it. Learn from it. Now that I'm no longer trying to control it all that is a bunch easier.

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-15T22:16:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/post-24335>

I'm trying to get at least 20-25 carbs a day in, I'm a little afraid to go too low. What does Jack say about that? I love mixed salads. But it can take a lot of salad to get very many carbs in, plus then I'm hungry. The obvious thing would be to go to different vegetables. I steamed some broccoli last night and have fresh green beans in the fridge.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-15T22:57:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/post-24360>

Why are you afraid of getting too few carbs? I read somewhere that there's no such thing as an essential carb. And if I understand what Jack said (hahahahaha, that's pretty funny. Me understanding what he says, I mean) in the dead of winter he goes totally zero carb for several weeks. He's eating seasonally if I read that correctly.

I'd suggest if your energy starts to really lag you might need to add a bit more carbs to your diet. If you're doing the leptin reset then your breakfast should have zero (or as close as you can get) carbs.

I think of paleo as Atkins on steroids. Especially for those of us who need to lose weight. The fewer carbs the better. Again, learning to listen to your body will help. If you start craving carbs then maybe you need to add a few. I'm eating lots of sweet potatoes lately. (that means about 1/2 a cup a day ... that's MY definition of a lot, lol). Jack recommended this book "Sweet Potato Power Discover Your Personal Equation for Optimal Health" which, of course, I promptly ran off to Amazon and d/l to my Kindle. I'm such a groupie.

Hope all that helps a little.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-15T23:53:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/post-24381>

Don't forget the fat. I eat two tblsp of coconut oil with almost each meal. I find that helps curb any hunger and I can go 5-6 hours between meals. For me, more protein was not better but more fat was! Maybe a salad, 3 oz of a protein and then some extra fat will help keep you full.

### nonchalant — 2012-05-16T00:16:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/post-24393>

I think you should control your hunger with enough protein and fat. Then when that is under control, begin to eat the carbs.

If you use up too much of your tummy space on carbs, you might not be getting enough protein and fat to be satisfied. Carbs are tricky, and you have to be careful with them. Protein and fat are self-limiting, if you're not being urged on by other carbs. Be sure to drizzle good butter over those veggies! I always eat the meat first now, then fill in with whatever veggies are appealing, until it's time to stop.

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-16T23:42:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/post-24700>

Darlene, you're funny! You groupie! I'm turning into a groupie, too, so don't feel bad! I just made an appointment with the only doc in town (rogue OB-GYN) who does BHRT. I love the midwest, but I gotta tell ya', we are the definition of CW 20 yrs ago. I'm getting my hormones checked. She just opened up to appointment days next week or else it would have been the end of June, so how's that for "meant to be"?? If I weren't looking around on here asking questions today and being told about hormones being wacked, I probably wouldn't have made that call! I've really been wanting to do this for 15 years, but I get that blank stare from most doctors! Darlene, do you think the HRT makes it hard to lose weight or did you gain when you started it? I've always heard that about progesterone, for example.

Regarding the carb thing, it's just hard to believe you can get all the nutrients you need from meat and fat! That's what I'm hearing you guys saying basically? Of course, my hearing isn't what it used to be either!!!!

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-17T02:48:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/post-24745>

Joan I didn't gain any weight when I started on the hormones. But remember I'm taking HUGE doses in a rhythmic pattern. The Wiley Protocol is meant to mimic your hormone production from when you were in your 20's. Most CW docs would pass out if they knew this.

### tes3@mac.com — 2012-05-17T16:42:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/post-24878>

If you are okay with dairy, ie, butter, then eat your meat the gourmet way. I put at least a tbsp of butter on steak, burgers, chicken, you name it. Butter and salt can make just about any meat yummy. And then, as Nonchalant says, butter the broccoli, too. Enough butter and meat, and you won't have cravings for carbs. At least, I don't. If I eat sufficient butter and meat, I have no cravings.

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-17T22:20:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-protein-for-lunch-and-supper.1476/post-24991>

All great ideas! And I have absolutely NO problem putting butter on everything! If we can put butter on things, can't we eat cheese also?

# Thread: Estrogen and the gut, candida too (can't the odd carb eating carnivore get a break?)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/estrogen-and-the-gut-candida-too-cant-the-odd-carb-eating-carnivore-get-a-break.1478/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-15T20:14:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/estrogen-and-the-gut-candida-too-cant-the-odd-carb-eating-carnivore-get-a-break.1478/post-24298>

[http://joe.endocrinology-journals.org/content/190/3/749.full.pdf](http://http://joe.endocrinology-journals.org/content/190/3/749.full.pdf)

Gastric estrogen directly induces ghrelin expression and production

in the rat stomach

Abstract

Ghrelin, an endogenous ligand for the GH secretagogue

receptor, is predominantly produced in the stomach. Little is

known about the regulation mechanism of gastric ghrelin.

**Here, we report that estrogen synthesized in the stomach

induces rat gastric ghrelin gene expression and production.We

established a gastric ghrelin cell enrichment method using

Percoll centrifugation and then studied the effect of estrogen

and/or its antagonist on ghrelin expression and production.

Treatment with estrogen for 8 h significantly increased the

**level of ghrelin expression, and ICI-182 780, an estrogen

receptor (ER) antagonist, completely reversed this effect.**

Reverse transcriptase-PCR analysis clearly showed that ERa

and aromatase are expressed in the female rat stomach.

Moreover, treatment with an aromatase inhibitor, 4-hydroxyandrostenedione

(formestane), significantly decreased the

level of ghrelin mRNA expression in minced stomach tissue.

In vivo studies revealed that the ghrelin mRNA expression and

production did not change in gonadectomized rat 3weeks after

surgery. These results strongly suggest that estrogen produced

in the stomach directly induces ghrelin expression and

production in both female and male rat stomachs.**

Journal of Endocrinology (2006) 190, 749–757

### chocolate — 2012-05-15T20:18:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/estrogen-and-the-gut-candida-too-cant-the-odd-carb-eating-carnivore-get-a-break.1478/post-24303>

[http://www.yeastinfectionadvisor.com/recurringyeastinfection.html](http://http://www.yeastinfectionadvisor.com/recurringyeastinfection.html)

Recurring Yeast Infection and How to Get Rid of It For Good

If you get a recurring yeast infection at certain times of the month you are not alone. Many women get them right at the start of ovulation and they last until menstruation, and then go away only to return again at ovulation the next month. Other women get them at other times of their cycle but this is not as common. If this is you, then this treatment plan will solve the problem in the least amount of time.

**In a scientific study done in 1999 by Paul L. Fidel Jr., Jessica Cutright, and Chad Steele of Louisiana State University, and published in the American Society of Microbiology in 2000, they found that progesterone has no effect on vaginal candida in mice but excess estrogen promoted its growth. They also found that excess estrogen reduced the ability of skin cells to prevent colonization of candida on the vaginal walls.

Estrogen is responsible for glycogen levels in the vagina and glycogen feeds yeast. Excessive levels have been linked to breast cancer and reduced function of the Thymic gland. The Thymic gland helps immune system t-cells that are grown in the bone marrow mature. At a certain stage of t-cell growth in the bone marrow the t-cells migrate to the thymic gland to mature. Upon maturity of the t-cells, the thymic gland releases them into the body.**

Because recurring yeast infection becomes hormone dependent in the vagina and feeds on imbalances of hormones or the sugar produced by excess estrogen, you should test your hormone levels to determine what they are or you could just treat yourself for excess estrogen, its your choice. The most accurate test is by saliva testing. This test can be done in the comfort of your own home by mail and I recommend the test below.

A 5 Panel Saliva Hormone Test is the best way to test for hormone imbalances related to recurring yeast infections.

What is the evolutionary purpose for yeast, to give b- vitamins to bring the metals back to the liver for redistribution?

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-15T20:46:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/estrogen-and-the-gut-candida-too-cant-the-odd-carb-eating-carnivore-get-a-break.1478/post-24319>

Hello everyone,

I want to try Dr. Kruse's protocol to see if it helps my AF.

Paleo diet, cold therapy, lights off after sun goes down, and try to sleep by 10pm...

Anything else that Dr. Kruse recommends?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-16T00:48:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-checklist.1482/post-24406>

Make sure the diet is keto-paleo with lots of fish. Also no exercise but walking is ok in the afternoon (2-5pm-ish) and stare at the sun first thing in the morning for ten minutes to trigger the cortisol.

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-05-16T01:33:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adrenal-fatigue-checklist.1482/post-24419>

I didn't to bed late to start with, so I'm thinking that catching those am rays (coupled with reducing inflammation) is what really helped me. I'm still not on the high end of the range but I figure I'll keep improving.

No cortisol pill for me!!!

He also mentions in his blog post foods to avoid and adaptagens that may help. In my experience, the adaptagens made me feel worse - in my lay person observation, they stimulated my adrenals to work and then I had a crash. So, I stay away from them!

The blog post is here

[http://jackkruse.com/what-might-casey-anthony-and-oj-have-in-common/](http://http://jackkruse.com/what-might-casey-anthony-and-oj-have-in-common/)

highlights:

Pearls for a Adrenal optimization:

Sleep by 10 PM

Sleep in until 8:00 AM

Avoid over training

Do the things you enjoy

Avoid coffee or other caffeinated beverages; steep your tea!

Eat early within thirty minutes of rising

Have a glass of water in the morning with 1/2 to 1 teaspoon of salt

Avoid grains such as bread

Avoid starchy foods such as potato

Mind body mastery/ Laugh several times a day

Take vitamin C, pantothenic acid, magnesium, and vitamin E (Mixed tocophrenols)

Take pregnenolone and DHEA, as needed

Avoid becoming fatigued

Avoid high glycemic fruits

Never skip breakfast ever

Avoid caffeine, chocolate, ephedra, guarana, kola nut, and prescription stimulants. (Ritalin)

**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions-about-my-test-results.1494/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Endless — 2012-05-16T09:07:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions-about-my-test-results.1494/post-24544>

I don't think my labs are as complete as some I have seen here...this is what I could get under my medical insurance. I'm listing the ones I am concerned about, not all of them. I'd appreciate any input!

A1C 5.3 (4.0-6.0)

Chol 200

tri 44

hdl 62

ldl 129

chol/hdl ratio 3.21

fasting glucose 5.5 (4.1-5.6)

CRP (low sens) 5.2 (

### Nourish — 2012-05-16T23:48:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions-about-my-test-results.1494/post-24703>

From what I know....A1C & Glucose are creeping up, CRP high, vit D low (want 120-150 in Canadian units), ferritin could be a bit higher, Hormones obviously low & FSH high - are you peri-menopausal? TSH is a little high too. The labs in Canada don't do rT3 anymore from what I've heard. Not sure why.

Same advice as usual....Paleo diet & CT should help sugars

### Endless — 2012-05-18T06:04:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions-about-my-test-results.1494/post-25105>

Thanks...yes, I would say I am peri-menopausal, I'm 43. I am puzzled by the low vit D as I've been supplementing faithfully. I was hoping it would be higher.

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-18T15:19:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions-about-my-test-results.1494/post-25149>

> **Endless;25187 wrote:** Thanks...yes, I would say I am peri-menopausal, I'm 43. I am puzzled by the low vit D as I've been supplementing faithfully. I was hoping it would be higher.

you D3 is good considering the time of year! JAck wants to see his patients at a minimum of 70 heading into to the fall equinox in the N.Hemisphere for winter (Spring Equinox for those in the S. Hemisphere)

mine in Jan was 65mg/dl - I had backed off supplementing for a while (B/c at the end of summer I was at 118mg/dl), then upped it to 5000IU/day from Nov - winter solstice; then cranked it back to 10000IU.... see my pcp next week - going to discuss getting labs run in the next couple of weeks... to see where I'm at...

looks like your lipid panel was a plain jane version - not an NMR or VAP; so your LDL was calculated - looks good, though the longer you're paleo and off grains - the higher your HDL will go which is good!

next time you get labs run - recommend getting DHEA-S as well as Pregnenolone as well..

# Thread: Can I ever enjoy soaking in the hot springs again if I do this?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-ever-enjoy-soaking-in-the-hot-springs-again-if-i-do-this.1495/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Endless — 2012-05-16T09:19:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-ever-enjoy-soaking-in-the-hot-springs-again-if-i-do-this.1495/post-24546>

I'm sure the answers I am looking for might be somewhere in the blog or the quilt...I have been working my way through all that but have limited computer time. I apologize if this is too basic a question, but I would greatly appreciate either a quick response from someone who has a better grasp on this than I do, or a point in the right direction so I don't have to sift through so many posts! I'm trying to read a little every day but getting anxious to start being proactive and could use a few pointers!

I am on the verge of CT....any day now, I swear! I love reading of people looking forward to it, it gives me hope My question is....is it possible to do CT, become cold adapted and live that way the majority of the time but still enjoy a hot tub, or a sauna, or a hot shower? Will that undo everything, or is it ok to indulge? I enjoy a hot soak in our natural hot springs....do I have to give it up? Or will I just want to give it up? What do you do if you live with someone who is not doing CT....will you be constantly fighting over the house temp?

Silly questions maybe, but I'm curious.....what has your experience been?

### primat — 2012-05-16T09:48:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-ever-enjoy-soaking-in-the-hot-springs-again-if-i-do-this.1495/post-24549>

In the Q&A after the last webinar Jack said that while adapting it's not a good idea to heat up by external means - but once you get there it's not a problem.

Edit: also; if you have been properly cold adapted, and then not doing any CT for a few months, it won't take long to get back to the same tolerance.

### neaderthal — 2012-05-16T17:48:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-ever-enjoy-soaking-in-the-hot-springs-again-if-i-do-this.1495/post-24600>

> **primat;24631 wrote:** In the Q&A after the last webinar Jack said that while adapting it's not a good idea to heat up by external means - but once you get there it's not a problem.
>
>
>
> Edit: also; if you have been properly cold adapted, and then not doing any CT for a few months, it won't take long to get back to the same tolerance.

Does "external means" include exposure to sunshine or just heated water and artificial heat?

### Ray — 2012-05-16T20:11:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-ever-enjoy-soaking-in-the-hot-springs-again-if-i-do-this.1495/post-24638>

> **Endless;24628 wrote:** ....is it possible to do CT, become cold adapted and live that way the majority of the time but still enjoy a hot tub, or a sauna, or a hot shower? ... will I just want to give it up?

I have not done any deep cold therapy soaks; however, with just ~60 degree F soaks, warm water has become significantly less comfortable to be in. It actually stings my skin a bit.

### AKMan — 2012-05-16T20:55:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-ever-enjoy-soaking-in-the-hot-springs-again-if-i-do-this.1495/post-24660>

Here's my take on this issue. If you are just getting into CT, you want to teach your body to respond to the cold stimulus. To do this, you employ face dunks, mild showers, mildly cold baths, and work your way over several weeks to really cold baths. During this period, it would be counter-productive to follow the CT session with a hot sauna, hot tub, or hot shower. During this period, you want your body to react to the cold on it's own and warm you from within. This means drip-dry if possible, wear minimal clothing, turn your thermostat down as low as you can.

Once you are used to this, a dip in the hot tub or hot springs would be fine. I find that a hot shower immediately after a really cold bath eliminates shivering completely, is this good or bad? Good if you have an appointment, bad if you want to maximize the cold's effect.

I have a hot tub on my deck. I set it to 90 degrees. It feels really warm, but still refreshing, and since it's lower than my body temp, should be a great happy medium. We have some natural hot springs near our house that are 110 degrees, we go there a couple times a year. Full of awesome minerals!

### janagram — 2012-05-18T17:52:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-ever-enjoy-soaking-in-the-hot-springs-again-if-i-do-this.1495/post-25191>

this is a tough one. a real sticking point. I looove my hot baths, hot tubs, etc., but I desire to benefit from CT...so...in our ancestral paleo past I'm pretty sure they never had a hot bath! Yikes!

### chocolate — 2012-05-18T18:16:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-i-ever-enjoy-soaking-in-the-hot-springs-again-if-i-do-this.1495/post-25207>

I started going back in the hot tub while I was on antibiotics and bouncing back and forth to the pool, just to really scorch the sugar. I think I'm soaking up water weight. I weigh more after the pool than before, nekked and dry. I don't worry so much about the chlorine any more, since I learned about those multiple personalities for elements. I drink about a liter and use the restroom a couple times (no idea how much) and weigh about three pounds more. If I had access to local mineral springs, I would think they were circadian. Actually, I do but they seem to be occupied by perves that like to take pix and feel their nudity is glorious. I wish I had the perception altering chemicals they had.

# Thread: Grass fed/wild....how strict, how important?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grass-fed-wild-how-strict-how-important.1496/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Endless — 2012-05-16T09:46:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grass-fed-wild-how-strict-how-important.1496/post-24548>

I am fairly lucky in my area, I have access (with advance planning) to a side of grass fed beef or a lamb. What about when you eat out, or run out of 'the good stuff' at home? I find myself torn between having enough animal protein and avoding any conventional meat. There is no grass fed beef available in stores here, I have to buy an entire cow or a half, in the fall.

In the meantime, should I supplement my protein with 'grocery store meat', or just do without??

### primat — 2012-05-16T10:08:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grass-fed-wild-how-strict-how-important.1496/post-24550>

Add more fish and seafood instead?

### maggie — 2012-05-16T14:10:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grass-fed-wild-how-strict-how-important.1496/post-24558>

You can order online, for example from U.S. Wellness Meats to tide you over. They deliver fast.

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-05-16T15:41:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grass-fed-wild-how-strict-how-important.1496/post-24565>

> **Maggie;24640 wrote:** You can order online, for example from U.S. Wellness Meats to tide you over. They deliver fast.

unfortunately that can\`t cross the border.

Endless check out Eat Wild BC there are lists of providers for each community and some keep product on hand for smaller orders during the year

[http://www.eatwild.com/products/canada.html#BC](http://http://www.eatwild.com/products/canada.html#BC)

I order from Redl Beef outside of Willie\`s Puddle and you can order small lots. they also show up at many farmer\`s markets but mostly in the Fraser Valley from Lilloet on down so somewhat outside of your area. I got a quarter last year and will get a half this year. in the mean time I am getting some small box amounts of the ground beef which is very good

[http://www.redlbeef.ca/ordering.html](http://http://www.redlbeef.ca/ordering.html)

### nonchalant — 2012-05-16T17:06:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grass-fed-wild-how-strict-how-important.1496/post-24593>

I eat a combination of grocery store meat and grass-fed local meats. The grass-fed is gradually increasing. I used to be concerned about getting the better fats from GF meat, but now I'm also concerned about getting the better protein profile from that meat.

But I don't think you should "do without" meat. Conventional meat is far superior to plant proteins.

### ealachan — 2012-05-16T19:39:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grass-fed-wild-how-strict-how-important.1496/post-24625>

I eat both conventional and grass-fed, mostly because I can't always afford grass-fed.

### Endless — 2012-05-18T06:06:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grass-fed-wild-how-strict-how-important.1496/post-25106>

Thanks everyone, and thanks for the links Colleen! I will check it out.
