# Thread: Teeth
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth.1499/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-16T16:13:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth.1499/post-24577>

I've gotten really interested in the teeth connection lately.

As a kid and young adult (OMG, how am I not still a "young adult") I had great teeth. Hardly even got plaque. Only one cavity. So much so that I got a little lax with going to the dentist. Last year I decided my teeth felt gross and I needed to go. Well, to my shock and horror - I had 9 cavities! OMG Seriously! I thought they were joking. Now, that I read Dr K's blog [http://jackkruse.com/the-teeth-in-disease/](http://http://jackkruse.com/the-teeth-in-disease/) I am curious. I am highly suspect that something is amuck with my hypothalmus (no TOM and no hormones to speak of) and I have Hashimotos. So, I read the blog but honestly, my brain is not getting it. What the heck does it all mean? I'm such a bottom line person.

So, this peaks my interest:

"He postulated that that dental caries was caused not by bacteria, but caused by inflammation that was due to hypothalamic interruption of hormones that controlled avascular structures in teeth and other endocrine organs"

I've read the advice on supplementing the fermented cod liver oil and K2. But what causes this interruption. And how to you reverse it. I mean, it can't be a co-incidence that I now have 9 cavities and keep getting plaque build up and have no hormones.

Can anyone explain this to me?

THANKS

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-16T16:32:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth.1499/post-24585>

here's a great place to start:

[http://www.sorrentinodental.com/Blog.html?entry=how-to-eliminate-tooth-decay](http://http://www.sorrentinodental.com/Blog.html?entry=how-to-eliminate-tooth-decay)

Jack mentions John in his blog. I actually had a great discussion w/John yesterday on FB. he gave me some great info yesterday.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-16T17:28:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth.1499/post-24597>

Thanks, Shijin.  Great info there but I am really looking for the link to the hypothalmus. How are teeth related to the hypothalmus? How do you turn your hypothalmus on? How does it get turned off? I'm reading google stuff and it says the hypothalmus is the link between the nervous system and the endocrine system. So, that makes some sense for me. My nervous system took a big hit a year and a half ago and now the fallout...I can see why Dr K strongly advocates for making the mind body connection.

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-16T17:43:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth.1499/post-24599>

Check out HPA axis - hypothalmus -Pituary - adrenal relationship - this in-turn impacts the Pituitary - Thyroid axis. I think there can be many causes why the HPA axis will turn off - stress, diet, lack of proper hormone production & leptin sensitivity (or resistance for that matter) - they're all inter-related.

focus on the Leptin Reset - BAB, lunch w/in 4-5hrs after that, dinner 4-5hrs after that (but at least 3hr before bed), get direct sunlight on your eyes 1st thing in the am, and CT. you'll see things reverse. If you have adrenal problems - make sure your supporting w/adaptocrins (maca, rohdilia, l-theanine, Bcomplex and vitamin c), depending on where your diurnal rhythms are - (mine were reversed High at night/low am) so I CT'd during the am. only recently have I been able to CT at night w/o having it impact my sleep - where I'm WIDE AWAKE - CT (even in the form of ice packs around the middle) will impact your adrenals positively

you also need to know where your hormones are at, where your cholesterol is, and what your thyroid is doing. once you have the whole picture - then you can focus on the things you need to work on

as for the carries - how long have you been grain free? I've been gluten free for 6yrs but only grain free in Nov 2010 - Jan 2011 I had my 1st cavity in 37yrs that I had to get filled (6mth earlier I had no cavities) - I was also nursing - I've known several women nursing who've gotten cavities while nursing - not sure why though? diet? I know that the mouth can be the first indication of disease presentation... considering the inflammation I have running around my body its a wonder I only had 1 cavity. this time around no cavities a yr & 1/2 later

### nonchalant — 2012-05-16T22:22:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth.1499/post-24674>

That is a great blog, Gretchen! Thanks for the link.

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-17T06:25:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth.1499/post-24817>

Since being on the reset, I have hardly any plaque. Went to dentist last week for cleaning and they were amazed at the lack of plaque and tartar.....ESP since 6 mo prior (and every cleaning for years prior to leptin rx in late Feb 2011), my gums would bleed if you looked at them sideways... **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gaining-weigh5t-on-lrx.1503/post-24644>

I know I read somewhere that it's not uncommon for women to gain some weight on the reset, but I can't for the life of me find it now. I've gained a pound each of the last two days (only started last Saturday) and it's a little scary! Does anybody know what the norm is for gaining, how long that goes on? Or if you know where Dr. K talks about it? Thanks for any help or advice! I'm trying not to bother him with another question!!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-16T20:37:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gaining-weigh5t-on-lrx.1503/post-24650>

Are you CTing or just doing the Leptin Rx? If you gain when you CT it's usually from hormones in your system. Sorry not sure about Leptin Rx since I lost during that phase and gained during CT. Have you had the lab tests to see where you are starting from?

Now doing post leptin and CT and weight seems to have stabilized. A lot of work has gone into the hormone aspect to get this far.

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-16T20:48:17+0300

Like I said, I read somewhere Dr. K said it's not uncommon for women to gain some during the reset, but can't find it.

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-16T20:54:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gaining-weigh5t-on-lrx.1503/post-24659>

If you gain on the LR its hormonally related. what you gain will depend on your own issues - this is why Dr K says Test, Test, Test... most of the discussion on weight gain is over in the Monster thread on MDA...

### tweety — 2012-05-16T20:55:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gaining-weigh5t-on-lrx.1503/post-24661>

If you gain either in CT or Leptin Rx its due to wacky hormones. I lost weight initially during the leptin Rx last summer. When I realized I needed to supplement hormones and began a BHRT protocol, I gained. Six months later I am still gaining. I have put on a total of 10lbs since November.  I am redoing the leptin Rx and trying to figure out my hormone issues.

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-16T21:05:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gaining-weigh5t-on-lrx.1503/post-24664>

Great! Last time I mentioned to my OB dr about a hormone panel, her eyes glazed over. I'm looking right now at a lab order sheet. How do I find out specifically what labs to ask for? On the MDA thread?

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-16T21:19:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gaining-weigh5t-on-lrx.1503/post-24665>

> **justjoan060;24746 wrote:** Great! Last time I mentioned to my OB dr about a hormone panel, her eyes glazed over. I'm looking right now at a lab order sheet. How do I find out specifically what labs to ask for? On the MDA thread?

the following are the Labs for women you want to get:

Thyroid Antibodies

reverse T3

T3 (Free)

Thyroid Profile II complete

ultimate thryoid Pannel W/basic Hormones

Testosterone Free, Direct

Hormone Panel

estradiol, sensitive

Progesterone

pregnalone

DHEA-S

Female ultimate Check up w/NMR

Ultimate Check up w/NMR

hs CRP

homocystine

NMR w/insulin resistance markes or VAP

HbA1C w/glucose free serum

insulin, fasting

IGF-1

Vit D 25 hydroxy

Iron, Serum w/TIBC

ferritin, Serum

CBC w/diff

Liver function

Kidney function

Comphrenshisive Metabolic Panel

Magnesium RBC

Renin & aldorsterone

GI effext MM Kit

Adrenal Stress (cortisol x4 & DHEA-s x2)

aa/EPA Ratio

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-16T23:49:36+0300

# Thread: Everyone, please read, someone has hacked Dr. Kruse's Fcebook account:
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1510/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MJ* — 2012-05-17T03:52:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1510/post-24757>

Hello Friends,

Something very serious has come to my attention, and I hope to nip this in the bud for the sake of a man's excellent reputation. A man who has very selflessly done a lot for all of us. I have been made aware that Dr. Kruse's Facebook page has been hacked! I read this written by Doctor Kruse, and saw the pic he posted showing someone was in his computer.:

**Someone using a windows machine and firefox browser was using my FB account last week. I just changed all settings........notified FB and the authorities. I am sorry if this caused any problems but I am not totally aware of what has been posted since May 4th it appears.

**

These people are threatened by Dr. Kruse because his work raises questions for them about what they do. Clearly this is something that I would not put past a person or group, who found a way to make so much trouble for him that he was taken off the Low-Carb cruise. They are using their hack to try and spread more lies and make more trouble, PLEASE don't believe the crap you might see on some of these Paleo sites about Dr. Kruse. The trolls are just at it again...

MJ

### MJ* — 2012-05-17T03:53:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1511/post-24759>

Hello Friends,

Something very serious has come to my attention, and I hope to nip this in the bud for the sake of a man's excellent reputation. A man who has very selflessly done a lot for all of us. I have been made aware that Dr. Kruse's Facebook page has been hacked! I read this written by Doctor Kruse, and saw the pic he posted showing someone was in his computer.:

**Someone using a windows machine and firefox browser was using my FB account last week. I just changed all settings........notified FB and the authorities. I am sorry if this caused any problems but I am not totally aware of what has been posted since May 4th it appears.

**

These people are threatened by Dr. Kruse because his work raises questions for them about what they do. Clearly this is something that I would not put past a person or group, who found a way to make so much trouble for him that he was taken off the Low-Carb cruise. They are using their hack to try and spread more lies and make more trouble, PLEASE don't believe the crap you might see on some of these Paleo sites about Dr. Kruse. The trolls are just at it again...

MJ

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-17T08:48:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1511/post-24835>

Is it just me or has the forum been unusually quiet this afternoon/evening?

Also some security tips. Be sure you don't use the same username/pw for multiple sites... Because as soon as bad folks get hold of a working username/pw combo, they crank it through banking sites, social sites, etc, to see what else they can get. Usually $$$ is the motivation but here the target seems to be just disrupting JK and those who support him, so, be careful out there.

And remember to love your enemies even as you are being attacked... Anyone can love people who are nice to them, it's much harder to love an enemy....so let's strive to take the high road (the hard things tend to be the things most worth doing, no?)

here is hoping that the disruptive folks relent and find peace within themselves enough that they don't need to attack others who are trying on some new thoughts for size. I'm willing to be my own guinea pig and try some things for myself to see what works..... Not sure why that seems so threatening!!! Peace to the haters..may you find your own way.

Anybody know about these things? I'm not sure if my FB account got hacked or somebody just attempted but didn't succeed. I "secured" my account using the FB function for that including changing my password. I chose a higher security level and am now being notified if someone logs in from somewhere else than my laptop.

How do I find out if someone actually used this account to post somewhere?

I'm also in the process of changing all other places I have to log in with a password no matter if it's the same or not. This is a tedious process as I use a lot of places I need to log into.

Do I need to change the password to my laptop itself?

I also had a total of 5 emails simply disappear into cyber space. They included lots of identifying personal information (name, address, birthdate, etc.). This problem might have been on the receiving end but I'm not exactly relaxed about having this stuff float around in cyber world. All of my other emails have been received fine as far as I know so I doubt my email account got hacked.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-17T17:13:56+0300

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-17T17:15:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-please-read-someone-has-hacked-dr-kruses-fcebook-account.1511/post-24902>

Soul,

The trick is regular change. The fact is that someone motivated will be able to hack. You've just got to defend against the base level ner-do-wells.

Set the ol outlook with random yet regular "appointments" to change passwords. Make yourself a mix-tape. of 12 songs. Each song represents a month. During that month use the first letter of each word in the chorus. make a list of allllllll the logins you need to really be secure. Banks, personal websites, etc. takes 5 mins once a month. That's probably 99% more secure than the rest of the world.

Come up with 3 rotating passwords for all the junk sites.

AND, most importantly, try not to be interesting or relevant. I'm so boring, there would just simply be no reason for anyone to bother looking at my \*\*\*\*.

### nonchalant — 2012-05-17T18:27:21+0300

>
> My favorite trick on passwords is to take a chorus from a favorite song, and use the 1st letter of each word.
>
>
>
> For instance:
>
>
>
> MHALL "mary had a little lamb"

I agree. But I had a password for my email account, using a similar method, and my email was hacked. My password looked very random, with a capital letter, a number, and even a special character, but it was only 9 characters long. I have since learned to combine two passwords together. The length seems to resist the brute force cracking.

> **ColdBren;25027 wrote:** Monte- thank you for your posts over at that other website, and for having the patience to deal with the pitchfork people.

You are welcome. This is personal for me actually. Jack is the only person to ever explain to me the complex mechanisms that went into my own transformations while blindly enduring CT. These were thoroughly life changing events and I had no clue how they happened. For 2 years I searched for the truth. Spent thousands of hours and hundreds of dollars looking to no avail. All these supposed experts had no answers... Then came Jack.

He explained the ancient pathway and cold thermogenesis. Even his revelations about our "primordial state" (darkness) had significant personal revelations for me. I've experienced everything he has talked about here. Cold adaptation, increased testosterone, effortless weight loss, extreme productivity etc etc. Remember him posting about the experiment where the people were convinced to stay underground for extended periods of time? Been there, done that. It's awesome. It's like unplugging yourself from the matrix. The movie does not do justice to just how HUGE of an effect this is.

Not only did Jack explain these things he gave me the tools to control them. This is so invaluable to me that I'm willing to endure whatever, whenever, however, to defend it.

# Thread: Breakin a new one in today!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breakin-a-new-one-in-today.1512/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-17T05:16:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breakin-a-new-one-in-today.1512/post-24799>

Been on the hunt for a doc for me and family. Found one I think! Love his personality. Very thoughtful, total geek who clearly is into what he does, and does it because he's into the topic and wants to help people. (me loves the geeks).

Dr. Jeffrey Gerber in Littleton. It was an hour from here, but we are gonna roll with him as a new family physician.

Anybody in Colorado - Highly Vouch For. He is going to be a speaker on the Low Carb Cruise next year. He's involved with LCHF community, and has heard of Jack. He's super passionate about carbs (or lack thereof). And I'll have him broken in! The more of us the better!

He met with me and my Husband today for the "introductory" meeting that seems to be an insurance thing to do. . .we way ran out of time before even getting to half of the things to discuss. He was good humored about me going down the lists of tests, and was happy to say "hmm, don't know of that" or "I can't order!"

I hope between the two of us he is curious enough to check out the site.

We are meeting again after labs - It's a small office, Him and his nurse/receptionist - they were so courteous about our time. None of the crazy waits and very personal attention. So refreshing.

At one point he looked and jokinly said "Ok, you've overwhelmed me!"

Plus, the first thing he said to me after introductions was "So, you've been sitting in icewater? How's that going?"

Good signs.

### admin — 2012-05-18T17:34:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-ks-face-book-hacked.1514/post-25182>

> **Souldanzer;25007 wrote:** I'd recommend everybody reset their password on here. Just sayin'. Not fun what I need to do today. Lucky me got a mac genius as one of my best friends... his evening is booked  I better cook a good chicken for dinner, lol. These friends need to be well paid.

Hey Optimizers,

No need to worry. The forum was not hacked, nor has it ever been. If you're concerned about someone hacking into your account, you may reset your password to something more secure. Use a mix of letters and numbers, uppercase and lowercase. If your name is "Anna", for example, you could use 4nNaB0BAnN4 (Anna BoBanna) as your password.

That goes for anything on the web. Whether it's here or linkedin or twitter or facebook or wherever.

### neaderthal — 2012-05-18T17:55:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-ks-face-book-hacked.1514/post-25192>

> **Jack's Marketing Monkeys;25264 wrote:** Hey Optimizers,
>
>
>
> No need to worry. The forum was not hacked, nor has it ever been. If you're concerned about someone hacking into your account, you may reset your password to something more secure. Use a mix of letters and numbers, uppercase and lowercase. If your name is "Anna", for example, you could use 4nNaB0BAnN4 (Anna BoBanna) as your password.
>
>
>
> That goes for anything on the web. Whether it's here or linkedin or twitter or facebook or wherever.

Isn't this the type of password that would be EASILY deciphered by a computer especially if one is using your own name? I'm not a computer security expert by no means, but I thought using any type of password that contained personal identifying information was NOT a good idea. Personally, I'm not at all fond of the lack of privacy ANY participation in using the Internet brings; so, these recent events leaves one like myself extremely wary. If I read the recommendations correctly from a link that was provided yesterday, it was suggested that a string of random words would be exponentially less likely to be hacked than this type of password. If anyone can contribute any useful suggestions, it would be much appreciated.

### admin — 2012-05-18T18:04:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-ks-face-book-hacked.1514/post-25200>

> **neaderthal;25274 wrote:** Isn't this the type of password that would be EASILY deciphered by a computer especially if one is using your own name? I'm not a computer security expert by no means, but I thought using any type of password that contained personal identifying information was NOT a good idea. Personally, I'm not at all fond of the lack of privacy ANY participation in using the Internet brings; so, these recent events leaves one like myself extremely wary. If I read the recommendations correctly from a link that was provided yesterday, it was suggested that a string of random words would be exponentially less likely to be hacked than this type of password. If anyone can contribute any useful suggestions, it would be much appreciated.

It was only an example of using a mix of letters and numbers and uppercase/lowercase. By no means am I implying that you should use your own name. A secure password is something that would never be easily guessed. You should stay away from common uses of your name or birthday or something like password123.

For more information look at: [http://www.microsoft.com/security/online-privacy/passwords-create.aspx](http://http://www.microsoft.com/security/online-privacy/passwords-create.aspx)

# Thread: Cure for a cold?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cure-for-a-cold.1517/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-05-17T16:51:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cure-for-a-cold.1517/post-24883>

I think I saw on here somewhere some ideas for fighting off sickness. I think it may have involved vitamin d or garlic.

Anyhow, im afraid my two babies and I are coming down with a cold. Theyve been congested and coughing the last couple of days. I woke up with a nasty sore throat today.

Any ideas, for me? For them? We leave on vacation Saturday morning and I want us all to feel well. Help!

### JC2K — 2012-05-17T17:36:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cure-for-a-cold.1517/post-24907>

Vitamin D's been it for me. Ever since I started boosting my D last year, I've been fairly bulletproof (was in the teens when I started supplementing). I work in an office with several hundred people on my floor, and they tend to pass the creeping crud around from their kids fairly often. You hear the coughing, wheezing, and sneezing in stereo sometimes. Same with travel, used to always get something after business trips cross country. Hasn't happened since. I've been taking pretty much taking 10k iu D3 a day since last year, my last blood test for D was 73. I'm aiming to get it up to 80 before I cut down to 5k iu a day and see how it stabilizes.

Which doesn't necessarily help you in your situation though, since you're already coming down with something. Probably wouldn't hurt, but I don't know if it would help that fast. I've tried garlic before, didn't really notice a difference in the outcomes, and it was rough on the ole gastrointestinal tract. Not sure how it would work with the babies either.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-05-18T21:30:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cure-for-a-cold.1517/post-25289>

> **Trisha;24965 wrote:**
>
> Anyhow, im afraid my two babies and I are coming down with a cold. Theyve been congested and coughing the last couple of days. I woke up with a nasty sore throat today.
>
>
>
> Any ideas, for me? For them? We leave on vacation Saturday morning and I want us all to feel well. Help!

Hi Trisha,

I know how scary it is to have a sick baby. But two! Here is what I do for my girls along with a nourishing traditional diet. I don't know if you are open to trying homeopathy, but I use Aconite 30c at the earliest stages of colds. Works very well with young children and is safe:

[http://eatkamloops.org/healthy-household-comfort-for-colds/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/healthy-household-comfort-for-colds/)

I hope you have a healthy holiday.

Cheers,

Caroline

# Thread: Sugar Can Make You Dumb Warn Scientists
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-can-make-you-dumb-warn-scientists.1518/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### skywaykate — 2012-05-17T18:26:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-can-make-you-dumb-warn-scientists.1518/post-24930>

[http://news.yahoo.com/sugar-dumb-us-scientists-warn-190918006.html](http://http://news.yahoo.com/sugar-dumb-us-scientists-warn-190918006.html)

> "The DHA-deprived animals were slower, and their brains showed a decline in synaptic activity," said Fernando Gomez-Pinilla, a professor of neurosurgery at the David Geffen School of Medicine at UCLA.
>
>
>
> "Their brain cells had trouble signaling each other, disrupting the rats' ability to think clearly and recall the route they'd learned six weeks earlier."
>
>
>
> A closer look at the rat brains revealed that those who were not fed DHA supplements had also developed signs of resistance to insulin, a hormone that controls blood sugar and regulates brain function.
>
>
>
> "Because insulin can penetrate the blood-brain barrier, the hormone may signal neurons to trigger reactions that disrupt learning and cause memory loss," Gomez-Pinilla said.
>
>
>
> In other words, eating too much fructose could interfere with insulin's ability to regulate how cells use and store sugar, which is necessary for processing thoughts and emotions.
>
>
>
> "Insulin is important in the body for controlling blood sugar, but it may play a different role in the brain, where insulin appears to disturb memory and learning," Gomez-Pinilla said.
>
>
>
> "Our study shows that a high-fructose diet harms the brain as well as the body. This is something new."
>
>
>
> ....
>
>
>
> "Our findings illustrate that what you eat affects how you think," said Gomez-Pinilla.
>
>
>
> "Eating a high-fructose diet over the long term alters your brain's ability to learn and remember information. But adding omega-3 fatty acids to your meals can help minimize the damage."

We already are understanding this here, but I think it also shows that insulin can affect the brain even if it's not from sugar sources, this doesn't say that but you know it's there, it's only a matter of time before scientists study it, and in the meantime, we're living it!

Need to convince my friend who understands wheat isn't good for you, but all those GF mixes she's using aren't any better!

# Thread: probiotics for dry eyes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/probiotics-for-dry-eyes.1522/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-17T23:00:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/probiotics-for-dry-eyes.1522/post-25003>

[http://www.eyeworld.org/article.php?sid=4964](http://http://www.eyeworld.org/article.php?sid=4964)

Investigators diluted Lactobacillus acidophilus into a saline solution to create the eye-drop solution.

Symptoms in all six patients who completed the study (one patient dropped out) were significantly improved at two weeks and four weeks. Clinical signs were also significantly better after four weeks (although not at the two-week mark). The impression cytology, which was performed in three patients, showed a down regulation of ICAM-1 and TLR-4 in two patients. **â€œWe were fairly surprised by the fact that every patient, regardless of statistically significant clinical improvement, described a positive effect of Lactobacilli eye drops on their symptoms, and none of them reported discomfort or the occurrence of side effects,â€￾ Dr. Iovieno said. He and fellow investigators are now analyzing the effects of prolonged oral consumption of Lactobacilli for more mild forms of eye allergies.

A number of allergy studies, some of which also track eye symptoms, are finding good results associated with probiotic use. A review article published in the December 2008 issue of Annals of Allergy, Asthma, and Immunology analyzed 12 randomized controlled trials that studied the effects of probiotics in allergic rhinitis and asthma. **â€œNine of the 12 randomized controlled trails that evaluated clinical outcomes in allergic rhinitis showed an improvement due to the use of probiotics,â€￾ wrote lead investigator Harissios Vliagoftis, M.D., Department of Medicine, University of Alberta, Edmonton, Alberta. â€œAll the \[trials\] that studied perennial allergic rhinitis showed lower symptom scoring with the use of probiotics compared with placebo. Also, five of the eight \[trials\] that referred to seasonal allergic rhinitis suggested an improvement in clinical outcomes,â€￾ he wrote. Four of the trials reviewed also tracked ocular allergy symptoms; all four of those studies reported decreased eye symptoms after probiotic use.** Although Dr. Vliagoftis and co-investigators wrote that the mechanism of action for probiotics in allergy** is not entirely clear, they may have an effect on immune regulation by altering the composition of gut flora. Others have postulated a connection to the so-called â€œhygiene hypothesis,â€￾ which is based on research that finds children exposed to more microbes earlier in life actually have a lower incidence of allergy and asthma than children who are exposed to fewer microbes.

# Thread: Why is this taking so long?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-this-taking-so-long.1528/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 36
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eve — 2012-05-18T04:47:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-this-taking-so-long.1528/post-25091>

Why is it taking so long for my leaky gut to heal?

I've been on GAPS intro more or less since Feb 2011. Before that I was gluten free for 2 years (but tried restarting it in Fall 2010 and failed) and was trying my best to be corn-free until I realized that corn is in nearly everything.

I've been doing the Leptin Reset since Jan 2012, with only one cheat late Jan (several glasses of wine one weekend). For the past couple of months, I have been doing CT face-dunking (1st month 2-3 times a week) and spot CTing on my stomach every day or every other day for the past 3 weeks. I've tried taking a bath a couple of times with a wool hat but it was pretty unpleasant even at 65 degrees.

In the past month, I've been consistently getting glutathione IV once a week, although it's expensive. I've been taking glutamine powder since Feb but reacted to all other supplements.

Since adding CT my sleep is better. And in the past month, my skin has been consistently smooth. After starting GAPS last year, my skin has a nice glow to it. Headaches are less and reactions to food intolerances are not as bad but still debilitating.

However, now to the not so good stuff...My leaky gut is terrible! And I fear that it is never going to get better. It seems that I react to everything -- gluten, corn derivatives, all grains, dairy, nuts, chocolate, coconut butter etc....And if it's not that either everything has corn in it (corn derivatives) or I react to it because it's not organic. I would kill for some bacon or sausages but can't find a brand even natural ones that I don't react to. Plus I'm sensitive to fragrances -- makes me feel nauseous. I tried bovine colostrum and was repulsed by the taste. I was taking probiotics since Sep 2011 but realized a couple of days ago that they are not gluten-free and that I was having a reaction to them.

I'm going to make some sauerkraut soon though to supplement.

I've also more or less weighed the same the entire 5 months on Leptin Reset and I need to lose at least 25lbs. I did start losing weight after I removed the poultry I was reacting to but then I tried some 100% chocolate and nuts this week and am now almost 10lbs up.

My doctor won't test my estrogen or progesterone levels. I'm not overly concerned about my hormones because my last month's period was finally regular and mostly cramp free (vs. my previously heavy ones). My HS-CRP was 1.6 last month...which I would think would be higher because of all my leaky gut probs. My O6:O3 ratio was 10.8:1...but I tested at a time I was reacting to some poultry that likely had some corn in it or wasn't truly organic. Once I took the poultry out, my soft skin returned.

I was on T4 meds for my thyroid since Feb 2008 but last month I convinced my doc to switch me to natural dessicated thyroid. When I was on T4 meds, my labs last month were 0.03TSH and free t3 bottom of range and free t4 middle of range.

Is there any hope for me? I can't even give up because I can't eat anything else and have to eat only meat and veggies by default. Yes, this is a post out of desperation.

### Glamorama — 2012-05-18T05:07:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-this-taking-so-long.1528/post-25093>

Have you ever considered that Candida overgrowth in your gut might be ther eason your leaky gut aint healing? This was the case for me. 8 months of constantly trying everything I could to fix my leaky gut, and I only mildly improved. Then I went and actively treated my Candida overgrowth, and while the die-off was horrific for the first 3 days, then prettyc rappy for the next 6 weeks, I came out on the other end feeling much better. Now I consider myself 90% healed, while before fixing the candida I was stalling at 50% healed without any signs of improving.

### Eve — 2012-05-18T05:47:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-this-taking-so-long.1528/post-25099>

Thanks for your response. I had considered Candida at one point but wasn't sure cause I don't have any itchy skin or yeast infections -- typical candida signs that I've read about it. But I'm willing to try anything. What did you use to combat it? Difflucan?

### Glamorama — 2012-05-18T06:18:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-this-taking-so-long.1528/post-25108>

> **Eve;25181 wrote:** Thanks for your response. I had considered Candida at one point but wasn't sure cause I don't have any itchy skin or yeast infections -- typical candida signs that I've read about it. But I'm willing to try anything. What did you use to combat it? Difflucan?

I didn't have any signs either, other than constant thrush back in my SAD-days, but I had a thick, yellow coating on the back of my tongue. THAT is a typical sign of Candida in the gut. I used kyolic garlic, daily coconut oil, but never Difflucan. I think Difflucan can be a good idea though, if you have the signs of candida. Another sign is yellow/amber colour around the irises in your eyes. I had it, but now its gone. So is the coating on my tongue.

### Glamorama — 2012-05-18T06:19:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-this-taking-so-long.1528/post-25110>

Kyolic garlic is a very storng and powerful antifungal. You start with 2 capsules daily, then build up to 4. The whole course should be an entire box, which is normally 200 capsules. Youc an buy it any good health-shop.

### bigknitwit — 2012-05-18T14:14:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-this-taking-so-long.1528/post-25146>

My situation is very similar to yours, and I totally feel your pain on the food front. I am down to meat and veggies as well (no bacon or anything cured, and no pork in general, no mushrooms). I have had 3 yeast tests (2 stool, one urine). The 2 stool tests returned negative for candida, the urine one showed yeast metabolites. When I eat something I'm sensitive to, I gain tremendous weight overnight, much like you describe.

My doctor recently tested me for the presense of mold (with a urine analysis from Realtime Labs), and my test confirmed exposure. Since the toxins are stored in the fat tissues, he had me do a 1 week weight loss diet (which was really difficult because I don't drop weight very easily anymore), and then re-test. I wil get those results on Monday morning, and then he will create some kind of treatment plan, based on my toxin load. He is also suspicious of lyme disease for me.

I also struggle with thyroid issues. With low free T3.

### vkiernan — 2012-05-18T14:30:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-this-taking-so-long.1528/post-25147>

I'm confused, there is something I am missing. Where are getting all the corn, etc. from? There is no corn, gluten, etc. on the leptin reset unless it is in your supplements. Are you saying that cuz the chicken was fed corn you are reacting to it? Could you give a day's sampling of your meals?

What about seafood? Can you eat that? Just make sure it is not farmed otherwise you will have the corn issue again. Have you sourced some grass fed and grass finished beef out? That would help with the meat part and it is usually cheaper if you can find a local farmer that sells it by the quarter or half if you have freezer room.

I also agree with Glam, if you have leaky gut that bad - you have Candida. Do you still crave sugar? I have Candida and don't have the symptoms you talk about and have tested positive for it. I can tell you I have treated it but still am not sure I have gotten rid of it. You have to work really hard and long to get rid of it. I am working on leaky gut too. Recently, I have added kombucha to my diet. I beleive that is helping too. I do the l-glutamine powder, aloe juice, bubbies sauerkraut and kombucha for leaky gut. I am not nearly as bad as you though.

You should read Mamagrok's success story. It is in the success stories area. She has battled the stomach stuff for a long time. Not saying it was leaky gut as I don't remember but she only eats fat and protein until whatever ails her is is gone. She is doing so much better now. She was on GAPS for a while before this too. Perhaps she will see this thread and chime in. She really is probably your best go to person.

On a positive note, sounds like you are having a some success with TOM. That is encouraging. Something has to be working for that to happen. Also, give you thyroid meds a little time to work. They should be better than t4 only for sure. I hope you get some great answers to help you.

### BJK77 — 2012-05-18T17:13:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-this-taking-so-long.1528/post-25175>

I'm in a similar situation and feel your pain!! I've been on GAPS for over 18 months and started the leptin rx January 1st of this year. I'm still dealing with leaky gut issues and can't tolerate many veggies. I also have issues with constipation/diarrhea and nearly always have undigested food in my stool.

I've had 2 stool tests done and neither showed any harmful overgrowths at all. I did have a lack of beneficial bacteria, but I do take huge amounts of probiotics and eat tons of fermented foods to try to help with that. I highly doubt I'm dealing with candida because I haven't had sugar cravings for over a year now. It just doesn't tempt me at all. If I get cravings for anything, it's veggies!

I only eat fats, meats and some dark leafy greens like kale with the occasional piece of broccoli or bit of cabbage. I have sauerkraut with every single meal and have started making some other ferments that I'm hopeful will benefit my gut.

I know part of my problem is definitely hormonal though as my TOM will come once every 6-9 months and my hormone levels are totally in the toilet

### Eve — 2012-05-18T18:49:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-this-taking-so-long.1528/post-25219>

> **vkiernan;25229 wrote:** I'm confused, there is something I am missing. Where are getting all the corn, etc. from? There is no corn, gluten, etc. on the leptin reset unless it is in your supplements. Are you saying that cuz the chicken was fed corn you are reacting to it? Could you give a day's sampling of your meals?

Yes, I'm not eating corn or gluten or grains...I was just venting that I couldn't eat them even if I tried. And I was referring to corn derivatives there on out. For example, Bubbies Sauerkraut has corn derivatives in it (not listed on ingredients list)...I belong to a Corn Allergy/Sensitivity forum and they have confirmed it as well...Crazy I know! Plus I have no issues with homemade sauerkraut.

Another example is veggies in the grocery store. I am in Canada and have to stay clear of veggies imported from the US because they are sprayed with a pesticide or preservative or whatever that is derived from corn. Local, unsprayed veggies on the other hand I am fine with.

With the poultry I was reacting to, I'm not 100% sure if it was corn deriviatives. I eat pork and beef that is fed corn and I am ok with it so I am thinking the chicken was rinsed in something derived from corn or a chemical that I'm reacting to.

This is what I eat on a daily basis

breakfast: 6-8 eggs fried in coconut oil and ~1/2 cup organic, free-range ground pork or beef

late lunch/dinner: local zucchini/broccoli and garlic stir-fry in coconut oil and salmon or shrimp

And yes, I have cravings usually all the time for desserts and pizza. I'm prone to binging on foods that I react to. For example, I caved and had some nut butter (which I'm sensitive to) this week and ate nearly all the jar in one sitting.

I am going to check that kyolic garlic out and I also have some olive leaf extract I am going to start up again. I also may ask my doctor about Nyastin (sp?), which is a prescription anti-fungal that is recommended by the GAPS doctors. My doctor is also going to refer me to an internal medicine doctor.

Thank you for your replies

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-05-18T18:58:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-this-taking-so-long.1528/post-25221>

However, now to the not so good stuff...My leaky gut is terrible! And I fear that it is never going to get better. It seems that I react to everything -- gluten, corn derivatives, all grains, dairy, nuts, chocolate, coconut butter etc....And if it's not that either everything has corn in it (corn derivatives) or I react to it because it's not organic. I would kill for some bacon or sausages but can't find a brand even natural ones that I don't react to. Plus I'm sensitive to fragrances -- makes me feel nauseous. I tried bovine colostrum and was repulsed by the taste. I was taking probiotics since Sep 2011 but realized a couple of days ago that they are not gluten-free and that I was having a reaction to them.

I'm going to make some sauerkraut soon though to supplement.

### hazyjane — 2012-05-18T21:11:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-this-taking-so-long.1528/post-25282>

I'd ditch the eggs for a few weeks. They tend to be an issue for leaky gut.

Here are some supplements that will fast-track leaky gut healing: Sea Cure whitefish peptides, N-Acetyl Glucosamine (NAG), L-Reuteri (a specific probiotic strain that rebuilds the villi and gut lining) and possibly cat's claw (also antifungal and immune boosting. Nutricology and Saventaro are good brands). Stay on these for at least 2 months. I was able to reverse a ton of food sensitivities using these.

Candida doesn't always cause skin or vaginal symptoms. It often causes bloating, headaches, brain fog, etc.

If candida is an issue, I also recommend a month of Candex enzymes. They work faster than antifungals do and some strains of yeast are so resistant that they're really hard to kill with antifungals. Candex doesn't work via an antifungal mechanism; it's an enzyme that dissolves the cell wall of the yeast and implodes it. The yeast isn't able to become resistant. It's good to take an immune booster like cat's claw along with it to help the suppressed immune system to recover and be able to keep the yeast from coming back (it's always in us- it's just a matter of keeping it balanced with good bacteria and a healthy immune system).

If someone is having a really hard time killing yeast, consider a heavy metal cleanse. A naturopath I know discovered that all of his difficult yeast patients had a lot of heavy metals and when they were removed, the yeast cleared easily. The best product for heavy metals that I know of is HMD Heavy Metal Detox.

Great stuff, Hazy Jane. Thanks! I just saw a Func Med doc and she diagnosed me with leaky gut (it wasn't a huge leap) but I wasn't really thrilled with the medicinal food that she wanted me to take - Metagenics Ultra Clear Sustain. It has like 10g of sugar per serving and like 34 carbs. I do NOT want to take it and was going to have to research alternatives - SO THANK YOU.

I also really like how she explained the connection between leaky gut and AF. Basically, if your gut is leaky, your body is under constant attack and your adrenals will be working over time.

My understanding about eggs is that it's the white part that people react to because that is the part of the egg that has the "defense mechanism" in it. Here is a nice article on eggs -

[http://paleodietlifestyle.com/an-egg-yolk-a-day-keeps-the-doctor-away/](http://http://paleodietlifestyle.com/an-egg-yolk-a-day-keeps-the-doctor-away/)

This part:

Unlike most animals, eggs can’t defend themselves by bitting or escaping and have to rely on some of the same mechanisms that plants, grains and seeds use to survive. The white is solely responsible for this part while the yolk is busy becoming a full-fledged animal. 13 of the 14 proteins found in the white have anti-microbial properties. This usually deters microbes and viruses, but can also affect us, especially when consumed in high quantity or when our gut health is already compromised.

The protein lysozyme, which is usually harmless, can bind with proteins like ovomucoid or ovoinhibitor and inhibit trypsin, a protease enzyme, from doing its job of digesting proteins. Even more insidious, some of the compounds can pass through the intestinal wall and can agravate autoimmune problems or damaged guts. This is why egg whites are best avoided when dealing with autoimmune problems.

### Eve — 2012-05-20T05:01:49+0300

> **MamaGrok;25713 wrote:** Okay, I'm gonna need something better than that.  This person loves mac & cheese, hates fat in yogurt, and has no proof against Bubbies except mild symptoms that could very well be ferments causing die-off in her poor abused gut.
>

Okay, maybe that wasn't a good source of evidence . But I know for certain I reacted to Bubbies and another store-bought sauerkraut that couldn't be explained by die-off because I don't have the same symptoms with my homemade sauerkraut. For example, with Bubbies I experienced my usual "corned" signs -- addiction to the food, headaches, extreme brain fog and fatigue, urinary frequency and insomnia. However, with my homemade sauerkraut I just get some fatigue and I feel better over time. And as I mentioned before, on the Corn Allergy/Sensitivity forum I frequent, other members have noted reactions to it.

You could try making your own sauerkraut and taking out the Bubbies and see if you notice a difference. However, I think it's likey that there is other corn hidden in your diet such as in supplements or veggies (if they're not local), which will confound your experiment.

Hi Eve,

I just listened to this lecture by Dr Natasha Campbell-McBride on mercola.com. I found it very good:

[http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2012/05/12/dr-campbell-mcbride-on-gaps.aspx?e\_cid=20120520\_WNL\_art\_1](http://http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2012/05/12/dr-campbell-mcbride-on-gaps.aspx?e_cid=20120520_WNL_art_1)

Eating more fermented foods might be helpful. If at all possible make your own fermented foods. If you make your own fermented foods you will get "wild culture" from your local area. It will help your immune system better than store bought fermented foods that are coming from God knows where.

Corn is very damaging for many people on SCD/GAPS. I will get massive blood blisters in my mouth after have popcorn. It makes no difference if the corn is organic and GMO free. My great grandfather used to say: "if the food does not agree with you, leave it alone." Good advice for everyone.

Cheers,

Caroline

### SeaHorse — 2012-05-29T14:43:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-is-this-taking-so-long.1528/post-28158>

Just another thought about ferments....they have a lot of histamine and if you are sensitive can cause pretty big reactions. I love fermented foods but I've given them up for the last few months after noticing swelling, bloat and allergy symptoms which were more than die off. It's a drag because they are such a good source of probiotics. There is an enzyme product HISTAME which can help breakdown histamine, but I'm staying away from the high histamine foods and oxalates for awhile and will try again later in the year after giving my gut a break. Can't do eggs at the moment either.

# Thread: chelation for cataracts
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chelation-for-cataracts.1532/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-18T16:19:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chelation-for-cataracts.1532/post-25161>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21271438](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21271438)

Topical metal chelation therapy ameliorates oxidation-induced toxicity in diabetic cataract.

Zhang M, Shoeb M, Liu P, Xiao T, Hogan D, Wong IG, Campbell GA, Ansari NH.

Source

Department of Biochemistry & Molecular Biology, University of Texas Medical Branch, Galveston, Texas 77555-0647, USA.

Abstract

Oxidative stress plays a critical role in cataractogenesis, the leading cause of blindness worldwide. Since transition metals generate reactive oxygen species (ROS) formation, metal chelation therapy has been proposed for treatment of cataracts. However, the effectiveness of most chelators is limited by low tissue penetrability. **This study is the first to demonstrate **that the topically applied divalent metal chelator ethylenediamine tetraacetic acid (EDTA) combined with the carrier and _permeability enhancer methyl sulfonyl methane (MSM)_** ameliorates both oxidation-induced lens opacification and the associated toxic accumulation of protein-4-hydroxynonenal (HNE) adducts.** Both in vitro (rat lens culture) and in vivo (diabetic rats), EDTA-MSM (1) significantly reduced lens opacification by about 40-50%, (2) significantly diminished lens epithelial cell proliferation and fiber cell swelling in early stages of cataract formation in vivo, and (3) notably decreased the levels of protein-HNE adducts. These findings have important implications specifically for the treatment of cataract and generally for other diseases in which oxidative stress plays a key pathogenic role.

PMID:

21271438

\[PubMed - indexed for MEDLINE\]

# Thread: Summer, Carbs and CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/summer-carbs-and-ct.1537/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bigknitwit — 2012-05-18T18:46:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/summer-carbs-and-ct.1537/post-25218>

I am confused about how these three things mingle. I have been CTing with a keto paleo diet along with the leptin reset, and have achieved at least some level of cold adaptation (based on minimal shivering post-CT and fully recovered in less than 1 hour). However, I would like to enjoy the upcoming season of summer, including being outside in the hot sun for several hours a day with my kids, and enjoying some of the fruits of summer (the strawberries, sugar snap peas, raspberries, etc, all from my own garden). I imagine I will not maintain ketosis all the time, and probably not most of the time. So, should I continue to do my daily 50 degree CT session? I get that if I want to continue healing, I need to maintain winter (keto diet + cold) until healing is completed. But I already know that won't be possible (I can't stay inside all summer long with 4 kids at home lol!!) I'm wondering if there's any benefit to continuing the cold exposure all summer, or if it may be somehow damaging.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/would-love-help-with-my-lab-tests.1542/post-25614>

Your thyroid tests aren't complete. You need a FT3 and a rT3 (taken at the same time). Your FT. 4 look a little low so I suspect your FT3 will also be low.

Your sex hormones also look a little low. I know you said you are post menopausal and maybe you don't want to add exogenous hormones to your body so take this FWIW. DHEA should be 275-400 and Kruse has said anything below 160 is deficient so you are right there. Testosterone Total s/b 60-76 for optimal. Menopause maybe different though for your sex hormones. I know way more about thyroid than I do hormones I just have a listing of optimized ranges. Hope that helps!

DHEAs part is 95%

second is cortisol 4%

the remaining 0.5% is everything else.

If you do not have good DHEAs & cortisol you can't even start conversation about good health.

But make sure that you do not use (doctor's favorites), direct cortisol supplementation.

That is for people on the way out (retirement homes and similar)

or

to survive few bad days

long term you must have cortisol made by your own body with just in time delivery

with best possibly tuned Circadian Rhythm.

\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Start someplace, do blood tests monthly, keep adjusting.

Supplementing DHEA is a two step process.

Start with 50mg/day DHEA keep adjusting per blood tests

# Thread: Paleo Physician's Network advice
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleo-physicians-network-advice.1545/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2012-05-19T00:46:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleo-physicians-network-advice.1545/post-25340>

I just recently went to a Chiropractor/MD in Pastoral Medicine(something like that can't remember what it said? not sure what that is exactly)

[http://newleafhealthandwellness.com/custom\_content/c\_46432\_meet\_the\_doctor.html](http://http://newleafhealthandwellness.com/custom_content/c_46432_meet_the_doctor.html)

Info about her is here. Visit was quite interesting...that applied kinesiology thing...weird. Nevertheless it seemed like a good visit and even if I'm skeptical about certain things I obviously won't write off everything because of that.

Anyway she gave told me she wanted me to start with a 3-5 day fast with these guidelines.

(Fast Guidelines

The goal of this fast is to quickly cut the inflammation in your body and regulate your blood sugar. A minimum of three days is required for best results. During the fast, do not take any supplements. Limit your dietary intake to a drink that is made up of freshly squeezed lime and/or lemon (with pulp), water, organic maple syrup (grade B is preferred), and brewed green tea (optional). Make 1 gallon each morning. Take sips of the drink every 15 minutes throughout the day.

Makes 1/2 Gallon

12 Tablespoons lemon or lime juice (~3 lemons)

8 Tablespoons Grade B Maple Syrup

64 oz filtered water

Exact proportions are not critical and you can make the drink as sour or diluted as you like.

If you are taking sips all day you should be able to stabilize your blood sugar without any foods. Expect to feel the need to urinate frequently. If you truly become hungry, you are allowed to eat one food (or a fresh veggie drink), but it must be a vegetable you rarely eat, such as Brussels Sprouts, cauliflower, etc. Do not add anything to the vegetables such as spices or dressings.

The strict limitation of food is important because youâ€™re trying to decrease the load on your immune system. Many people are sensitive to the foods that they eat most often. The goal of the original three days is not to decrease calories, but rather to decrease the load of foods your body reacts to. The reason lime and lemon juice are used is due to its alkalinizing properties which are usually required. Organic grade B maple syrup is suggested because it is sweeter than other grades, and therefore less maple syrup is needed to counter the sour taste. Green tea can be added to the mixture if you prefer and you can drink hot green tea all day during the initial 3-5 days.)

I'd like to know what people think about this fast and also applied kinesiology. Also I asked a few questions of the fast which I will also ask here. I read that raw unfiltered honey is beneficial for allergies as well as being healthy all around as long its not overdone partly in an article by Mark Sisson on marksdailyapple.com.

Anyway I e-mailed her asking if I could substitute Honey for the Grade B Maple syrup and how much and if using lemon juice concentrate stuff would be ok or if I should make sure to buy lemon/lime and make it myself. The lemon and lime stuff I have includes a few preservatives, but I haven't researched enough yet on how bad if at all they are. Sodium Benzoate, Sodium Metabisulfite and sodium sulfite. Just the benzoate in the lime.

### indigogirl — 2012-05-19T02:08:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleo-physicians-network-advice.1545/post-25349>

The drink seems remarkably like the Master Cleanse diet that Beyonce went on to lose weight for her role in the movie Dreamgirls.

I do think applied kinesiology has its merits. Many people think its woo woo.

Personally I am skeptical of a plan that says it can reduce your inflammation that quickly...but you need to do whats right for you.

### Raiken3712 — 2012-05-19T03:24:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleo-physicians-network-advice.1545/post-25358>

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-05-19T17:13:45+0300

# Thread: Dry Ice to Cool CT Bath?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-ice-to-cool-ct-bath.1546/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jim — 2012-05-19T04:47:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-ice-to-cool-ct-bath.1546/post-25406>

As my input water temperature goes up, I am looking for more efficient ways to cool down my tub water. I was in my local grocery store and noticed that they are selling dry ice. That got me thinking that I could use that to cool my tub water. Does anyone have any experience using dry ice? How much should I use? Can I let it touch the water directly? What are the safety concerns if any?

Thanks!

### indigogirl — 2012-05-19T05:15:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-ice-to-cool-ct-bath.1546/post-25417>

I don't know...dry ice can burn you...when you put it in a liquid it makes "fog" ...think a witch's cauldron on Halloween

### kcorcoran@comcast.net — 2012-05-19T06:05:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-ice-to-cool-ct-bath.1546/post-25429>

Dry ice is frozen carbon dioxide (co2). It's been awhile since physical chemistry, but dry ice sublimates (goes from frozen to gaseous) at normal room barometric pressure, so there's a good chance you could asphyxiate (deprive oxygen) yourself if you try this. depending on how much you use and room ventilation of course. You could find yourself nodding off into a deep sleep for good.

### MJ* — 2012-05-19T06:46:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-ice-to-cool-ct-bath.1546/post-25438>

> **incorky;25512 wrote:** Dry ice is frozen carbon dioxide (co2). It's been awhile since physical chemistry, but dry ice sublimates (goes from frozen to gaseous) at normal room barometric pressure, so there's a good chance you could asphyxiate (deprive oxygen) yourself if you try this. depending on how much you use and room ventilation of course. You could find yourself nodding off into a deep sleep for good.

Oh that seems too dangerous in a tub...no Jim no, we'd miss you...

### Nymue — 2012-05-23T15:42:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-ice-to-cool-ct-bath.1546/post-26650>

I wanted to chime in on this back when I first saw the thread, but my account hadn't been activated yet. Better late than never! I tried the dry ice route, as it's available at my local grocery store. 5 lbs. of the stuff took about half an hour to fully melt, and that was with help from me, swirling it around first with a broomstick (talk about looking like a witch stirring her bubbling cauldron, ha!), then with rubber gloved hands. I knew I couldn't get in until it was fully dissolved. So, after waiting all of that time, 5 lbs. of dry ice cooled my 80Â° tap water down to..... 75Â°! How disappointing. It's a good thing though, it cost me $1.49/lb. and would've been a VERY expensive way to CT.

I've gone the route now of filling up my largest tupperware containers w/water and freezing them. That gets my water down to about 63Â°. If I bought a 5lb. bag of ice or 2 and dumped that in, I think I'd be able to get it into the 50s.

### Jim — 2012-05-23T16:53:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-ice-to-cool-ct-bath.1546/post-26667>

> **Nymue;26742 wrote:** I wanted to chime in on this back when I first saw the thread, but my account hadn't been activated yet. Better late than never! I tried the dry ice route, as it's available at my local grocery store. 5 lbs. of the stuff took about half an hour to fully melt, and that was with help from me, swirling it around first with a broomstick (talk about looking like a witch stirring her bubbling cauldron, ha!), then with rubber gloved hands. I knew I couldn't get in until it was fully dissolved. So, after waiting all of that time, 5 lbs. of dry ice cooled my 80Â° tap water down to..... 75Â°! How disappointing. It's a good thing though, it cost me $1.49/lb. and would've been a VERY expensive way to CT.
>
> I've gone the route now of filling up my largest tupperware containers w/water and freezing them. That gets my water down to about 63Â°. If I bought a 5lb. bag of ice or 2 and dumped that in, I think I'd be able to get it into the 50s.
>
>
> Thanks for the information. I haven't tried it yet. I have been buying 10 lb bags of ice for $2.19. Three of those got my 64 F tap water down into the 40's. I was hoping the dry ice would work better than regular ice since it is much colder. It sublimates (goes from a solid to a gas) starting at -109 deg F.
>
>
>
> I found the following on [Wikipedia](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Freezing_mixture). I don't think I will be going the acetone route!!
>
>
>
> The simplest and cheapest cooling bath is an ice/water mixture, which maintains a temperature of 0 Â°C. For lower temperatures, three main types of cooling baths are typical:
>
>
>
> Ice
>
>
>
> A slurry of ice and an inorganic salt such as sodium chloride or calcium chloride can provide temperatures down to about −40 Â°C. The temperature depends on the amount and type of salt used, based on the freezing point depression effect.
>
>
>
> Dry ice
>
>
>
> A slurry of dry ice and a suitable organic solvent, such as acetone, can provide temperatures down to about −100 Â°C (with diethyl ether). Temperatures in the range of -12 Â°C to -78 Â°C can be conveniently generated with ethylene glycol / ethanol / dry ice mixtures.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-23T18:33:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-ice-to-cool-ct-bath.1546/post-26712>

Do you have a spare cooler? You could put water in containers (water jugs, ziploc bags, ice trays, etc.) in the cooler along with the dry ice. That might be enough to freeze the water in the containers or at least get it good and chilled. When we had a 2-day power outage last fall, we bought dry ice and it stayed frozen in coolers for days. Dry ice isn't cheap but it might be more cost-effective than buying ice.

### Nymue — 2012-05-24T00:30:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-ice-to-cool-ct-bath.1546/post-26846>

> **August;26807 wrote:** I think dry ice is considerable colder than what we are going for . . cold enough to cause major frostbite/tissue damage . . if the carbon dioxide does not do you in . . (which I think it might . . .)

Yup, this is why you'd have to wait for all of the dry ice to dissolve before getting into the tub. You want to make sure that it doesn't touch your skin and that you're not sitting there breathing in all of that carbon dioxide!

The cooler I grabbed it from at the store said that it is 5 times colder than ice. So I figured a 5 lb. block should be the equivalent of 25 lbs. of regular ice. No dice. Even so, 25 lbs. of ice would've been cheaper.

### Nymue — 2012-05-24T00:32:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-ice-to-cool-ct-bath.1546/post-26847>

> **Lyndra;26804 wrote:** Do you have a spare cooler? You could put water in containers (water jugs, ziploc bags, ice trays, etc.) in the cooler along with the dry ice. That might be enough to freeze the water in the containers or at least get it good and chilled. When we had a 2-day power outage last fall, we bought dry ice and it stayed frozen in coolers for days. Dry ice isn't cheap but it might be more cost-effective than buying ice.

The issue with using dry ice this way is that it won't stay frozen for long! I initially bought 2 10 lb. bags of dry ice. I used 5 lbs. the first night and put the rest in the freezer, w/the intention of returning the one unopened bag to the grocery store after my experiment didn't go as planned. Well, it should have been obvious that my freezer is not cold enough to keep something that is -109Â° frozen! Duh moment on my part!! Expensive duh moment, too.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-29T06:34:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-ice-to-cool-ct-bath.1546/post-28141>

Bummer it didn't last. I wonder if your freezer actually heated up the dry ice and melted it? Your freezer is intended to stay at around 0 degrees so if you put something super cold in there, it might not work as intended. In our case, we had the dry ice in a portable cooler. Once we were done with it, I opened up the cooler and it took the better part of a day for it to melt/evaporate.

# Thread: Coconut oil and the trots
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-and-the-trots.1550/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tes3@mac.com — 2012-05-20T01:12:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-and-the-trots.1550/post-25596>

Hi All,

If I eat dried coconut flakes or shredded coconut, I notice virtually no digestive disturbances. However, when I eat even a little bit of the coconut manna or coconut oil (extra virgin organic), I get "the trots", gas, bloating and all around misery.

What gives? Any thoughts? Thanks!.... Tom

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-20T04:25:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-and-the-trots.1550/post-25631>

Coconut oil is notorious for creating serious die-off - it is anti-microbial and kills the bad guys off. Part of its glory! Try starting off with a tsp of CO per day, then gradually working up to 1T/day, then slowly working up to 2 or 3 or 4 or whatever your goal is per day.

### tes3@mac.com — 2012-05-20T17:32:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-and-the-trots.1550/post-25695>

Die-off!! Thanks, MamaGrok. I keep discovering that digestive issues = friendly and unfriendly bacteria in the gut. No wonder I bloat like a balloon. Okay, so the idea is to use a small amount of CO, kill off some bad guys, and slowly increase. Got it.

Other than the gut issues, I do feel really good when I consume coconut. It's odd how I don't get this reaction from canned coconut milk or dried coconut. Just straight oil and the manna. The manna is the worst, I have to say. Huge digestive upset.

Thanks!.... Tom

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-20T17:38:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-and-the-trots.1550/post-25698>

Yes, it's the oil that is so powerful a detox agent. I wonder if the manna does it most for you b/c it's so delicious that it enables you to eat more oil than you ordinarily would? (Manna has a lot more oil than milk or plain coconut flesh)

I'm the opposite - I have built up a large tolerance for the oil, but can't yet tolerate the flesh at all, not even in manna. It seems I've pretty much fixed my bacterial imbalance, but still have a lot of physically damaged gut to fix.

# Thread: Doctors?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctors.1558/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sem — 2012-05-20T18:41:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctors.1558/post-25722>

How does everyone find good, progressive doctors they can trust? I've wasted so much money and time with doctors who haven't a clue! It is so frustrating. Anyone know of someone good in Los Angeles? Thank you.

### ColdBren — 2012-05-20T20:32:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctors.1558/post-25756>

i am in LA too, hoping someone answers you. I am considering going to holtorf med. group. they have a lot of info online and seem to be more progressive. They don't take insurance though.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-20T23:14:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctors.1558/post-25820>

Never trust a doc, conventional or not, if you can help it. Instead, listen and then go think for yourself. Be the one in charge..... is my humble opinion.

### L8F — 2012-05-21T04:23:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctors.1558/post-25872>

I found mine at Genova Diagnostic site, referral area. Got a bunch of names, looked them all up and chose one that looked right after calling etc. It has been amazing to have a bigger picture view. My personal opinion is that having the experiential knowledge coupled with one's own body of knowledge/personal research etc is the best combo.

I lost quite a bit of trust in the intellectual curiosity of physicians during my recent foray into Paleo and all of this LR stuff, and realizing that there is so much bias and broken-ness in medicine in general. That being said I feel that the knowledge of a good physician who can work with you, and help guide the direction of one's treatment has been something I could never have done on my own. I know a lot of conventional medicine, and now read for 6 months straight on the rest of this stuff, but still was at a loss about the treatments that are out there for a variety of problems. My new doc was great in pulling it all together in the span of an hour and a half, and had seen it all before. That was a huge weight off my chest.

I am a big fan of researching and learning, and directing the avenue to take in one's own care, but at a certain point I needed to have someone else treat me as a patient, and give my trust--with questions every 5 minutes--to them. It was like I could exhale finally. I have made a huge leap in progress with this help. (Not free though, mucho bucks)

Also think another good way to try might be to get a consult with someone like Chris Kresser, or some of the hormone guys Dzugan etc. Or there is the thought of getting oneself to another part of the country with a known quantity. For me that would be out near Austin with a guy named Lane Sebring, a Paleo doc x 12 years. He spoke at Paleo Fx and seems to know his stuff well. (But again, I would try to get an idea by talking to the clinician first, "Have you worked with X Y Z before, and can you rec something other than XYZ that I have tried thus far..." type thing...Most of us here have learned a lot, and tried a lot, and done lots of investigations on our own already. That calls for someone a little more experienced...

### johnnyb — 2012-05-21T05:59:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/doctors.1558/post-25891>

> **Souldanzer;25909 wrote:** Never trust a doc, conventional or not, if you can help it. Instead, listen and then go think for yourself. Be the one in charge..... is my humble opinion.

+1

I'm very fond of my Dr. She's does practice CW but seems very mindful that it's my life and cooperates to that end.

### Huck — 2012-05-21T23:03:49+0300

However sad this is, I seem to be homing in on something tweakable after 12 years of being told to eat less and exercise more. The list of low T consequenses over time is not pleasant reading, though - so I'm doubling my life insurance...

I tried to boost testosterone with pregnenolone and dhea and ended up with an enlarged prostate which caused urinary problems, blood in urine, UTI, high PSA, and extremely high dhea. After about 3 weeks of antibiotics and stopping dhea, 7-keto, and pregnenolone, I am back to normal. I mention this just as a reminder to get labs done and keep track of when you start and stop supplements.

For now, my focus is on losing weight and then I will work on boosting testosterone through exercise.

Approved by:

American Academy of Neural Therapy and

Institute of Neurobiology (Bellevue, WA, USA)

Institute for Neurobiologie (Stuttgart, Germany)

Academy for Balanced NeuroBiology Ltd (London, United Kingdom)

This lecture was presented by Dietrich Klinghardt M.D., Ph.D. at the Jean Piaget Department at the University of Geneva, Switzerland Oct. 2002 to physicians and dentists from Europe, Israel, several Arab countries and Asia.

### chocolate — 2012-05-21T17:41:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-klinghardt-neurotoxin-elimination-protocol.1568/post-25971>

[http://findarticles.com/p/articles/mi\_7396/is\_315/ai\_n42061272/pg\_5/](http://http://findarticles.com/p/articles/mi_7396/is_315/ai_n42061272/pg_5/)

A sensible approach to eliminating neurotoxins

by Dietrich Klinghardt

Dosage: Take 15 drops of BioPure cilantro tincture in hot water 3 times per day prior to a meal and at bedtime (during the vagus-dominant sleep phase, many detox functions are most active), or 30 minutes after taking chlorella. Cilantro causes the gallbladder to dump bile--containing the excreted neurotoxins--into the small intestine. The bile release occurs naturally as we are eating and is much enhanced by cilantro. As noted, if no chlorella is taken, the abundant nerve endings of the ENS reabsorb most neurotoxins as they travel through the small intestine. Gradually increase dose for full benefit. In the initial phase of the detox, cilantro should be given 5 days on, 2 days off.

The proprietary (or not) protocol is pretty simple.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-05-21T17:49:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-klinghardt-neurotoxin-elimination-protocol.1568/post-25973>

> **chocolate;26062 wrote:** [http://findarticles.com/p/articles/mi\_7396/is\_315/ai\_n42061272/pg\_5/](http://http://findarticles.com/p/articles/mi_7396/is_315/ai_n42061272/pg_5/)
>
>
>
> A sensible approach to eliminating neurotoxins
>
> by Dietrich Klinghardt
>
>
>
> Dosage: Take 15 drops of BioPure cilantro tincture in hot water 3 times per day prior to a meal and at bedtime (during the vagus-dominant sleep phase, many detox functions are most active), or 30 minutes after taking chlorella. Cilantro causes the gallbladder to dump bile--containing the excreted neurotoxins--into the small intestine. The bile release occurs naturally as we are eating and is much enhanced by cilantro. As noted, if no chlorella is taken, the abundant nerve endings of the ENS reabsorb most neurotoxins as they travel through the small intestine. Gradually increase dose for full benefit. In the initial phase of the detox, cilantro should be given 5 days on, 2 days off.
>
>
>
> The proprietary (or not) protocol is pretty simple.
