# Thread: garlic is bio active selenium...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garlic-is-bio-active-selenium.1570/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-21T17:30:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garlic-is-bio-active-selenium.1570/post-25965>

[http://www.cancer.gov/cancertopics/factsheet/prevention/garlic-and-cancer-prevention](http://http://www.cancer.gov/cancertopics/factsheet/prevention/garlic-and-cancer-prevention)

What is garlic?

Garlic is a vegetable (Allium sativum) that belongs to the Allium class of bulb-shaped plants, which also includes onions, chives, leeks, and scallions. Garlic is used for flavoring in cooking and is unique because of its high sulfur content. In addition to sulfur, garlic also contains arginine, oligosaccharides, flavonoids, and selenium, all of which may be beneficial to health (1).

The characteristic odor and flavor of garlic comes from sulfur compounds formed from allicin, the major precursor of garlicâ€™s bioactive compounds, which are formed when garlic bulbs are chopped, crushed, or damaged (2). **Bioactive compounds are defined as substances in foods or dietary supplements, other than those needed to meet basic nutritional needs, that are responsible for changes in health status.**

Do findings from population studies offer evidence that garlic may prevent cancer?

Several population studies show an association between increased intake of garlic and reduced risk of certain cancers, including cancers of the stomach, colon, esophagus, pancreas, and breast. Population studies are multidisciplinary studies of population groups that investigate the cause, incidence, or spread of a disease or examine the effect of health-related interventions, dietary and nutritional intakes, or environmental exposures. An analysis of data from seven population studies showed that the higher the amount of raw and cooked garlic consumed, the lower the risk of stomach and colorectal cancer (5).

The European Prospective Investigation into Cancer and Nutrition (EPIC) is an ongoing multinational study involving men and women from 10 different countries. This study is investigating the effects of nutrition on cancer. In the study, higher intakes of onion and garlic were associated with a reduced risk of intestinal cancer (6).

The Iowa Womenâ€™s Study is a large prospective study investigating whether diet, distribution of body fat, and other risk factors are related to cancer incidence in older women. Findings from the study showed a strong association between garlic consumption and colon cancer risk. Women who consumed the highest amounts of garlic had a 50 percent lower risk of cancer of the distal colon compared with women who had the lowest level of garlic consumption (7).

Several population studies conducted in China centered on garlic consumption and cancer risk. In one study, investigators found that frequent consumption of garlic and various types of onions and chives was associated with reduced risk of esophageal and stomach cancers, with greater risk reductions seen for higher levels of consumption (8). Similarly, in another study, the consumption of allium vegetables, especially garlic and onions, was linked to a reduced risk of stomach cancer (9). In a third study, greater intake of allium vegetables (more than 10 g per day vs. less than 2.2 g per day), particularly garlic and scallions, was associated with an approximately 50 percent reduction in prostate cancer risk (10).

Evidence also suggests that increased garlic consumption may reduce pancreatic cancer risk. A study conducted in the San Francisco Bay area found that pancreatic cancer risk was 54 percent lower in people who ate larger amounts of garlic compared with those who ate lower amounts (11).

In addition, a study in France found that increased garlic consumption was associated with a statistically significant reduction in breast cancer risk. After considering total calorie intake and other established risk factors, breast cancer risk was reduced in those consuming greater amounts of fiber, garlic, and onions (12).

### chocolate — 2012-05-27T05:25:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garlic-is-bio-active-selenium.1570/post-27795>

[http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-1304121/Garlic-tablets-Can-lead-lowering-blood-pressure.html](http://http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-1304121/Garlic-tablets-Can-lead-lowering-blood-pressure.html)

A garlic tablet a day... It could keep high blood pressure at bay

Read more: [http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-1304121/Garlic-tablets-Can-lead-lowering-blood-pressure.html#ixzz1w25kwVaE](http://http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-1304121/Garlic-tablets-Can-lead-lowering-blood-pressure.html#ixzz1w25kwVaE)

> In the trial, researchers from the unversity's general practice department gave capsules containing 960mg of aged garlic extract to patients with high blood pressure.
>
>
>
> Those who took the supplements every day had much lower readings after 12 weeks.
>
>
>
> A high blood pressure reading is one that exceeds 140/90 millimetres of mercury. The first figure, the systolic pressure, corresponding to the 'surge' that occurs with each heart beat.
>
>
>
> Patients in the trial who had a systolic reading above 140mmHg saw it drop 10.2 lower on average than a control group on a placebo or dummy pill.
>
>
>
> This reduced the blood pressure of some patients down to healthy levels.
>
>
>
> Dr Ried said 'This reduction is clinically significant, as a drop in systolic blood pressure by 5mmHg reduces the risk of cardiovascular disease by eight to twenty per cent.'
>
>
>
> Read more: [http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-1304121/Garlic-tablets-Can-lead-lowering-blood-pressure.html#ixzz1w26TSBqe](http://http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-1304121/Garlic-tablets-Can-lead-lowering-blood-pressure.html#ixzz1w26TSBqe)
>
>
> Garlic is thought to have an antihypertensive effect because it stimulates production of certain chemical substances called nitric oxide (NO) and hydrogen sulphide (H2S), which helps relax blood vessels.

### chocolate — 2012-05-27T06:23:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garlic-is-bio-active-selenium.1570/post-27801>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/6698185](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/6698185)

Hypolipidemic effects of garlic oil in rats fed ethanol and a high lipid diet.

Shoetan A, Augusti KT, Joseph PK.

Abstract

Feeding of ethanol and a high fat-high cholesterol diet to rats markedly increased the total lipids in the liver, and cholesterol and triglyceride levels in the serum, liver and kidneys. However, when ethanol mixed with 0.5% garlic oil was fed to animals maintained on the high fat-high cholesterol diet, these lipid levels were significantly reduced to levels near to those seen in untreated control rats. Garlic oil did not reduce the serum albumin or the total proteins of liver, kidneys or serum when fed along with ethanol. Probably the garlic oil enhances the catabolism of dietary cholesterol and fatty acids.

### chocolate — 2012-07-06T20:33:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garlic-is-bio-active-selenium.1570/post-35909>

[http://findarticles.com/p/articles/mi\_m0887/is\_12\_29/ai\_n56719683/](http://http://findarticles.com/p/articles/mi_m0887/is_12_29/ai_n56719683/)

Antibacterial ability of organoselenium compounds

Selenium (Se) was considered/identified as a toxic compound initially during its discovery. However. in the past, scientist eventually proved that this trace mineral plays a vital role in the regulation of immune system in both human and animals. **In addition, organoselenium is reported to have the better bioavailability and less toxicity than inorganic selenium. Organoselenium compounds have been identified to exhibit antibacterial, antiviral, antifungal. antiparasitic, antihistamine, and anticancer properties.** Extensive studies have also been conducted to synthesize stable organoselenium compounds, which has potential application as antioxidants, enzymemodulators, antitumors, antivirals, antimicrobials, antihypertensive agents, and cytokine inducers. However, limited number of organoselenium compounds were evaluated for its' antimicrobials feature.

Benzoylselenoglycolic acid, 3-\[(phenylcarbonyl)-selenyl\] propanoic acid, and their esters (8 derivatives) were synthesized and screened for antibacterial activity against E. coli. Bacillus subtilis, Staphylococcus aureus, and Salmonella typhimurium by the researchers from Provimi India Innovation Centre. The level of bacterial inhibition was determined quantitatively by minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) of the newly synthesized compounds.

The two mentioned organoselenium and the eight synthesized derivatives of the novel compound demonstrates moderate to significant antibacterial activity on the pathogenic bacterial species in the report. Significant inhibitory activity was achieved at the low concentration of 0.1 mg/ml against both gram positive and gram negative bacteria by the compound derivatives except S. typhimurium (for which the inhibitory effect was shown in much higher concentration range at 1-10mg/ml).

The study not only successfully synthesized eight novel compounds, the MIC of the organoselenium compounds also showed that they were efficient for application as the antibacterial agent on the pathogenic germs. Further investigation on the potential biological activity of the novel compounds is still in progress at Provimi India Innovation Centre, Bangalore.

Details: P.M. Radhakrishna, Provimi India Innovation Centre, Provimi Animal Nutrition India Ltd., Bangalore 560 106. India. Phone: 91-80-2856-2298. E-mail: [pmradhakrishna@provimi.in](mailto:pmradhakrishna@provimi.in).

COPYRIGHT 2010 Frost & Sullivan

COPYRIGHT 2011 Gale, Cengage Learning

### vlynnb — 2012-05-22T04:53:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/season-eating-figure-out-whats-in-season-where-you-live.1571/post-26187>

Epicurious map, I would describe it as a curiosity. For Idaho in May it lists apples, asparagus, and potatoes. Asparagus, yes. The only other things coming out of the ground is very early lettuce and it is only baby size in Southeastern Idaho. Earliest apples come on in mid July and potatoes are a fall crop. Is it accurate for other areas?

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-22T04:57:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/season-eating-figure-out-whats-in-season-where-you-live.1571/post-26189>

I saw some inaccuracies as well. Most apples aren't mature until aug/sep/early oct.

As for potatoes new potatoes can be harvested now if planted in feb, but are better left until late aug/early sept ( if you eat them. I've fully converted to cauliflower smashers so don't see much reason to eat potatoes). Look around and you'll find what grows regionally.

### vlynnb — 2012-05-25T05:17:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/season-eating-figure-out-whats-in-season-where-you-live.1571/post-27251>

I definitely agree that being aware of what is growing when around you works better than relying on a chart. There may be places in Idaho where some planting can occur in Feb. allowing for new potatoes now but not here. Most things are just being planted and we had a frost again last night, we can have them every month of the year.

I haven't done much gardening in the past but am learning. Too bad I probably won't be able to eat the famous nightshades that grow so well here. Maybe should look into cauliflower.

### Raiken3712 — 2012-05-21T18:06:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-05-02-2012.1572/post-25980>

Labs done on 05/02/2012 Second set of numbers are lab reference ranges. The Functional medicine Chiropractor had a second set of functional medicine ranges that aren't listed here that are obviously a bit different and she did stress that the cholesterol being high wasn'mt a big deal which she should considering shes on the Paleo Physicians network listing.

Cholesterol, Total 201 125-200

HDL Cholesterol 44 >40 m

Triglycerides 137

### Shijin13 — 2012-05-21T19:45:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-05-02-2012.1572/post-26030>

Quick look -taking a break from excel hell...  1st question - have you been CTing??? b/c that can throw testing off!

HDL is low - you want to get it up above 60mg/dl at a minimum

Trigs - too HIGH; you want to get that DOWN! High Trigs and Low HDL usually are an indicator of small pattern LDL (BAD!!!) How long have you been grain free? you need to push 100% paleo to adjust on this. no dairy, ghee allowed; also since your HDL is below 60 you should limit your nightshades.

the other things that pop out to me: your Fasting BG is HIGH - you are diabetic at 138!!! Dr K likes to see FBG below 80mg/dl fwiw! you need to ditch all grains/sugars, diary etc and go KETO until under control! You HbA1C is HIGH as well!

TSH is off the charts! your T3 looks a bit on the low side, while your T4 is mid range. I expect what T4 is being converted is being turned into Reverse T3 instead of T3. recommend next time around you get both free T3 and Reverse T3 tested as well.

Also notice you're don't have an hsCRP - that's a big one you should get - helps tell the picture.

Additionally - did you get any hormones run? if so post those when you get in...

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-21T21:27:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-labs-please.1580/post-26063>

About Naturethroid (NT): It takes almost 6 weeks for the synthroid to be gone and the tests to reflect the NT. When did you start the NT. Also, what most people see in natural dessicated thyroid (NDT) products is that the TSH gets extremely suppressed. With that in mind it looks like you are not on enough NT. Your T3 is way too low but you really need the FT3 and rT3. You FT4 is also too low. Hey the good news is your antibodies are low/non-existent!

Not sure about your other labs except your sodium is low. I just salt my food and that brought mine up.

### BJK77 — 2012-05-21T22:18:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-labs-please.1580/post-26086>

> **Patty Cakes;26154 wrote:** About Naturethroid (NT): It takes almost 6 weeks for the synthroid to be gone and the tests to reflect the NT. When did you start the NT. Also, what most people see in natural dessicated thyroid (NDT) products is that the TSH gets extremely suppressed. With that in mind it looks like you are not on enough NT. Your T3 is way too low but you really need the FT3 and rT3. You FT4 is also too low. Hey the good news is your antibodies are low/non-existent!
>
>
>
> Not sure about your other labs except your sodium is low. I just salt my food and that brought mine up.

Thanks Patty Cakes! I started the NT on January 1st, so it had been 4+ months on the NT prior to testing. I thought for sure my doc had ordered a free T3 and RT3, so I'm not sure why they aren't on here. There were so many tests on that paper I think the lab tech got totally confused and probably just missed them!

Yes, I'm super happy about the antibodies! This is the 3rd time I've had them tested now and I've always had the same result, so I think I can safely say I don't have Hashi's . . . one good thing to know

I can't understand why my sodium would be low. I put a good 1-1.5 teaspoons of sea salt on every meal and I sometimes drink salt water throughout the day too. I guess I'll have to try increasing it.

### bigknitwit — 2012-05-21T22:33:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-labs-please.1580/post-26099>

I switched from Synthroid to Armour back in February. I thought I was taking enough Armour to be "equivalent" to what I was taking with synthroid, and I even monitored my temperature along the way. By the beginning of May I was seriously hypothyroid (elevated TSH, depressed fT3/fT4), and showing signs of serious adrenal weakness (low blood pressure, nausea, even hypoglycemia symptoms, zero stress tolerance). It was awful. I am only now starting to climb out of it.

My point with all of this is that you look like you need to up your thyroid NT dose, never mind what "equivalent" dose guidelines are.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-22T03:03:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-labs-please.1580/post-26153>

> **BJK77;26177 wrote:** Thanks Patty Cakes! I started the NT on January 1st, so it had been 4+ months on the NT prior to testing. I thought for sure my doc had ordered a free T3 and RT3, so I'm not sure why they aren't on here. There were so many tests on that paper I think the lab tech got totally confused and probably just missed them!
>
>
>
> Yes, I'm super happy about the antibodies! This is the 3rd time I've had them tested now and I've always had the same result, so I think I can safely say I don't have Hashi's . . . one good thing to know
>
>
>
> I can't understand why my sodium would be low. I put a good 1-1.5 teaspoons of sea salt on every meal and I sometimes drink salt water throughout the day too. I guess I'll have to try increasing it.

Diuretics can "suck" the sodium out of our systems too! Since your sodium is low you may want to check your adrenals. Not sure of the connection but I think there is one there.

Also go to [www.stopthethyroidmadness.com](http://http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com) and read up on how to increase the dose. You can easily have happen what happened to bigknitwit several times if you don't raise it fast enough. But you don't want to raise so fast your body goes hyper. Most docs don't give patients the opportunity to do it themselves (my new doc does) so read up and be diligent. It worked for me as I was self treating when I first found out I had hypothyroid.

### bigknitwit — 2012-05-22T15:02:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-labs-please.1580/post-26298>

They didn't check your TgAb antibodies though. You need to have both antibody tests run to rule out Hashimoto's (it's not unusual for doctors to only check up on Tpo like yours did). Also, if you are concerned about adrenal function, you could ask to have an 8am fasting cortisol ordered. It's certainly not as telling as the 4 point saliva test, but it does start to give you an idea of your adrenal function. YOu may want to consider having aldosterone checked as well, given the low sodium. I second going to stopthethyroidmadness for more feedback. There are some very knowledgeable people over there with excellent insights.

# Thread: Gaps diet?

**Subforum:** Optimal Kids

### Sem — 2012-05-21T22:05:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gaps-diet.1582/post-26077>

Anyone have success doing this with your kids? Any advice? My kids have lots of food allergies, ADHD, eczema, etc. we've cut out gluten and way back on sugar but I don't think it's enough. Thinking of trying GAPS.

### Shelley — 2012-05-24T04:52:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gaps-diet.1582/post-26943>

Hi Sem

Yes - we've been eating GAPS for 2.5 years now for ADHD, Aspergers, asthma, eczema, and dysbiosis. Certainly its really hard to adjust at the beginning but I reckon its all worth it. We've left behind several of those issues and are working to get rid of them all.

Shelley

### Sem — 2012-05-24T05:39:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gaps-diet.1582/post-26954>

Thanks for responding Shelley. That's really impressive. How long did it take before you saw changes and in what areas? I just ordered the book so will read that and see what'd I can figure out how to implement this.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-08T23:52:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gaps-diet.1582/post-30201>

> **Sem;26168 wrote:** Anyone have success doing this with your kids? Any advice? My kids have lots of food allergies, ADHD, eczema, etc. we've cut out gluten and way back on sugar but I don't think it's enough. Thinking of trying GAPS.

Hi Sem,

I have also had success with the SCD/GAPS diet. My youngest daughter had serious asthma as a infant and toddler. She is also prone to hallucinations and behavior problems. I put her on the diet when she was about five years old. She is now nine. (The younger the child is on the diet the better the results. Sometimes they make a full recovery and can eat any food without issue. Adults or older children do not fair as well.)

She is off all medications for asthma and she rarely sees things that are not there anymore. Her behavior is much improved and she can learn better. She is still below grade level but improving fast. On the SCD/GAPS she is a bright delightful child. We have to be very careful with her diet or we can have up to 72 hours of hell. We have reintroduced some foods but I sometimes wonder if it is really worth it.

### vlynnb — 2012-06-12T07:14:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gaps-diet.1582/post-30664>

Caroline, Can you give me some references in relation to the hallucination and diet connection?

### Sem — 2012-06-12T10:10:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gaps-diet.1582/post-30666>

Caroline, I would love to hear more about your experience. Did you ease into the diet or go right to intro? I'd like to get through intro before school starts back up but don't really have a block of time where we are not traveling until August. I am thinking that I can start with things like bone broth, etc until then. I can also start cutting some other things out. Any tips and tricks are much appreciated!

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-14T18:24:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gaps-diet.1582/post-31139>

> **vlynnb;30768 wrote: Caroline, Can you give me some references in relation to the hallucination and diet connection?**

**

I would suggest reading two books on the topic:

1\. **Breaking the Vicious Cycle: Intestinal Health Through Diet** by Elaine Gottchall

(She talks about her daughter having some sort of night terror/seizure; got me thinking about my own seizure disorder which has since resolved on the diet.)

2\. Gut and Psychology Syndrome** by Dr Natasha Campbell-McBride

(Amazing story about her son and Autism. Lots of references to mental illness and gut-brain-axis disorders.)

Here is more information about the SCD/GPAS and my personal story. I have not written much about Erika's problems. I'm not sure if she would want me to:

[http://eatkamloops.org/specific-carbohydrate-diet-scd/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/specific-carbohydrate-diet-scd/)

[http://eatkamloops.org/specific-carbohydrate-diet-a-personal-story/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/specific-carbohydrate-diet-a-personal-story/)

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-14T18:43:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gaps-diet.1582/post-31143>

> **Sem;30770 wrote:** Caroline, I would love to hear more about your experience. Did you ease into the diet or go right to intro? I'd like to get through intro before school starts back up but don't really have a block of time where we are not traveling until August. I am thinking that I can start with things like bone broth, etc until then. I can also start cutting some other things out. Any tips and tricks are much appreciated!

I started on the SCD about six years ago. I did not know about Dr Natasha Campbell-McBride's work in the UK on GAPS. I was eating out of the industrial food system at the time. The diet still helped me. I have since started eating organic food and pasture based meats. I do well on a very high fat diet.

When you are talking about doing the "intro" I am assuming you are referring Dr Natasha Campbell-McBride's GAPS program. Am I right? I have never done the "intro" and always felt good on the diet but I have found some people have serious neurological or physical problems starting the program. (Whatever their symptoms they had would get worst for a time.) My advice is to go slow if you have any serious neurological problems. Use fermented foods for at least a month or more before trying therapeutic probiotics.

Here are some common problems:

[http://eatkamloops.org/specific-carbohydrate-diet-common-problems/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/specific-carbohydrate-diet-common-problems/)

Here is about six years of SCD/GAPS/modified paleo diet recipes:

[http://eatkamloops.org/specific-carbohydrate-diet-scd-recipes/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/specific-carbohydrate-diet-scd-recipes/)

If traveling is an issue, these are some things I have learned over the years. Erika is the child on the right. She is also on SCD/GAPS:

[http://eatkamloops.org/eating-nourishing-traditional-foods-while-traveling/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/eating-nourishing-traditional-foods-while-traveling/)

Part of my volunteer work is peer support for SCD/GAPS. I am not a medically trained but I do have some experience with the diet. If you need help just email me, I would be happy to help if I can:

[info@eatkamloops.org](mailto:info@eatkamloops.org)

### vlynnb — 2012-06-18T20:44:27+0300

# Thread: Seasonal Eating
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonal-eating.1584/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AKMan — 2012-05-22T02:21:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonal-eating.1584/post-26142>

There are lots of green things popping up in the far north! I have been giving much thought to seasonal eating in Alaska as I watch the seasons progress.

In Late Fall/Winter (Nov - Apr), meat would be the mainstay--protein/fat. There are also frozen and dried berries, dried mushrooms, seeds, and bones (broth). Alaskan Eskimos would raid ground squirrel nests for seed caches, many squirrels also cache mushrooms which dry and can be eaten all winter long. Cold and darkness rule.

Late Winter thru Late Spring (Apr - May) would be the hungriest month. Animals have used their fat stores, cached fruit/fungus is gone, fish are harder to catch as ice is thickest now. This would be a time of lean meat, starvation, and eating borderline foodstuffs--leather hides, sinew, dried grass...Light increasing, still very cold.

Late spring (like now) - ice melts and millions of migrating birds appear. Eggs would be on the menu for a few short weeks a year. Fish are easy to catch as they begin pre-spawn feeding patterns, 24 hours of daylight mean lots of time to forage for food. Green shoots appear such as the edible fireweed and raspberry leaves.

Late Spring thru Mid Fall (May - Oct) - Time of plenty, 24 hr sun, mild temps: 40's-70's. Food to eat would not be an issue, but you'd start to think about stockpiling food for winter. Berries start to ripen as early as mid-June and are found until freezeup in Oct. Many edible mushrooms, seaweed, greens.

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-22T05:40:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonal-eating.1584/post-26226>

Thanks for sharing with us what you've learned. I find it fascinating!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-22T05:45:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonal-eating.1584/post-26233>

One thing I'm noticing is, as my environment is getting sunnier and hotter, I'm enjoying my CT more - but finding staying keto and sticking the RX is much tougher. Also, my advances seem to be slowing.

@ Jack or ?

Is it TOO much of an uphill battle to combine summer and being a full on cold water mammal? What does progress in the summer looking like? Longer days/Hotter days = more CT? At some point there is going to be a 'not enough hours in the day to be in the bath'.

Ponder Ponder.

# Thread: Questions about labs....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-about-labs.1586/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### justjoan060 — 2012-05-22T05:00:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-about-labs.1586/post-26194>

In reading many, many threads, it seems like a lot of you have a really good grasp on your labs and get them done routinely. How does that work? I'm not going to be able to get a doctor to order the same labs that frequently I don't think - or afford it. I'm sure they're not all covered by insurance. Do some of you order them online, then send them in to be read? Read them yourself? If someone can shed some light on how this all works the best and cheapest, I'd appreciate it. If you order them, where? Blood, saliva or urine? I'm a newbie, as you can tell. Appreciate any input you all have!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-05-22T21:07:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-about-labs.1586/post-26435>

Here are a couple of places to get labs online:

privatemdlabs.com

ineedlabs.com has changed but go there and you will be redirected

directlabs.com

I read them myself and do a lot of research to check the info against my results. From what I understand you want saliva for the 4x diurnal cortisol test (adrenals), blood for sex steroid hormones and electrolytes, liver panel, cholesterol and most others. Magnesium should be RBC Magnesium test or as Dr. K recommends the Exatest (not sure where to get that w/o a doc).

### JC2K — 2012-05-25T22:55:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-about-labs.1586/post-27442>

For me, I've gotten my blood tests through Life Extension (lef.org) and fulfilled through Labcorp. I'm sure I could get some through insurance, but not necessarily the ones I want. I'm not going for monthly tests though. I just did them to get a baseline, and I'll probably get them again in 3 months or so to make sure they're consistent and no major problems. In terms of cost, I can afford it now, so your mileage will vary. But like a lot of things, it comes down to what's important to you. I figure that you can't really know anything without a baseline to start from.

### lisaeh3 — 2012-05-22T19:08:05+0300

### kcorcoran@comcast.net — 2012-05-22T23:11:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs.1589/post-26487>

I'm not a doc, but your TSH is suppressed and your not on thyroid meds correct? tpo and thryoglobulin, and TBI antibodies should be tested to rule out hashimoto's or graves disease. A reverse t3 and ferritin should be tested too at the minimum. Get an anti-aging doc to work on thyroid with you. Your cholesterol is good, which seems to indicate that your thyroid might be ok.

Looks like you're taking too much DHEA, is that a DHEA or DHEA-s level on your report?

Do you still have ovaries? What problems are you trying to treat resolve? Are you having menopausal symptoms?

The cardio CRP can definitely be lower! Goal is to get it close to zero.

Your chloride is low, are you restricting salt?

> The cardio CRP can definitely be lower! Goal is to get it close to zero.
>
> Your chloride is low, are you restricting salt?

Thank you, incorky! No I am not on thyroid meds but have a lot of fatigue and pain. The test for Dhea was the S level. I have also stopped supplementation. My ovaries were removed. I just started bio-identical hormones yesterday. My problems I am wanting to resolve are fatique, foggy thinking, depression issues and treating my fibromylagia issues and possible biotoxins.

I know my cardio CRP needs to be lower but I was pretty surprised it was not higher due to my health issues.

No I am not restricting salt and interested in why my chloride is low as well. Also want to know why my insulin is low too.

### gsteppe14@gmail.com — 2012-11-25T15:56:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs.1589/post-65574>

I'd get fasting c-peptide tested along with fasting blood sugar to gauge your insulin status.Â  HbA1c, too, while at it.Â  Do you have any hypoglycemic symptoms?Â  Nothing else is remarkable except that your Alkaline Phosphatase, which is supposedly normal but somewhat high given the wide range they're using.Â  What lab is this?Â  It's high due to non-liver related factors.Â  Not a major issue but I'd watch it.Â  Otherwise your liver #s are excellent.Â  Low FT3 and probably high Rt3 but do you have overt hypothyroid symptoms?Â  It's possible.Â  Your WBCs are low-normal so you could have some subclinical symptoms.Â  If you don't, I wouldn't worry.

### ealachan — 2012-05-22T21:56:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dim-supplementation-makes-for-colorful-urine.1595/post-26455>

I've been taking a DIM supplement (the one recommended in the "Jack Recommends" section) for a couple weeks now. I take two pills in the morning with breakfast, and by mid-afternoon I notice that my urine is a fairly bright orangey color. Is anyone else experiencing this? I've had vitamins change my urine colors before, but not quite so marked as this.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-22T22:06:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dim-supplementation-makes-for-colorful-urine.1595/post-26461>

Every time I open the "vitamin" cabinet, it dings my "bs" meter. It was funny, when the Local gals were over, I showed them my vitamins and they said they had the same vast collections.

The root of my concern I think is this: if we supplement too much, will then our bodies stop producing and reacting appropriately?

Will my body cease to make VitD because it's expecting it as a pill? And on and on.

# Thread: My Oil Pulling N=1
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-oil-pulling-n-1.1598/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 45
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AKMan — 2012-05-23T02:06:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-oil-pulling-n-1.1598/post-26523>

I've mentioned Oil Pulling a few times, even 'asked Jack'. He said he likes it. Well here's the deal, and my 6 month N=1.

Oil Pulling as explained in this study [http://www.jisppd.com/article.asp?issn=0970-4388;year=2008;volume=26;issue=1;spage=12;epage=17;aulast=Asokan](http://http://www.jisppd.com/article.asp?issn=0970-4388;year=2008;volume=26;issue=1;spage=12;epage=17;aulast=Asokan)

"Oil pulling has been used extensively for many years, without scientific evidence or proof, as a traditional Indian folk remedy to prevent teeth decay, oral malodor, bleeding gums, dryness of throat and cracked lips, and for strengthening the teeth, gums, and jaws."

It is often touted as a cure, or detox, for many medical conditions--but not proven. This study examined it's effect on dental health, and that is where I focused my N=1.

From this study: "The mechanism by which oil pulling therapy causes plaque inhibition and reduction in S. mutans is not known. The viscosity of the oil could probably inhibit bacterial adhesion and plaque coaggregation. Other possible mechanism might be the saponification or the 'soap-making' process that occurs as a result of alkali hydrolysis of fat. \[21\] Sesame oil is a vegetable fat and when acted upon by salivary alkalis like bicarbonate, the soap making process is initiated. Soaps are good cleansing agents because they are effective emulsifiers. Emulsification is the process by which insoluble fats like sesame oil can be broken down into minute droplets and dispersed in water. Emulsification greatly enhances the surface area of the oil, thereby increasing its cleansing action."

My experiment:

I get a dental cleaning every 6 months, have had 2-3 cavities in the past 20 years, and I have one crown on a tooth that cracked. I have about 10 'old-school' fillings.

My hygenist always says I need to brush and floss better and have lots of gingivitis/caries. My Dentist says my receding gums will cause problems one day. I have numerous sensitive teeth.

For the past 6 months, I have practiced daily oil pulling. I use about 1 tsp of pure sesame oil. I put it in my mouth and 'swish' it around for 15-20 minutes. It's very relaxing and a good time for self-reflection and mindfulness as you cannot speak during this time. After the time is up, I spit it in the sink and immediately brush without toothpaste.

Today I had my 6 month dental appointment. The hygenist was amazed that there was not plaque buildup and the cleaning took about 1/3 of the time it normally does. At my last visit, they noted in my records I had 11 pockets greater than 5 mm. The deepest was 9 mm. I had 5 more borderline pockets of 4 mm. The goal is under 3 mm.

After 6 months of oil pulling, I now have only 1 pocket deeper than 3 mm. The 9mm pocket is now 4mm. All of the rest were 1's and 2's. This may not mean much to you right now--but it is significant!

The dentist was shocked and said my teeth, gums, and mouth were one of the healthiest he's ever seen. We talked at length about oil pulling. I sent him the study linked above. He wants to talk more after he's read it.

I think that oil pulling also activates leptin receptors and helps in other ways unrelated to oral hygeine, but oral hygeine is just about as important as gut health in the grand scheme.

Hope you find this interesting.

### AKMan — 2012-05-23T08:16:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-oil-pulling-n-1.1598/post-26626>

> **Jim;26679 wrote:** I use coconut oil myself however I am not as fastidious as AKMan. I do believe that it is very effective and I am inspired to do it more regularly.

Everything I've read says use sesame oil or sunflower oil. Not sure why. CO seems like a great oil to try. You spit it out in the end, so if you are worried about PUFAs from sesame oil, it's not an issue, I believe.

From the study linked above: "Oil pulling therapy can be done using edible oils like sunflower or sesame oil. Sesame oil is derived from the plant Sesamum indicum , which is considered the queen of the oil seed crops because of its many beneficial effects. \[3\] "

and

"Sesame oil has three lignans: sesamin, sesamolin, and sesaminol; they have antioxidant properties and they also potentiate vitamin E action. Sesame oil has high amounts of polyunsaturated fatty acids; and lipid peroxidation is reduced, thereby reducing free radical injury to the oral tissues"

### nonchalant — 2012-05-23T18:37:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-oil-pulling-n-1.1598/post-26713>

As someone mentioned before, oil pulling is a great way to pass CT time in the tub.

### AKMan — 2012-05-23T18:43:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-oil-pulling-n-1.1598/post-26718>

> **DaveK;26724 wrote:** Hi AKMan,
>
>
>
> How did you time your oil pulling sessions in relation to your meals? Before, after, any time at all?

I get home from work 30 minutes before my wife. As soon as I walk in the door, I take a swig of sesame oil right out of the bottle. As I'm doing my 'get home chores' I swish the oil around my mouth. When I see my wife pull up in the driveway, I spit it out and brush with water. We eat dinner about an hour later.

I think doing this before dinner (or BAB, lunch) has the added benefit of signaling your mouth's leptin receptors that you are eating and it preps your digestive system for the meal. That's just my half-thought-out theory, but I'd bet it's doing something with your leptin sensors in the mouth.

For people who wish to try it. I get my sesame oil in the Asian section at Safeway. I use the Dynasty brand because it is 100% pure sesame oil. They also have blends with canola and soybean oil--I don't use those. For your first time, just use a tiny bit, like half a teaspoon. It will 'grow' as you swish it because it mixes with your spit. It is this mixing, or emulsifying, that is supposed to be the real magic behind it's effectiveness. I will have to try CO and see if it emulsifies the same as sesame oil.

I don't find the taste bad at all, kind of like it actually. Just make sure you don't try to talk with a mouthful of oil, it will ruin your shirt!

### AKMan — 2012-05-23T18:45:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-oil-pulling-n-1.1598/post-26719>

> **Nonchalant;26805 wrote:** As someone mentioned before, oil pulling is a great way to pass CT time in the tub.

YES! I have done this, too! The timing is about right as well. Plus, having the oil in your mouth is a great way to make sure you keep your breathing and school-girl screams under control. (OK, so maybe I don't scream like a school-girl, but stepping into cold water makes me want to!).

### AKMan — 2012-05-23T18:54:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-oil-pulling-n-1.1598/post-26725>

I have another study on oil pulling, a triple-blind, 20 subject random control type study. It's on pdf and too big to attach, and I don't have a link. If anyone wants to see it, PM me and I'll email it to you. Here is the conclusion of this study:

"Within the limits of the present study, it can be concluded that

Oil Pulling has the ability to reduce plaque and gingivitis. It

emerges from this study that an individual is able to maintain

low levels of plaque and gingivitis even if he is performing

Oil Pulling, just for 45 days.

This study is just the tip of an iceberg and we recommend that

further longitudinal studies with larger sample size should be

conducted. Research comparing the use of different types of

oils for the Oil Pulling procedure, and their effect on oral

hygiene are needed. Also microbiological analysis of saliva

and plaque if considered will add more internal validity to

such studies. Further, studies comparing the effect of various

commonly prescribed mouthwashes and Oil Pulling on the oral

hygiene are warranted."

They also talk about other oils:

"Though refined sunflower oil has been used in the present study, there has been mention of other oils like sesame oil, groundnut

oil, olive oil, mustard oil etc. Liquids from water and milk to extracts of gooseberries have been used for Oil Pulling. Some

oils are more palatable than others in their raw state. Hence the author after trying various types of oils, came to a

conclusion to use the most palatable, the refined sunflower oil for this study."

### AKMan — 2012-05-23T19:02:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-oil-pulling-n-1.1598/post-26734>

> **Cavemam;26787 wrote:** Thanks Akman! So helpful. I guess I'm in the same boat, I've been thinking of doing it - but in the back of my mind it was relegated to "ridiculous \*\*\*\* hippies do". Looks like I might be a hippie, cause after your post, I think I'm going to start!

The only drawback I can see to widespread use is compliance. Not everybody has a 20 minute period every day they can do this. I'd also love to see a trial on a 'minimum effect dose', maybe you only need 5 minutes a day or only need to do it once a week to be effective. The literature I read all says 10-20 minutes every day. The studies all show significant results after 30-45 days.

Another reason this will never go mainstream is there is no money in it for anyone. It's so simple and cheap it's only being studied in 3rd world countries where they can't afford mouthwash and toothpaste--shame really! But CT is kind of in the same boat, no money for the big drug companies, maybe Rubbermaid, but not Monsanto.

### MJ* — 2012-05-23T19:08:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-oil-pulling-n-1.1598/post-26735>

All this of course after flossing. I couldn't do the mag oil. I'm not that picky with food but DANG I could not handle the taste of that mag oil in my mouth! The BF is now just brushing with baking soda and we are both going to try the oil pulling with CO. I can't use sesame due to a gluten cross reaction. I love the taste and really wish I could use it. ### JC2K — 2012-05-23T23:06:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-oil-pulling-n-1.1598/post-26818>

> **AKMan;26718 wrote:** Everything I've read says use sesame oil or sunflower oil. Not sure why.

My recollection is it originated in India and the traditional oil is sesame oil. It's a popular oil in India, used for a lot of misc other things. My personal theory is it's a similar situation to Ghee. Ghee and Sesame Oil last a long time in the India heat before going rancid (big when you don't have refrigeration). Sunflower oil is also mentioned, but I don't know if it is as traditional as sesame because it goes rancid fast in heat. Seems to have similar effects though, which brings up the question as to whether or not it is the oil, or just the action of swishing something for 20 minutes with a fairly clean substance (so you could possibly use any oil, like olive or coconut, etc).

### N~Doro — 2012-05-24T18:43:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-oil-pulling-n-1.1598/post-27049>

> **JC2K;27073 wrote:** I don't think anyone really knows for sure. Or rather, you don't hear about the ones that failed (mouthful of motor oil anyone?  ), only the ones that succeeded.

For as much anecdotal evidence as you could want about as many different kinds of oil as you can think of

[http://www.earthclinic.com/Remedies/oil\_pulling.html](http://http://www.earthclinic.com/Remedies/oil_pulling.html)

My experience: quitting grains made the biggest improvement to my teeth and gums. I did start oil pulling a few months into the paleo thing. It definitely made my teeth whiter as well as continuing the improvement to sensitive teeth and a few 4 or 5 depth pockets. The last time I went to the dentist for a cleaning I could tell they were trying to come up with something to do to fill twenty minutes. It used to take close to an hour to scrape the plaque. The hygienist waited for the dentist to leave then asked me in detail what I was doing and where to read more about it. Said she loved the color of my teeth and couldn't believe I never had them whitened.

I used to hate to floss because my gums always bled, but now it feels great. My whole mouth seems so much healthier.

I use sesame oil sometimes, but mostly CO for the antibacterial/anti-fungal properties. I have read that if you use olive oil for too long it can turn your teeth GREEN. I can just picture cavemam now...

"06/12/2009: Sandy from Bangalore, India: "Dear Earthclinic,

Based on all feedback received for oil pulling so far (as seen on your oil pulling pages), I have created the following list of cures by oil-pulling using various oils:

1\. Sesame Oil:

Cleared vaginal infections, got rid of tooth/gum/mouth/bad-breath problems, tightened loose teeth, heart felt better, depression replaced by happy thoughts, better vision (eg. Colors become vivid), no underarm odor, reduces cravings and helps de-addiction, alertness increased, pimples decreased, cured Temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJD, TMJ or TMD), moisturized skin, healed dry/cracked heels, hair grew back on bald spot on head, sinuses cleared, did not catch colds/flu, brought color to cheeks/complexion looks healthier, cured the keratosis pilaris on upper arms, more energetic, didn't get allergies, looked 10 years younger, open pores on face closed, wrinkles disappeared, swollen glands under neck became normal for HIV+ person, anaemia cured within 1 month, lipase enzyme levels brought back to normal within 1 month, bloating/edema gone, decreased fibromyalgia pain, got deep sleep/ insomnia cured, dark circle around eyes decreased, cured hangover after alcohol consumption, digestion improved, back pain decreased, neck pain decreased, joint pain decreased, hives cured in 2 weeks, periods was not painful, no cramping during periods, experienced colon cleansing, eczema disappeared, skin glowed, stopped a headache, faded age spots, healed canker sores, subsided PMS, eliminated asthama, lost weight, cured diarrhea.

2\. Sunflower oil

Got rid of tooth/gum/mouth/bad-breath problems, teeth whiter and sensitive condition corrected, sinus cleared, cured toothache, blood cholesterol profile got balanced when oil-pulled first thing in the morning, postmenopausal hot flushes decreased, wake up refreshed, hair grew thicker, think clearly, swollen lymph nodes reduced, energy increased (many people said), reduced dependence on caffeine-oil pulling gave a huge energy rush-oil pulling twice a day eliminated 2 cups of coffee from that day, blood circulation improved, cold hands and feet became warmer, cravings decreased, skin looks great, eliminates dry eyes, clears out sugar from body faster, pulmonary fibrosis cured, cancer cured, stopped vaginal discharge, clearer breathing, better sleep, lungs not congested anymore, coughing eliminated, herpes simplex virus eliminated from body, cold sores and canker sores healed, cleared allergies, hangover (due to alcohol) eliminated, lost weight especially around lower abdomen, lost 4 pounds in 20 days by oil pulling twice a day, gave sound sleep, body aches and strains gone, fatigue gone, irregular periods became regular, ear itch gone, clearer eyes and clearer vision, indigestion problems and gases eliminated, cracked heels healed, dark circle under eyes eliminated.

3\. Olive Oil

Better mouth health, jaw pain gone, sinus cleared, finger nails grew fast and had white tips, hair grew fast and is thick, better energy, better mental clarity, mood elevation (many people said this), cracked heels got healed, softer hair, softer skin, no wheezing or coughing, eyes stopped feeling itchy or dry, beard got thicker and softer, de-addicted from tobacco, alcohol, caffeine (many people talked of de-addiction), back pain reduced, cleared nasal and ear blockage, lost weight doing oil-pulling using extra virgin olive oil, pores on face became small, no undereye puffiness, better vision, lines around eyes smoothened out, brain fog cured.

4\. Coconut oil

Appetite suppressed and lost weight (many people said this), relaxing, pain in nerve decreased, joint pain gone, breathing easier, snoring gone, bloating/oedema gone in just 6 days, blood pressure stabilized, got rid of tooth/gum/mouth/bad-breath problems, gap between teeth closed, sinus cleared, heightened sense of taste, cleared inner ear infection, got rid of rocking/imbalance sensation, skin glowed and moisterized, increased energy, increased mobility, agility and vigour.

5\. Cod liver oil

Got rid of teeth problems, better skin

6\. Avocado oil

Makes hair healthy and shiny, bags under eyes flattened, sinus cleared

7\. Cedar nut oil

Whiter teeth

8\. Canola oil

Better mouth health

9\. Walnut oil

Got rid of gum problems

10\. Castor oil

Induces sleep

11\. Black cumin seed oil

Drained mucus

12\. Safflower oil

Pulling sensation in arm gone, Got rid of tooth/gum/mouth/bad-breath problems, lost weight, energetic and active, Rheumatoid Arthritis cured, chocolate addiction gone, hair and skin looks good, scar gone, periods improved, PMS gone, voice improved, got good sleep, hair shedding decreased."

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-23T18:15:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-transfusions-yes-or-no.1606/post-26698>

Ok, I'm doing my research on Blood Transfusions (for me, suggested treatment for severe low blood issue).

Before I ask Jack the wrong way and get told "NO ANSWER FOR YOU!"  I wanted to see what you guys know about it. This is like my very own little research based medical brain trust. Akman, add that to your list!

seriously, those who have access to all those studies - anything? Ladies with realworld knowledge. . .know anything?

### MJ* — 2012-05-23T18:32:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-transfusions-yes-or-no.1606/post-26710>

Unless it's an emergency the only blood transfusion I want is from my own blood or from people I know - it's like are you going to do all this good eating and then take blood for a wheater? ...I dunno you just don't know where that blood's been...

### MJ* — 2012-05-23T18:42:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-transfusions-yes-or-no.1606/post-26717>

The Boston Globe Magazine reports that blood specialist Dr. Charles Huggins admits that blood “must be considered unavoidably non-safe.” He describes blood as “the most dangerous substance we use in medicine.”

This article goes on to address [5 myths](http://http://fleur-hupston.suite101.com/blood-transfusions-safe-medicine-a69848) about blood transfusions

### MJ* — 2012-05-23T18:59:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-transfusions-yes-or-no.1606/post-26728>

read [this](http://http://www.anemia.org/patients/blood-donation/deficiencies.php) for clues as to why blood can be low and possible alternatives...

### Martha Ray — 2019-12-04T16:46:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ox-bile-bile-acid-factors-recommendations.1607/post-283077>

**
**Got Bile? Part 3 shared by Martha Barger**

**Martha Ray's Notes:** There is some conflicting info out there as to what foods to avoid or eat…
These tips from [Bile function and liver: Foods that help increase bile production](https://www.belmarrahealth.com/bile-function-liver-foods-help-increase-bile-production/)
By: [Dr. Victor Marchione](https://www.belmarrahealth.com/author/drmarchione/) | [Liver](https://www.belmarrahealth.com/category/liver) | October 10, 2016**

**Bile production is largely based on the foods we eat. Certain foods can increase bile production.
These foods include celery, radish, and artichokes.
Here are some tips to naturally increase your liver’s bile production:**

· Drink a glass of warm water with lemon upon awakening

· Avoid eating sugars and processed foods

· Consume bitter foods

· Eat garlic, onions, and carrots

· Drink tea made from dandelion, peppermint, green tea, or ginger

· IF dairy does not bother you and you own a share of a A2 grass fed cow prepare and drink homemade buttermilk and yogurt

****Tips to keep your liver healthy** Below you will find helpful tips that can help keep your liver healthy.**
· Don’t drink too much alcohol

· Don’t **overindulge** in fatty foods

· Be mindful of medications

· Don’t touch or breathe in toxins

· Don’t smoke and avoid second-hand smoke

· Eat foods **high in selenium**, such as **Brazil nuts** (My note: No more than 2 peeled at a time), brewer’s yeast, **brown rice, garlic, onions,** and **molasses**.

· [Eat foods](http://www.belmarrahealth.com/the-best-foods-for-your-liver/) **high in methionine** as they can detox the liver. These foods include **eggs, fish, legumes,** and **seeds**.

· →Consume **high sulfur foods**, such as **broccoli**, **cauliflower**, cabbage, **Brussels sprouts** for further detoxifying

· Eat foods with **vitamin B5**, including **whole grains**, **chicken**, wheat bran, and **nuts**.

· Eat foods high in **vitamin** **B1** that can reduce toxic effects, such as **wheat germ, peas**, and **soybeans**.

· Consume coffee, as it contains phytonutrients – be mindful of additives such as cream, milk, and sugar, and **don’t have more than three cups of coffee a day.**

**And Opposite advise from [Biliary Health: The Liver and Gallbladder](https://thewholejourney.com/biliary-health-the-liver-and-gallbladder/) _the Whole Journey_**
**Things to** →**reduce or avoid for gallbladder health:**

· **→Foods from the cabbage family: broccoli, cabbage, lettuce**

· **Reduce Sodium**

· **Reduce cholesterol rich foods** such as [red meats](http://www.grasslandbeef.com/StoreFront.bok?affId=165711), dairy products and eggs

· **Reduce foods known to be common allergens** such as gluten-containing grains, soy products, corn, peanuts and citrus.

· **Avoid large meals** especially before bed time. Try not to eat 3-4 hours before bed.

**Tips for supporting the liver and gallbladder:**

· **Eat foods that promote bile formation** such as: raw shredded beets, organic apples and ginger.

· **Eat bitter foods** such as dandelion greens and other dark leafy greens. Bitters stimulate the flow of bile in the gall bladder, helping us digest fats. Try adding them in salads, or even eating a few leaves while preparing a meals so that the bile is ready to emulsify whatever fat you consume.

· **Take** [**fish oil capsules**](http://thewholejourney.com/ONE-Omega) – It contains omega 3 oils which are known to block cholesterol formation in bile

· **Vitamin C rich foods** such as papaya, red bell peppers, strawberries, oranges and lemons.

· **Hydration:** Drink at least 8 glasses of mineralized water a day. We recommend 1 liter per 50 lbs of body weight.

· **Foods rich in pyridoxal, folate and cobalamin** are three effective nutrients that support the livers biochemical pathways. (see below)

· **Pyridoxial rich foods:** Potatoes, bananas, garbanzo beans, and chicken breast.

· **Folate rich roods:** Beef liver, spinach, and asparagus.

· **Cobalamin rich foods**: Trout, salmon, beef,.

· **Eat foods high in minerals:** copper, zinc, selenium and potassium such as: squash, avocados, shellfish, and spinach.

· **Stress management:** Breathing, yoga, regular exercise

· **Support the pancreas** by stabilizing blood sugar.

· **Increase antioxidants:** Raw cacao, blueberries, prunes, pomegranates, kale, brussel sprouts, and beets

· **Avoid hydrogenated fats and oils**

**Liver and gallbladder cleansing juice recipe:**

3 kale leaves

1 handful dandelion greens

3 stalks of celery

1 handful of parsley

1 large beet

1” section of ginger (peeled)

2 green apples

1 cucumber

2 lemons

by Emily Potter

Bile certainly is beautiful.  **(**My note: I have taken [_NutriCology_ Ox Bile](http://www.nutricology.com/ox-bile-500-mg-100-vegicaps) 125 mg and 500 mg and [_Standard Process_ Cholacol](https://www.standardprocess.com/Products/Standard-Process/Cholacol) and Not good to take _bile salts_ ongoing per [Michael McEvoy with _Metabolic Healing_](https://metabolichealing.com/) Michael works a lot with practitioners… teaching them about _genetics_ and the _related metabolic processes_. Additionally: [http://www.thesaurustea.com/loose-herbal-teas?product\_id=161](http://www.thesaurustea.com/loose-herbal-teas?product_id=161) Peppermint is an herb and contains no caffeine. **Peppermint is consumed after meals** as the oils stimulate the flow of bile to the stomach. Helps relieve gas pains. [Peppermint may support liver function](http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/natural-health/liver-cleanse-top-reasons/) by promoting the flow of bile. Bile helps digest fats and encourages normal cholesterol levels; normal cholesterol levels reduce the workload placed on the liver.**)**

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-12-04T18:06:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ox-bile-bile-acid-factors-recommendations.1607/post-283084>

> [Martha Barger said:](/goto/post?id=283078)

> ****YOU NEED BILE to detox metals and improve digestion**.**

Thx for sharing great ideas from the experts.

One thing we know is that a healthy gall bladder and bile flow is a prerequisite for successful detoxing through liver. Liver's detox processes needs normal bile production and flow to take toxins that liver has collected and to carry toxins via bile into intestines for elimination in feces.

It is counterproductive to encourage liver detox function with faulty bile performance-- toxins then exit liver and back up into blood and then recirculate through enterohepatic system to brain and other organs.

**Dr Chris Shade** has done some outstanding research on this and even has his own company that distributes metals detox products. He always stresses the bile factor big time as part of a detox regimen. He recommends bitters to stimulate bile flow, and phospholipids (preferably in liposomal form but lecithin can work) to ensure toxin-laden bile flows into bile ducts and gets the nasty stuff into intestines for elimination. You need binders present in intestines to ensure toxins are escorted safely out of system and into toilet (otherwise, toxins are reabsorbed in intestines and get recirculated).

Coffee enemas and liver compression massage can also help. I am a big fan of coffee enemas although I dislike coffee drinking. The lemon juice/olive oil purge has a lot of fans too.

# Thread: chelation uptake and intracellular biology...  the ultimate salt bath yeah right
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chelation-uptake-and-intracellular-biology-the-ultimate-salt-bath-yeah-right.1610/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-05-23T22:03:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chelation-uptake-and-intracellular-biology-the-ultimate-salt-bath-yeah-right.1610/post-26806>

[http://www.princeton.edu/~cebic/chelbindintro.html](http://http://www.princeton.edu/~cebic/chelbindintro.html)

Chelation, uptake, and binding of trace metals

> **This allows scientists to study how marine microbes respond to extremely low concentrations of available iron. It is assumed in these experiments that terrestrial siderophores are different enough from marine siderophores that terrestrial-siderophore-bound iron will be unavailable to marine microbes. And for the most part it seems to be true.\[B/\]
>
>
>
>
>
> > But Martinez, Haygood, and Butler have found that marine and terrestrial siderophores can be very much alike. **The scientists isolated and characterised a marine siderophore, desferrioxamine G, that is almost identical to desferrioxamine B. Because a marine bacterium produces desferrioxamine G, it is very likely that it, and maybe some other marine bacteria, will be able to take up and utilize desferrioxamine-B-bound iron. So it may be necessary to reexamine those experiments that assumed that the complexed iron was unavailable to all marine microbes.
> >
> > **
>
> **
>
>
> **In the presence of sunlight, iron(III) aquachelin (a marine siderophore) undergoes a photochemical reaction that results in the chemical transformation of the ligand and the reduction of Fe(III) to Fe(II)**. The photochemical reaction breaks the ligand apart, producing a peptide component and a fatty acid component. The peptide component can still bind iron but with lower efficiency than the whole ligand. Experiments in which the authors used labeled iron siderophores (Fe59) showed that iron was more bioavailable in the presence of photolyzed ironsiderophores than unphotolyzed iron siderophores, consistent with the hypothesis that photochemical lability of iron from iron siderophore complexes is important in iron cycling in the marine environment.****

### janagram — 2012-05-23T22:29:06+0300

### chocolate — 2012-05-24T07:48:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chelation-uptake-and-intracellular-biology-the-ultimate-salt-bath-yeah-right.1610/post-26981>

I have a few units. I've spent the past ten years in a redevelopment fight with the bullet train. I won the right to not be utterly destroyed. No money in self preservation sometimes. I was going to public hearings and getting objections on record and having a few really smart and powerful people helping behind the scenes. My community has mostly illegal immigrants for population density. We have 1000 people. It was a tough fight. Kinda like being a troll guarding a mountain. I have the only physical access to a lot of very convenient infrastructure, think natural gas, Warren Buffet, Desert Express Harry Reid('s descendents). I would have sold out if they would have offered a fair price. I was being harassed to sell. They have a habit of abusing power, buying low from the owner and selling high to the redevelopments. Which they control. Anyway, my husband and I came to the desert to coast into old age. It turned into an insane abuse of process and a huge war against big forces. Its like a chess game and you have to find opponents to every move they are making in power, and attend every hearing that is relevant. Silence is approval. Even if notification is a 1 inch ad in a newspaper no one ever reads. But we won. This area is the filthiest politics anywhere. So I just collect rent and am trying to figure out what to do next. My husband is a used car/hot rod dealer small time, but a very good accumulator. We have a hundred acres and we are over a very wet aquifer with rights. Its unique for the area. The train is funded, the money is...we're the Route 66 rural side of the mountain and permanently protected. And his shop is in town. I have some really great horses but need to get rid of them. They are stud quality males and that's a problem. Who knows?

# Thread: Got my labs back and I'm a little confused...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/got-my-labs-back-and-im-a-little-confused.1613/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-05-23T23:38:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/got-my-labs-back-and-im-a-little-confused.1613/post-26832>

Got my saliva test results this morning...And I don't know what to think. I was expecting something wrong with the cortisol since my energy is really low in the morning and because of bad sleep (and also increased waist line), but it appears to be pretty normal. Was also expecting higher estradiol (fat in thigh and hips).

Estradiol: 0.8 (1.3-3.3)

Progesterone: 210 (30-300)

Ratio Pg/E2: 263 (Optimal: 100-500 when E2 1.3-3.3 pg/ml)

Testosterone: 134 (16-55)

DHEAS: 4.0 (2-23)

Cortisol morning: 9.1 (3.7-9.5)

Cortisol noon: 3.6 (1.2-3.0)

Cortisol evening: 0.6 (0.6-1.9)

Cortisol night: 0.7 (0.4-1.0)

And no surprise DHEA is in the toilet...Hello insomia...Is high testosterone a problem??

If cortisol is ok and it's not adrenal fatigue, then what's wrong with me?? Any input is welcome, as always

Those are tests from last month (blood test)

Ferritine 92 (11-90 ug/L )

Vit B12 1043 (>135 pmol/L)

Folic Acid >45.4 (>14 nmol/L)

TSH 2.16 (0.35-5.50 mUI/L)

Free T3 3.9 (3.5-6.0 pmol/L)

Free T4 16.8 (10.0-23.0 pmol/L)

Both anti-thyroid are negatif

Glucose 4.6 (4.0-6.2 mmol/L)

Urea 5.3 (303-8.8 mmol/L0

Creatinine 66 (42-859 umol/L)

Glomerular filtration 107 (42-89 umol/L)

Sodium 139 (135-145 mmol/L)

AST 25 (13-39 U/L)

ALT 33 (8-31 U/L)

Phosphatase ALC 46 (36-110 U/L)

GGT 19 (7-33 U/L)

Serum Iron 11.0 (5.6-31.6 umol/L)

Transferritine 2.21 (1.85-3.51 g/L)

Iron/Trans 0.20 (0.06-0.44)

Trigly 0.24 (0.43-2.69 mmol/L)

Cholesterol 5.27 (3.40-7.30 mmol/L)

HDL 2.81 (0.90-2.38 mmol/L)

C-T/HDL-C 1.9

LDL-C 2.35 mmol/L

Testosterone T. 1.3 (0-2.9 nmol/L)

If that can be useful: I started supplementing with those in the last month:

Pregnenolone: started with 50 mg and went up with 100mg after the testing

7 Keto DHEA: 50 mg per day. Started 1 week before testing.

Taking adrenal support for 6 weeks now

Small dose of DHEA cream (1 mg per day)

Prometrium 100mg at night

Sleep has improved in the last week. Not always good quality sleep, but at least I'm sleeping. Always getting up low energy though and felling better at night.

One last thing...Since I'm in Canada, I took longer to send the kit back to the lab. The shipping took 2 days...Could that have any incidence on the results?? \*\*Edit: called the lab and they said the samples are stable for 30 days, so that is not an issue...

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-05-24T16:14:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/got-my-labs-back-and-im-a-little-confused.1613/post-27010>

I've been doing some reading about PCOS. Since I was sure I was dealing with adrenal fatigue, I never look into that.

I came to the Leptin reset because of bad sleep. and here are my other symptoms:

\- Gained 30 lbs in the last year and can't lose it. Gained around the waist (always had a tiny waist) and at the thighs (always have been a battle)

\- Adult acne since 25 y/o or so. Bad complexion as well. Much better since going primal, but my skin looks old and not clear. Every acne cyst left darker skin coloration.

\- Amennorhea in 2010 for a year before doc put me back on BCP. Stoppped it a few months later. Menses came back, are irregular and very heavy (always been light since first menses at 14 y/o)

\- Mood swings and some agressiveness. Always thought it was because of PMS, dieting and lack of sleep.

\- Hair is thin

\- No abnormal body or facial hair

\- wakening around 2 or 3 o'clock every single night

\- Low energy all day, and I'm feeling better at night.

When I tested testosterone (blood) in April, in was ok, and now it is sky high with saliva testing. Should I test again? Saliva or blood testing?

Should I keep taking adrenal support supplements? And what about Dim and Estro Detox?

Thank you for your help!

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-05-25T00:59:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/got-my-labs-back-and-im-a-little-confused.1613/post-27190>

Someone? Please, I need your opinion.

### kcorcoran@comcast.net — 2012-05-25T03:14:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/got-my-labs-back-and-im-a-little-confused.1613/post-27229>

Not a doc, just my opinion. I'd use blood tests for all the sex hormones and DHEA-s and morning cortisol(8am). I'm leery of saliva tests since that led me down the wrong path.

Dim is an aromatase inhibitor, which I thinks means that it inhibits the conversion of testosterone to estradiol. However, I'm not sure I'm correct.

For me, if I take too much DIM, it lowers my estrogen too much. However, I'm 48 and have high FSH and irregular periods, so take with a grain of salt.

Estrogen levels vary so much during the cycle, you really need to know your cycle day for interpretation.

Do you have cysts on your ovaries? Had a transvag ultrasound?

**

AbetterME;27106 wrote:** I've been doing some reading about PCOS. Since I was sure I was dealing with adrenal fatigue, I never look into that.

I came to the Leptin reset because of bad sleep. and here are my other symptoms:

\- Gained 30 lbs in the last year and can't lose it. Gained around the waist (always had a tiny waist) and at the thighs (always have been a battle)

\- Adult acne since 25 y/o or so. Bad complexion as well. Much better since going primal, but my skin looks old and not clear. Every acne cyst left darker skin coloration.

\- Amennorhea in 2010 for a year before doc put me back on BCP. Stoppped it a few months later. Menses came back, are irregular and very heavy (always been light since first menses at 14 y/o)

\- Mood swings and some agressiveness. Always thought it was because of PMS, dieting and lack of sleep.

\- Hair is thin

\- No abnormal body or facial hair

\- wakening around 2 or 3 o'clock every single night

\- Low energy all day, and I'm feeling better at night.

When I tested testosterone (blood) in April, in was ok, and now it is sky high with saliva testing. Should I test again? Saliva or blood testing?

Should I keep taking adrenal support supplements? And what about Dim and Estro Detox?

Thank you for your help!

I am not a doctor.

I think that you have low testosterone, but do blood test again.

Test also, blood

total testosterone

free testosterone

SHBG

\--------------------------------

You are low on estradiol, may loose bones.

Consider working on more complete

Steroid Hormone Panel.

You have most tests already, you are missing pregnenolone.

Likely your pregnenolone is low.

Your current use of 100mg supplemental pregnenolone should help some,

but

in my experience

all kinds of pregnenolone pills, creams, drops were rather ineffective.

When I argued I was given runaround that it does not show in blood or saliva for x or y reason.

Miraculously when I eat

Nutricology Micronized Lipid Matrix MLM pregnenolone, 150mg pills scored

I am able to find my pregnenolone in the blood tests.

I suggest first blood testing, then eating plenty of above kind of pregnenolone.

First thing pregnenolone does is convert to progesterone (according to body requirements).

Look at the chart,

[http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/c/c8/Hormones\_estradiol%2C\_progesterone%2C\_LH\_and\_FSH\_during\_menstrual\_cycle.svg](http://http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/c/c8/Hormones_estradiol%2C_progesterone%2C_LH_and_FSH_during_menstrual_cycle.svg)

first 13 days of menstrual cycle progesterone level look real low.

Please note that men live with that level and it is very important for them to have this level (and not less).

I am a men. 72yo

I started with almost non existing pregnenolone and progesterone.

I am now using two tabs of above pregnenolone (150mg/wakeup, 150mg/4hr latter)

and

my preg and prog are at good levels.

See my supplementation and resulting blood levels here:

[http://www.musclechatroom.com/forum/showthread.php?15433-JanSz-Labs-amp-Tests&p=166492#post166492](http://http://www.musclechatroom.com/forum/showthread.php?15433-JanSz-Labs-amp-Tests&p=166492#post166492)

pregnenolone & progesterone are highly influencing cortisol levels. They down-metabolize (also) to cortisol.

This may help in your currently weak cortisol in afternoons.

In the future you would have to mind your menstruation cycle and do blood draws on 3-4 day and 19-21 day.

When posting results always provide information about when you have drawn your blood in relation to menstrual cycle.

You would have to stop progesterone supplementation

hopefully all together

but at least in first 14 days

\------------

If you would study major Steroid Hormones, assuming their content in the blood as 100%

you would find that

DHEAS participation (when in healthy level) is 94.99%

[http://img824.imageshack.us/img824/1103/image83k.jpg](http://http://img824.imageshack.us/img824/1103/image83k.jpg)

That is a lot.

You are not going to accomplish much if you do not attend to this elephant.

Good way to do it is to first take 100mg/wakeup of 7ketoDHEA.

(I take 100mg/wakeup and 100mg/4hrs latter).

Then you take plain DHEA pills, enough of them to see results in blood.

If able, always do blood testing at LabCorp.

Desirable DHEAs, Women 275-400 μg/dL

Aim at the top levels, as long as you are not getting excessive

testosterone (currently low)

hair

acne

Some people are looking at chart and see that all starts cholesterol----pregnenolone----everything else

ans are hoping to raise DHEA by supplementing pregnenolone

that is not happening. (one explanation is in the above table that says DHEAs=elephant, all else are ants)

I have not figured convenient way to stay in touch with this board.

Drop in here in totally random ways that are hard to reproduce next time.

Can be PM-ed here

and always include links to the stuff you want comments on.

[http://www.musclechatroom.com/forum/showthread.php?20061-Dr.-Patricia-Kane&p=171942#post171942](http://http://www.musclechatroom.com/forum/showthread.php?20061-Dr.-Patricia-Kane&p=171942#post171942)

You may find out there how I supplement my Fatty Acids (and why).

The Omega6/Omega3 stuff

/////////////

At wakeup look (almost) straight at the sun for 15 min, or high intensity light.

...

Thank you all for your answer. JanSz, I'm so glad to have your opinion, I know you are good at this stuff. I'm in Canada, so I can not test through LabCorp. I have an apointement on Monday for more blood test. I was expecting menses today but apparently it's late... Hopefully it will come this week-end!! Last 2 months cycles was 26 days. I would love to have dr Kruse's input, but for now,

I think of RT3 as an indicator

so

I make an effort to mess with it least possible.

For example, direct T3 supplementation would lower RT3 and make one happy (quickly).

Most who do that, get no joy out of better (lower) RT3.

Instead,

I pay close attention to my micronutrients and Fatty Acids.

You know about Fatty Acids (details) if you have followed (bottom) my previous post.

Many more details than is usually talked about.

### nonchalant — 2012-05-24T05:11:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nice-ice-price.1618/post-26949>

I keep seeing references to $2-$3 bags of ice. Have you seen those stand-alone ice vending places? They are a small building, about the size of a semi-truck trailer. For $1.50, I can get a 20lb bag of ice. At another brand vendor, I can get 16lb loose ice for $1. Just bring your own bag or an ice chest.

Here's a random pic I found:

[http://www.polarmatic-ice-vending.com/](http://http://www.polarmatic-ice-vending.com/)

Yes, I have one of these businesses very close to my home in Houston. 20 lbs for $1 was the price last time I was there. There are two chutes at the one I frequent - one for a bag of ice, the other for placing a cooler under.

Nice little niche in the ice market. Wonder how much it profits on a month? Houston is HOT for 6+ months out of the year.

### fitness@home — 2012-05-27T04:15:38+0300

August,

The ice place near me takes both cash and debit cards. It also sends the 20lb bags of ice down the chute in a bag, you just have to put a twist tie around it (these are provided also). I will be making a trip over there in the AM to pick up 4 bags. Fortunately, we have a chest freezer.

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-28T09:10:17+0300

### fitness@home — 2012-06-11T01:58:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nice-ice-price.1618/post-30456>

Stopped by the self-serve ice place this morning to restock my freezer and ran into the manager who was there to check on things. She said they have 50 of these ice houses throughout Texas. I asked what the initial start up cost was for one and it is around $150k.

20 lbs of ice for $1 is what most of their locations sell for. She said they move a large volume of ice at that price.

She then asked what I needed five bags of ice for...a camping trip? When I told her I used it for ice baths it lead to a conversation on the benefits of CT. I directed her to this website. My good deed for the day
