# Thread: Glucose deprivation activates a metabolic and signaling amplification loop leading to
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glucose-deprivation-activates-a-metabolic-and-signaling-amplification-loop-leading-to.2093/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-06-28T23:06:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glucose-deprivation-activates-a-metabolic-and-signaling-amplification-loop-leading-to.2093/post-34229>

Glucose deprivation activates a metabolic and signaling amplification loop leading to cell death

[http://www.nature.com/msb/journal/v8/n1/full/msb201220.html?WT.mc\_id=FBK\_NPG](http://http://www.nature.com/msb/journal/v8/n1/full/msb201220.html?WT.mc_id=FBK_NPG)

> **In cancer cells dependent upon glucose for survival, glucose withdrawal activates a positive feedback loop involving reactive oxygen species (ROS), ROS-mediated inhibition of tyrosine phosphatases, and tyrosine kinase signaling. This loop amplifies ROS to toxic levels, resulting in cell death.**
>
>
>
> In cancer cell lines dependent on glucose for survival, glucose withdrawal induces supra-physiological levels of phospho-tyrosine signaling, even in cells expressing constitutively active tyrosine kinases.
>
> Unbiased, mass spectrometry-based phospho-tyrosine profiling demonstrates that glucose withdrawal induces a unique signature of phospho-tyrosine signaling associated with focal adhesions.
>
> The glucose withdrawal-induced phospho-tyrosine signature results from a positive feedback loop in which reactive oxygen species (ROS) oxidize and inhibit protein tyrosine phosphatases, causing increased tyrosine kinase signaling, thereby inducing further ROS generation until cells undergo ROS-mediated cell death.
>
> The glucose withdrawal-initiated positive feedback loop illustrates the complex, systems-level integration of metabolism and tyrosine kinase signaling in cancer cell homeostasis.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: One pound of weight loss equals four pounds to your knee joints
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-pound-of-weight-loss-equals-four-pounds-to-your-knee-joints.2100/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-06-29T00:22:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-pound-of-weight-loss-equals-four-pounds-to-your-knee-joints.2100/post-34258>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15986358](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15986358)

**Weight loss reduces knee-joint loads in overweight and obese older adults with knee osteoarthritis.

Messier SP, Gutekunst DJ, Davis C, DeVita P.

Source

Snow Biomechanics Laboratory, Department of Health and Exercise Science, Wake Forest University, Winston-Salem, North Carolina 27109, USA. [messier@wfu.edu](mailto:messier@wfu.edu)

Abstract

OBJECTIVE:

To determine the relationship between change in body mass and knee-joint moments and forces during walking in overweight and obese older adults with knee osteoarthritis (OA) following an 18-month clinical trial of diet and exercise.

METHODS:

Data were obtained from 142 sedentary, overweight, and obese older adults with self-reported disability and radiographic evidence of knee OA who underwent 3-dimensional gait analysis. Gait kinetic outcome variables included peak knee-joint forces and peak internal knee-joint moments. Mixed regression models were created to predict followup kinetic values, using followup body mass as the primary explanatory variable. Baseline body mass was used as a covariate, and thus followup body mass was a surrogate measure for change in body mass (i.e., weight loss).

RESULTS:

There was a significant direct association between followup body mass and peak followup values of compressive force (P = 0.001), resultant force (P = 0.002), abduction moment (P = 0.03), and medial rotation moment (P = 0.02). A weight reduction of 9.8 N (1 kg) was associated with reductions of 40.6 N and 38.7 N in compressive and resultant forces, respectively. Thus, each weight-loss unit was associated with an approximately 4-unit reduction in knee-joint forces. In addition, a reduction in body weight of 9.8 N (1 kg) was associated with a 1.4% reduction (0.496 Nm) in knee abduction moment.

CONCLUSION:

**Our results indicate that each pound of weight lost will result in a 4-fold reduction in the load exerted on the knee per step during daily activities. Accumulated over thousands of steps per day, a reduction of this magnitude would appear to be clinically meaningful.**

note the absence of warm adapted rats**


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: It's not clear to me how well, HCG and LR can work together...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-not-clear-to-me-how-well-hcg-and-lr-can-work-together.2103/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jmathisen — 2012-06-29T01:38:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-not-clear-to-me-how-well-hcg-and-lr-can-work-together.2103/post-34273>

Or maybe I'm confused. I'm thinking about doing HCG in a few weeks, but from reading some info at hcgdietinfo and some other sources, the 2 plans, with the exception of being low in calories, don't really seem to overlap.

So are people eating the LR diet, but taking HCG? Or eating the HCG protocol, taking HCG, not doing LR, and just doing CT?

Part of my issue is that I'm trying to stay basically near-zero carb, as I'm T2D, so LR diet fits well.

But I also don't usually have hunger/appetite issues, I've done 30 day water fasts with no trouble after day 2 or so.

I'm going to stay LR for a few more weeks, but wanted to accumulate some info for what to do after my 8 weeks is up.

Thanks for your time.

### colleencoble — 2012-06-29T04:08:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-not-clear-to-me-how-well-hcg-and-lr-can-work-together.2103/post-34309>

They don't overlap. They are two totally different things. Some who are following Dr. Kruse have tried to eliminate the fruit and eat 200 calories of extra protein in their place and have done fine. Some have also eaten one of their meals at breakfast to try to stay on the same timing schedule as the LR plan.

I personally would love to see someone try CT with NO hcg but follow the hcg diet. I think they might do the same thing but no one has tested it. Some have tried CT with hcg and many of us got hungry so it was like they were fighting each other. I tried doing hcg with added fat but it was a failure.

Good luck, whatever you decide!

### LisaAPB — 2012-06-29T06:57:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-not-clear-to-me-how-well-hcg-and-lr-can-work-together.2103/post-34328>

Are you trying to lose fat? That lack of appetite thing you've got going on should make that fairly easy without hcg.

### jmathisen — 2012-06-29T19:12:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-not-clear-to-me-how-well-hcg-and-lr-can-work-together.2103/post-34390>

Yes, trying to lose some more fat, while becoming LS.

37 lbs of fat came off with LR in about 5 weeks, but I've been stuck at 265 now for 8 days. So I'm wondering if my body has adapted to LR diet, and maybe I need to shake things up a bit and do something different to get things fired up again.

I mean, I could just fast again for a while, but I don't think that will really train my system to be LS and Doctor Kruse doesn't support IF during LR, so I'd like to do whatever keeps me on the path to becoming LS, while still unloading another 70lbs or so.

The other reason was that I'd read somewhere that HCG tends to remove visceral fat, which I carry a lot of. Ice bags on the tummy on the days I don't do full immersion for CT would seem to be targeting the fat just under the skin. Which I don't mind getting rid of, but in terms of long term health, the visceral fat seems to be more dangerous. I guess CT seems to target sq fat in general, (although I do realize that's not it's only goal/benefit).

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-06-30T01:15:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-not-clear-to-me-how-well-hcg-and-lr-can-work-together.2103/post-34472>

You've been stuck for 8 days? Don't see it as a stall. This is not a magic diet, the scale won't move everyday. You have lost a big amount in only 5 weeks, give you body the chance to adapt.

What is your diet like? Are you having enough fat? Do you CT? Have you done any lab testing? HCG with bad hormones can screw you even more, be careful.

Fat around the waist mean high cortisol...If you are careful with the light and you sleep properly, it will go down, but again, you have to be patient.... Keep reading the blog, you'll find many answers to yours questions.

### colleencoble — 2012-06-30T06:17:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-not-clear-to-me-how-well-hcg-and-lr-can-work-together.2103/post-34540>

I agree, 8 days is nothing, I have 10 day stalls on rounds of hcg. Stalls are perfectly normal. Your body is just shifting fat and water around.

### colleencoble — 2012-07-02T05:38:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-34948>

That's what others of us have noticed. CT on hcg seems to cause more hunger. But I was immersing when I had hunger. Spot CTing might not cause that problem. Let's tweak your dose before we assume anything. What dose are you on? RX sublingual or homeopathic?

### kalibex — 2012-07-02T15:24:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-34980>

Hi, Colleen! Sublingual, Corion, 150 IU. Just started a new vial, have now been stalled 7 days...  Hunger can hit mid-morning (as opposed to being able to wait until lunchtime), and also late evening (so hungry last night, had to have an extra fruit in order to get to sleep).

### colleencoble — 2012-07-02T15:27:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-34982>

Stalls are normal. Corion is very strong. Try skipping your dose this morning. If better by 4, you could take evening's but cut it drastically, to 100 or so. Then tomorrow try 125 2x a day. Good luck! If you're not feeling better by about 4 or 5, let me know.

### kalibex — 2012-07-02T17:30:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-35029>

Thanks; unfortunately already took this am's dose, but can skip tonight's and see how I feel tomorrow am, then reduce the dose as you suggest and see how it goes. Will report out how it's going tomorrow pm.

Your experience and advice is much appreciated.

### kalibex — 2012-07-04T14:02:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-35444>

Great news, Colleen - I've started to lose again, and the hunger's back down again. I never had this sort of titration issue the last (and first) time I did a round, so thanks again so much for your voice of experience!

### colleencoble — 2012-07-05T02:39:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-35561>

I'm so glad you're better!

### kalibex — 2012-07-07T01:15:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-35978>

Alas, I might have to swing back the other way, increase the hcg now - hunger's breaking through again. I guess I'll try going back to the 150 IU dose.

The only other explanation that I can think of is that I'm close to my 'true' weight - which I sincerely hope is not the case...

### colleencoble — 2012-07-07T01:47:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-35988>

You may still be taking too much. It's not uncommon to have to go under 125 and stay there.

### colleencoble — 2012-07-07T01:47:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-35989>

The only way to know for sure is to skip a dose. If much better by late afternoon, you'll know for sure your dose is too high again.

### kalibex — 2012-07-07T02:12:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-36003>

Hunh...then I guess I'll skip tonight, then bump it down 25 more units to make it 100...sure hope I can get the balance right.

### kalibex — 2012-07-07T23:36:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-36137>

Woke early this morning, already hungry...skipped a.m. dose also. Thus far today, eating to hunger, had food at: 6 am, 10:40 am, 1:30 pm - total so far of about a protein and a half, a fruit, and a veg. Napped, just woke and starting to feel hungry (though not quite ravenous yet).

So...continue to titrate down? Or am I now getting too little?

### colleencoble — 2012-07-08T01:28:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-36145>

If the dose is too high, you should feel better through the evening. You can't tell until at least mid afternoon.

### kalibex — 2012-07-08T01:39:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-36147>

Ok; I'll do the new smaller (100 IU) dose starting tonight, and see how I feel tomorrow and the day after. Thanks!

### colleencoble — 2012-07-08T01:52:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-36149>

I wouldn't go down that far. You were doing 150 2x a day, correct? I'd try 125 first.

### kalibex — 2012-07-08T02:07:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-36151>

Except I just got done trying 125 IU at the end of this week...then was starving by mid-afternoon yesterday (ie, I get up kind of early - 6 am-ish, so that lately I'm getting genuinely hungry for lunch by mid-morning, then starving for supper by mid-afternoon).

I could try the 125 IU again and see if I'm ok for the rest of the weekend.

It is true that all this trouble started with a new bottle of Corion...

### colleencoble — 2012-07-08T02:27:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/developing-tolerance-on-sublingual-did-ct-spot-icing-cause-it.2111/post-36155>

Oh okay. I missed that part. Then yes, take it down to 100. Corion is very strong and it's a fresh bottle.

# Thread: The 5 Pillars of Performance
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Barry — 2012-06-29T19:35:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-34408>

Through what I've studied, practiced and experienced ... I have identified 5 key factors that govern athletic performance. This excludes exercise. They are factors which have been discussed and mentioned in my threads and in jacks blogs. They are all interrelated. You must have all 5 on order to get optimal performance . And it relates more to endurance performance then strength or speed.

As primordial living is about having "fun" , let's have some ! Give your 5 , in no particular order and if someone gets it right , well, I'll give them a pat on the back.

You can guess too Jack ;-)

### AKMan — 2012-06-30T00:15:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-34459>

1\. Genetic potential

2\. Proper nutrition

3\. Functional endocrine system

4\. Healthy guts

5\. Good sleep

### Barry — 2012-06-30T12:03:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-34555>

> **AKMan;34618 wrote:** 1. Genetic potential
>
> 2\. Proper nutrition
>
> 3\. Functional endocrine system
>
> 4\. Healthy guts
>
> 5\. Good sleep

Too general. The pillars are specifics . Think adaptations

### AKMan — 2012-06-30T19:01:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-34622>

1\. Fasted state training

2\. CT

3\. Ketogenic seafood diet

4\. Darkness after sunset

5\. Kerrygold butter in your Bailey's (Bulletproof shooters)

### Barry — 2012-06-30T19:02:39+0300

3 out of 5 !!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-11T05:24:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-36736>

Why not light cycles? (Obviously I don't know what you believe 4 & 5 are.) Light cycles/circadian rhythms directly impact not only our health/inflammation/insulin but they are a key hormonal pathway... which ties to optimal levels of T and growth hormone etc. So doesn't getting the melatonin-cortisol axis right directly and hugely impact optimal performance, at least the sort that doesn't kill you young?

### Barry — 2012-07-12T23:04:26+0300

### Barry — 2012-07-14T23:24:32+0300

> **johnnyb;37801 wrote:** How about positive mental attitude? A thought can change your DNA. I bet it can also keep one's elite butt moving.

I'll give you that one. But lets call it Epigenetics !! And yes, its huge when it comes to athletes and adaptations. Where you train, how you train, who you train with... besides nutrition.... make a massive difference to training adaptations

1 left !

### Lyndra — 2012-07-15T02:04:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-37883>

> **Barry;38008 wrote:** I'll give you that one. But lets call it Epigenetics !! And yes, its huge when it comes to athletes and adaptations. Where you train, how you train, who you train with... besides nutrition.... make a massive difference to training adaptations
>
>
>
> 1 left !

In your first post, you said these 5 key factors relate more to endurance performance than strength or speed. Could you elaborate on how the 4 identified so far are endurance-specific, especially #3 & #4? I've been asking myself what is different about endurance performance versus speed & strength and would like to understand your take on the specificity of these pillars to endurance. Thanks!

### Barry — 2012-07-15T17:36:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-38044>

> **Lyndra;38051 wrote:** In your first post, you said these 5 key factors relate more to endurance performance than strength or speed. Could you elaborate on how the 4 identified so far are endurance-specific, especially #3 & #4? I've been asking myself what is different about endurance performance versus speed & strength and would like to understand your take on the specificity of these pillars to endurance. Thanks!

Lyndra, its all to do with fat/keto adaptation. This is key for endurance, particularly ultra endurance. Read up on RER and how its measured and what it means. All of my pillars lower this.

Speed and Strength performance is not driven by RER. While my pillars still can help these athletes, they have more of an indirect relationship.

### Lyndra — 2012-07-15T20:10:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-38088>

> **Barry;38008 wrote: I'll give you that one. But lets call it Epigenetics !! And yes, its huge when it comes to athletes and adaptations. Where you train, how you train, who you train with... besides nutrition.... make a massive difference to training adaptations
>
>
>
> 1 left !
>
>
> Barry;38213 wrote:** Lyndra, its all to do with fat/keto adaptation. This is key for endurance, particularly ultra endurance. Read up on RER and how its measured and what it means. All of my pillars lower this.
>
>
>
> Speed and Strength performance is not driven by RER. While my pillars still can help these athletes, they have more of an indirect relationship.

I get how keto-adapting affects endurance performance, but I'm still not following how "where you train, how you train, who you train with" relates to endurance specifically and not speed/strength.

### Barry — 2012-07-15T21:04:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-38097>

> **Lyndra;38257 wrote:** I get how keto-adapting affects endurance performance, but I'm still not following how "where you train, how you train, who you train with" relates to endurance specifically and not speed/strength.

I never said epigenetics doesn't relate to speed and strength .. Of course it does . What I'm saying is that the 5 pillars combined together apply more specifically to endurance performance.

### Lyndra — 2012-07-15T21:25:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-5-pillars-of-performance.2114/post-38104>

> **Barry;38266 wrote:** I never said epigenetics doesn't relate to speed and strength .. Of course it does . What I'm saying is that the 5 pillars combined together apply more specifically to endurance performance.

I didn't mean to imply you said otherwise. I'm looking forward to #5. Enjoy your time at the TdF.

# Thread: Iron labs  hypothyroid
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iron-labs-hypothyroid.2115/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lyndra — 2012-06-29T20:03:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iron-labs-hypothyroid.2115/post-34413>

I did some recent labs and would like help interpreting.

Based on recent thyroid tests, I am hypothyroid. Results from a health fair screening I do every year showed declining hematocrit and iron levels, so I decided to do more iron tests.

Health Fair Labs:

<table style="width: 100%"><tbody><tr><td></td><td>4/22/10</td><td>4/15/11</td><td>4/27/12</td><td>Range</td></tr><tr><td>Hemaglobin</td><td>15.8</td><td>14.6</td><td>14.1</td><td>11.9-15.9</td></tr><tr><td>Hematocrit</td><td>46.5</td><td>43.1</td><td>41.7</td><td>35.0-49.0</td></tr><tr><td>Iron, serum</td><td>137</td><td>129</td><td>98</td><td>40-175</td></tr></tbody></table>

I ordered various iron & B12 tests through Life Extension and just got the results:

<table style="width: 100%"><tbody><tr><td></td><td>6/25/12</td><td>Range</td><td>Notes</td></tr><tr><td>TIBC</td><td>191</td><td>250-450</td><td>Low</td></tr><tr><td>UIBC</td><td>95</td><td>150-375</td><td>Low</td></tr><tr><td>Iron, serum</td><td>95</td><td>35-155</td><td></td></tr><tr><td>Iron saturation</td><td>50</td><td>15-55</td><td></td></tr><tr><td>Vitamin B12</td><td>1113</td><td>211-946</td><td>High</td></tr><tr><td>Folate, serum</td><td>18.1</td><td>&gt; 3.0</td><td></td></tr><tr><td>Methylmalonic acid</td><td>2.2</td><td>1.6-29.7</td><td></td></tr><tr><td>MMA-normalized</td><td>0.7</td><td>0.4-2.5</td><td></td></tr><tr><td>Ferritin, serum</td><td>105</td><td>13-115</td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

The health fair tests had me a bit worried as serum iron & hematocrit were declining, but the LEF tests look good to me. Maybe my iron level was too high and now I'm in a better range? Seems like B12 is too high and I should cut out the supplement.

Any opinions out there?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-06-29T20:14:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/iron-labs-hypothyroid.2115/post-34416>

My B12 was the same way, but I think mine was around 1,400! My doc had me cut my dose in half to every other day. That kept it in the very top of the high range.

I'm curious about your TIBC and UIBC being low. Mine are too and I've been told that it just means that if you had to take iron supps you don't absorb it very well. This was not by my doc so not sure. Your ferritin looks good tho.

# Thread: Craving fat?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-fat.2116/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cookworm — 2012-06-29T20:42:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-fat.2116/post-34425>

Pre-paleo, I always craved fat and sugar. Finally since paleo, my sweet tooth has really calmed down, but I am REALLY craving fat--A LOT!--now. I know that as long as I'm having healthy fats, I shouldn't worry, but I'm wondering if this is a good thing (is my body is learning/being trained to run on fat more now rather than glucose?) or a bad thing? Trying not to be "fat phobic", but I just want to make sure everything is working correctly.

### Shijin13 — 2012-06-29T20:54:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-fat.2116/post-34430>

There are days I will eat 1-2 TBS of Coconut oil w/a meal. There are days I do this with Ghee, Duck Fat and Bacon Fat. if you're body is craving it, add it to your meal. put it over your veggies, your meat

### PrimalPam — 2012-06-30T16:30:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-fat.2116/post-34581>

I've been a sugar-craver my entire life, until adhering strictly to Paleo and I, like you, crave fat at times too. Don't be afraid of it; just eat it WITH your meals.

I've gotten to where I only eat twice a day, and each meal contains a LOT of fat. Breakfast, I make my version of BP Coffee. I add about 2 tbsp. of CO, about 3 tbsp. of CO Milk, and I cook my eggs in 1 tbsp. of CO. Dinner, if it's a salad, I use about 2-3 tbsp. of CO as my dressing. If not, then I eat about 2 tbsp. of CO Manna as my "dessert". Or if I have veggies, I use Ghee. If it's a sweet potato night, nothin' beats Ghee & salt on a sweet potato.

The key with the fat: Don't eat it as a "snack". In fact, this is the first time period in my life I haven't had snacks. When I started the LR Protocol, the hardest thing to wrap my mind around was to eat twice (maybe 3) times a day, period. Now, about 6 months into it, I eat the BAB (with tons of fat!), then I'm good for the rest of the day.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-30T23:46:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-fat.2116/post-34680>

I wouldn't worry about it. Fat is good for you especially from an animal or seafood source. Of course, avoid industrial vegetable oils. Ugh. If you are like most North Americans and have been avoiding fat like a good citizen should, you are likely fat deficient. Eat your fill.

### Glamazon — 2012-07-01T05:27:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-fat.2116/post-34741>

Dr. Kruse has said over and over again, fat burners instead of sugar burners. I am going to make some 1/2 mct oil and 1/2 part butter to take with me so I can take a bite now and then when I get cravings. Fat is the only thing that addresses my cravings I have found. I crave it.

### chocolate — 2012-07-01T21:47:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-fat.2116/post-34859>

[http://www.disease-treatment.com/showthread.php?t=69735](http://http://www.disease-treatment.com/showthread.php?t=69735)

> oleic acid in olive oil stimulates **GLP-1 release from cultured mouse
>
> intestinal endocrine L cells via PKC-zeta but independent of MAPK or
>
> PI3K; arachidonic acid but not other long chained fatty acids caused
>
> similar effects \[PMID 17110421\]
> [http://www.glucagon.com/glp1.html](http://http://www.glucagon.com/glp1.html)
>
> The biological activities of GLP-1 include stimulation of glucose-dependent insulin secretion and insulin biosynthesis, inhibition of glucagon secretion and gastric emptying, and inhibition of food intake**. GLP-1 appears to have a number of additional effects in the GI tract and central nervous system, as reviewed in Diabetes 1998 47(2):159-69, Minireview: the glucagon-like peptides. Endocrinology. 2001 Feb;142(2):521-7, Development of glucagon-like peptide-1-based pharmaceuticals as therapeutic agents for the treatment of diabetes. Curr Pharm Des. 2001 Sep;7(14):1399-412. Review and Biological actions and therapeutic potential of the glucagon-like peptides. Gastroenterology. 2002 Feb;122(2):531-44.

I just tripped over this... I love both of these links.

### Dali Dula — 2012-07-01T22:26:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-fat.2116/post-34867>

The challenge is figuring out how to get enough fat into your body. I put fat in and on everything. Grass fed butter, CO, lard, tallow, bacon fat, avocados, olive oil. If i reheat something it gets more fat even if I already cooked it in fat. Drizzle fat over everything. Cook mushrooms in fat. Lacto fermented veg is good drenched in fat. Don't fear the fat. Eat fatty BAB you won't be hungry all day. Eat fatty dinner early you won't be hungry all night. You know you are eating enough fat when you don't get hungry for twelve hours.

### chocolate — 2012-07-01T22:57:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/craving-fat.2116/post-34870>

I agree that the challenge is getting enough fat....I keep falling back to caffeine free tea and fat. I know that what I want and it works.

# Thread: Need some help to understand labs

### klellja@gmail.com — 2012-06-30T20:34:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-help-to-understand-labs.2124/post-34639>

Hello,

I'm trying to understand how to interpretate labs, especially the lipid panel. My curiosity is coming from the changes I've seen in my labs.

Don't know where to look for that information ! If you have some links, don't hesitate to post them here.

I got tested on 13th june 2012. My previous labs were in november 2010.

Lipid panel

**Cholesterol** : 239\* (

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-01T17:14:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-help-to-understand-labs.2124/post-34786>

**klellja;34799 wrote: Hello,

### klellja@gmail.com — 2012-07-01T18:39:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-help-to-understand-labs.2124/post-34807>

Thanks for your comment Souldanzer !

I'm red-haired with light skin & lots of freckles but I don't get easily burn and this is the first year I see my skin lightly suntan. Before that I was avoiding the sun like the plague & got easily sunburnt.

Unluckily I just have the regular CRP (which is ok) and not DHEA level. Sleep seems ok, I get tired around 10pm but I can't go to bed at this time everyday for social reasons. I'm the first awaken in the morning (around 7am), no matter what time I went to bed the day before.

T4 Free : 11.6 pmol/L

T4 free : 0.9 ng/dl

TSH : 1.51

(this is only what I got for hormones)

I definitely eat a paleo diet, basically meat, fish & veggies (I like home made veggies juices) with lots of coconut oil. My favourite fat is CO & I use red palm oil also. I totally cut out dairy 2 weeks ago as I feel crappy everytime I eat cheese and can't help overeating them. I love apples & berries -it's the season!!. My diet is quite simple : egg yolks fried in coconut oils, juices (carrot, cabbage, fennel, ginger, lettuce,..), meats like pork chop, steak, lamb chop, chicken wings + stews, salmon, sardines and other available seafood (2-3 times a week). Nuts are rare. I eat chocolate 100% -pure cocoa! No sugar! Saveurs & Nature brand or Moulin des Moines. I'm lucky to live in Belgium- I don't need lots of variety & feel fine with that.

# Thread: Beach outing ideas
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beach-outing-ideas.2126/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cookworm — 2012-06-30T20:56:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beach-outing-ideas.2126/post-34644>

I know this might be a dumb question, but it's often very hard for me to think outside the box--I'm willing to try new things, but don't always know what my options are.

I'm hoping to go on a beach outing in a couple of weeks; we'll be there all day. I'll have a cooler, and what I always did pre-paleo was to pack some Subway sandwiches (they seemed to hold up pretty well). I'm not at the point right now where I go without eating lunch because I still get hungry (wish I could...I \*HATE\* dealing with lunch!), so I have to plan for a leptin/paleo-friendly lunch.

Any ideas for things that can last with limited cooling?

### janagram — 2012-06-30T21:06:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beach-outing-ideas.2126/post-34647>

hardboiled eggs and even some cooked meats are fine...like salami, bacon, prosciutto,mustard, dill pickles, (leave the mayo at home) and then, since it's midsummerish...perhaps a banana? Oh did I forget cheese? If you can have it....I stick to goat or sheep cheese, slices of manchego or petite basque (costco)...but most of all...have fun!

### PrimalPam — 2012-06-30T21:16:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beach-outing-ideas.2126/post-34652>

You could make yourself a Paleo salad. Buy the organic pre-washed greens, and whatever else you like in a salad. Keep in separate plastic containers. Bring some crabmeat (you can find it in the deli section; make sure it's just crabmeat), and some CO for dressing, which you can put in a small plastic container.

Pre-Paleo, I did the Subway sandwich thing too, and then I switched to their salads. Since I know they're not using organic, plus their dressings aren't good, I'd bring my own and assemble it.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-01T00:13:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beach-outing-ideas.2126/post-34684>

A few years ago I got my daughter a Chinese Lunch Kit. I can tell you it caused social problems with her peers at school! I never heard the end of it.

Nevertheless, I love the simplicity of these stainless steel kits and use them all the time for camping and road trips. Here are a few pictures. You could fill the containers with what ever you please.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34844=293-chinese-lunch-kit.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34844=291-lunch-kit-1.jpg)

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/34844=292-lunch-kit-2.jpg)

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-01T17:30:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beach-outing-ideas.2126/post-34790>

When I go ski I bring pre-cooked burgers, pemmican and coconut oil. A baggie with a few raw veggies. Late in the season the car gets hot some days and they all do well in a cooler. You could bring canned tuna if you're weary. But I'm super simple and don't care much as long as it's protein and fat

### cookworm — 2012-07-01T19:30:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beach-outing-ideas.2126/post-34827>

Oooo--I forgot about pemmican! ♥

### cookworm — 2012-07-01T19:30:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beach-outing-ideas.2126/post-34829>

Are these a similar idea to the Japanese bento boxes?

### kjgraffin@yahoo.com — 2012-07-01T21:45:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beach-outing-ideas.2126/post-34858>

I like mixing up some canned salmon or sardines with some dijon mustard & maybe some dill relish. Then you could eat it with some celery or cucumber slices.

# Thread: Hypercholesterolemia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypercholesterolemia.2128/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Souldanzer — 2012-06-30T21:13:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypercholesterolemia.2128/post-34651>

What is it, really?

I've had high cholesterol since I was a teen and my ED took control of my life. I've known for a long time that is co-occurs with anorexia and even if you fix the anorexia, most are still stuck with high cholesterol for life.

I just got diagnosed with heart disease in probably more than one way and my cardiologist made sure to put it all over my medical records in BIG LETTERS. My last internal med doc suggested statins but then said that wouldn't be so good for a woman of child-bearing age.

I looked at my own numbers and they don't look so scary to me at this point:

Total 281

HDL 106

LDL 163

VLDL 12

Trigs 61

HS-CRP 0.47 (was drawn a couple months after the lipid panel though)

This was a non-fasting blood draw which I think affects at least trigs result. My ratios are fine.

Am I looking at a problem here or is this just CW threatening?

What are acceptable or good cholesterol numbers.... you don't want them too low as well!

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-01T03:30:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypercholesterolemia.2128/post-34719>

> RESULTANT HORMONE CASCADE
>
>
>
> Leptin resistance occurs first. Then insulin resistance happens next. This eventually leads to adrenal resistance. Cortisol is the stress hormone that allows for fight or flight syndrome (life or death). This is the hormone that allows you to run away from a hungry lion fast and live. **Evolution always makes sure cortisol production stays ready for action at the expense of the other hormones that also are made from the same precursors. That precursor is pregnenolone. Pregnenolone is made from cholesterol, and cholesterol is made from LDL. So anytime the body is stressed or inflamed, it up-regulates cholesterol production to make more lifesaving hormones. It requires T3 and vitamin A as cofactors to complete this step. Blocking cholesterol production will increase cellular stress. This is why biochemically, to me, no statin drugs have ever made any sense under any circumstance in medicine. Moreover, this is why there is a chronic association of cancer to low cholesterol levels in the literature. In times of infection or stress, LDL levels always need to rise to protect the cell.
>
>
>
> A diet should provide a substrate of animal protein to sustain the hormone substrates. This is why a Paleo diet is ideal for hormone health because it provides ample substrate for all hormones. A Paleolithic diet is best for longevity in my opinion. It supports all of our hormones that are present in abundance in youth and health. Many other physicians, like Dr. Ron Rosedale, tell folks that protein stimulates the mTOR pathway which leads to death. The real issue I have there is that he gives no context to the statement. Here is my context. **His belief is only true if the background cellular terroir has a high HS CRP back drop, and if your gut is leaky as shown by a low HDL level. If your HS CRP is treated aggressively with a Paleo diet, your HS CRP will be low and your HDL will be high, and then a moderate protein diet is protective.** So I go hard after HS CRP in all of the patients I work with. It is a staple in my practice. Once solved, it is hard-core Paleolithic Robb Wolf style advice to affect the most important part of our biology.
>
>
>
> HORMONES. So anytime cellular stress is high (high HS CRP), it also forces all the hormone backbone substrate called pregnenolone to be shunted to cortisol production. This is called pregnenolone steal syndrome. What exactly does this mean, Doc? DHEA, Androstenedione, Vitamin D, testosterone, estrogen, and aldosterone production all fall dramatically. These are all of the hormones that are made from a common precursor. Remember that chronic Leptin resistance leads to huge hypercortisolism all the time!** NEVER FORGET THIS BIOLOGIC FACT! So that means we need to understand well what high cortisol does to the cellular terroir as well. (Levee 1)

I get that my elevated LDL means I still have inflammation/stress. However, my pregnenolone tested at upper end of range..... so that would mean I'm actually converting my LDL to the precursor. Am I understanding right that this conversion requires T3 and vitamin A? So.... that would mean my thyroid isn't necessarily in such bad shape?

Now, my DHEA is sorta low, as all other hormones, so therefore the pregnenolone has to go somewhere else.... and the only thing I didn't test was salivary cortisol. I sent this one in a week ago and am waiting for results.

Next, low HDL means leaky gut, but my HDL is high. Yet I have a leaky gut according to my low DHEA (learned that from Dr K). But not too bad, b/c preg is still pretty good. Yet even my hs CRP isn't all terrible in itself. I know it needs to improve.... I will ask for a lipid panel with my next hopefully more useful doc on Tuesday and will ask to include this measure.

So I guess my conclusion is my high LDL is from a leaky gut that's not too bad which sends my cortisol haywire. D used to be low but is fixed now with supplementation. T is a bit low, and I have no idea about aldosterone. E looked fine to me but my P/E ratio was estrogen dominant.

Therefore, my main concern would be my "not too bad" leaky gut.... correct? (yes, besides the other minor issues like fixing my brain and heart.... can't hurt to look ahead a bit ) And a leaky gut implies LR by definition, correct? But if I was entirely LR I shouldn't be converting my LDL to preg b/c my thyroid would then be offline.... which it should be anyways b/c of AN. So how AN am I exactly at this point?

### JanSz — 2012-07-01T18:15:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypercholesterolemia.2128/post-34804>

Get DHEAs, pregnenolone and cortisol to (very) good levels.

What is the particle size of your LDL.

\====

big fluffy ----good

or

small like bullets

...

### Lyndra — 2012-07-01T18:40:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypercholesterolemia.2128/post-34808>

Hmm, yours labs are similar to mine. I'm thinking my LDL is high because of poor thyroid function. You need T3 and vitamin A to convert LDL to hormones, so if T3 is lacking, your LDL won't get converted.

There are ways to test for your aldosterone level - check out stopthethyroidmadness.com. You can check your blood pressure lying down and then immediately after standing up. Also check the responsiveness of your pupils in total darkness by holding a flashlight to the side of yours eyes to see whether your pupils adjust or keep flickering. Symptoms of low aldosterone (which I have) include feeling lightheaded when you get up from sitting or bending over.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-01T22:19:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypercholesterolemia.2128/post-34865>

> big fluffy ----good
>
> or

> small like bullets
>

Thanks, Jan. I'm needing another round of testing I'm realizing. I just sent in my salivary cortisol and waiting for results. Haven't done the particle size testing but I'm pretty sure doc will do it now due to changed circumstances..... otherwise, I'll order it myself. Will be back to this thread once I get this organized.

I think I actually pushed the DHEA up too much for a little while... sleep went bye bye after being great for weeks. Now backed down and I'm sleeping again.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-01T22:26:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypercholesterolemia.2128/post-34866>

> **Lyndra;34968 wrote:** Hmm, yours labs are similar to mine. I'm thinking my LDL is high because of poor thyroid function. You need T3 and vitamin A to convert LDL to hormones, so if T3 is lacking, your LDL won't get converted.
>
>
>
> There are ways to test for your aldosterone level - check out stopthethyroidmadness.com. You can check your blood pressure lying down and then immediately after standing up. Also check the responsiveness of your pupils in total darkness by holding a flashlight to the side of yours eyes to see whether your pupils adjust or keep flickering. Symptoms of low aldosterone (which I have) include feeling lightheaded when you get up from sitting or bending over.

I pass all of those tests on a normal day. Every once in a while I get light headed getting up but it's getting more rare. I get checked for orthostatic hypotension every time I see a doc (no matter which one out of my collection) b/c of my history it's standard for me - I've never failed it in the office (but then I'm so nervous there....). I used to have to be careful getting up but now I don't even remember and just up and go. Goes well most of the time.

How's your pregnenolone, lyndra? B/c mine was rather unexpectedly high. Which actually would mean I \*do\* convert and must have at least moderate amount of T3, no? I think my problem is somewhere else..... all that preg probably goes to cortisol? Which should shut off my thyroid and stop conversion but that's not what my labs are saying.

I'll put the aldosterone on my list for next round of testing.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-01T22:30:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypercholesterolemia.2128/post-34868>

The other thing that came to me this morning (while raiding Lafayette's Vitamin Cottage of their entire supply of Artisana and unadulterated coconut milk ) .... AN is a disease of hypercortisolism, which then would set one up for elevated cholesterol... that's why all AN's have high cholesterol!!!! Bingo... that took a while to register!!

Now why doesn't it resolve with remission of AN? B/c there's still inflammation going on that was caused by AN? Or b/c people never fix the underlying leaky gut that pretty much every ED person has?

And can my cortisol even still be that high b/c AN is in remission.... and I'm not just following CW meal plans from my nutritionist like some others do.... I eat on my own, I eat without counting calories?

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-04T15:08:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-can-i-do-with-23andme-results.2130/post-35457>

Husband and I both got back our 23andme results. AND snagged a 4th of July consult while the gettin was good. Before getting the consult, I loved loved having the 23andme stuff . . . but I too don't know that I would have made the correct inferences from the data. It was great to see I did not have some things (alzheimers and bc genes). When Jack looked at the results and helped, wow - he was able to tell me some things I did not realize. Same for my Husband. . it turned 'pay attention to light' into a theory that he "tries" to do to something that if he does most likely will increase the quality and span of his life. THAT was worth it.

I found out that I have a super sensitive and great for adaptation gut. I totally had thought I was not a leaky gut person. I've never had any digestion issues and SEEM to be able to eat any types of foods. SO now I have a whole new batch of things to consider.

Another great rabbit hole for me to start exploring was Genetic BMI, I need to research more on this - but perhaps knowing will help me be more content at a certain weight rather than fighting to lose down.

### Birdy — 2012-06-30T23:49:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-tub-placement-dilemma.2132/post-34681>

Right now I have a tub on a small landing paved with brick. I just got a great new umbrella fabric (umbrella broke in a windstorm). My plan is to rig up a pulley system amongst the trees, so I can have some shade for the CT session. Then I can bring the fabric down over the tub when I am not using it. Right now I cover the tub with an old window between uses but it gets too hot. I really would like some shade for morning CT, and some protection for the tub, but would I also miss all the nature? I have some time for this, because right now, the tub heats up so much that it is a one-use deal with cold tapwater and then watering the plants. In the winter I would like to not have to replace the water so often. An alternative is to move the tub to a shady part, but more in view of the neighbors. What would you do?

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-01T17:26:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-tub-placement-dilemma.2132/post-34788>

Maybe some sort of reflective covering like you use in car windows?

### NakedToTheWind — 2012-07-01T19:53:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-tub-placement-dilemma.2132/post-34834>

There is a material that I have used for similar purposes. It is available at Home Depot for about seven dollars per sheet of 8 foot by 4 foot size. It comes with a reflective aluminized mylar on one side. It's mass is only a few ounces per sheet. It can be cut to various sizes with just a little box cutter knife. An insulating light weight box to cover your pool could be constructed from this material using nothing more than duct tape to join the pieces together.

[http://www.insulfoam.com/](http://http://www.insulfoam.com/)

### Birdy — 2012-07-02T07:42:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-tub-placement-dilemma.2132/post-34965>

hmmmm, maybe I'll switch to some sort of foam. Just that it looks ugly.

### NakedToTheWind — 2012-07-02T19:33:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-tub-placement-dilemma.2132/post-35063>

> **Birdy;35125 wrote:** hmmmm, maybe I'll switch to some sort of foam. Just that it looks ugly.

The white side with the blue writing would not show in an application such as you would need to construct. You would have to put the silver side out to reflect the sun. It would then look like a UFO had landed on your pool. And even more so if you took care to put the joining tape on the inside surface or used metal aluminum tape on the outside. The whole thing would be easy to move by the weakest people but not strong enough to stand or sit on unless you used the thickest panels.

# Thread: All night shivering after ocean CT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-night-shivering-after-ocean-ct.2133/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Caroline Cooper — 2012-07-01T00:29:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-night-shivering-after-ocean-ct.2133/post-34685>

Hi Friends,

I have a question about CT. I am new to CT and had my first ocean experience. I did about 20 minutes in the water. I don't know what the water temperature was but it didn't feel horribly cold to me at the time. I have been doing tub CT at about a temperature of 16C(62F).

My temperature after the ocean was 34.5C(94.1F). Yes, that is right. I felt find and my temperature was back to a low normal temperature (36.5C/97.7F) by bedtime. But during the night I had a really tough time. I had a series of shivering fits, making it difficult to sleep. The shivering fit was centered around my kidney area. I was in a tent and during the night I put on a jacket under my sleeping bag but I was still cold and shivering most of the night.

Does anyone know what causing this type of shivering fit centered around the kidney area? Is this just normal and I got colder then I thought?

### WannaBeOptimal — 2012-07-06T03:17:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-night-shivering-after-ocean-ct.2133/post-35766>

I have no idea, Caroline...surprised no one has chimed in on your problem.

Bump

### msclman — 2012-07-11T20:14:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-night-shivering-after-ocean-ct.2133/post-36893>

i could be wrong but hypothermia is below 95 degrees no? i try not to go below it. i do 60 - 80 minutes in 53 degree water and have serious shivering for about an hour but its not localized and does go away. I would post the question in the "ask jack" section.

### Destiny — 2012-07-12T15:11:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-night-shivering-after-ocean-ct.2133/post-37129>

> **msclman;37059 wrote:** i could be wrong but hypothermia is below 95 degrees no? i try not to go below it. i do 60 - 80 minutes in 53 degree water and have serious shivering for about an hour but its not localized and does go away. I would post the question in the "ask jack" section.

It was answered by "ask Jack" on 7/1 there. See below:

My bet is you might have a thyroid issue that is co-morbid......hard to say with this limited data.

### growltgc — 2012-07-23T21:39:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-night-shivering-after-ocean-ct.2133/post-40434>

the first time I did ocean swim (firs time any type of CT) I shivered the longest. I shivered ~20 minutes after a 20 minute swim. All the times afterwards I have only shivered for 10 minutes or so. I'm not sure why there was this variation.

I probably reduce the shivering time a lot by laying in the sand. I lay completely in the same and flip over once or twice to new hot sand, then the shivering is gone. And the air temp where I swim has been 62-68 F. the water temp 53-59 F

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-07-24T20:07:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-night-shivering-after-ocean-ct.2133/post-40757>

When I started I had random shivering up to 12 hours after my baths in 53F.

It didn't last, and I don't do that now.

# Thread: one keto adapting athlete's journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-keto-adapting-athletes-journey.2140/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### pbj27349 — 2012-07-01T14:18:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-keto-adapting-athletes-journey.2140/post-34759>

I stumbled upon this interview with Steve Phinney at

[http://www.meandmydiabetes.com/2011/04/14/steve-phinney-low-carb-preserves-glycogen-better-than-high-carb/](http://http://www.meandmydiabetes.com/2011/04/14/steve-phinney-low-carb-preserves-glycogen-better-than-high-carb/)

and was impressed enough by his reasoning that I purchased both of his and Volek's art and science books. I also did a google search for keto adapted athletes and found Barry's thread about fasted state training. In it Jack said

"Im hoping that more keto adapted athletes open up and talk about what they are doing."

Well, I will divulge. I will use this thread as a training/diet journal. I am training for this.

[http://www.setupevents.com/?fuseaction=event\_detail&eventID=2091](http://http://www.setupevents.com/?fuseaction=event_detail&eventID=2091)

I have done this race a few years ago. My time was 50 minutes. My goal for this one is to get under 40 min. I started keto adapting 23 May 2012. I am keto adapting because of all of the positive health effects associated with said state, and if keto adaptation enables me to perform better in the aquathlon then I will be a convert striving to stay in ketosis for life.

I am almost done with my mug of plain black coffee, so now it is time for a bowel movement and then a workout (in a fasted state of course). I'll check back in after the workout while I break my fast.

Peace,

Paul (in Snow Camp NC)

### Lyndra — 2012-07-01T18:49:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-keto-adapting-athletes-journey.2140/post-34812>

Cool. I'll be interested in hearing how that works for you. I've read about a number of people using a keto diet for ultra events but haven't heard much about people using it for shorter duration events. Good luck and let us know how it goes!

### pbj27349 — 2012-07-01T18:52:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-keto-adapting-athletes-journey.2140/post-34813>

This morning's workout out in my front yard done barefoot went as follows:

parallel bar dips supersetted with full close grip pullups

(1+1/:15) \* 79 for a total time of 19:52. That amounts to 79+79/19:52.

The dips were done with a controlled descent down to below the upper arms parallel with the ground like this

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WYwiTxnWtho\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;WYwiTxnWtho\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WYwiTxnWtho\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;WYwiTxnWtho)

I keep my knees tucked slightly forward throughout the rep and my head is forward so that I am looking down at my knees throughout as well. I use a little forward knee kip to get through the sticking point.

The pullups are started from a full dead hang and I touch my chest to my thumb knuckles at the top. I use a little knee kip to get all the way up. The descent is controlled. The full range of motion was inspired by

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XvM3JYC--hM&feature=related\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;XvM3JYC--hM\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XvM3JYC--hM&feature=related\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;XvM3JYC--hM)

Sun Yang has a long, clean, powerful stroke (no bubbles and only 27 strokes per 50m). I am aspiring to that.

This workout was better than the previous one because I lasted longer going at the same pace (17:19 vs 19:52).

After catching my breath and drinking some water, I proceeded to the next part of the workout which was:

jumping alternating lunges supersetted with jumping deadlifts

(8+8/:25) \* 32 for a total time of 13:13. That amounts to 254+254/13:13 (I only did 6+6 for the last superset).

I count the back knee touches to the ground for the lunges and hold my arms out to the sides for balance.

I touch the ground at the bottom of each jumping deadlift rep. Actually, I call these jumping toe touches. As I go up, I swing my arms up out to the sides to use my whole body to get me some air time.

This workout was better than the previous one (10:43 vs 13:13)

Ideally, I would like to have my total times for each section of the session be between 20 and 30 minutes, but I recently increased the pace for both sections and dropped the total times below 20. So, I can stay with these paces for a few more workouts.

These supersetting workouts are my eggs and butter workouts because they are convenient. I can do them at home first thing in the morning to take advantage of the fasted training effects. I will have to do some 1500m swims and 5k runs for specificity, but these home workouts enable me to simulate race demands more frequently.

Speaking of eggs and butter, I ate 8 home grown chicken eggs scrambled in a stick of salted butter and washed them down with a quart of goat meat broth. The broth was from meat I butchered myself and cooked in a crock pot with salt, garlic, and minced onion for seasoning. Supplements included 500mg of ascorbic acid, b50 complex, and a slow mag. For dessert, I drank about a pint of plain heavy whipping cream. Yum!

I am 6' 3.5" tall, and I weighed 205lbs during this workout. I started out at 220 to 225 at the beginning of keto adapting back in May. I think a lot of the lost weight was glycogen/water because there was one night during the process where I got up three times to urinate. I usually only get up once during the night. The next morning I weighed in at 210 down from 220. It was a lot of pee. I have been pretty stable at 205 to 210 since then. I do look leaner now and I thought I was lean before. The superficial veins are busting out more so now than ever before. So much for "fat makes you fat". It ain't so when keto adapted.

Tomorrow will be a much deserved day off. I've done six days in a row of fasted morning workouts. I'll jump back in Tuesday morning bright and early. Until then,

Paul

### pbj27349 — 2012-07-01T23:38:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-keto-adapting-athletes-journey.2140/post-34877>

Addendum,

second and Last meal of the day consisted of a chunk of cheddar cheese and about a pint of home made goats milk ricotta cheese washed down with the remainder of the quart of heavy whipping cream that I started this morning. It was a refrigerator cleaning meal heavy on the dairy. I had a six hour feeding window from 9AM to 3PM. I took another slow mag and vit. C. The rest of the day shall be food free. I like to go to bed with an empty stomach. I feel that my sleep efficiency is higher that way.

Sweltering in NC,

Paul

### earlyriser — 2012-07-02T02:45:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-keto-adapting-athletes-journey.2140/post-34919>

I am looking forward to following along with this!

### Barry — 2012-07-02T17:46:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-keto-adapting-athletes-journey.2140/post-35034>

Paul,

Fair play, best of luck with this. Just a few things

\- use CT if you can too, we need to see the affect of combined CT + FST

\- I'm not sure you will see direct performance gains at that distance/duration. The real benefits of FST/CT are the adaptations that improve RER/RQ i.e. exercise economy. You really don't see the performance gains with this until you go long... I think its needs to be minimum marathon distance and longer.. so 70.3, full ironman, ultramarathons etc. A 1500m swim and 5K run is done a lot closer to threshold. Not sure if keto will increase your performance at this pace. But will be interesting to see.

\-for running, get out fasted and run long, and run off-road, the further into the wilderness the better... thats all I'm going to say !

You will definitely reap the longevity awards by doing this, I'm just not so sure on peak performance. And it is this place where we have a trade-off.... perform better/live shorter... or perform equally, potentially slower but live longer !!

Good luck

### AKMan — 2012-07-02T18:33:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-keto-adapting-athletes-journey.2140/post-35044>

Paul, Barry - Do you feel there is a need to replenish glycogen stores in the muscles after an all-out workout or event? CW says you need carbs to do this, how can it be done while in ketosis?

### Barry — 2012-07-02T19:25:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-keto-adapting-athletes-journey.2140/post-35061>

Akman

a few things to explain:

1\. The Sport

First thing to decide is whether you actually need to use glucose as the primary fuel. If you are going sub max i.e. long distance, steady state... then through keto/FST/fat adaptation... you can easily perform using ketones and fatty acids as your primary fuel. Now if you are going all out, 5/10K runner, Competitive Cyclist doing TT's etc, then you will dip into glucose more. How much all depends on your adaptations. But first things first, glycogen replenishment is only needed if you are using glycogen as your primary fuel. You're either carb adapted.. not doing FST/CT or keto.... or you are doing those things, but race at such a high intensity that you need to add carbs in at certain times

2\. Upregulate Fat Usage: so one of the main aims of the whole FST/CT thing is to lower your RER at higher intensity levels. For example, the typical sports physiologist will say that you can only burn fat in zone 1/2. However, what I;ve found is that you can upregulate this process so that you can burn fat in zone 3/4... maybe dip into zone 5. In other words, you don't use glucose... you use fats.. so you don't need to glycogen replenish

3\. Gluconeogensis: people seem to forget that there are other ways of making glucose other than eating carbs. Amino Acids, Intermediates and Glycerol can be converted to glucose. So there will always be glucose in the muscle.

Also, remember, you can still eat carbs... and if you are an athlete, I think you can eat more than 50g.

### pbj27349 — 2012-07-04T23:00:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-keto-adapting-athletes-journey.2140/post-35533>

2 July 2012 was a day off for me and a day of dietary transition. Sunday evening while lying in bed trying to relax and nod off my heart rate felt faster than normal, so the following morning I measured my RHR. It was 45 bpm which is significantly higher than what it has been (in the low 30's as low as 31) before I embarked on keto adaptation more than a month ago. I searched for anything about ketosis and heart rate. It seems that others have experienced elevated RHR's while in ketosis. The proposed explanations for why this might happen were inadequate IMO. Low magnesium? I've been supplementing and drinking meat broth. Low potassium? Again meat broth and adding sodium as well. It just doesn't make sense to me. If RER goes down when keto adapted and fat is THE preferred fuel for the heart then why would RHR increase?

To test the idea that ketosis increases RHR, I decided to carb load on Monday and Tuesday. My RHR this morning was 37 BPM. That is closer to my previous norm. My Tuesday and Wednesday morning fasted workouts went according to my planned progressions. I think that I am going to continue eating a large mixed macros lunch and an evening low fat carb load meal, something that will clear from my stomach quickly so that I do not feel like I have a brick in my gut at bed time. My thinking is that I will be glycogen repleted and have an empty stomach for the following morning fasted workout in which I will deplete much of the glycogen. I will continue monitoring my RHR to see where it settles at.

### pbj27349 — 2012-07-04T23:40:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-keto-adapting-athletes-journey.2140/post-35538>

7-3-12 morning workout fasted + plain black coffee

dips supersetted with full close grip pullups

1+1/:15 \* 92

totals were 92+92/22:57 better than 7-1-12

jumping alternating lunges (back knee touches ground) supersetted with jumping toe touches

8+8/:25 \*40

totals were 318+318/16:35 better than 7-1-12

Did not eat until lunch at work. Ate goat meat, broth, cheese, dates rolled in shredded coconut + supplements.

Supper was 1/2 gallon skim milk inoculated with plain yogurt with live active cultures and supplements.

7-4-12 fasted + plain black coffee

dips and full pullups

1+1/:15 \* 108

totals were 108+108/26:57 better than 7-3-12

JAL and JTT

8+8/:25 \* 48

totals were 384+384/19:57 better than 7-3-12

waited until lunch time to break the fast. Ate goat meat, broth, cheese, dates, quart of plain yogurt.

body weight is holding steady between 205 and 210.

I am in ketosis only intermittently now after my fasted workout and before I break the fast at midday. I am shooting for an eight hour or shorter feeding window a la leangains but without the obsessive compulsive consumption of BCAA's before and after exercise. I don't mind if I lose some lean mass. It would be beneficial for me to get down below 190lbs for the event that I am going to race. The only way I can get there is to lose some lean mass. My BF% is already very low. My overarching goal is to get under 40 minutes in the aquathlon which means under 20min for the 1500m swim and under 20min for the 5k run. elite athletes at these distances have a lower (body weight/height cubed) than I have. That is my version of the body mass index. Mass is proportional to volume (or height cubed).

Barry,

Do cold water only showers with well water count? It's been so damned hot that is what I've been doing. The water takes my breath away a little at first and then I get used to it. They are only 5 min showers though.

For this race, I want to emphasize peak performance, and I doubt that I am doing the volume of training that would shorten my telomeres significantly. I would like to be able to stock up on muscle triglycerides and tap into them easily as well as glycogen during race day.

Thanks for your input.

### Barry — 2012-07-05T01:04:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-keto-adapting-athletes-journey.2140/post-35544>

> **Anna K.;35635 wrote:** Barry, just to clarify about replenishing glycogen stores after a hard workout. I understand your explanation above from the perspective of fat adaptation for performance, but what about muscle mass? If your goal is to bulk up and build muscle mass, do you need to replenish glycogen after a work out? For a fat adapted person do carbs help to increase muscle mass or help speed up the recovery? thanks!

Hi Anna

Muscle is not made from glycogen. Its made from amino's, fats and water. Do you see polar bears replenishing glycogen ?? ;-)

Muscle mass comes from protein synthesis and an anabolic hormonal environment. Insulin is a strong anabolic hormone and it is triggered by glucose. However, its not the only thing that triggers insulin. So muscle building and recovery can still happen without the need for carbs. Carbs might be effective in certain cases but they're not the only option.

### Barry — 2012-07-05T01:19:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-keto-adapting-athletes-journey.2140/post-35546>

> **pbj27349;35702 wrote:** Barry,
>
>
>
> Do cold water only showers with well water count? It's been so damned hot that is what I've been doing. The water takes my breath away a little at first and then I get used to it. They are only 5 min showers though.
>
>
>
> For this race, I want to emphasize peak performance, and I doubt that I am doing the volume of training that would shorten my telomeres significantly. I would like to be able to stock up on muscle triglycerides and tap into them easily as well as glycogen during race day.
>
>
>
> Thanks for your input.

Cold showers need to be longer I think. Ideally CT ice baths pre and even post training (although I'm not a huge fan of that). Really would need to get some hormonal profiling to measure this. Maybe run your CT options by Jack and see what he thinks.

You are now doing CKD. You are not fully keto. Not sure if you are going to optimize RER. You would be better off just having carb re-feed days when you feel they are needed rather than doing carbs everyday. Also, when you are eating carbs, put them in with your meal post work-out. Better for insulin sensitivity and protein synthesis that way.

### pbj27349 — 2012-07-06T03:13:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-keto-adapting-athletes-journey.2140/post-35763>

7-5-12 fasted morning workout under the influence of plain black coffee

full close grip pullups supersetted with two chair pushups (like this [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=imD9WQYkFro\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;imD9WQYkFro\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=imD9WQYkFro\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;imD9WQYkFro) but with feet on ground)

### pbj27349 — 2012-07-06T03:45:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-keto-adapting-athletes-journey.2140/post-35781>

I should probably change the name of this thread to "one fat adapted athletes journey". I am intermittently in ketosis due to the combination of intermittent fasting and fasted workouts. I was curious to find any discourse comparing "fat adaped" vs "keto adapted" and I came across this.

[http://www.marksdailyapple.com/what-does-it-mean-to-be-fat-adapted/#axzz1zi30i3zm](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/what-does-it-mean-to-be-fat-adapted/#axzz1zi30i3zm)

Sisson is of the opinion that one can be fat adapted without being keto adapted, whereas, I think, Phinney and Volek would disagree. I found a couple of abstracts that support Sisson.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/12388154](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/12388154)

[http://www.ajcn.org/content/77/2/313.abstract](http://http://www.ajcn.org/content/77/2/313.abstract)

It would appear that intermittent fasting combined with high fat meals can increase stored triglycerides in the muscles and increase the muscles abilities to oxidize the fat without even exercising. Add fasted exercise in there and the effects would be amplified I bet.

So, I guess I'm striving to maximize my capacity to burn fat (IMTG's) during exercise while having well stocked glycogen stores come race day.

This is an experiment with an N=1. It's the workout data along the way that I hope will be informative with the race result as the end point.

### pbj27349 — 2012-07-07T18:26:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/one-keto-adapting-athletes-journey.2140/post-36101>

7-7-12

break fast consisted of

10 eggs scrambled in half a stick of butter, a couple of slices of cheese, a pint of heavy whipping cream, a little peanut butter with honey, and a pint of plain yogurt (with active cultures)

supps: c 500, b50, slow mag

I'm shooting for this to be the only meal of the day. Feeling pretty full right now.

Looking forward to 10 \* 500m runs on the shortest interval possible for tomorrow morning's fasted fun.

I like the sponge analogy in this article.

[http://www.t-nation.com/readArticle.do?id=1628994](http://http://www.t-nation.com/readArticle.do?id=1628994)

Doing glycogen depleting exercise in a fasted state maximizes carb tolerance I think. empty muscle sponges (heightened carb tolerance) increase muscle insulin sensitivity. High muscle insulin sensitivity keeps insulin levels lower until glycogen stores are full. Lower average insulin levels improve prospects for increased longevity. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15771611](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15771611)

Glycogen depleting exercise in a fasted state combined with intermittent fasting

(reminder: [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/12388154](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/12388154))

will increase my ability to store and access muscle glycogen and muscle triglycerides simultaneously. That's my story and I'm sticking to it. [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CbxLbDdfhbY\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;CbxLbDdfhbY\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CbxLbDdfhbY\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;CbxLbDdfhbY)

We shall see how this hypothesis works for my performance in the aquathlon.

Until tomorrow,

Cheers.
