**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-37104>

Yeah you can try deepak chopra too. he is superb in meditation or spiritual pratices.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-07-16T20:25:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-38426>

I was going to sign up for a paid mindfulness course last year through UCLA onlines courses - it was about $300 or so.

But luckily, I missed the sign up deadline by 3 days. I say luckily, because then I found this free site - and I really enjoyed my classes:

[https://sites.google.com/site/mindfulnessonlinecourse/](http://https://sites.google.com/site/mindfulnessonlinecourse/)

### janagram — 2012-07-16T22:41:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-38488>

thanks, villja, for this link. I read and really liked it. Am thinking about doing it.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-07-17T15:36:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-38692>

> **janagram;38663 wrote:** thanks, villja, for this link. I read and really liked it. Am thinking about doing it.

Honestly, going in I was a little worried they would try to sell me something.

Every week I expected it - it never happened. It really gave me a great beginner's education in mindfulness meditation.

I always tried to read on how to do it in the past, and I never got anywhere. The key is just to do it every day and you get into a pattern.

The popularity of this free course is exploding and the membership is way up - so it may not be as personalized as it was when I took it. But I think it would still be worth taking the time and doing it.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-17T15:39:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-38693>

Looks like the next class starts in Sept.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-07-17T17:02:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-38713>

> **Nonchalant;38871 wrote:** Looks like the next class starts in Sept.

I guess that's a change with the increased numbers. When I did this last fall, there was no time to start. You could just join and everyone was on a different week's lesson.

### BJK77 — 2012-07-17T19:19:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-38768>

Thanks for posting this. I've been meaning to start a thread asking how others practice mindfulness. I've tried and struggled with this for so long - can never seem to clear my mind. I think I'll sign up for this class.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-07-17T20:51:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation.2218/post-38802>

> **BJK77;38946 wrote:** Thanks for posting this. I've been meaning to start a thread asking how others practice mindfulness. I've tried and struggled with this for so long - can never seem to clear my mind. I think I'll sign up for this class.

Let me know how it goes. With all of the changes, I wonder how it will be....

But when I took it, the way it worked is you were assigned homework in 1 week increments (a week was loosely 7 to 12 days depending on your speed). There were also 2 or so mp3s you could download that had guided meditations. Once you completed your homework and write up, then you submitted it to the instructor. And after reviewing, you'd get the material for the next week. I was always slow, so I took much longer than 7 days for each course block. But it was one of the better things I've ever done and I'm glad I took the time to stick with it.

# Thread: Travel and Breakfast
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/travel-and-breakfast.2220/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### colleencoble — 2012-07-05T18:21:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/travel-and-breakfast.2220/post-35645>

I'm going to be in a hotel for about 12 days starting next week. The last time I traveled I went ahead and had eggs. I was gone 4 days and by the time I got home, the pain at the base of my thumbs was bad. So I need to figure out something else for breakfast. I'd thought about taking my smoothie blender and making smoothies but I'm not sure I'll have a refrigerator for the spinach and coconut milk I use. Those of you who have autoimmune issues, what do you eat for breakfast when you travel? Funny how a year ago, I had no idea that eggs were inflammatory and that the pain in my thumbs was from eggs. LOL

### MartiD — 2012-07-05T18:37:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/travel-and-breakfast.2220/post-35654>

Most hotels can get you a small refrigerator if you tell them you have medication that has to be cold. Can you cook some beef up before hand to microwave? I took a jar of coconut oil to Vegas to get some fat in my coffee to help with hunger. We did have a restaurant available so I just bacon and egg omlete every morning. I don't have AI so probably can't help much with food options but ask for a frig!

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-05T18:41:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/travel-and-breakfast.2220/post-35656>

Colleen

what about canned fish? easy. couple of cans of sardines, some tuna, salmon etc... Bring you're hand blender - you'll be happy you did... it will make everything easier... what about home-made jerky from grass-fed/finished meat? also pre cook up some bacon - in the oven; throw into a plastic baggie.... easy food on the go!

### colleencoble — 2012-07-05T18:50:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/travel-and-breakfast.2220/post-35660>

Oh both great suggestions, friends! Thanks! I'll be on the road for 6 days total, 3 days there and back, then staying in Orlando for 6 days.

### BJK77 — 2012-07-06T03:07:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/travel-and-breakfast.2220/post-35757>

+1 on the canned fish. I travel frequently for work and have learned to love mashed sardines and avocado. They both travel very easily or can be bought just about anywhere and it makes for a good, filling breakfast.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-06T04:57:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/travel-and-breakfast.2220/post-35793>

Canned Fish-especially salmon

Lox

Cooked hamburgers

Cooked up bacon

Sauerkraut

CO put into a smaller jar

Chocolate bar of your choice

Skewers of meat of choice

I call the hotel ahead and get a fridge reserved if they don't already have one in the room. One time I forgot and that hotel didn't have any so I put my food in a garbage bag full of ice. Sometimes you just have to innovate!!

Then if you can get ot a grocery store pick up sushi, salads, and add to the food.

# Thread: More CT goodness...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-ct-goodness.2221/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jmathisen — 2012-07-05T20:52:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-ct-goodness.2221/post-35697>

As T2D, wound healing has been slow. I scratched my leg on a blackberry bush a couple years ago, and it took about 8 months to fully heal.

So been CT'ing for several weeks, and slid into 2nd base at my softball game on the artificial turf (my only exercise, 70 min game, 1x/week), and ripped a big old gash/turfburn in my leg.

A little over 2 weeks later, it's almost completely healed. I attribute the change to CT, because even with the prior injury, I had a good handle on my BG/insulin levels...

Yeehaw!

### NWgirl — 2012-07-06T03:41:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-ct-goodness.2221/post-35779>

congrats... wow that is amazing! Thanks for sharing your successes, really helps us newbies.

Kim

### BJK77 — 2012-07-06T14:17:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-ct-goodness.2221/post-35821>

That's awesome! So good to hear these stories of CT in action

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-07-07T23:43:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-ct-goodness.2221/post-36141>

Impressive! How long have you been dx with T2 DM? Any lost sensations on feet/toes? Did CT allow you to lower medication?

Congrats on the good work, keep going

### jmathisen — 2012-07-08T03:37:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-ct-goodness.2221/post-36164>

> **AbetterME;36307 wrote:** Impressive! How long have you been dx with T2 DM? Any lost sensations on feet/toes? Did CT allow you to lower medication?
>
>
>
> Congrats on the good work, keep going

Diagnosed several years ago, Tried just metformin, then metformin+actos, but dropped the actos. 2 years ago, started 18u of lantus 1x/day and Apidra/Humalog as-needed for BG control.

Since LR and CT I have stopped all insulin, and cut back metformin to 1000mg 1x/day, BG well under control. BP was down from 140/90 to 110/70 as well.

Just had checkup a few days ago, no issues in extremities.

Only side effect I've noticed so far which is contra to normal results is libido is completely gone.

# Thread: Coconut oil in homemade mayo?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-in-homemade-mayo.2223/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cookworm — 2012-07-06T01:51:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-in-homemade-mayo.2223/post-35733>

Has anybody tried to use coconut oil to make homemade mayonnaise? Does it turn out/is it good?

### Glamazon — 2012-07-06T02:28:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-in-homemade-mayo.2223/post-35739>

I use MCT oil. Half extra virgin olive oil, the light kind, and half MCT oil. I am not a fan of the flavor of coconut. This tastes great. Very light. I used either some apple cider vinegar or lemon juice to make a basic mayo.

I have also heard of people making baconnaise but I don't know how they keep the bacon grease from being too hot to curdle the egg.

### cookworm — 2012-07-06T03:08:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-in-homemade-mayo.2223/post-35758>

Baconnaise?!?!? I think I may have died and went to heaven!!  So, is the process the same as for regular homemade mayonnaise (just subbing liquified bacon fat/grease for the same amount of oil?)?

### janagram — 2012-07-06T05:12:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-in-homemade-mayo.2223/post-35797>

its most likely gooood. Just let the fat cool as much as you can without hardening....

I made it with coconut oil! I just barely melted it...it came out great! I didn't mind the flavor, it was good.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-07-07T01:49:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-in-homemade-mayo.2223/post-35991>

I tried the bacon mayo and it turned out really weird with a greasy mouthfeel. It got hard in the fridge and wasn't that good in my opinion. I tried the coconut oil mayo and the taste was definitely off and it also got hard in the fridge. My fave has been with mct oil, or a combo of that with light olive oil. I put my recipe up on my blog, Ive enjoyed it so much!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-07T03:50:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-in-homemade-mayo.2223/post-36026>

My fav is half CO oil and half macadamia nut oil. Gives it a nutty/buttery vibe.

### Birdy — 2012-07-07T07:21:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-in-homemade-mayo.2223/post-36058>

I like it with pure virgin olive oil. It has an intense olivey flavor, but I really like it that way. I usually add ume plum vinegar and powdered mustard instead of the usual.

### Glamazon — 2012-07-07T07:32:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coconut-oil-in-homemade-mayo.2223/post-36060>

> **cookworm;35921 wrote:** Baconnaise?!?!? I think I may have died and went to heaven!!  So, is the process the same as for regular homemade mayonnaise (just subbing liquified bacon fat/grease for the same amount of oil?)?

yes. make some and let us know.  I wonder if you would do half bacon fat and half oil if it would not get so hard in the fridge. I think we are just looking more for the flavor.

# Thread: Behavioral issues after starting CT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NWgirl — 2012-07-06T03:46:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/post-35782>

Ok so I am a total newbie to CT baths, have only done two. My first one was full bath 55 degrees for 20 minutes. 2nd one was the next day, same temp for 30 minutes. I really am loving it! It makes me feel good and it's not as bad as I thought it would be.

My problem is I seem to have developed tourette's or something since the few days I've started. I actually said something really rude to my husband, and chewed out one of my daughters for something weird too. My emotions are kind of up and down and now when I start talking to my family they are trying to judge if I'm 'normal' or 'crazy' at that moment.

Could this have anything to do with the CT? I can be emotional with hormones and irritable but this seems different to me. I'm not sure how to deal with these symptoms but I want to keep going, so please don't tell me to slow down!  Did this happen to YOU? If so what did you do?

thanks for your help...

Kim

### vkiernan — 2012-07-06T03:53:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/post-35785>

Sounds like detox to me. It's happening pretty fast though at least from my own experience. I think it took me at least a week or more before "stuff" started happening. I say keep on a going and get thru it unless you get to bad to tolerate than slow it down some. It is not linear so you may go thru some bad stuff then be ok then go thru some more bad stuff. There are a few threads out there that have a lot of good into them. One of them is symptoms I have not seen before and the other is the benefits of CT or something like that. You should be able to find them under the CT forum. Btw, I kept going. I did go thru a major hormone detox which lasted 2 weeks. It didn't start until a month or so after I started. It was baaaaad and I was a wreck but I have been pretty good since as far as detoxing.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-06T04:07:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/post-35787>

Someone said this somewhere.... "Too much T is nothing compared to E-rage"... or something like this

I actually said something really rude to my husband, and chewed out one of my daughters for something weird too. My emotions are kind of up and down and now when I start talking to my family they are trying to judge if I'm 'normal' or 'crazy' at that moment.
>
>
>
> Could this have anything to do with the CT? I can be emotional with hormones and irritable but this seems different to me. I'm not sure how to deal with these symptoms but I want to keep going, so please don't tell me to slow down!  Did this happen to YOU? If so what did you do?
>
>
>
> thanks for your help...
>
>
>

YES. Welcome to the Estrogen Rollercoaster. I started supplementing with DIM, which seems to have helped quite a bit. Fight through it...it does eventually get better!

### MartiD — 2012-07-06T18:08:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/behavioral-issues-after-starting-ct.2225/post-35865>

I have noticed that I snap quicker and more intensely. I never really lose my temper but I really snapped at my 17 yr old son the other day after he smarted off to me. Maybe I would have anyway just because it pissed me off but I did notice I was more intense than normal. I think it scared the sh&% out of him so maybe it was a good thing! He hasn't smarted off to me since then! LOL

### jmathisen — 2012-07-08T03:40:46+0300

Huh, I find almost the exact opposite. Much mellower, and way less stressed. Part of it I think is the forced hour of relaxation during CT reading a book, and just chillin'. (literally and figuratively).

Nickie

I will get some DIM. Thanks you guys..

Nickie

I have tried a couple brands now too. I remember 2 weeks of hell from a hormone standpoint that I went thru from CT'ing and things have not been that bad since for detoxing anyway.

Of course there is a chance I might have improved on the hormone front anyway, after that initial evil burst of detox. But DIM also seems to help with the all over body pain I still get after CT....not sure exactly what that's due to...estogens?(Or maybe xeno estrogens?).

Anyway...glad you're through the worst now.

It's tough. There should be an "I survived E rage" T shirt.  For the husbands/significant others too!

# Thread: Testosterone increases: Sodium Reabsorption
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/testosterone-increases-sodium-reabsorption.2227/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-07-06T05:03:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/testosterone-increases-sodium-reabsorption.2227/post-35796>

[http://www.hindawi.com/journals/aps/2011/817835/](http://http://www.hindawi.com/journals/aps/2011/817835/) warm adapted rat study

Testosterone Increases: Sodium Reabsorption, Blood Pressure, and Renal Pathology in Female Spontaneously Hypertensive Rats on a High Sodium Diet

AbstractEstrogen (E) and testosterone (T) are important in the sexually dimorphic pattern of blood pressure (BP) development. The goal was to examine the effects of endogenous E and exogenous T in the development of hypertension in female spontaneously hypertensive rats (SHR) on a high sodium diet. Female SHR (N=27, 5-week) were divided into four groups: (1) control (n=8), (2) ovariectomized (OVX, =26), (3) testosterone implants with intact ovaries (T, =6)and (4) ovariectomized + testosterone implants (OVX+T, =7). T was given immediately after OVX and replaced every two weeks and they were fed a 3% NaCl diet. BP was measured weekly and plasma norepinephrine (NE) analyzed by HPLC. OVX+T females exhibited the greatest elevation in BP

(190 Â± 4.0 mmHg) compared to controls at 15 weeks of age

(140 Â± 3.4 mmHg, < .001) and a pattern of hypertension development similar to that of male SHR. Females with T treatment showed evidence of glomerulosclerosis. **In conclusion, T accelerated the development of hypertension similar to the BP pattern observed in males; the presence of ovaries attenuated the T induced increase in BP; T increased renal sodium reabsorption, and T increased glomerulosclerosis.**


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: The 2nd most important thing you need to know about Diet and Exercise
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-2nd-most-important-thing-you-need-to-know-about-diet-and-exercise.2245/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Barry — 2012-07-06T20:36:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-2nd-most-important-thing-you-need-to-know-about-diet-and-exercise.2245/post-35910>

To follow on from my "there ain't no"... something else has come to my attention recently that I think is worth sharing.

I see a lot of questions and comments about "should I work out in the morning or evening" or "should I eat x amount of this food" or "should I run twice a week" ... etc etc

I see a big problem with this approach. The problem is that people are forgetting what the most important decider is when it comes to making individual choices... the most important decider is YOU ! It does not matter what the theory says or what us so called "experts" know... at the end of the day, we are all different, this goes without saying.. and no one dress size fits all. There are some people who thrive on nightshade vegetables and others who are intolerant. There are some who look and feel great after eating 300g of carbs and others who gain weight. There are some who sleep like babies after having a coffee in the evening and others who suffer insomnia. My point is that despite what the science says, we still don't fully understand how the body works. Its way more clever than we realise and its far more in tune with what you need/want compared to any book, blog or forum.

There are 2 important points I need to make here:

1\. I do think that those who listen to their own bodies in order to make decisions need to be healthy, LS, IS, fit, lean etc. If not, then you are not going to receive the right signals. Therefore, you need to follow the theoretical recommendations and modern principles (Jacks work) until you get to a state of self regulation, i.e. healthy.

2\. By listening to the body and allowing it to make the decisions for you, you are empowering the big player in this whole game - Epigenetics ! If you do what you feel is right for you and you enjoy it, then your gene's will express themselves accordingly ;-)

"Beyond the walls of intelligence, life is defined"

Barry

### fitness@home — 2012-07-06T21:00:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progesterone-estrogen-ratio.2246/post-35918>

So, I have my Genova Diagnostics complete hormone test results in hand. Interesting...so much information. Think it will be quite useful in my consult later this month.

It has five different ratios listed on it, but NOT the Progesterone / Estrogen ratio. Have looked online for a formulas on how to convert the results. Let's just say I am mathematically challenged and leave it at that.

Anyone out there care to take a stab at it?

Pregnanediol (24 hr urine) / **15.19 / Reference Range 0.30-4.20 micromol/24 hr

Listing each Estrogen result since I am not sure which is used in the calculation:

Estrone (24 hr urine) / **26.3 / Reference Range 3.0-52.0 mcg/g Creat.

Estradiol (24 hr urine) / 6.9** / Reference Range 1.0-17.0 mcg/g Creat.

Estriol (24 hr urine) / 262.3** / Reference Range 9.0-60.0 mcg/g Creat.

Thanks in advance,

Dawn

Sample-Report

Tests

Complete Hormones™

DESCRIPTION: Complete Hormones™ is the most comprehensive urinary hormone metabolism

evaluation designed to assist in the prevention and treatment of hormone-related symptoms and

conditions. This provides clues about menstrual irregularities, infertility, menopause, fatigue, breast

cancer, and osteoporosis.

ANALYTE LIST:

Turn Around Time: 21 Days

Specimen Requirements: 120ml Aliquot of urine (24-hour urine collection) OR 120ml Aliquot of

urine (First morning void)

### Lyndra — 2012-07-07T00:45:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progesterone-estrogen-ratio.2246/post-35966>

I had the same test done and would be interested in knowing more about how to interpret the results. My doc indicated the estrogen numbers are unreliable because I've been taking them orally (sublingually). I also don't have a direct progesterone result; only pregnanediol & pregnanetriol so can't calculate the P/E2 ratio.

### JanSz — 2012-07-14T22:17:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progesterone-estrogen-ratio.2246/post-37830>

> fitness@home

post-menopausal, no cycle

Your Complete Hormones test from Genova is urine test.

With that kind of test one measure Pregnenadiol and that represents/approximates Progesterone levels.

This is not direct Progesterone measurement, so you can't get P/E ratio.

Testosterone could be raised little higher.

Your Progesterone appears to be high, likely from supplemental progesterone.

Your ZRT morning cortisol is high, likely from supplemental progesterone.

Etiocholanone is little high, ----> highish E/A ratio, 5bR is stronger than 5aR, progesterone inhibits 5aR, you have little too much progesterone

THE is little high, too much cortisol, you have little too much progesterone

You are using

daily Last used); 100 mg topical Progesterone (compounded)

(daily Last used); 1 mg topical Testosterone (compounded)

consider reducing progesterone and increase testosterone.

\------------------------------------------

I prefer using pregnenolone,

hoping to raise progesterone (and cortisol)

For myself (male) I use 300mg/day (spilt wakeup + noon) pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix 150mg tabs, from Nutricology.

That raises my progesterone from (almost bottom in preg & prog) to low single digits on progesterone.

You (women, post-menopausal ) would likely feel better at low double digits (15 ???)

It is good to do blood tests (LabCorp) in paralel with urine tests.

There is a nice, most comprehensive that I know of, urine test at

Meridian Valley Lab, [www.meridianvalleylab.com](http://http://www.meridianvalleylab.com)

[http://www.lmreview.com/articles/view/select-the-right-hormone-test-for-your-patient-using-bio-identical-hormone-/](http://http://www.lmreview.com/articles/view/select-the-right-hormone-test-for-your-patient-using-bio-identical-hormone-/)

\---

### fitness@home — 2012-07-14T23:33:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progesterone-estrogen-ratio.2246/post-37843>

Thank you JanSz,

I appreciate the input. Will look into the other tests for future use.

One step at a time

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-14T23:39:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progesterone-estrogen-ratio.2246/post-37845>

### JanSz — 2012-07-14T23:58:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/progesterone-estrogen-ratio.2246/post-37853>

> **Shijin13;38013 wrote:** Fwiw my experience pregnenolone doesn't raise my Pg I've had to up my Pg to get it even close to the bottom of the Pg:E2 reference range.

When I was using first class transdermal pregnenolone and pregnenolone in pills and other types of pregnenolones,

I also was not getting anything in my blood.

I solved this problem by using

pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix from Nutricology.

At one time I was using 900mg/day

now

300mg/day

That raises my serum pregnenolone, but it still is within range.

I am dosing pregnenolone while aiming at good progesterone and cortisol levels.

Specially cortisol around 20 in the morning.

That keeps me only slightly over men's range for progesterone that is the same as woman's in first half cycle.

That is rather low progesterone~1.5ng/mL

Coleen is post-menapausal, and she likes to keep her progesterone at 10x of above level, flat throughout the month.

That may require more pregnenolone.

And

monitoring by frequent testing.

//

\------------------------------------

If someone produces at least some pregnenolone,

Saving grace may be situation when by adding progesterone,

pregnenolone is not called for and backs up to higher level.

That could be checked and verified.

# Thread: Can one eat fermented veggies?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-one-eat-fermented-veggies.2249/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-07T01:12:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-one-eat-fermented-veggies.2249/post-35976>

Home again, Home again, Jiggity Jog. . .

GLAD to be home and away from all the family food and road food! I ended up eating some jarred fermented cabbage for dinner tonight - have you hear anything one way or another on if this messes up the process?

I thought it might actually be a good thing, and help with my lack of movement so to speak.

Thoughts from the Learned?

### caroline — 2012-07-07T01:18:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-one-eat-fermented-veggies.2249/post-35979>

I would love to know this too

### colleencoble — 2012-07-07T01:48:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-one-eat-fermented-veggies.2249/post-35990>

It shouldn't be a problem. It's still just cabbage.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-07T01:54:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-one-eat-fermented-veggies.2249/post-35995>

> **colleencoble;36155 wrote:** It shouldn't be a problem. It's still just cabbage.

Thanks! So glad to hear that. I shall weigh for the first time in long time tomorrow a.m. Pants are all big on me now, so I'm hopeful.

One question, considering the "toll" your body undertakes and the fact that you tend to lose more lbds at the start of a round rather than the end - would a strategy of doing two short rounds with plenty of time to get your body back in tip-top shape be more productive?

i.e. you would lose more lbds per day on average AND you would end up with less damage from not being optimal as long?

Any opinions are welcome!

### colleencoble — 2012-07-07T01:56:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-one-eat-fermented-veggies.2249/post-35997>

I really really dislike doing short rounds. I see way more stability with the long rounds, probably because some of the initial weight loss is water weight. When you do a long round, the body starts adding back in water while the fat is going so the losses are REALLY losses. Dr. Simeon never did short rounds unless the patient had 15 pounds or less to lose.

# Thread: cortisol and blood pressure
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-and-blood-pressure.2257/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eve — 2012-07-07T16:38:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-and-blood-pressure.2257/post-36078>

My last thyroid panels in Jan and March 2012 revealed that I had a very low TSH, like 0.09. Free t3 was at the bottom of the range and free t4 was in the middle. At this time, I was taking Synthroid 50mg (since Feb 2008) and 30mg natural thyroid (Allergy Research Group) (the latter which I started in February, I think). In April, I switched completely to a natural thyroid (Erfa brand -- Canadian), 1 grain. I don't have Hashi's.

I am going to get a new thyroid panel done this Monday, but in the meantime, I am stumped how my low TSH and low free t3 suggests pregnenolone steal syndrome and high cortisol. However, I consistently have very low blood pressure like 90/60, which would suggest that I have lower cortisol levels. (I'm finally going to get a Salivary Hormonal Test done but that won't be for a few weeks).

Any ideas for the inconsistency? Thank you.

### chocolate — 2012-07-07T17:39:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oral-mercury-chelation-general-guidelines.2259/post-36094>

[http://livingnetwork.co.za/chelationnetwork/chelation-the-andy-cutler-protocol/general\_guidelines\_cutler/](http://http://livingnetwork.co.za/chelationnetwork/chelation-the-andy-cutler-protocol/general_guidelines_cutler/)

### chocolate — 2012-07-07T22:28:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oral-mercury-chelation-general-guidelines.2259/post-36129>

really nice blog compare and contrast of chelation methods

interesting point... but i like the idea of cilantro pulling poison out of the brain and laying it in the peripheral tissues....

> Dr. Natasha Campbell
>
>
>
> Does metal toxicity have anything to do with gut flora? Author of the GAPS diet and recent interviewee on The Underground Wellness Podcast, Natasha Campbell thinks so:
>
>
>
> "The digestive track all spread out would cover a tennis court, and it's the perfect place for anything harmful in the environment to settle, yet the good bacteria there chelate (remove) chemicals and toxic metals - if they can't destroy it, they grab and it take it from the body.
>
>
>
> Two groups of rats were given organic mercury. One group were given a powerful antibiotic, the other group were not. The mercury got into the bloodstream of only about 1% of those without the antibiotic, and 90% of those with the antibiotic. Keep gut flora healthy and strong and it will protect you. (When antibiotics are taken they wipe out bad bacteria AND beneficial bacteria.)
>
>
>
> **If you have healthy solid gut flora, it will chelate the mercury and take it out."**
>
>
>
> This theory could explain why some people are not sensitive to their amalgam fillings, presumably because they have very healthy gut bacteria.
>
>
>
> Key Points:
>
>
>
> Does not support chelation therapy, instead recommends fixing the gut and focusing on the repopulation of beneficial gut flora.

[http://whatidontknow.net/blog/eight-years-and-counting-my-chelation-story/](http://http://whatidontknow.net/blog/eight-years-and-counting-my-chelation-story/)

[http://mercurystories.com/stories/](http://http://mercurystories.com/stories/)

# Thread: CT to manage back and sciatic pain
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-to-manage-back-and-sciatic-pain.2260/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nate A — 2012-07-07T18:18:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-to-manage-back-and-sciatic-pain.2260/post-36098>

Hi all ~ I still have quite a bit of reading to do on this site, so I apologize for asking questions that I could probably find answers to on my own after some thorough research. Thing is, I'd like to start doing CT tonight in order to try and help alleviate some bad back and sciatic pain I'm suffering through right now.

A little background - I had a microdiscectomy performed on my L4/L5 this past January. The surgery was great for getting rid of my degenerative disc pain and did cure my sciatica pain - briefly. For the sake of brevity, I'll skip all the details, but once again, I have herniated my L4/L5 and have a bulging L5/S1. I am now suffering from sciatica down my left leg which becomes bilateral as the day wears on. It's far worse now than it was the first go round. This was all confirmed by an MRI last week. Docs want to go conservative with treatment once again, which I'm fine with, as the surgery was not fun (I was actually wheeled in for emergency surgery - it got that bad last time). Thing is, nothing about the conservative approach last time worked, and I tried everything available to me here in Germany (I'm an American living and working here).

I currently am on no medication to help out. German docs don't believe in using narcotics for pain relief and NSAIDs do diddly squat for me, besides irritate my stomach. I'm hoping CT can help.

I can stand cold very well, so I want to jump right in. I don't have much fat on me anymore (been Paleo for two years), but still should be fine with submerging myself. I can have my wife help get me in and out of our deep tub. I guess I'm just looking for any tips or tricks or suggestions on what I should try. Water temp, length of time, do I need to wear wool knickers/hat/shirt/socks or can I just go in naked? Any help would be appreciated.

thank you!

### Huck — 2012-07-07T18:46:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-to-manage-back-and-sciatic-pain.2260/post-36103>

I had severe back and sciatica pain and 2 doctors said I needed to have surgery. Being the stubborn person I am, I went fishing.

When I got back, I started swimming 5 times a week. One leg just kind of dragged along so I was swimming with my arms and 1 leg. When I finished swimming, I went to the jacuzzi and tried to stand on my toes. In other words, go from flat footed to raised heels. At first nothing happened because the nerve wasn't responding, but over a few weeks I got to where I could get up on my toes. Doctors had both told me i would never get nerve sensation back, but I did.

Back is not perfect, but other than a little stiffness, it is fine. I remember also using a lot of ice on my back, to the point it numbed the nerves.

### PrimalPam — 2012-07-07T19:29:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-to-manage-back-and-sciatic-pain.2260/post-36113>

Nate, I saw your other post in "Ask Jack", and I read with interest, as I just re-herniated my disc (is that the proper way to say it?) last Sunday. Happened 2 years ago, and this was the 2nd time. Improper lifting; trying to clean out my closets and lift all the heavy boxes and file cabinets; yes, I was rounding my back, not bending my knees. Was in excruciating pain, could barely get off the couch.

I've been full-tub CT'ing 5 x/week for 3 weeks (52F, 40 mins), and I was so upset that I couldn't do it on Sunday. In fact, I haven't gotten into a tub yet. I'm too afraid I won't be able to get out! But the spot-ice CT does help a lot: Whenever you can, lay on your stomach and put ice packs on the areas. Do not use heat. If you internet search "Lower back pain", you will find more and more advice NOT to use heat, but rather ice. Duh....they're all realizing that heat only draws blood to the surface, temporarily making you feel better, but ice actually lowers inflammation. My entire family came in town this week for the 4th, and my dad showed up with his heat pack strapped to his back. I told him to throw that thing in the trash and go buy the gel flexible ice packs instead.

If you have a spotter (i.e. your wife) to help you in & out of the tub, by all means, do it. Get the water down to around 50F and spend 45 mins to an hour every day, plus the spot CT.

I'm going in the tub today for the first time in almost a week (it's only been 6 days; it feels like months because I really miss it!), as I finally feel like I'll be able to move.

I, like you, am not on any medication. Even the first day, I refuse to take NSAIDS. However strange this sounds, I like to actually FEEL my pain, so I can feel if I get better. I haven't taken so much as a Tylenol in over 20 years and don't want to start now. I've been, all week, practicing getting up from the chair, picking things up off the floor, etc., using only my legs and not rounding my back. It has helped, along with the spot CT.

### Nate A — 2012-07-07T20:05:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-to-manage-back-and-sciatic-pain.2260/post-36120>

Huck and Pam,

thank you for the replies. Swimming is out of the question right now for me. There is a pool in the town to the south of me, but with 5 month old twin daughters, it's out of the question for now. I just don't have the time or the means of getting there while my wife is at the office.

Pam - I've been icing my lower back every hour and a half to two hours for 20 minute intervals. Lying on my stomach is very uncomfortable for me right now, so I've been doing it stretched out in my recliner which is the best way I've found yet. I will be climbing in to the tub before bed tonight also, hoping that can help me with the pain and get some rest. It's been really hard to sleep since re-injuring my back, as I'm sure you know! I learned about the no heat thing when I went through all this crap with my back previously, but it's good to hear it reaffirmed.

I know what you mean about feeling the pain, but sometimes, it's nice to have something to take the edge off. I've been living with chronic pain due to various maladies for nearly two decades - and it gets old. If it weren't for the horrendous gout that I suffered with for 16+ years, I may have discovered just how bad my back was earlier. Instead, I just ignored it. Then, by going Paleo, I was able to curb the gout attacks for good, and my back pain took center stage. I've also suffered a lot of other injuries, had three knee surgeries (probably having a fourth soon), two jaw surgeries, broken bones, etc... Injury prone? Who me? One of my problems right now is that I can't use my knees to even bend to pick anything up due to the lateral meniscus being torn in the right one. Oh, the irony. Doing stretches to help my back and I tear my meniscus? jeez..... and I'm only 38.

I'll try the CT tonight and report back tomorrow. I really hope it helps, but I'm not expecting much from just one try. Thanks a lot for the advice and I hope your CT goes well! Here's to healing up as soon as possible!

### PrimalPam — 2012-07-07T21:12:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-to-manage-back-and-sciatic-pain.2260/post-36124>

Re: CT. Treat it like Paleo: a lifestyle choice. Not something just to do when the pain flares up. Dr. K. says 2-5 x/week. I aim for 5 x/week, and I treat it like I used to treat my aerobic exercise (that I no longer do...I mean, how many cavewomen did jazzercise? lol). So, the CT is something that's part of most of my days: I eat my dinner, then get into the tub for 40 mins. It helps remove toxins, lowers inflammation, helps sleep dramatically.

In fact, I attribute CT to the fact that this week, even though my back was in such pain, that I was able to sleep through every single night. I'd wake up and realize that I just couldn't get out of bed easily: I'm sure you know the drill: prop myself up on my arms, swing my legs out, move extremely slowly. But during the night, I didn't wake once.

So treat CT as an adjunct to your already terrific choice of Paleo eating. Lots of high quality proteins and fats and CT = a healthy lifestyle.

### Nate A — 2012-07-08T07:04:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-to-manage-back-and-sciatic-pain.2260/post-36184>

That's good advice, Pam. I think I will make CT part of my daily routine as I really enjoyed it last night. I'm a bit weird and had no problem with full immersion in 55 to 60 degree water for 30 minutes last night. It did help me get to sleep a lot easier, although I did wake up a few times from back spasms and pain. It's also why I'm up at 5am this morning too. Damn back.....

The tub was a bit awkward to get in and out of and I did get impatient filling it, using some warmer water to finish it off. I think I can get the water down to 50 easy enough, I'll just have to start filling earlier. My wife swears that the water was more like 40, but after years of surfing in places such as Oregon, Alaska, and Northern Japan, I can gauge water temps quite well. I am going to pick up a thermometer today or tomorrow just to make sure, though.

Thanks again for the info and advice. I will continue to check in here and let you know how things go!

### PrimalPam — 2012-07-08T16:58:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-to-manage-back-and-sciatic-pain.2260/post-36199>

Re: Getting in & out of the tub. Sounds like your tub is a lot deeper than mine (mine is a standard-sized tub). Make sure your wife is around to help you. I didn't CT for a week in the tub because I'm single and was worried I wouldn't be able to get out! But I did it yesterday, and it was fine. And my back does feel better today.

I know what you mean about getting impatient while the water fills the tub. My tap is coming out over 80F these days, so it takes a lot of ice to get it to the low 50's. I go ahead and get in as soon as I turn the tap on, so I can soak up all the CT goodness.  I throw all the ice in, then turn the tap on, then get in. The initial water temp is much lower, due to less warm tap water, and it evens out at the low 50's. But that way, I have more cold water over my lower body while it fills.

I'd aim for 45 to 60 mins in the low 50's (and yes, buy a thermometer; I've become obsessed with checking it every 10 mins or so). You can make ipod playlists, read a book, or just close your eyes and meditate to pass the time. Do this about 5 x/week and I really think you'll see results.

I do hear you on back pain waking you up. People who don't have back pain just don't understand this. I do, literally, feel your pain.

Hello all, first post here on the forum. Had a quick question regarding CT. I will be beginning my first session of CT tomorrow and am curious regarding the frequency of the treatments. Is CT something i should strive to do every day, or is it something I need to build into? Basically should i try to do this 7 days a week or should I start with once or twice and progress from there? Thanks in advance.

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-07-07T23:37:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frequency-of-ct.2263/post-36138>

You want to do it as much as possible, especially if you have medical conditions and fat to loose. Follow the protocol (you can find it in the blog in Febuary archives). Do a little every day, increasing the time and lowering the temperature, and take a break if you feel like it...Try it and you'll see how it goes. Good luck

# Thread: Hey cowgirls and boys,  a little info on slaughtering and seasons?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-cowgirls-and-boys-a-little-info-on-slaughtering-and-seasons.2267/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-08T02:41:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-cowgirls-and-boys-a-little-info-on-slaughtering-and-seasons.2267/post-36158>

Last year when we got our beef and pig, we bought it early - and then they slaughtered them in Late november.

I'm out, and want to buy more. . .and a lamb. Do these animals have a season? Is there something I should be looking for when screening a supplier? How often should I stock up? Should I try to buy a years worth - or can you purchase more than one time of the year?

Also, I want to look for someone who won't dry age the beef any suggestions on how to go about that?

Thanks!!!!

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-07-08T04:55:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-cowgirls-and-boys-a-little-info-on-slaughtering-and-seasons.2267/post-36173>

I don't have any good info for you and am very interested in responses you get. But I was wondering, what's wrong with dry aging the meat?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-08T06:40:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-cowgirls-and-boys-a-little-info-on-slaughtering-and-seasons.2267/post-36178>

From what I was told by our butcher, the best time to slaughter is spring into early summer because the cow will have just come off feasting it's way through wonderfully lush pasture. This will help put as much fat as possible on a grass-fed cow.

Why no dry aging?

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-10T03:03:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-cowgirls-and-boys-a-little-info-on-slaughtering-and-seasons.2267/post-36433>

> **Cavemam;36324 wrote:** Last year when we got our beef and pig, we bought it early - and then they slaughtered them in Late november.
>
>
>
> I'm out, and want to buy more. . .and a lamb. Do these animals have a season? Is there something I should be looking for when screening a supplier? How often should I stock up? Should I try to buy a years worth - or can you purchase more than one time of the year?
>
>
>
> Also, I want to look for someone who won't dry age the beef any suggestions on how to go about that?
>
>
>
> Thanks!!!!

There is definitely a lambing season (when lambs are born) and then shortly thereafter slaughtered. At least here in New Zealand when they range free on the hills and ranches. Our organic rancher sells whole lambs, butchered, shortly after lambing season. You can just freeze and then eat as required. I've heard you can get NZ lamb all over the US - maybe you can even get whole lambs? (We export 99% of our lamb and our seafood, so it's actually cheaper in the US my friends tell me.)

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-11T02:58:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-cowgirls-and-boys-a-little-info-on-slaughtering-and-seasons.2267/post-36701>

I think it's kind of hard to tell...of course, the pastures are most lush at the beginning of summer, but grazing cows might also spend some time eating cover crops (turnips, radishes, etc.) in the fall leading into winter. During the winter, grass-fed cattle will get hay, so I doubt they would have the best taste as when coming off of lush summer grasses. I think if you're sourcing your beef well, it probably doesn't matter exactly when you buy it.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-11T03:01:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-cowgirls-and-boys-a-little-info-on-slaughtering-and-seasons.2267/post-36705>

Oh good thought PC, but then you are way more of an expert than I am! Here in SD county a lot of the cattle is not fed hay (or so I am told) in the winter because we have the best green pastures then. No snow, some rain, etc. My take home is to know the farmer and know the butcher.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-11T04:13:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hey-cowgirls-and-boys-a-little-info-on-slaughtering-and-seasons.2267/post-36721>

On the non aging, I've heard/read/seen that with quality grassfed the aging doesn't do anything but dry it out and make it a little tougher, and that packaging/freezing as soon as possible after the kill makes it cook up nice and tender and moister.

I am not in the google mood, so I could probably look it up. I'm so paranoid about information being tainted by CW and unfound dogma that I hardly have the interest to use the google fu anymore.

On another note, I love the vacuum sealed portions - but don't love the plastic leeching into my meats. But, if it could be as long as a year before it gets eaten, the paper doesn't seem good either. Anybody have em paper AND vacuum seal? I can imagine the call I make to the butcher to ask THAT one!

# Thread: Infant Potty Training and Baby Led Weaning
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/infant-potty-training-and-baby-led-weaning.2270/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nate A — 2012-07-08T06:21:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/infant-potty-training-and-baby-led-weaning.2270/post-36176>

We are having GREAT success using both of these methods with our five month old twin daughters. After our daughters were born, both my wife and I were at home with the girls for the next two months - me recovering from back surgery and her recovering from the birth (although frankly, because of the shape she was in due to eating Paleo, she could have run a marathon two days after giving birth!). We read a lot during this time, and came across both of these subjects on various, I hesitate to say obscure, but lesser-known Paleo/Alternative Eating blogs.

We've been very surprised how little is known about these subjects in the western world. Not even the doctors we've been to have heard about either Infant Potty Training or the Baby Led Weaning. I wanted to mention them here because I think they are beneficial to any new parents, making things so much easier for both the baby and the adults (parents or even child care specialists). Here are the basics on each:

IPT - you basically learn to read the signs your baby/infant gives before they either pee or poo. This may sound tough, but it actually is quite easy after a few days of diligence. When you see your baby give a sign, be it grunting, arching their back, turning red, contracting their little belly, etc, you simply take the baby to the potty (we purchased four baby potties to put around the house), set them down, and let them do their thing. We've established a signal with our girls so that they know when it's time to go. Even at only five months old, they've both established some bladder control. From a father's perspective (mine), it is so much easier than cleaning up after a poopy diaper, and it saves money on diapers and wipes. Learn more [HERE](http://http://www.babycenter.com/0_infant-potty-training-what-it-is-and-how-to-do-it_1745035.bc)

We used this book to get started: [Infant Potty Training: A Gentle and Primeval Method Adapted to Modern Living](http://http://www.amazon.com/Infant-Potty-Training-Primeval-Adapted/dp/1888580305)

BLW - Besides breast feeding, this is about as Paleo as you can get with feeding your baby. It's another simple concept, but one that has been lost in the western world for various reasons (bad advice, profit motives and lazy parenting). Again, you just have to pay attention to your baby's wants and needs. When they start showing interest in the food you are eating, it's time to let them start experimenting. This usually happens around 5-6 months of age. We simply cut up some of the food we're eating for dinner in to small, bite-sized pieces, and put them on the tray in front of our girls and let them go at it. Yes, you can even do this with meat. This is so much better and healthier than feeding mush to a baby, and if you think about it, it makes perfect sense. Saliva is an important part of digesting food and extracting nutrients from the food you eat. Feed a baby mush and they simply slurp it down. It's essentially pre-digested and no work goes in to eating that Gerber crap. Give a baby a piece of sweet potato or avocado or cooked egg yolk or even hunk of steak, and they will chew and gnaw and work on that piece of food until they can swallow it - even without having any teeth. For more information, check out this site: [http://www.babyledweaning.com/](http://http://www.babyledweaning.com/)

If anyone has any questions, I'd be happy to answer them!

Our girls at dinner:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Working out the logistics of feeding herself

Tell me she's not having fun!

### kathylu — 2012-07-08T22:21:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/infant-potty-training-and-baby-led-weaning.2270/post-36233>

Love it! I fed my baby the same way, though I did start him on raw egg yolk at 4 months.

I tried IPT, but had very little success with it. I'm sure it was me and not the method.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-08T23:06:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/infant-potty-training-and-baby-led-weaning.2270/post-36240>

We tried the IPT, but alas I think neolithic life got in the way. I was working from home, but not available to check every look - and gave up fairly quickly.

The food thing I think is spot on. I couldn't BF, but we did NOT do babyfood. Well except banannas mixed with avocado, and sweet potatoes etc.

# Thread: Optimal best practices for transitioning from P2-P3
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-best-practices-for-transitioning-from-p2-p3.2275/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-08T22:57:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-best-practices-for-transitioning-from-p2-p3.2275/post-36237>

Hola Great Resources,

I'd like to hear from you that have been successful with maintenance what you think your best practices are when going from P2-P3, There seems to be many more differences of opinion on how to proceed - seems like the P&I advice is too broad.

I've seen studied warnings about too much fat too soon, and simply eating too much right away, and to "test" foods etc.

Vs.

Anything you want except starches and sugars.

I'm looking forward to starting P3 with a big ol bullet proof coffee - but more important is putting the scales in my favor as far as sustaining the LIW weight.

Also, for Steak Day - what about added fats? or a fat fast during the day by adding HWC or CO in the coffee? I've not seen any ideas for why steak day works other than it's really just a type of IF - in which case seems like I could put some nice kerrygold herb butter on my steak.

Speaking of steak, just saw the costco flyer had Organic Grass Fed NY strip steaks. . 36 of them for $135! Frozen, price includes shipping.

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-07-09T15:38:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-best-practices-for-transitioning-from-p2-p3.2275/post-36314>

Unfortunately this is one of those YMMV things. I\`ve seen people jump into BAB after their 3 days transition and do fantastic but I\`ve also seen the opposite. a couple of things that seem to cross the various suggestions: leave cheese and nuts for the 2nd or 3rd week of P3, do be careful with dairy as it can show very quickly on the scale if dairy is not for you.

### MartiD — 2012-07-09T18:47:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-best-practices-for-transitioning-from-p2-p3.2275/post-36337>

I went directly into the Leptin RX with second round and continued to lose but I know not everyone does that well. You could give it a try and see how you react. Were you doing the LR before? I think that might have helped me as I did the LR after my first P4 for a month and then did a second 24 day round and right into LR.

### colleencoble — 2012-07-09T19:30:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-best-practices-for-transitioning-from-p2-p3.2275/post-36345>

I immediately add butter and heavy cream. Though just butter is better because the cream has that lousy thickener in it. I add coconut oil right away too. I double my meat if I can get it down. Sometimes I can't on the first day of P3. I also eat an avocado on my first day because I'm usually craving them. LOL

I've tried other correction days. Salmon works almost as well as steak but not quite. Chicken doesn't work well at all. Neither does lean fish so I think the fat is important. I usually have coffee with cream throughout the day until time for steak. The weight loss is the same for me with or without cream and the fat makes it nicer.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-09T19:36:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-best-practices-for-transitioning-from-p2-p3.2275/post-36346>

> **colleencoble;36511 wrote:** I immediately add butter and heavy cream. Though just butter is better because the cream has that lousy thickener in it. I add coconut oil right away too. I double my meat if I can get it down. Sometimes I can't on the first day of P3. I also eat an avocado on my first day because I'm usually craving them. LOL
>
>
>
> I've tried other correction days. Salmon works almost as well as steak but not quite. Chicken doesn't work well at all. Neither does lean fish so I think the fat is important. I usually have coffee with cream throughout the day until time for steak. The weight loss is the same for me with or without cream and the fat makes it nicer.

Good to hear this perspective - so far my experiences have been similar to yours. Also, we get raw pastured heavy cream that is just shy of butter (it's almost too thick for a spoon) so I have no qualms about additives. I just ordered 36 6oz steaks, so I should be ready!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-09T19:37:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-best-practices-for-transitioning-from-p2-p3.2275/post-36348>

I've been doing leptin RX for around 5 months, a very low carb version. . .so my hope is that set me up well for this.

### Sem — 2012-07-11T17:44:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-best-practices-for-transitioning-from-p2-p3.2275/post-36826>

There are two versions of HWC without the carrageenan - Clover and Strauss. Both organic. There may be more but that's all I've seen so far.

### colleencoble — 2012-07-13T23:01:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-best-practices-for-transitioning-from-p2-p3.2275/post-37602>

Yum, cavemam! Sounds terrific.

I can't find those brands here in Indiana. But butter is fabulous, much to my surprise. LOL

### caroline — 2012-07-15T10:48:49+0300

Did you lose at all on Leptin RX? I am just in the middle of a short round and also wondering how to proceed with leptin RX. When I did the reset before - I went up a couple of pounds. I think I have to cut out the bacon - because of salt/nitrates. At least that is my gut feeling.

> **caroline;38170 wrote:** Did you lose at all on Leptin RX? I am just in the middle of a short round and also wondering how to proceed with leptin RX. When I did the reset before - I went up a couple of pounds. I think I have to cut out the bacon - because of salt/nitrates. At least that is my gut feeling.

Salt is never a problem on weight gain, more CW. It's an underlying issue not the sodium. Now, other chemicals in processed foods, which are also often high in salt make one swell, but salt isn't the culprit - it's just been demonized.

So I did lose a bit of weight on the Leptin RX/Paleo Stuff. I thought it was fairly minimal, but got compliments and noticed. I changed a couple of sizes and tightened up as much as I was going to get.

The really amazing thing was I didn't GAIN weight on that way of eating, all while eating to satiety and definately NOT dieting. That was a huge thing for moi.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-16T01:02:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-best-practices-for-transitioning-from-p2-p3.2275/post-38157>

> **Cavemam;38312 wrote:** SNIP ... The really amazing thing was I didn't GAIN weight on that way of eating, all while eating to satiety and definately NOT dieting. That was a huge thing for moi.

That would also be me. I cannot believe how much fat I was chowing down on esp when I took to drinking BPC cause I drink it with FOUR TBS of butter. I WAS using 8TBS when I first started drinking it that way (hahaha I was telling myself that 4 TBS was 4 ounces. Um, did the part of my brain that handles math go on vacation??)

for me I had begun to wonder if I just needed a boot in the hypothalamus to get this moving. which is why I'm on a 21 day round. We'll see how it goes. I plan on moving off the hcg and back onto ketogenic paleo, not necessarily back on the reset because if I lose like I hope I will it will me I have definitely returned to leptin sensitivity.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-16T01:10:06+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-best-practices-for-transitioning-from-p2-p3.2275/post-38248>

# Thread: Pins and needles?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pins-and-needles.2282/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sem — 2012-07-09T09:12:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pins-and-needles.2282/post-36295>

Hi all - I've been doing the Leptin RX for the past month or so but fell off the wagon the past few days. I've been experiencing really annoying pins and needles or prickly feeling all over my body. I had this a few weeks ago too. Do you think there's any relation or just some other random weird thing going on? Thanks.

### Destiny — 2012-07-09T13:10:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pins-and-needles.2282/post-36303>

> **Sem;36461 wrote:** Hi all - I've been doing the Leptin RX for the past month or so but fell off the wagon the past few days. I've been experiencing really annoying pins and needles or prickly feeling all over my body. I had this a few weeks ago too. Do you think there's any relation or just some other random weird thing going on? Thanks.

Sem, a fried of mine was experiencing the same thing. Dr. K said that she needs to see a doctor. If you search it, it says that it can be many things - neuropathy, diabetes, MS, hypothyroid, etc.

### Sem — 2012-07-10T06:39:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pins-and-needles.2282/post-36473>

Thanks Destiny. My OB/GYN reminded me today that I had a similar complaint before when my hormones were wacky and I had no estrogen. She is going to do a full panel once I get my period and hit day 18. She ordered progesterone so I get my period since its been more than two months! Oy.

### Destiny — 2012-07-10T13:05:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pins-and-needles.2282/post-36493>

> **Sem;36639 wrote:** Thanks Destiny. My OB/GYN reminded me today that I had a similar complaint before when my hormones were wacky and I had no estrogen. She is going to do a full panel once I get my period and hit day 18. She ordered progesterone so I get my period since its been more than two months! Oy.

That is good that you are on it! Let's know how that goes!

### 2bls — 2012-07-12T20:54:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pins-and-needles.2282/post-37233>

interesting that this is mentioned, I have been experiencing something similar; a sort of vibration;humming feeling throughout my body; almost like micro-tremors for about 2 months now. I also have a very hard pulse at times. It's much worse if I'm feeling stressed or upset or really tired. The only thing that is giving me any relief from it at all is to take Holy Basil and Relora as soon as I wake up and again before bed. It doesn't eliminate it completely but at least I can function as I need to. I'm planning to get a full work-up by a Dr. Michael Rahman a Naturopathic Doctor and Microbiologist. I need to save some $$$ first as the consult alone is about $400. In the meantime, I'll stay with the Holy Basil and Relora with maybe some other adrenal support things thrown in. I've sort of concluded its very high Cortisol and or Pregnenolone Steal syndrome...but then again, I could be completely off base.

As far as Leptin RX - its going very well, I have dropped 12 lbs in the last 7 weeks without doing anything but the Leptin RX rules...

# Thread: Help with labs I have and ones to run

### SeaJane — 2012-07-09T17:39:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-labs-i-have-and-ones-to-run.2286/post-36322>

I am new here and am trying to sort out some labs I have and also have a question about ones to run.

First, I am 51 years old, pre-menopausal. I am 5ft 5in and weigh 121lbs. I am gluten and dairy free and have been mostly grain free for 3 years. The last 3 months, I have started some mix of leptin reset, auto-immune diet so I don't snack, eat between 35 -50 grams of protein in the morning, no fruit, no eggs, carbs approx 25 - 30g/day.

I was diagnosed with Graves disease (autoimmune hyperthyroidism) in the fall of 2008. I was on meds approx 6 months before becoming euthryroid. Last summer I was feeling hypothyroid and had labs run. At that time, my FT3 was low (1.8 range 2.3-4.2) although my FT4 was 1.14 (range 0.75-1.76) and TSH was 0.76 (range 0.3 - 2.5). FT4 and TSH were comparable to what mine have been when I was feeling good so only FT3 was low. We re-ran labs including thyroid antibodies last July. The results were FT3 2.0, FT4 1.16, TSH 0.86, and all my anti-bodies were high (in fact higher than when I was diagnosed with Graves) Anti-Thyroglubulin 1559 (

### fitness@home — 2012-07-09T20:48:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-labs-i-have-and-ones-to-run.2286/post-36369>

Jane,

I just had the Genova Diagnostics Complete Hormones test done recently. It is a 24-HR urine collection test and not inexpensive by any means. On the other hand, it gives a LOT of information. Glad I had it done - it will provide a lot of info for my consult. A doctor has to order it for you.

In my early 50s also and working toward getting my hormones to the perfect place for me...

### JanSz — 2012-07-09T22:58:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-labs-i-have-and-ones-to-run.2286/post-36390>

**PSJane;36488 wrote:** I am new here and am trying to sort out some labs I have and also have a question about ones to run.

First, I am 51 years old, pre-menopausal. I am 5ft 5in and weigh 121lbs. I am gluten and dairy free and have been mostly grain free for 3 years. The last 3 months, I have started some mix of leptin reset, auto-immune diet so I don't snack, eat between 35 -50 grams of protein in the morning, no fruit, no eggs, carbs approx 25 - 30g/day.

I was diagnosed with Graves disease (autoimmune hyperthyroidism) in the fall of 2008. I was on meds approx 6 months before becoming euthryroid. Last summer I was feeling hypothyroid and had labs run. At that time, my FT3 was low (1.8 range 2.3-4.2) although my FT4 was 1.14 (range 0.75-1.76) and TSH was 0.76 (range 0.3 - 2.5). FT4 and TSH were comparable to what mine have been when I was feeling good so only FT3 was low. We re-ran labs including thyroid antibodies last July. The results were FT3 2.0, FT4 1.16, TSH 0.86, and all my anti-bodies were high (in fact higher than when I was diagnosed with Graves) Anti-Thyroglubulin 1559 (>Spectracell Comprehensive Nutritional Panel

[http://www.healthremedies.com/intracellular\_vitamin\_analysis\_spectracell.html](http://http://www.healthremedies.com/intracellular_vitamin_analysis_spectracell.html)

\======================

Get this test

Get it either via dr's script or buy it on your own.

Repeat this process 2x/year until all is right, then 1x/year

Essential & Metabolic Fatty Acids Analysis (EMFA) by Genova Diagnostics

[http://www.healthremedies.com/essential\_metabolic\_fatty\_acids\_analysis\_emfa\_genova\_diagnostics.html](http://http://www.healthremedies.com/essential_metabolic_fatty_acids_analysis_emfa_genova_diagnostics.html)

Attention;

test results are ok

test conclusions, often may not be ok

read more on my post #1 in this thread, here:

start with:

Why 4:1 Ratio Oil?

Why 4:1 Ratio Oil?

Goal is (LA/ALA)=(4:1) O6/O3

Goal is (LA/ALA)=(4:1) O6/O3

Highly abbreviated version;

eat fish but not fish oil

eat krill but not krill oil

When you read post #1 including what is under links, you will find your self in very familiar territory of dr Kruse (at least that is how it was meant to be.

\======================

There is more, but you have to start somewhere.

# Thread: Thinking of sharing my very crazy road
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-sharing-my-very-crazy-road.2287/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ealachan — 2012-07-09T17:50:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-sharing-my-very-crazy-road.2287/post-36324>

I would be interested in hearing more of your story, of course...but I think you already know that.

### vlynnb — 2012-07-09T18:37:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-sharing-my-very-crazy-road.2287/post-36333>

Please share your road with us. I have always been mindful of the current conventional wisdom on health and nutrition and my late husband, who was a busy family practice physician who died young of cancer, was of course very interested. I tried to live as healthful a life as possible (more than a little perfectionism imposed on others in the process - one of my children, in all seriousness and with no manipulative motives, told me that he didn't feel loved because I didn't buy cupboards full of junk food like other mothers). And now, what is practical versus optimal? I know Dr. K. has addressed that but I am still struggling. My family thinks I am OCD , which I am not, but they are still disillusioned if I fall off the path to my optimal.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-09T18:39:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-sharing-my-very-crazy-road.2287/post-36334>

I know this is very cliche but if you inspire one person, isn't it worth it? I think so and look forward to reading it myself!

### vlynnb — 2012-07-09T20:08:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-sharing-my-very-crazy-road.2287/post-36358>

Just read this on the How Do We Fix Our Thinking thread (I think). I think it will help me to not ruminate over how I could have done things differently:

From Dr. K.

> Just sitting outside on the dock finishing up the days final consult watching an amazing sunset develop. Seeing this sunset is so dynamic like our lives should be. It doesn’t matter where you are right now, you are nowhere compared to where you might go. No one can go back and start a new beginning at life, but any one of us can start today and make a new ending to our life.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-09T22:21:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thinking-of-sharing-my-very-crazy-road.2287/post-36384>

Thanks to some good advice, I have left out a LOT of fun life details. . . if I ever do a book, but here is the 1st half nitty gritty with photos.

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2377-A-fat-kid-s-tale](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2377-A-fat-kid-s-tale)

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-09T17:58:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sale-on-steak-patties-costco.2288/post-36326>

Hi All,

Saw this today. . .looks like a good deal. I always question how properly raised cattle can come in any quantities to supply a place like Costco, so my little red flag went off a little bit.

I am however going to order some steaks and patties, those little packages thaw quick and will make quick meals easier.

[http://www.costco.com/Browse/Product.aspx?prodid=11757332&whse=BC&topnav=&cm\_sp=RichRelevance-\_-categorypageHorizontalTop-\_-PopularProductsInCategory&cm\_vc=categorypageHorizontalTop|PopularProductsInCategory](http://http://www.costco.com/Browse/Product.aspx?prodid=11757332&whse=BC&topnav=&cm_sp=RichRelevance-_-categorypageHorizontalTop-_-PopularProductsInCategory&cm_vc=categorypageHorizontalTop|PopularProductsInCategory)

they probably were grass fed - but they probably aren't grass-finished. which is how Costco gets the quantities. of course - maybe they're buying from suppliers similar to US wellness....but I doubt that....

### txritchies@att.net — 2012-07-09T18:30:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sale-on-steak-patties-costco.2288/post-36332>

Gotta love Google...

[http://sommersorganic.com/default.asp](http://http://sommersorganic.com/default.asp)

Looks like the Sprouts in our area carries this product too.

### kathylu — 2012-07-09T19:02:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sale-on-steak-patties-costco.2288/post-36340>

Great article on how the big beef companies source, raise and finish their meat. It looks like Sommers Organic is ok (beef is grass finished), except for the fact that the country of origin is Argentina and Uruguay. If you are concerned about carbon footprint, this beef travels 9,000 miles to reach us. I prefer to be a locavore, but I can understand the advantages of buying this product. I get my beef from a farmer 20 miles down the road.

[http://santacruzwire.com/index.php/food/24-business/198-buying-beef-in-santa-cruz.html](http://http://santacruzwire.com/index.php/food/24-business/198-buying-beef-in-santa-cruz.html)

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-08T20:24:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sale-on-steak-patties-costco.2288/post-43701>

Reporting back on this. . .we have LOVED these. The steaks are super easy to deal with. They are thin! So what we do is season them and leave them sitting out for about 10 mins while preparing other stuff, then toss em on the hot grill. Because the inside is still cold/frozen it works PERFECT! They are seared yet not overcooked.

The beef patties are similarly easy to deal with. It's nice having a food on hand that can be pulled out of the freezer and prepared right then.

My only complaint is the insane amount of packaging (as is everything at costco) and I hate having things in plastic. . .storing my food in paper and glass is probably too much to ask of them for now. . . so I'll just enjoy the ease until then and not think of the hormonal disruption!
