# Thread: The Waiting Game
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Souldanzer — 2012-07-09T21:14:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36374>

\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-09T22:00:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36379>

worry about what you can control... that's all I can say... my job revolves around the mantra of "hurry up and wait" I've been doing that for over 20yrs now. you will always wait on someone else to get you what you need to get things done... its more frustrating when you see the path before you...but others that you need on the path aren't quite there yet.....

### johnnyb — 2012-07-09T22:59:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36391>

...but I'm waiting as fast as I can.

### caroline — 2012-07-10T08:54:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36479>

At least you are taking lots of positive steps and one day soon it will start falling into place. At least you are taking action - I know so many really unhealthy people who leave everything to their Doc and they are getting nowhere. You are moving toward optimum - just not as fast as you want but you will get there if you persist.

> **Souldanzer;36540 wrote:** How do you guys deal with waiting.
>
>
>
> I feel like I'm constantly waiting. Waiting for this test, waiting for that approval, waiting for appointments, waiting for docs to come back from vacation, waiting for someone to call me back,.... being put on hold.
>
>
>
> I'm tired of the waiting. My life's going by while I wait for things to happen. While I sit at home waiting to feel better. I don't feel well enough to do purposeful stuff or maybe I just can't come up with things. I can't even read a friggin' book.
>
>
>
> I know sometimes we have to do the uncomfortable in the now to get to the bigger goal. I feel like I'm missing out  With AN at least I had learned to blame myself.... but this.... nobody left to blame.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-10T17:39:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36551>

> **johnnyb;36557 wrote:** ...but I'm waiting as fast as I can.

I'm trying really hard!!

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-10T17:46:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36553>

\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`\`

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-10T18:01:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36559>

> **Souldanzer;36719 wrote:** Thx, Shijin.
>
>
>
> I don't know if I agree with that. I don't want to spend my life waiting for things. I want to spend my life doing and accomplishing stuff. And I don't want to be frustrated all the time, either. Part of the problem here is that my life's so limited. Maybe I need to work on removing the limitations. Maybe I need to reconsider my attitude towards rx meds?
>
>
>
> I'm scared of meds and for a reason. Had I taken everything a doc ever prescribed me OMG I'd be in such a mess. I'm afraid if I give in taking meds with the hopes of feeling better and being more able to work on other things than just my health, I'm going to get stuck in the medicated place.... instead of finding true health.

I can see where you're coming from... but sometimes we have to wait for a reason, and that reason may not be clear to us at the time. You can accomplish things that you can control. IMO I think your frustration is coming from the lack of responsiveness you think you should see from the medical community. You know what you want to do and yet you can't do it w/o them, but it takes 6wks to get an appointment to determine if the doc you're seeing is the one to help you implement your plan... yeah its frustrating. Its a scream at the top of your lungs and throw things frustrating. Work on the things YOU CONTROL. Accept that you don't control everything. That's one of the hardest lessons I've ever learned... and I'm still learning it w/DH and his health.

as far as meds - go with your gut on that. Its taken me 10yrs to get to where I'm at regarding the control of my health and finding the right doctors. It doesn't happen overnight - the old mantra sometimes the best things are worth the wait is true. I think we've forgotten the practice of patience in this digital age, of immediacy. we want and expect things to happen as soon as we figure out what we want - but that's not how life works.

hang in there. you'll get there at the right time and place with the right doc.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-10T18:06:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36561>

thing is how much can I really accomplish in the meantime with a hurting head and a non-functioning brain..... get my point? Maybe it needs to be the meds or at least a trial of it then. And that IS under my control.

I'm also wondering how much "ill" I've grown accustomed to. When I tell a new doc I've been having a rather severe headache for 4 years now and am not using pain medication, they kinda look at me like I've lost it. It's become my life.

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-10T18:20:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36567>

> **Souldanzer;36727 wrote:** thing is how much can I really accomplish in the meantime with a hurting head and a non-functioning brain..... get my point? Maybe it needs to be the meds or at least a trial of it then. And that IS under my control.
>
>
>
> I'm also wondering how much "ill" I've grown accustomed to. When I tell a new doc I've been having a rather severe headache for 4 years now and am not using pain medication, they kinda look at me like I've lost it. It's become my life.

How much of that is b/c they expect people to ask for medication to stop the pain. You are so out side their "Normal" patient they're puzzled, intrigued, fascinated. They might even think you're crazy.

I do understand that functioning w/pain is hard - what it comes down to is what is your trade off? some functioning, no functioning, or fully functional. only you can make that decision. and it will be a decision every one here will support you in, even if some don't agree - we're not walking in your shoes and can't make decisions for you... you'll figure it out..and what ever you decide it will be the correct decision for you at that given moment in time.... no one can take that away from you

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-10T19:12:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36578>

Thanks, Shijin. I know I have to make that decision. I'm also becoming super aware of how communication gets missed as I am reading medical records occasionally these days. Every visit pretty much there is a wrong impression of what I am there for and what my goals are.

I just received the records from my second opinion cards and she just got it all wrong. I just don't want to end up on the conveyer belt, ya' know. I've worked so hard to avoid that and feel that if I give in to meds, that's where it's going to take me. And on the other hand, maybe a lifetime of meds would be better than what I have.

I know only I can decide this.... I think I will discuss this with the new neurologist though that apt is 10 days away.

### janagram — 2012-07-10T19:17:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36581>

I like your inquiry...you don't take anything for granted. Keep inquiring into what you want your life to look like, feel like, etc. Right now, you're taking care of yourself, and it's an important job. Wishing you the best!

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-10T20:15:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36600>

Thx, j. yes, sometimes I forget that what I'm doing is important. I get trapped in what I don't have.

### primat — 2012-07-11T13:56:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36774>

I'm also unvoluntarily playing the waiting game, and feel for you.

I can't seem to get access to my brain at all anymore, which is insanely frustrating since I know I'm really not stupid. It's been two months since my MRI came out clean and the endo have failed to call back or contact either me or my regular doctor. Now it's a month vaccation or two. In six months the sick leave payment stops and I can enjoy a 60% cut in income. The added stress of all this is weighing me down in itself. I am back in the saddle again on controlling the binge eating, but right now it feels like it's completely futile.

'Then it comes to be the soothing light at the end of the tunnel is just a freight train coming your way'

/rant

### Dali Dula — 2012-07-11T15:01:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36785>

I don't know your condition or limitations, but while i am waiting for all those frustrating things I put into practice the things I safely can. Start walking if you can handle that. Apply all of JK's protocols if you can and haven't done so. Set goals and knock them down. You don't have to wait to achieve goals even if they are small. Small victories can be inspiring. Do not wait for the rest of the world. Don't let yourself become a victim of the system. I am no fan of rx, but sometimes you do need them as a bridge as long as you are headed in the right direction. Treat yourself well and keep moving. Wish you the best.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-11T19:05:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36859>

> **primat;36940 wrote:** I'm also unvoluntarily playing the waiting game, and feel for you.
>
>
>
> I can't seem to get access to my brain at all anymore, which is insanely frustrating since I know I'm really not stupid. It's been two months since my MRI came out clean and the endo have failed to call back or contact either me or my regular doctor. Now it's a month vaccation or two. In six months the sick leave payment stops and I can enjoy a 60% cut in income. The added stress of all this is weighing me down in itself. I am back in the saddle again on controlling the binge eating, but right now it feels like it's completely futile.
>
>
>
> 'Then it comes to be the soothing light at the end of the tunnel is just a freight train coming your way'
>
>
>
> /rant

When something like this happens I become PITA patient. You've got to call and call again until they react. Or find a different doc. 2 months vacation is not acceptable in my case. They've got patients and they need to make sure they're taken care of while they disappear. Now \*I\* was complaining about my doc being gone for a couple of days  That's different. Speaks to my impatience.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-11T19:16:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36864>

> **Dali Dula;36951 wrote:** I don't know your condition or limitations, but while i am waiting for all those frustrating things I put into practice the things I safely can. Start walking if you can handle that. Apply all of JK's protocols if you can and haven't done so. Set goals and knock them down. You don't have to wait to achieve goals even if they are small. Small victories can be inspiring. Do not wait for the rest of the world. Don't let yourself become a victim of the system. I am no fan of rx, but sometimes you do need them as a bridge as long as you are headed in the right direction. Treat yourself well and keep moving. Wish you the best.

Thanks DD. What you say rings true and that's how I've been approaching things for the last few months. Hardcore CT, eating super clean AI, turning off the lights. I think part of the problem is that I am starting to feel better (really?) and so I want to do more and now I start to feel limited. But looking at it from a different perspective, that would be a good development

It's hard to set goals and knock them down if your brain isn't functioning correctly. My brain has gotten better a bit now and the only change I've made really is supplemental O2 which my docs are telling me I don't need. There's only so much you can fix if your brain isn't getting the nutrition it needs.... know what I mean? I think I might have reached that point.

I got a good night's sleep last night (11hrs! - restarted a small dose of DHEA) and I'm not quite as impatient anymore today. Onto more small victories while I prepare for the bigger ones

### BJK77 — 2012-07-11T19:40:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36874>

Hey Soul,

I can totally relate to what you're saying. Patient has never been a word used to describe me

You're obviously dealing with different challenges than I am, but what's working for me as I wait and wait to get test results, hear back from the doc, or just wait the 6 weeks it takes to get into my doctor is to fine tune my "plan of attack!" I read and re-read Dr. K's blogs that apply to my situation and highlight areas that I need to focus on or that hit home for me.

I'm a bit anal and a total list maker, so I'll jot down what I need to be doing right now (Ct, leptin rx, light cycle, etc) and what my next steps are going to be (additional testing, supplements, etc). It helps me feel like I'm moving forward while I'm waiting and then I find I feel more ready and prepared when I get into the doc next.

Yep, I'm simple minded and you're probably already doing this, but thought I'd put it out there anyway!

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-11T19:48:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-36879>

Hey BJ, thanks for the input. My stickies are out of control!!! They're everywhere, electronically on my Mac, all over my coffee table, in my bag, on my fridge, .... Lol. My brain needs a little help  Fortunately, some of my planning skills are slowly reappearing, which is, to say the least, ridiculously helpful.

I'd like to spend quite a bit of time re-reading stuff on the blog but the state of my cognitive function limits that. One reason I'm considering going on meds.

I'm also constantly working on perfecting everything else.... more shellfish, more sun, more cold, more barefooting in the dirt. Now I just have to learn to get off the computer

Btw, I think I found my spunk for life.... now I need to create the body to live it!!

### Lyndra — 2012-07-12T02:50:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-37020>

> **Souldanzer wrote:**
>
>
>
> Btw, I think I found my spunk for life.... now I need to create the body to live it!!

Yay!

Here's a quote I stumbled across that a friend sent me when I was going through a tough time. Maybe some will resonate with you.

_1\. Make no judgements.

2\. Have no expectations.

3\. Give up the need to know why things happen as they do.

4\. Trust that the unscheduled events of our lives are a form of spiritual direction.

5\. Have the courage to make the choices we need to make, and accept what we cannot change.

\- from Anatomy of the Spirit, The Seven Stages of Power and Healing, by Caroline Myss

_

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-12T20:25:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-waiting-game.2293/post-37224>

Thanks lyndra. I was able to let go of the waiting for today. Making the best out of my day or at least trying to.

# Thread: FAT - where do you get yours?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-where-do-you-get-yours.2314/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### vkiernan — 2012-07-10T14:16:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-where-do-you-get-yours.2314/post-36496>

I have to do a Metametrics GI Effects test and am not supposed to do coconut oil 3 days prior. My problem is I am not supposed to do dairy and that leaves butter out. How am I supposed to get enough fat in? I have fried up bacon but that only gives me a limited amount to work with. Looking for ideas. Thanks all.

### BJK77 — 2012-07-10T15:11:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-where-do-you-get-yours.2314/post-36511>

How about lard, tallow, or palm oil? All are good to use for sauteeing veggies or just dumping on meat.

### vkiernan — 2012-07-10T15:23:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-where-do-you-get-yours.2314/post-36520>

I will be thrilled to be able to use ghee. I forgot about lard. I actually have some. Got to get some of that red palm oil and try it. Thanks.

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-10T15:30:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-where-do-you-get-yours.2314/post-36525>

> **Jack Kruse;36679 wrote:** or ghee.......

2nd the ghee.... I make my own w/Kerry Gold grass feed butter - I've got the process down pat... [http://www.aayisrecipes.com/2007/05/14/how-to-make-ghee/](http://http://www.aayisrecipes.com/2007/05/14/how-to-make-ghee/)

its pretty easy - and way effing cheaper than buying it at the store. IMO Kerry Gold unsalted grassfed butter makes the best ghee - I've used Organic Valley cultured butter - its good but not as good as KErry Gold ghee.... the kids actually ask for spoonfuls of ghee initially DH was hesitant to let them eat it... I told him - if they're asking for it, there's a reason why - let them eat ghee!

### SeaHorse — 2012-07-10T16:19:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-where-do-you-get-yours.2314/post-36541>

I'm a big duck fat fan...you can buy it in small tubs in the freezer at my natural food store. 1-800-Dartagnan I add it to some of the grassfed hamburger that doesn't have enough fat. YUM

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-10T17:00:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-where-do-you-get-yours.2314/post-36543>

Duck Fat and Ghee! The ghee I use is [http://www.amazon.com/Purity-Farm-Organic-Clarified-13-Ounce/dp/B0046IIPMW/ref=sr\_1\_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1341928759&sr=8-2&keywords=ghee+purity](http://http://www.amazon.com/Purity-Farm-Organic-Clarified-13-Ounce/dp/B0046IIPMW/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1341928759&sr=8-2&keywords=ghee+purity). They say theirs is casein free and comes from grass-fed cows.

# Thread: +++Help Please Osteo+Neuro+locking jaws
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LilyJ — 2012-07-10T19:33:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36588>

I'm hoping some of you have been able to heal your medical issues and can send me in the right direction with labs and supplements. My history ---fully menopausal since 41 (I am 47 currently), history of full body auto-immune reactions incuding iritis, knee's sound like I'm walking on broken glass, osteopenia (neck reading was almost full osteoporosis a couple of years ago), hands are starting to shake a lot (even without coffee). In the last 3 months my jaws are starting to catch and I can't lift my left arm out to the side without discomfort. I have had pneumonia once and pleurisy off and on over the last 4 yrs. I have been LC for 12 years and have managed to gain 30lbs over the last 5 yrs. I am 5'3 165lbs currently. My vitamin D is low but I am unable to take supplements without severe bone pain. I have just started taking magnesium citramate 150-300mg, potassium citrate 200-400mg and Krill oil 1000mg. I take no other medications. Working around the house is really my only form of exercise. Any comments or suggestions would be very appreciated. Thanks!

### LilyJ — 2012-07-10T19:35:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36589>

I forgot to mention that my fingers have looked like sausages the last 3 months.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-10T21:59:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36621>

Welcome LilyJ! I would say more magnesium, to start with. Magnesium threonate if you can get it, and/or magnesium malate. I take 1000 mg of magnesium daily, but I had to gradually build up to this level. I take a combination of various magnesium chelates, but mostly mg. malate.

You are low carb... Have you eliminated all grains? Especially gluten in your diet? That stuff really attacks the joints.

I like the krill oil you're taking, that should also help.

### LilyJ — 2012-07-10T23:00:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36624>

I basically eat beef, a veggy here and there and an apply or blueberries once or twice a week. I eat no grains, rice or potatoes. I eat eggs also but have cut them back recently to see if that would help with the inflammation. I used to be able to eat ground beef for every meal and the inflammation would go away but that hasn't been working for me now. I will look at other forms of magnesium. Thanks Nonchalant

### Joann — 2012-07-10T23:26:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36625>

Do you use BHRT? Read Dr K blog on autoimmune ......no eggs, bone broth. also anti-inflammatory supplements like curcumin etc and I think blood tests for your own info to see whats up?

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-11T01:11:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36668>

Sounds like autoimmunity and inflammation... and I'd be focussed on the gut (and HUGELY tied to light cycles and their importance). Have you read this blog as a starter? [http://jackkruse.com/could-it-be-the-gut/](http://http://jackkruse.com/could-it-be-the-gut/) You might also want a consult pronto and a lot of testing done with your doc... DHEA levels, hsCRP, vitamin D, thyroid and hormones, etc.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-11T01:14:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36671>

Other good blog posts:

[http://jackkruse.com/why-leaky-guts-lead-to-ms/](http://http://jackkruse.com/why-leaky-guts-lead-to-ms/)

[http://jackkruse.com/the-sunshine-of-your-life/](http://http://jackkruse.com/the-sunshine-of-your-life/)

### LilyJ — 2012-07-11T23:15:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36948>

I haven't used BHRT. For several years I thought I would never use them and didn't mind the thought of aging gracefully but I feel as though I'm entering a point of no return with the inflammation causing joint destruction and/or some sort of cartilage disorder. I will look into curcumin. I did pick up some K2 and resveratrol today.

### LilyJ — 2012-07-11T23:26:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36952>

I haven't read the gut posts but I will do it today. Now that I think about it, in 1990, six months prior to the first autoimmune attack (the dr at that time said the sed rate was worse than someone that was 80 yrs old with severe arthritis) I contracted C Diff after a few days of cefuroxime treatment. I suppose that's when it all started. Thanks.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-12T00:20:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-36981>

> **LilyJ;37118 wrote:** I haven't read the gut posts but I will do it today. Now that I think about it, in 1990, six months prior to the first autoimmune attack (the dr at that time said the sed rate was worse than someone that was 80 yrs old with severe arthritis) I contracted C Diff after a few days of cefuroxime treatment. I suppose that's when it all started. Thanks.

If I understand the mechanism correctly, it all started with stress of some sort. Then when you got C Diff, your genes seized the opportunity, shuffled the deck, and unfortunately you ended up with an AI issue. You might want to consider a consult with Dr. K., as KiwiLauren mentioned. You're at the right place, Lily. Hope you can find relief soon.

### LilyJ — 2012-07-13T02:59:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-please-osteo-neuro-locking-jaws.2324/post-37361>

I remembered something else...shortly before hitting menopause at 41yo I did a liquid diet for 6 weeks...ugh!

# Thread: 5 garden weeds you a can eat
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5-garden-weeds-you-a-can-eat.2326/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-07-10T20:23:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5-garden-weeds-you-a-can-eat.2326/post-36602>

[http://www.livescience.com/21496-5-edible-garden-weeds.html](http://http://www.livescience.com/21496-5-edible-garden-weeds.html)

> Has the heat killed your garden and left nothing but weeds? Then why not eat those weeds?
>
>
>
> Last year at this time we reported on five healthy weeds likely growing in or near your yard: dandelion, plantain, purslane, lamb's-quarters, and stinging nettles. Each one of these is a tasty powerhouse of nutrition. Raw purslane, a rich source of omega-3 fatty acids, offers a perfect tart punch to any smoothie; lamb's-quarters, one of the most nutritionally dense foods known, cooks in seconds in a stir-fry and has a nutty spinach taste.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-10T20:30:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/5-garden-weeds-you-a-can-eat.2326/post-36604>

# Thread: Yellow pee on DHEA?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yellow-pee-on-dhea.2327/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-07-10T21:10:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yellow-pee-on-dhea.2327/post-36616>

I am taking :

no b vitamins outside of meat

k2... natto kinase... maybe I need something else

zinc

selenium

iodide

7 keto

no carnitine for two days

no ala for two days

no marijuana milkshake for two days

added dhea four days ago

ate liver two days in a row

edit: actually I'm taking pantothenic acid also... kind of made from b vitamins.

[http://wikidoc.org/index.php/Human\_urine](http://http://wikidoc.org/index.php/Human_urine)

Colour

**The typical bright yellow colour of urine is caused by the pigment urochrome, but also from the degradation products of **bilirubin and urobilin**.** Clear colors are a sign of hydration and are the preferred colors of urine.

I know I'm drinking a gallon of liquid a day. I don't think its the typical bright yellow pigment urochrome because of all the water. It seems the other two color causers would be detox... Any thoughts or experience would be appreciated.

### chocolate — 2012-07-10T23:34:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/yellow-pee-on-dhea.2327/post-36628>

I guess the liver is B-Vitamins...I couldn't have imagined so much...maybe I just ate too much.

# Thread: Absolutely LOVING the kippers...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/absolutely-loving-the-kippers.2330/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jmathisen — 2012-07-10T22:14:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/absolutely-loving-the-kippers.2330/post-36623>

I eat sardines quite a bit, and enjoy them, but somebody picked up some kippers for me at the local market. OMG! Delish!

Not as high in the O-3/O-6 as the sardines, but the flavor was awesome!

Something that delish has probably got to be not good for me. I'd eat kippers over chocolate.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-10T23:28:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/absolutely-loving-the-kippers.2330/post-36626>

Thanks for the idea, jmathisen. I used to adore sardines, but since I'm eating seafood every day, I'm starting to get critical of their taste. Perhaps it's the olive oil they are canned in. I'll try the kippers; I've actually purchased a tin of them but they have been languishing in my pantry.

### ealachan — 2012-07-10T23:37:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/absolutely-loving-the-kippers.2330/post-36629>

Nonchalant, have you tried water-packed sardines? I tried water-packed first, then oil-packed, and I far prefer the water-packed ones!

### jmathisen — 2012-07-11T00:35:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/absolutely-loving-the-kippers.2330/post-36657>

Hi Nonchalant...

I pour the sardines out on a papertowel, pat 'em "dry", slice 'em, and then sprinkle tabasco sauce on them. Once the oil is gone, they're pretty good.

Also slice 'em, stir 'em in with a bit of spicy hot sausage and some tabasco and eggs and make a scramble. Mucho goodo.

I even found kippers at the 7-11 24 hour market, so I'm really set when I have to work late and forgot to pack protein.

### zoebarry@yahoo.com — 2012-07-12T22:49:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/absolutely-loving-the-kippers.2330/post-37260>

You've inspired me to purchase my first can of kippers yesterday! I will let you know if I like them!

Grace

### nonchalant — 2012-07-12T23:42:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/absolutely-loving-the-kippers.2330/post-37284>

> **luvmyzoe;37427 wrote:** You've inspired me to purchase my first can of kippers yesterday!

I bet our friends in England are laughing at us!

### SeaHorse — 2012-07-12T23:54:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/absolutely-loving-the-kippers.2330/post-37289>

Bar Harbour brand makes fabulous kippers with cracked black pepper!

### jmathisen — 2012-07-13T11:21:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/absolutely-loving-the-kippers.2330/post-37446>

My favorite is the smoked ones, but so far, I like 'em all.

### kathylu — 2012-07-14T17:20:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/absolutely-loving-the-kippers.2330/post-37764>

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-10T23:37:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-dhea-7-keto-dhea-are-you-using-and-like.2331/post-36630>

I am looking for a new brand of DHEA and 7-KETO DHEA. I have been using LEF but want to change because of the rice flour. I would prefer pharmaceutical grade and micronized but would love to hear everyone's suggestions anyway!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-11T01:20:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-dhea-7-keto-dhea-are-you-using-and-like.2331/post-36672>

Maybe I'm going a little overboard and the micronized and pharmaceutical. Maybe I just need a good brand with as little fillers as possible. I've seen the NutraBio for the regular DHEA. Does anyone know of a pure powder for the 7-keto version?

### SeaHorse — 2012-07-11T16:14:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-dhea-7-keto-dhea-are-you-using-and-like.2331/post-36809>

I would be interested in finding out some websites for super clean supplements too...I think Souldanzer knew of one, but I can't find the thread. I tossed a bunch of stuff a while ago because of the fillers etc. and I can't find good replacements locally.

There is a Nature's Plus DHEA (micronized) that has silica and veg.cellulose only, no mag.stearate. I don't know about the cellulose...do you? I'm not taking it yet, but I bought it anyway.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-dhea-7-keto-dhea-are-you-using-and-like.2331/post-36834>

I know that when taking thyroid medication the cellulose can hinder the absorption, on some people. I would really rather just get the straight powder but in pill form. I hate taking pills but the powder is something I end up forgetting to take!

Check out Nutrabio for clean supps. [http://www.nutrabio.com/Products/dhea.htm#.T\_2WvnAhJws](http://http://www.nutrabio.com/Products/dhea.htm#.T_2WvnAhJws) I think that is one Souldanzer used but wish I had info on other sites as well.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-12T05:04:17+0300

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-12T05:07:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-dhea-7-keto-dhea-are-you-using-and-like.2331/post-37072>

From what I read it looks like the transdermal makes the DHEA more absorbable:

_Most DHEA taken orally is eliminated as soon as it enters the system from the digestive tract by the first pass effect of the liver. Most of the effective DHEA from oral supplementation is never available to the body to do any good. Very little ends up being available to the body to utilize as a basic building block or pro-hormone._

Most DHEA metabolism occurs in the skin. Dr. Fernand Labrie and a team of Canadian researchers published an important study in February 2008 that showed this. Most DHEA is actually used by the body in the biggest organ we have – the skin, or dermis.

I found this one: [http://store.twist25.com/](http://http://store.twist25.com/)

I want to be able to make my own and I think it's totally doable. The one above has 50mg/cc.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-12T05:38:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-dhea-7-keto-dhea-are-you-using-and-like.2331/post-37086>

> Sucks for sure either way on the heart or brain. I sure hope you find that path very soon so you can get on with your life. Dumb question, but how does the heart affect your hormones? I am trying to think this out but can't.

My preg wasn't trashed but everything else. I stared at it and couldn't make sense of it. So this was the explanation. I'm pretty sure my preg probably was trashed at some point in time but is recovering now. That would be good  It also means that I'm still making T3 b/c that's required for conversion of LDL to preg. Which is even better

My heart is affecting my brain b/c there's a hole that allows the old blood coming from the brain to mix with the fresh blood going to the brain. That means my O2 in my blood to my brain is lower than it should be b/c of the "used" blood it's mixed with. Brains need lots of O2 to function well. If they don't get it, neurons go dormant or die. So basically, I have a half dormant brain b/c of low O2 and that part of my brain isn't functioning and making no hormones and such. The old blood that goes back to my brain also has toxins that should not be going back to my brain - and that causes severe headaches. Am I making sense?

### BJK77 — 2012-07-12T16:59:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-dhea-7-keto-dhea-are-you-using-and-like.2331/post-37163>

This preg/DHEA stuff has me so confused. I've been supplementing fairly high doses after my consult with Dr. K. I was only taking 50 mg DHEA and he said I could go as high as 3-4 times that amount. I did so fairly quickly and my sleep and energy massively improved, but now I'm back to feeling super fatigued with some serious muscle weakness again . . . just can't figure this all out.

I'm taking the regular DHEA - micronized, pure powder stuff and I do it sublingually. I know it's converting to T, but I'm not getting any of the negative side effects like chin hair, rage, etc.

What are the side effects of too much preg? I'm currently taking 200 mg total - 100 mg around 6 AM and another 100 mg at 3 PM (same with DHEA).

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-12T17:45:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-brand-of-dhea-7-keto-dhea-are-you-using-and-like.2331/post-37180>

Preg would be heart racing and palps, feeling overstimulated, I think.

The DHEA I think can have the opposite effect - making you fatigued. I'd drop the dose some and see if you do better. Or just skip a day, that's what I do. If I feel better that day, I drop the dose. I'm not sure what you and I are taking is comparable at all to what most people on here are taking b/c it might get absorbed a lot better than swallowing pills.

I sure wish it was easier to get docs on board for tests. Why does it have to be so complicated? You can't just fix it magically by taking a pill and then all levels are good. They know this! I am curious too why you are fatigued? Wonder where you are at with the thyroid?

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-11T00:25:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-looks-body-size.2334/post-36652>

I don't get it. Women keep complaining that their self-worth is all based in looks and weight. But then, why is the first thing most women do is comment on another woman's looks and degree of thinness???

I hated this and hated it and hated it some more when I was putting on weight during recovery. Constant comments on the size of my body. All from women. Men... could care less.

I lost it over it once and a very dear (male) friend told me that he doesn't get it either. Whenever a woman meets a woman they have the need to comment on looks, particularly body weight. When a guy meets a guy the last thing on their mind to say is "You look great. You are so thin today!".

When did it ever become another woman's business what the size of my body is today???

Ready to duck from the flying tomatoes and eggs or whatever is going to come my way. But I'll say it anyways: Women, you are complaining about something that you are creating yourself. Quit doing it and it will go away.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-11T04:54:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-looks-body-size.2334/post-36730>

What people notice about others, is reflective of how they view themselves. Most women are constantly self evaluating, and in turn evaluating themselves compared to others and expressing on it. I do this too, regretfully. I'm not proud, but it gives me a little schadenfreude thrill when I see someone I didn't like in my past that got fat. Of course, these days that's just about everyone from High School!

Do you not watch what other people eat now? When I ate like the rest of the world, and it wasn't top of mind - I could care less, and never noticed. Now that I've gone down the rabbit hole I can't help but notice what people put in carts, feed their kids, or eat at restaurants. I would never say anything - but god knows I note it in my judgmental little brain.

Over the 4th, I couldn't stop pondering and watching as all the super pale people were slather their kin with sunscreen (and trying to slather my son!).

I don't think this is a failing of women, I think it's human nature. It stands out with women as they are always thinking about their own food/size/body/attractiveness.

I wonder when people are going to de-volve so much they start flinging their poo.

I mean, not to be an ingrate, it was nice to hear, but as this conversation reminds me, BFD ladies. Its not about the outsides. It's who I am on the inside. I kept steering convo away from looks, the Zone (diet she is on) etc. Sometime its like we have nothing else to talk about. Yeah, what does that tell me. d'oh!

...

### PrimalPam — 2012-07-14T16:31:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-looks-body-size.2334/post-37744>

Yes it's all about skinny for women. In my friends group, each person always looks at other women (and each other), remarking how "great" she looks, if she's lost weight since the last time we saw each other. One friend is going through a terrible divorce, and she's one of those who simply can't eat under stress, so the already-thin girl has lost another 10 lbs., which, she tells me privately, is not something she's proud of or happy about and how she realizes her nutritional needs are suffering. Yet, all the other friends keep telling her how terrific she looks!! What a nutso world.

I, too, wish I had friends IRL to whom I could talk about this stuff! How great CO is for your skin, how turmeric reduces inflammation, how seafood has all the right nutrients & fats for your brain health. But no, it's all about how many calories this has.

I'm 50, and have had many of the same friends since my 20's, and we are all within about 10-15 lbs. of our 20's weight. I'm actually the only one who gained significant weight (got up to the low 160's from 20's weight of mid-120's). Many in the group are even much thinner now, due to extreme exercising and calorie-control. But I now see their health issues with a magnifying glass: extreme use of prescription drugs, hair loss, vertigo, depression, arthritic conditions. And EVERY SINGLE ONE of my friends takes prescription sleeping pills. We all went on a trip last year, about 8-10 of us, and I was the ONLY ONE who did not take sleeping medication.

The fact that I can sleep for 8 hours straight is the most amazing part of this WOL (way of life) for me. That alone is worth more to me than any pair of skinny jeans (ooooh, but the fact that I can now fit in a lot of my old clothes IS fun too!).

### BJK77 — 2012-07-14T18:06:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-looks-body-size.2334/post-37774>

Yep, have to agree. We women bring this all on ourselves . . . well, with the help of the circus-like media, that is. I wish I could say I was immune to it all, but I definitely have some self image issues. I do find myself comparing my body to others around me, but I'm getting a bit better with it.

DH has always told me I have a majorly distorted body image and he likes me better with a little meat on my bones! I used to cringe at that, but I'm slowly learning to accept and even like my new found curves. If those curves bring me health, they are sooo worth it

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-16T10:57:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-looks-body-size.2334/post-38314>

Wanted to add this to this thread - JK FB inspiration today:

> **Why compare yourself with others? No one in the entire world can do a better job of being you than you.**

Love it & Live it.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-16T11:10:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-looks-body-size.2334/post-38315>

> **Shijin13;37665 wrote:** I can do w/o the xfit. to intense for my workouts. Though I do love my OPI malaga wine nail polish. I may not be able to let that go! I loved living in CO (minus the BS I put up w/in school - but that's what I get for choosing to go to that little piece of heaven at the foot of the Rampart Range)

I yet have to get my first manicure ever. I would destroy it in a day or less me thinks. I will need to find a very special man who doesn't mind me being natural  I fall off shoes with heels. I will wear a dress these days OMG primarily b/c it's comfortable yet during a photo shoot years ago another woman had to give me a speed lesson in sitting and moving in a dress. Hilarious.

I'm not saying these things necessarily are bad in themselves. There's healthy ways to do these things. If you enjoy your nail polish - go for it! If it starts to dominate your life needing to do all those things woman is supposed to do and if there is competition as the underlying drive .... maybe think again. I've tried some feminine stuff on for fit and I feel like a muppet in most of that stuff. It's just not me. So I'm not participating.... not in the skinny madness, not in the nail polish madness, not in the fashion madness (I do some of the fashion in my own special way- for sure not one size fits all!). ### Shijin13 — 2012-07-16T16:15:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-and-looks-body-size.2334/post-38344>

Soul - I got into my mani/pedis when I was in the military - lets be honest camoflauge is not attractive on ANYONE - even HOT SF guys. But its really not attractive on women - though it is more attractive on women than a flight suit - which is just a nomex leisure suit!

The jobs I did - I worked my butt off - there's no way make up was an option - I didn't really learn how to apply make up until my late 20's so it looked natural - and not like I painted my face on!

# Thread: Thyroid Nodule - anyone knows something about it please chime in!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### youthfuliz — 2012-07-11T02:39:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-36695>

I have two thyroid nodules. I noticed one lump last week and had it checked out. It turned out I have two, a dominant one on the left and a smaller one on the isthmus.

I read intensively online and gathered information from this site and elsewhere, especially the mda iodine thread where Grizz gathered a lot of information on iodine deficiency. I watched the Dr. Brownstein video and read articles. I've been supplementing iodine for 3 days and already feel better. The large nodule shrunk about 50% so I know I'm doing the right thing. Heartbeat was at 55/minute last week, today it's 66. My normal heartbeat is about 66 and it was the first time last week that I ever noticed it dropped to below 60. Taking the co-factor vitamins too, not much detox except for more bowel movements.

Blood test done on last thursday came back today, shows that I have normal thyroid hormone level:

T4: 8 (range: 4.5 - 12)

TSH: 2 (range: 0.4 - 4.5)

Ultrasound results said the nodules are most likely benign. A biopsy is suggested since the nodules are larger than 1cm. I'm thinking whether a biopsy is necessary... it's intrusive, may cause damage to my throid. So maybe supplementing iodine and see in three months? If the nodules shrink more I may not need a biopsy.

Please if you have any experience in this area let me know what you think! Thanks.

### youthfuliz — 2012-07-12T21:11:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-37236>

Decided not to get a biopsy. The nodules are most likely benign. I don't want a biopsy to complicate them. They're shrinking too with the iodine supplement. Will repeat ultrasound in three months.

Iodine is good not just for thyroid, but for your brain, and all sexual organs - breasts, ovaries, prostates... Grizz gathered alot of information on iodine, search his id you'll see information in his signature. Also Dr. Brownstein has books and youtube videos out there for those who are interested.

### AKMan — 2012-07-13T20:24:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-37544>

Let us know how it works out. Sorry no one replied, but what you have is not very common. However, someday someone will do a search for thyroid nodules on this site and hopefully find this post. Please update when you have results!

### LightForTheNight — 2012-07-13T20:45:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-37553>

I've done some research on T. Nodules, and from what I've gathered they are benign in 95%+ of the time. The iodine along with selenium seem to be the best alternatives, and Brownstein and Abraham are definitely leading those fronts. I'd be interested to see if CT has any affect because of its ability to stimulate the thyroid.

### youthfuliz — 2012-07-14T16:03:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-37731>

Thanks Akman.  The information online has been very helpful to me. I'd very glad if one day my experience will help others.

### youthfuliz — 2012-07-14T16:07:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-37733>

> **LightForTheNight;37720 wrote:** I've done some research on T. Nodules, and from what I've gathered they are benign in 95%+ of the time. The iodine along with selenium seem to be the best alternatives, and Brownstein and Abraham are definitely leading those fronts. I'd be interested to see if CT has any affect because of its ability to stimulate the thyroid.

Yeah you're right. iodine/iodide needs selenium to work. Just got my Brownstein books on Iodine and Thyroid. As far as I know nobody has talked about CT and thyroid before Dr. Kruse. We are the n=1.

### youthfuliz — 2012-07-14T16:20:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-37739>

One week update:

Doing well with iodine natural therapy outlined by Dr. Brownstein and others. The nodules have shrunk by maybe 60%. Now they're almost not noticeable if I look in the mirror. When I swallow I still see them moving up and down, but much smaller than before. I have been taking 50mg iodine+iodide from day 2 (on day 1 I ate lots of seaweeds with brazil nuts). The nodules shrunk very quickly at first, then continued to shrink, but at a slowly rate, from day 3 on. Every morning I notice the lump is a bit smaller.  I had almost no detox symptoms from it, except for yellowish urine and stinky sweat. I took 75mg yesterday, am taking 62.5mg today, will go back down to 50mg tomorrow. I'm also taking the cofactors.

### janagram — 2012-07-16T04:11:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-38255>

I have one, but waited to respond as I don't know much about it. I've had it for 20 yrs...I've had it aspirated way back then..benign. It makes me worry a bit about my thyroid but...doc's don't say much.

It's size of walnut.

### youthfuliz — 2012-07-16T18:20:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-38366>

Janagram,

I'm feeling much better with iodine supplementation. My nodules have shrunk to maybe 40% of their original size... I need to repeat the ultrasound to be accurate so that's just my own observation. I do show it to one of my colleagues from day one when I first noticed the lump and she was surprised how fast it is shrinking. I have more energy too and can focus on the important things in life instead of finding excuses to delay doing them. I plan to repeat ultrasound in three months to see clearly how much iodine has helped. Right now my neck looks quite normal, you won't notice my nodules if I don't tell you about them. But when I swallow you still see the lump (much smaller than before) moving up and down. From what I read many people have benign nodules that they don't even know about so it's probably nothing to worry about. But if you want to look into iodine deficiency here're what I find helpful:

[http://joannebrophy.com/programs/iodinetherapy.htm](http://http://joannebrophy.com/programs/iodinetherapy.htm)

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Kd34EJ5E3bI\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;Kd34EJ5E3bI\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Kd34EJ5E3bI\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;Kd34EJ5E3bI)

There're a lot more information online if you do a search. MDA has an iodine thread that's quite helpful too. We also have an iodine thread here if you haven't noticed yet:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?987-The-iodine-Thread](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?987-The-iodine-Thread)!!!

Doctors usually don't do much about nodules except for making sure they're benign and watch them. Or offer to opearate when they're too large. There're a few iodine literate doctors who treat thyroid problems, and problems with breasts, ovaries and other sexual organs with iodine (and maybe thyroid hormones if necessary).

### janagram — 2012-07-16T18:34:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-38370>

thanks, youthfuliz. Mine is apparently not visible either, but palpable. Thanks for tips about iodine, I have begun taking it. So glad you are getting results.

### Gracelind — 2012-07-17T09:27:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-38669>

Multi-nodular goiter here--5 or 6 nodules (don't have report in front of me right now.) Iodine uptake exam was negative so, I'm told, the nodules are benign. My rheumatologist told me his wife has had the same for twenty years with no apparent ill effects. No one has suggested aspiration.

Incidental finding on a CT for something else 3 years ago showed small calcified thyroid nodule but didn't mention any additional nodules. All of my docs shrugged off that finding.

Discovered the nodules this winter, running my hand up my neck and feeling a lump. We stopped cooking with salt many years ago, so I'm clearly iodine deficient but in the last year I've been adding Celtic Sea salt to food on my plate. Started with a low dose of Lugol's, working up to about 25 mg about the time I found the lump. I was worried that taking the iodine had brought it out. But after some weight loss my doc says it's more noticeable with no fat on my neck to hide it.

So,youthfulize, I'm very interested in yours shrinking with a higher dose of iodine. You mentioned the cofactors. Are you also taking selenium and Vit C? Thanks for posting your experience! This is yet another area where there's a deep divide between CW and alternative treatment.

### youthfuliz — 2012-07-17T14:50:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-nodule-anyone-knows-something-about-it-please-chime-in.2337/post-38684>

Hi Gracelind,

Thanks for sharing your experience. I'm taking iodoral, 25mg at breakfast, 25mg around 2pm. The co-factors that I take are

1\. celtic salt, 1/2 tsp in water (I do additional salt loading once or twice when I feel need help to detox)

2\. Vitamin C, 3000mg at night

3\. Magnesium malate, 500mg at night

4\. Selenium, currently 200mg + 1 or 2 brazil nut (I should cut this down to 200mg, or 1 or 2 brazil nut, but I just started so I'm taking a little more)

5\. ATP cofactors (this is B2 + B3 in specific ratio for detox)

I also eat plenty of oysters... like every other day  and mushrooms, and I have a recent crush on cilantro. I take other supplements, most what Dr. Kruse suggested in his Top Ten Supplements blog... unfortunately I wasn't taking iodone before last week, which is about the only thing I didn't get out of that list. Also taking Vitamin A or veal liver, D3, Fish oil, Krill oil...etc.

I read about thyroid nodules/goiters online and started to supplement iodine within two days of noticing the lump. The first couple of days I supplemented... the lump shrunk by 50%... it was very obvious to me that iodine was working! And I had more energy, better mood, better focus. So I read more and had more confidence... I was trying to increase to 75mg, but felt a bit jittery so backed down to 50mg. The lump is not noticeable anymore, but palpable, and it's getting smaller each day. I hope in 3 months when I repeat the ultrasound it will be much smaller.

From what I read, thyroid nodules/goiters means iodine deficiency. Which also means the rest of our body needs more iodine too, not just the thyroid. Too bad the CW doctors are ignoring this. But luckily we have easy access to internet to educate ourselves. And I love the fact that great doctors are using the internet to reach us... like Dr. Kruse, like the iodine doctors.

Here is Dr. Kruse' suggestion:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2374-Iodine-Deficiency-and-CT](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2374-Iodine-Deficiency-and-CT)

I'm probably not hypothyriodism... but I did have some symptoms... like laziness, haha, lack of motivation to get things done, not exactly like me. Blood test shows normal TSH and T4.

Are you still taking lugol's? From what I read if iodine supplementation makes the thyroid enlarge, it's because the body was deficient for a long time and when there's iodine the thyroid tries all it could to grab that iodine so it enlarged itself to store up (something like that), and that kind of enlargement will go away, back to normal within days/weeks if you keep supplementing iodine... the thyroid will realize that it doesn't need to hoard eventually. I think that's the reason why iodine supplementation can shrink nodules/goiters... the need for them to be there is gone when you're iodine sufficient. At least I think that's why my lump is shrinking. And I love the new energetic me! My productivity increased a lot in the last several days.

Please keep us posted how you're doing, and I'll update too from time to time. We need to know more about thyroid and iodine.

I just heard about this on Real Food Summit podcast...I've also looked at Dr. Mercola and Mark Sisson's they are all very expensive.

This one is 50.00 for one month supply Mark's is 100 dollars his supplement set. Mercola's is also really expensive for just 1 month worth.

Are these things worth it? People talk about taking a multi-vitamin to fill in the gaps if you don't eat the best you can but...that's so much money. I don't know about spending so much on something I'm not sure how it'll help me if at all?

### nonchalant — 2012-07-11T15:41:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-multivitamins.2343/post-36802>

I agree, I'm a bit suspicious about multivitamins. I'm currently taking one from Life Extension, called Two-Per-Day tablets (though I just take one a day.) I don't care for the type of vitamin E included. They do include some ingredients for which there is no RDA, like Alpha Lipoic Acid and Choline and others. I found that I could stop taking my selenium, but the magnesium included is just Mg oxide. I do like that it includes boron, zinc, and other items I wasn't supplementing otherwise. They are about $17 for 60 day's worth (4 months the way I use them.)

### bubbles — 2012-07-27T21:51:04+0300

# Thread: When CT doesn't get easier...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-ct-doesnt-get-easier.2357/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bigknitwit — 2012-07-11T19:43:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-ct-doesnt-get-easier.2357/post-36876>

What kinds of issues are at play when CT does not seem to get easier, or progress in tolerance?

I'm guessing #1 is low Free T3 levels, since FT3 (and vitamin A) is needed to activate the UCP3 pathway (the heating one). Did I get that right? Leptin is also needed here, so lingering leptin resistance too.

What else? Does Ferritin levels play a role?

I seem to be able to do 20 minutes in the tub without much problem, but getting beyond that get asymptotally more difficult. Occasionally I get a day where I can get to 30 without serious willpower. However, I often CT 2-4 times/day, just in these 15-25 (if I'm lucky) minute amounts. I can't imagine an hour like a lot of people speak of...

### LightForTheNight — 2012-07-14T04:25:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-ct-doesnt-get-easier.2357/post-37675>

I find it interesting that you mention vitamin a and ferretin. My thoughts on the subject would be that you should try eating more eggs and liver a few times a week. Ferretin is a protein that helps carry iron, right? Liver is a most potent form of heme-iron and especially vitamin a. This also seems to go along with Jack's ideology towards eating a more Pablo-diet in conjunction with CT.

### PrimalPam — 2012-07-14T17:48:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-ct-doesnt-get-easier.2357/post-37769>

I find that if I don't eat a high fat/high protein meal and drink a large glass of ice water just prior to CT, I can barely make it. I shiver like crazy and can't wait to get out. And when I do get out, I shiver and have numb fingers & toes for over an hour. But when I eat high fat/high protein and drink ice water, then almost immediately get into the water, it's relaxing and enjoyable.

I posted this question on this thread a while back, but no one answered it. Is this because my body is busy metabolizing all the stuff I just ate & drank, so my core is warmer while my exterior is in the low 50's?

The other day, I felt "fine" and did the CT bath without having eaten yet, as I wasn't hungry but wanted to get the CT bath done for the day. It was horrible, shivered the entire time. Water temp was a relatively warm 57-58F that day! But yesterday, I did the protocol correctly, and the water temp was 48F, and I was absolutely fine.

### janagram — 2012-07-14T17:52:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-ct-doesnt-get-easier.2357/post-37771>

I don't get eating high protein and high fat meal and then pouring a glass of cold water over it....something about that..seems against digestion....may not be but...?

### LightForTheNight — 2012-07-16T16:50:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-ct-doesnt-get-easier.2357/post-38354>

Something I used to do before I heard anything about Jack is put my hands in ice, with just enough water to make it cold as all get-out. That hurt like a \*\*\*\*\*. Oh my god, it's the most intense thing ever. I heard it was good for increasing circulation though, and I read a study that was published in the nytimes? about people with some disease where they have terrible circulation to their hands curing it by doing what I conveniently nicknamed 'ice-hands'. I've done this periodically, for 30 seconds- 1 minute intervals (4x) for a few months. I've also been taking only cold showers since January. I know Jack somewhere says that a possible transition to cold baths is to start with cold showers, though he says he didn't do this himself. I've taken 30 minute cold showers since before hearing about Jack. And I don't want you to think I'm superhuman freak, I'm not, I've always hated the cold, cuz I got cold so easily, I only started embracing the cold because I've been led to believe from independent research that it will help me to tolerate more cold in the future.

I took my first ice bath yesterday, and I was like, this is it? This doesn't hurt nearly as bad as ice-hands, lol. I was shivering for the next 10 minutes after I got out of the bath, but I slept like a freaking baby last night.

So from my own personal experience I would say, try interval ice-hands and if you take cold showers, do squats while you're in there to warm up and stay in for at least 15 minutes. For the ice-hands, I would do 4 intervals at 45 seconds to start with, with a 1 minute 15 second breaks between each set. Fill a bowl with ice, and put just enough water in so that your hands will be covered. Your hands will hurt when you first do it, like ow, wtf is this \*\*\*\* kinda hurt. But after the second or third interval they get relatively numb and with only 45 second intervals I haven't even come close to any sort of nerve damage or anything, but it definitely makes me better appreciate what true freezing is like, and makes thins like cold showers or cold baths feel much easier.

But take what I say with a grain of salt, I'm a bit of an eccentric.

### PrimalPam — 2012-07-17T02:20:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-ct-doesnt-get-easier.2357/post-38568>

I've been doing 5 x/week for 6 weeks now. I do 40-45 mins @ 50-55F. I could definitely do longer, but frankly, I get bored & have stuff to do. I figure, 45 mins is very do-able and I'm able to keep it up.

Yes, it's freezing cold and yes it "hurts". But it also feels good...in a weird way. Maybe because I've seen such huge benefits in such a short time, and I realize how good it is for me.

I make iPod playlists that are 45 mins long, and turn it up really loud on my docking station. I lay on my back to start in my tub (standard size), but since my stomach & breasts are out of the water, I turn over half-time and finish up. The music helps a TON. I lay there and sing really loud the whole time! Has become very meditative and therapeutic.

I keep my fingers & toes out of the water. I used to keep my legs out from the knees down, out, but now I'm keeping my entire foot, right up to the toes, in. Workin' up to putting the toes in.

# Thread: Decline of immune system with aging may have a genetic cause
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decline-of-immune-system-with-aging-may-have-a-genetic-cause.2358/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-07-11T19:56:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decline-of-immune-system-with-aging-may-have-a-genetic-cause.2358/post-36883>

[http://esciencenews.com/articles/2012/07/11/decline.immune.system.with.aging.may.have.a.genetic.cause](http://http://esciencenews.com/articles/2012/07/11/decline.immune.system.with.aging.may.have.a.genetic.cause)

> Decline of immune system with aging may have a genetic cause
>
> Published: Wednesday, July 11, 2012 - 10:53 in Biology & Nature
>
>
>
> Important insights that explain why our ability to ward off infection declines with age are published in a new research report in the July 2012 issue of the Genetics Society of America's journal, Genetics. A team of U.S. scientists identified genes responsible for this decline by examining fruit flies -- a model organism often used to study human biology in an experimentally tractable system -- at different stages of their lives. They found that a completely different set of genes is responsible for warding off infection at middle age than during youth. Many of the genes identified are also present in humans, so this study opens doors to understanding genetic interactions that underlie why older people have more trouble fighting off infections than do younger people.
>
>
>
> "We believe we have identified genes that contribute to the age-related deterioration of the immune response to infection," said Jeff Leips, Ph.D., a researcher involved in the work from the Department of Biological Sciences at the University of Maryland, Baltimore County. "Because many of the genes that we have identified also occur in humans, we hope that such knowledge will lead to new treatments to maintain immune function as we age."
>
>
>
> To make this discovery, Leips used fruit flies of different genotypes that were derived from a natural population. Flies of each genotype were infected with bacteria at two different ages -- when they were young, at an age equivalent to human teenagers, and when they were older, in what might be the equivalent to early middle age in humans. The researchers then measured the ability of the flies to clear the bacterial infection at each age while simultaneously assessing how the expression of genes responded to infection. Genes whose variation in expression were associated with the ability to clear the infection were identified for each of the different ages at the time of infection. Surprisingly, the genes were different -- there was no overlap in the sets of genes associated with the ability to clear infection across ages.
>
>
>
> "The notion that the genes responsible for immune function are almost entirely different in middle age than in early adulthood is tantalizing," said Mark Johnston, editor-in-chief of the journal GENETICS. "As the average age of the U.S. population increases, understanding how to maintain strong, healthy immune systems could help many of us live longer, healthier lives."


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: To Your Health: Moderate Alcohol Confers Some Benefits, Some Harms for Women's Health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-your-health-moderate-alcohol-confers-some-benefits-some-harms-for-womens-health.2360/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-07-11T20:05:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-your-health-moderate-alcohol-confers-some-benefits-some-harms-for-womens-health.2360/post-36889>

[http://abcnews.go.com/Health/drinking-alcohol-moderate-amounts-reduces-womens-osteoporosis-rheumatoid/story?id=16750770&abcnews#.T\_2w0vXkaSo](http://http://abcnews.go.com/Health/drinking-alcohol-moderate-amounts-reduces-womens-osteoporosis-rheumatoid/story?id=16750770&abcnews#.T_2w0vXkaSo)

> Researchers at Oregon State University studied 40 postmenopausal women under age 65 who reported drinking up to two drinks per day in the year before the study, watching what happened when they asked these women to stop drinking for two weeks.
>
>
>
> When these regular moderate drinkers cut out alcohol, the researchers found that their blood showed higher levels of biomarkers linked to bone turnover, a natural process that goes awry when more bone is lost than is replaced, which leads to osteoporosis. When the women started drinking again, their bone turnover seemed to improve even after one day of moderate alcohol consumption.
>
>
>
> Ursula Iwaniec, one of the authors of the study, published today in the journal Menopause, said alcohol seemed to benefit these postmenopausal women, but it may not be the best solution for women hoping to improve their bone health.
>
>
>
> "I wouldn't start drinking just for this reason that it's going to make my bones better," she said.
>
>
>
> That moderate drinking seems to affect women's health is not surprising. Alcohol raises levels of estrogen, the hormone that affects many aspects of women's health, including arthritis and osteoporosis. Alcohol also raises the "good" cholesterol, HDL, and can have positive effects on blood pressure and other risk factors for cardiovascular disease.

I don't think my genes got the memo.

### BJK77 — 2012-07-11T21:32:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-test-results-help.2362/post-36910>

Just got back my salivary test results for cortisol and sex steroids and I'm totally confused. I started supplementing with DHEA and pregnenolone in small doses about a month before this test, but then stopped for 5 days prior to doing the test. All my blood work has always shown all sex hormones to be SUPER low and I can't believe this minimal amount of supplementation caused such an increase in my DHEA.

I also don't quite know how to interpret the cortisol levels. What are we aiming for here? And how do I better optimize my cortisol levels? My sleep is better thanks to the DHEA supplementation. I generally wake with the sun and get light in my eyes immediately. I start wearing my blue blocker glasses about 30-45 minutes prior to darkness (around 8:15-8:30) and all lights go off when it's completely dark outside, generally around 9:00, and I'm usually in bed very shortly after that. What else can I do?

Thanks for any input! By the way, the previous results were done in 11/2011, so pre-leptin rx and pre-CT.

Cortisol Morning: **6.1 (Range 3.7-9.5) PREVIOUS: 9.4

Cortisol Noon: **3.0 (Range 1.2-3.0) PREVIOUS: 1.0

Cortisol Evening: **1.5 (Range 0.6-1.9) PREVIOUS: 0.8

Cortisol Night: **0.4 (Range 0.4-1.0) PREVIOUS: 0.3

Estradiol: **0.7 L** (Range 1.3-3.3) PREVIOUS: 1.1

Progesterone: 55 L** (Range 75-270) PREVIOUS: 16

PG/E2: 79 L** (Optimal: 100-500) PREVIOUS: 15

Testosterone: 87 H** (Range 16-55) PREVIOUS: 20

DHEAS: 28.1 H** (Range 2-23) PREVIOUS: 5.7

As for your cortisol, it looks mostly good to me but what do I know. I would like to know Dr. K.s thoughts on it. Looks like you have the right curve except noon might be a tad high in comparison to the rest of your ranges.

Then skiing accident and TBI happened and I upped it back to 120mg for a couple of weeks since stress drops DHEA, along with preg 80mg. That's when I started having trouble sleeping again. Felt exhausted.

I then dropped everything and 3/4 days later I was sleeping again. I've been experimenting since and I appear to do best on 40-50mg DHEA and no preg. I think I have a cortisol problem much more than a thyroid issue.

> I keep thinking there is no point to doing labs for me if my brain is constantly under assault from low O2 and toxic blood. Like that's going to have to be fixed first before I even start drawing labs and working on hormones. I'm still resistant to going on long-term BHRT. I still am in denial and think that my body and brain will heal given the right circumstances.

Just a thought Souldanzer, but I know Dr. K told men that pregnenolone and DHEA can sometimes manage the hormone imbalance, but they have to get HSCRP under 1 first. So the same could be true for women- we may be able to manage our hormones through those but we probably need to meet other requirements first, whether using BHRT for a better ratio or using the same HSCRP level. I don't know your HSCRP, but with all that you are experiencing it seems yours would be high. Is it?

So in other words, you may want to consider BHRT for short term, and then see about switching to long term maintenance with DHEA and pregnenolone.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-13T19:18:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-test-results-help.2362/post-37513>

Tweety the problem is my hsCRP is .47 - so it's not inflammation that's keeping my hormones from happening. Low inflammation and crappy hormone panel indicates a brain issue (yes, it just clicked in my brain Dr K - not sure why I wasn't understanding this quite like this before - other than you gotta tell me at a time when there happens to be some O2 in my brain). Basically, it's either I permanently damaged my brain from my previous life and then I'll be stuck with lots of BHRT or I'll get lucky and fixing the hole in my heart combined with maybe some HBOT will do the trick.

I'm planning on the latter for now

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-13T19:26:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-test-results-help.2362/post-37520>

> **Destiny;37618 wrote:** It does! I have been taking all the supplements dr. K suggested since January. Plus the orange glasses. I feel it is a bit calmer but still waking up at night. I can feel the surge. I guess it takes time.

It can take a long time for cortisol to go back to normal. Plus I'm sure there is a synergistic action taking place between some of the other hormones as well. Maybe you WERE at the lows and now you are starting to go back up (not down!). I like that thought better!

### BJK77 — 2012-07-14T03:17:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salivary-test-results-help.2362/post-37653>

> **Souldanzer;37680 wrote:** Tweety the problem is my hsCRP is .47 - so it's not inflammation that's keeping my hormones from happening. Low inflammation and crappy hormone panel indicates a brain issue (yes, it just clicked in my brain Dr K - not sure why I wasn't understanding this quite like this before - other than you gotta tell me at a time when there happens to be some O2 in my brain). Basically, it's either I permanently damaged my brain from my previous life and then I'll be stuck with lots of BHRT or I'll get lucky and fixing the hole in my heart combined with maybe some HBOT will do the trick.
>
>
>
> I'm planning on the latter for now

Why does it seem like I could write most of your posts Soul?!?! Once again, I'll say "same thing here." I know I'm dealing with a brain issue. My hsCRP was 0.05, which I was happy with . . . . until I realized that meant my brain was fried since I pretty much have no hormones at all.

I'm also coming to the realization that I have had an ED without even realizing it. I've been going back and re-reading several blog posts trying to make sense of them through this ridiculously thick brain fog and it dawned on me that I never read the ED blog - just assumed it wouldn't apply to me. Finally read it tonight and wow! Major eye opener and not in a good way

It's almost funny too because Dr. K mentioned something about my test results being indicative of an ED during my consult. Even then I didn't put it all together. I knew I never had a healthy self image and I could eat and eat and eat (always eating more than my 6'2 husband), but I never really thought of it as compulsive or binge eating. Now I realize that's exactly what it was. I could NEVER go without food, so AN wasn't something that I ever worried about. I do remember trying to binge once a super long time ago, but wasn't successful and never tried again, so I guess you could say I don't or didn't have an extreme form, but man could I pack away the food! I still have moments with trigger foods (cabbage, for whatever reason) that I could just eat tons of, but I do feel more in control than I ever have before.

Truly frightening reality to think that my brain could be permanently impaired. I'm gonna try to be hopeful (like you) and plan on being able to fix this mess I've created.
