# Thread: Ions, not particles, make silver toxic to bacteria
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ions-not-particles-make-silver-toxic-to-bacteria.2364/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-07-11T22:41:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ions-not-particles-make-silver-toxic-to-bacteria.2364/post-36936>

[http://esciencenews.com/articles/2012/07/11/ions.not.particles.make.silver.toxic.bacteria](http://http://esciencenews.com/articles/2012/07/11/ions.not.particles.make.silver.toxic.bacteria)

Ions, not particles, make silver toxic to bacteria

> Rice University researchers have settled a long-standing controversy over the mechanism by which silver nanoparticles, the most widely used nanomaterial in the world, kill bacteria. Their work comes with a Nietzsche-esque warning: Use enough. If you don't kill them, you make them stronger.
>
>
>
> Scientists have long known that silver ions, which flow from nanoparticles when oxidized, are deadly to bacteria. Silver nanoparticles are used just about everywhere, including in cosmetics, socks, food containers, detergents, sprays and a wide range of other products to stop the spread of germs.
>
>
>
> But scientists have also suspected silver nanoparticles themselves may be toxic to bacteria, particularly the smallest of them at about 3 nanometers. Not so, according to the Rice team that reported its results this month in the American Chemical Society journal Nano Letters.
>
>
>
> In fact, when the possibility of ionization is taken away from silver, the nanoparticles are practically benign in the presence of microbes, said Pedro Alvarez, George R. Brown Professor and chair of Rice's Civil and Environmental Engineering Department.
>
>
>
> "You would be surprised how often people market things without a full mechanistic understanding of their function," said Alvarez, who studies the fate of nanoparticles in the environment and their potential toxicity, particularly to humans. "The prefix 'nano' can be a double-edged sword. It can help you sell a product, and in other cases it might elicit concerns about potential unintended consequences."
>
>
>
> He said the straightforward answer to the decade-old question is that the insoluble silver nanoparticles do not kill cells by direct contact. But soluble ions, when activated via oxidation in the vicinity of bacteria, do the job nicely.
>
> To figure that out, the researchers had to strip the particles of their powers. "Our original expectation was that the smaller a particle is, the greater the toxicity," said Zongming Xiu, a Rice postdoctoral researcher and lead author of the paper. Xiu set out to test nanoparticles, both commercially available and custom-synthesized from 3 to 11 nanometers, to see whether there was a correlation between size and toxicity.
>
>
>
> **"We could not get consistent results," he said. "It was very frustrating and really weird."
>
>
>
> Xiu decided to test nanoparticle toxicity in an anaerobic environment -- that is, sealed inside a chamber with no exposure to oxygen -- to control the silver ions' release. He found that the filtered particles were a lot less toxic to microbes than silver ions.
>
>
>
> Working with the lab of Rice chemist Vicki Colvin, the team then synthesized silver nanoparticles inside the anaerobic chamber to eliminate any chance of oxidation. "We found the particles, even up to a concentration of 195 parts per million, were still not toxic to bacteria," Xiu said. "But for the ionic silver, a concentration of about 15 parts per billion would kill all the bacteria present. That told us the particle is 7,665 times less toxic than the silver ions, indicating a negligible toxicity."
>
>
>
> "The point of that experiment," Alvarez said, "was to show that a lot of people were obtaining data that was confounded by a release of ions, which was occurring during exposure they perhaps weren't aware of.**

more on the link

### kathylu — 2012-07-11T22:48:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ions-not-particles-make-silver-toxic-to-bacteria.2364/post-36939>

Hmm, wonder what makes colloidal silver such a good antiseptic/antibiotic then? I thought it was suspended silver particles, not ions.

Caroline

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-09-28T22:32:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/canadians-where-can-we-get-optimizing-labs-done.2372/post-55081>

> **lioness7;55767 wrote:** Just wondering - what tests you were able to order through the local pharmacy through the two sites?

pharmasaves offer the tests for about $300 per test but you can get it more reasonably priced at around $130 if you join canaryclub.org (for free) and order it online. saliva and blood spot tests do not require a physician to order in Canada

hebsie is right about the RT3 Lifelabs stopped doing them in 2009 (and even then they shipped it out)

If you are seeing a naturopath they can order a full thyroid panel through Quest (I believe) and the draw is done in Lifelabs but sent out to the Quest labs in the US and the whole thing is a few hundred $.

fwiw ZRT just bought Rockymountain labs so you might as well order online at the lower ZRT price.

### MartiD — 2012-07-12T15:34:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hashis-and-hives.2377/post-37135>

Ive been having several bouts with hives for last 7 months and after being tested by a Allergist found out I'm allergic to most things I breathe. I had issues and shots as a kids but never really as an adult until my out breaks in January. I've been starting injections but had another outbreak on Saturday. I'm attributing it to a night of cuddling with my very freaked out dog on the 4th...he hates fireworks. He is unfortunately my highest allergy! My last outbreak was in February when I had feather pillows at a hotel (second highest allergy)! Anyway, I'm taking Allergra and Zantac per my Dr orders and it helping some with rash but minimal. I've been reading about hives and came across a large amount of people who have hives from their Hashis. Any one with Hashis have this issue? I don't think I have hashis as I have none of symptoms but I am trying to get in with a new Dr and get a full blood work up.

### colleencoble — 2012-07-12T16:26:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hashis-and-hives.2377/post-37151>

I used to. I was allergic to everything and had a very bad candida infection. I also found a naturopath MD who did a acupressure technique called NAET ([www.naet.com](http://http://www.naet.com)) that used acupuncture. She cleared out all of them. Now there is an easier way with a laser though. I had hives at least once a week. I haven't had them now in about 10 years, I think.

### SeaHorse — 2012-07-12T17:22:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hashis-and-hives.2377/post-37168>

MartiD....check out the thread on Histamine... I only test "allergic" to sugar, asparagus, almonds and beans....but I have serious histamine reactions to lots of foods...there are lots of pathways to getting hives...temperature, contact allergies, emotions, airborne allergies etc. Do you find CT helps? I use to go out in the middle of winter with hardly any clothes to chill my skin when I had chronic hives. I also got a case that lasted six weeks before I realized my antidepressant was the culprit (effexor....lots of people have the same reaction after months of being on it) I read a lot about Hashis and thyroid at the time because it seems that, as you said, chronic hives and thyroid issues can go together. In my case getting all my hormones optimal will help change my immune responses...could this part of your healing process too?

### MartiD — 2012-07-12T17:49:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hashis-and-hives.2377/post-37182>

SeaHorse, I did see that thread and will re-read it. My first outbreak was in January 2012 after I had lost about 50 lbs, been paleo since October and in much better health than I had been for 10 years! I originally thought it was epson salt baths that caused the outbreak but then ruled that out with my second out break 6 weeks later. After the testing in March I realize the 2nd outbreak was the feather pillows at the hotel the weekend of my outbreak. My Allergist said that since I had issues a a kid that it has resurfaced due to my age and it is very common. I do CT most every day although it isn't deep CT...water is pretty warm here in Mid-Missouri but at least once a week I try to get the water down to 55 degrees with lots of ice. I do spot CT my stomach most every day. I thought the CT bath I took Saturday morning might have been the cause but remembered that my arms were showing an outbreak that morning already. I really think it was a delayed reaction to the dog from Wednesday night but I may never really know for sure.

Hopefully the full blood panel (if I can ever get into my Dr.) will reveal if it is something else.

### Martha Ray — 2021-03-04T05:59:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hashis-and-hives.2377/post-296003>

> [MartiD said:](/goto/post?id=37135)
>
> Ive been having several bouts with hives for last 7 months and after being tested by a Allergist found out I'm allergic to most things I breathe. I had issues and shots as a kids but never really as an adult until my out breaks in January. I've been starting injections but had another outbreak on Saturday. I'm attributing it to a night of cuddling with my very freaked out dog on the 4th...he hates fireworks. He is unfortunately my highest allergy! My last outbreak was in February when I had feather pillows at a hotel (second highest allergy)! Anyway, I'm taking Allergra and Zantac per my Dr orders and it helping some with rash but minimal. I've been reading about hives and came across a large amount of people who have hives from their Hashis. Any one with Hashis have this issue? I don't think I have hashis as I have none of symptoms but I am trying to get in with a new Dr and get a full blood work up.

Well I'm relating to your post but your request was from 2011 did not read the whole thread yet to find help and find out what became of your story.

I am suffering with extremely dry skin Hives upper arms and now eczema on face looking for answers...I made a post in the beginner section but no one has noticed it yet and well I'm not very patient... so I found your Hives post. I do not want to take drugs Ketoconazole Cream 2% or Zantac as I hate pharmaceuticals and OTC drugs. I am taking 1 pharmaceutical NP thyroid for hypothyroidism.. not believe to caused be Hashimoto's but maybe caused by pool chemicals fluoride, chlorine and bromide harming my thyroid.

Here is what I posted.
Upper arm rashes (_hives like_) began onset Dec 2019 when HVac heat came on and they have gotten progressively worst since. I have experienced occasional twinges of upper arm discomfort in the past when exposed to 'foam' throw pillows while traveling and a few times out of the blue perhaps from eating out...I've thought it was a signal for some toxin exposure. I have **mild MCS and EMF s**ensitivity as well as **food sensitivities** to gluten, nightshades, MSG, **Oxalate** and high histamine foods...Also **sensitive to nickel**...(had to stop wearing earrings about 14 years ago) so I question a lot of things.

We replaced our HVac and duct work went from fiberboard to metal duck work...rashes have only been worse. I asked the installer to please wipe down the metal with rubbing alcohol before installing...I do not believe they did that as when I asked they said it would be fine since all metal was covered over...I should have pressed harder as I have no idea how much nickel might be the duck work. I has been 4 months getting worse was as weather has gotten colder and the system runs more. Vents are also is putting out more dust than I think they should. We have two _Austin Air_ units I run to try and keep particles down.
I am the worst at night when I lay down. Bed is dunlop-latex have had 10+ years. Took all bedding mostly organic cottons and down pillows to commercial washers and washed them and dried them multiple times and I replaced any old foam ones with perma-loft down alternative –that I know bothered me topically. Replaced a lot of older bed sheets and linens as well.

I have a very long list of things I've questioned and a very long list of things I'm trying...some are a natural antihistamine recipe, coptis tincture, black seed oil, oregano oil, ..... **salt and baking soda baths and drinking 1 tsp charcoal powder in water helps when I'm at my worst**.

DO doctor suggested:
**\-GI Revive** by _Designs for Health_
[https://shop.designsforhealth.com/gi-revive-powder?quantity=1&custcol\_dfh\_size=30](https://shop.designsforhealth.com/gi-revive-powder?quantity=1&custcol_dfh_size=30) for gut lining and skin.
and
\-Rubbing my arms regularly with **coconut oil with drops of essential oil** of either lavender or oregano.
**He suspects** a 'bug' **… parasites.**

So I wonder about...
Parasites? HVac, w/ metal duct work? EMF? Oxalate dumping?
**Food ?** \-may need to do an **elimination diet**\-..I mostly eat seafood and other meats, leafy greens and non- starchy veggies. But I have had too many food indiscretions in regard to my sensitivities pushing the envelope this past year at least more so than preceding decade. Hoping to get strict and stay there. I Intermittent fast daily 18/6 for over 4 years now and do 3 day water fasts 2 or 3 times a year.

**I suspect any and all of the boat load of supplements** I take-- working on a list of current ones. I have done a ton of looking at and learning and I have experienced a lot of them. I have a 57 page _Word_ document 11-point font, single line spacing of what I've taken over the past 12 years after cancer. Some stuff for upper right quadrant pain, possible candida, lower right quadrant pain, other rashes for 5+ years (after seeing many derma and MD’s I finally Dx myself with **_Intertrigo)._** One spot cleared completely and one spot controlled w/ rotations of **B-6 & keeping dry)**, blood clot, ulcer, hypothyroidism, inflammation, multiple severe sore throats, joint and musculoskeletal pain and multiple other strange side effects and sensations again long list of those.
I also hate fillers in supplements but fall for the propaganda and 'hope' in a bottle mentality. The illness is that I keep buying and trying them. **There needs to be some experts who help people evaluate what supplements they take to see if they are at all needful.** I took _Biotin_ but upped it and added **_ALA_** due to Dec 2020 **_Spectracell Micronutrient_** test showing low in those—‘me thinks’ a possible misnomer to think that pills will help.

I **suspect my pharmaceutical drug** and fillers as well. I take only **NP thyroid** brand desiccated thyroid now. Had to change brands during time of rashes worsening.
**I hate pharmaceuticals**\-one conventional MD prescribed **Ketoconazole Cream 2% last month** for my arm rashes ...but while I filled it I have not been able to take it, first due to hating drugs and secondly because I also found it has a **_black box warning_**.

Lastly I have one crown that might have decay under it set to see new biological dentist in March.

I have not had back to back pain free weeks since 2009 cancer and cancer treatments of cut, poison and burn. I feel I have reasonable internal health...no colds or flu for the past 7 years. Digestion is good and elimination and sleep are fair.

I am thankful for the suffering I have had because it keeps me seeking recovery and optimal health.

**Anyone with clues to upper arm rashes?**

### Dan2 — 2021-03-04T09:15:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hashis-and-hives.2377/post-296006>

[@Martha Ray](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22506/)

"I have mild MCS and EMF sensitivity as well as food sensitivities to gluten, nightshades, MSG, Oxalate and high histamine foods"

"I suspect any and all of the boat load of supplements I take-- working on a list of current ones. I have done a ton of looking at and learning and I have experienced a lot of them."

Eczema and rashes can be because of disregulation of the immune system, and that can happen from long-term use of small amounts of plant medicines in supplements that are marketed sometimes nowadays as being something good to take regularly as a preventative even though the plant would've traditionally in herbal medicine have been recommended to be used as a shorter-term thing and with a specific kind of preparation method to fit the reason for using that plant, because with longer use and/or a preparation method that changes the chemical composition to something that doesn't fit as well for the condition then the chemical effects can (more or less gradually depending which chemicals from which plant and preparation method) impair, disregulate the immune system. That could increase sensitivities to other chemicals, whether in plants or in modern synthetic chemical products.

So what supplements do you use (including topically)?

### Martha Ray — 2021-03-05T08:37:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hashis-and-hives.2377/post-296027>

Give me some time to get them all listed...
Up too late tonight getting ready for company tomorrow
a few of them
\-[_CoQ10 & BioPQQ with Shilajit_](https://globalhealing.com/coq10-biopqq-supplement) _Global Healing Center_
\-Enzymes Nzimes- _Enzymes Inc._ [Genuine Nzimes](http://www.enzymesinc.com/genuinenzimes-original.html) _Dr. Howell’s Original Formula with every meal_ and [Ox Bile](http://www.nutricology.com/ox-bile-125-mg-180-caps) NutriCology with meals Ox Bile with very heavy fat and meat meals
\-_Zen Mind_ _NutriCology_ occasionally at night
\-[Curamin Extra Strength by _Terry Naturally_](http://www.vitacost.com/terry-naturally-curamin-extra-strength-60-tablets-1)

\-Multiple, Vit C, D, B Comples, Omega's,Vegetarian Tryptophan
and about 10 other bottles from _Food Research_ I don't take these daily

Local DO suggested these very recent additions
_\-Lithium 1mg Pure Encapsulation_ just began last week
_\-GI Revive_ _Designs for Health_ just began last week
**\-**[Antronex](https://www.standardprocess.com/Products/Standard-Process/Antronex#.VmNvb7grJMw) by _Standard Process_

\-[Magnesium L-Threonate _Dr. Mercola_](http://products.mercola.com/magnesium-supplement/) take daily 3 caps with each of my two daily meals Breakfast at 11 and dinner between 5 and 6
\-Cod Liver Oil _Carlson_ occasionally with dinner
\-Quercetin _Pure Encapsulation_

Began ALA and Biotin as tested low on _Spectracell_ micronutrient testing at the end of Dec 2020
\-[Alpha Lipoic Sustain with Biotin](http://www.jarrow.com/product/281/Alpha_Lipoic_Sustain) by _Jarrow_ time released
\-Biotin 5,000 mcg _Healthy Origins_

\- Nettle Powder
\-Moranga Powder Mercola

I think I need supplement rehabilitation. While I do not take many regualrly some for only short trys.... I still take a lot of different things over time. And I keep buying new ones.

Beginning to think of them in the same way as pharmaceutical drugs. I only take one pharmaceutical, decicated thyroid (porcine) but the hives began getting bad about the time I began taking a new brand so think I need to consider rehab from that drug as well.
The newer brand I've been using this last year is _Food Research_ [https://www.buyfoodresearch.com/](https://www.buyfoodresearch.com/) similar to _Standard Process_ [http://www.standardprocess.com/](http://www.standardprocess.com/) in some ways.

### Dan2 — 2021-03-05T12:53:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hashis-and-hives.2377/post-296032>

The general approach for supplement rehab could be:

\-- To use less of the vitamin or mineral supplements, get those nutrients in an animal food so it's a form that'll be most useable and you're not getting fillers in supplements or extra chemicals in plants that would have the nutrient. Exceptions could be fruit for vitamin C (and rose hips tea is good for vitamin C) and seaweed for iodine.

\-- Instead of the supplements that have a mixture of herbal ingredients that are extracted and sometimes standardized (GI Revive Designs for Health, Terry Naturally Curamin, maybe others you didn't say yet) and so have proportions of chemicals that affect the immune system differently than the proportions from another preparation method would, maybe using a different preparation method with some of the ingredients.

With the Curamin, it has frankincense and turmeric extracts and turmeric essential oil; you could try inhaling and/or putting on topically frankincense and turmeric essential oils so it's not affecting the immune system the same way because of not being digested and it might still do the pain relief. (Or maybe you don't use that one often, but still the example of a different preparation than standardized extracts mixed with fillers.)

And the GI Revive -- those herbal ingredients (other than okra) are usually made into a tea because the herbal chemicals being mixed with more volume of liquid than just stomach juice (and the temperature of the tea too) changes the digestive effect; and even though it's small amounts of each, digesting them all together and as powder instead of infusion might be enough difference for your digestion and immune system when you already have chemical sensitivities. The GI Revive ingredients are like a mixture of bone broth, fruit jam, and herb powders. Each of them can help with the digestive system somehow, so on paper it could seem like adding each of them to the formula adds another benefit to the digestive system that makes the supplement better in more ways, but when they're all digested together the immune system doesn't react to it as a makes-sense-on-paper combo, it'll react to it like it's a bone broth fruit jam with herbal powder and that's a kinda weird combo. Whereas maybe drinking a tea of some of the herbal ingredients, or drinking bone broth, or eating fruit at different times would affect the immune system better.

And then you could use the Zinc L-Carnosine (also called polaprezinc) in the GI Revive as a separate supplement, maybe with one or another thing that it would combine better with than the whole GI Revive mixture (probably bone broth because the l-carnosine and zinc are both in meat (but separately, not as the one-molecule zinc l-carnosine chelate)). "...since 2009 cancer and cancer treatments of cut, poison and burn." The Zinc L-Carnosine is probably a good recommendation by the DO.
"Clinical studies have also shown its efficacy for oral mucositis, esophagitis, proctitis, taste alteration and dermatitis during and after radiotherapy... Per preclinical data, zinc L-carnosine is superior to zinc sulfide for mucositis.\[10\]" ([https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zinc\_L-carnosine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zinc_L-carnosine))
"Mucositis is the painful inflammation and ulceration of the mucous membranes lining the digestive tract, usually as an adverse effect of chemotherapy and radiotherapy treatment for cancer." ([https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mucositis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mucositis))

And back to the idea of making a tea with herbal ingredients instead of digesting it as a powder, maybe the moringa and nettle would digest better that way. I mean those probably aren't a problem because it's such small amounts of something that can be eaten as food, but generally with an herb that can be eaten it'll still digest better to cook it or make a tea with it instead of powder in a capsule washed down with water.

\_\_\_

About Alpha Lipoic Acid...

"I took Biotin but upped it and added ALA due to Dec 2020 Spectracell Micronutrient test showing low in those—‘me thinks’ a possible misnomer to think that pills will help."
‘me thinks’

The Jarrow ALA + Biotin has 300 mg ALA. ALA is a chelator of metals, but doesn't hold onto the metal more than about a few hours; so it can cause metals to get moved around, become unbound from the ALA, and the metals need to be moved out of the blood and put somewhere. That can cause immune system reactions. People use ALA as a metal chelator by dosing it regularly around the clock to reduce symptoms from the metals redistributing from the blood after becoming unbound from the ALA.

[https://andy-cutler-chelation.com/](https://andy-cutler-chelation.com/)
\[Under the "DR ANDY CUTLER’S ORAL CHELATION PROTOCOL" heading then the "ALA" heading\]
"Start ALA at low doses of 12.5mg to ensure few or no adverse effects, and add it after many rounds of DMSA alone. ALA can increase side effects in a mercury toxic person and you may need to reduce the dose to 6.25mg if that is the case. It is important not to rush this process. Although the goal is to eventually get to higher doses as stated in AI (100-200mg ALA per dose), that is very high dose and can cause bad or intolerable side effects in some, so work up SLOWLY. It can take some people years to get to this point. Starting low and working up is the safest way to proceed to avoid exacerbating symptoms. The higher dosage seems to make a more dramatic difference ultimately, but it takes a long time to get there safely."

(Related to the Andy Culter chelation methods in general -- there's a better chelator than ALA now, that was invented recently, that has a much longer half-life and so's safer, called NBMI or OSR or Emeramide.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/depression-and-nnemf.21987/#post-293328](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/depression-and-nnemf.21987/#post-293328) )

So that 300 mg of ALA is a lot in context of it being a chelator, although they're also talking about that being a lot for someone who has enough mercury for it to interact much with. But it's still a lot compared to there being much smaller "doses" of lipoic acid in foods that have relatively a lot of it. I made a post on the Andy Cutler Forums asking about lipoic acid from food instead of supplementing ALA, and that has some info about amounts of it in food and differences of the food form from supplemented ALA; maybe read just the first eight paragraphs:

[https://andy-cutler-chelation.com/mercury-detox-forums/viewtopic.php?f=2&t=341](https://andy-cutler-chelation.com/mercury-detox-forums/viewtopic.php?f=2&t=341)

So instead of supplementing amounts of ALA that can cause chelation immune reactions, you could probably get enough lipoic acid from meat and eggs to more gently raise the level from the test result.
(And I saw in another thread you asked if local eggs are good to eat year-round; yeah they are.)

\_\_\_

"I only take one pharmaceutical, decicated thyroid (porcine) but the hives began getting bad about the time I began taking a new brand so think I need to consider rehab from that drug as well."

This is a desiccated thyroid that's popular on the Ray Peat Forum:

[https://lifegivingstore.com/grass-fed-beef-thyroid-natural-desiccated-thyroid-ndt/](https://lifegivingstore.com/grass-fed-beef-thyroid-natural-desiccated-thyroid-ndt/)

[https://raypeatforum.com/community/forums/natural-dessicated-thyroid.113/](https://raypeatforum.com/community/forums/natural-dessicated-thyroid.113/)

Also, I just saw this:

[https://raypeatforum.com/community/threads/another-great-ndt-source-potenttially.38916/](https://raypeatforum.com/community/threads/another-great-ndt-source-potenttially.38916/)

That one's 45 g for $55
[https://organsupplements.com/collec.../thyroid-support-hyperthyroid-and-hypothyroid](https://organsupplements.com/collections/glandulars/products/thyroid-support-hyperthyroid-and-hypothyroid)

compared to 5 g for $30 from LifeGivingStore's
[https://lifegivingstore.com/grass-fed-beef-thyroid-natural-desiccated-thyroid-ndt/](https://lifegivingstore.com/grass-fed-beef-thyroid-natural-desiccated-thyroid-ndt/)

Maybe there's a difference even though for the ingredients it just says "beef thyroid". In that Ray Peat Forum thread it says there's more moisture; that's probably not enough to be much of the weight difference though.

Another glandular that might be helpful to use, to help regulate your immune system, is beef thymus gland. This one's a much better price than most glandular supplement companies:
[https://www.curease.com/product-page/argentine-beef-thymus](https://www.curease.com/product-page/argentine-beef-thymus)
That business also has beef adrenal for the same prices.
I'm not sure with thymus how much of the dried powder would be an overdose. Thyroid can be overdosed because of the hormones in it, and thymus has hormones in it too (that affect the immune system). I was told by the owner of a glandular supplement company that the desiccated powder is usually equal to 5-6x of a fresh gland. So maybe about a teaspoon isn't too much (after increasing to that)?

### Martha Ray — 2021-03-07T03:54:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hashis-and-hives.2377/post-296087>

Wow Dan2 Thank you for all the infomation! Will study this!

# Thread: N = 1 Ketogenesis and BP
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1-ketogenesis-and-bp.2380/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dan in Utah — 2012-07-12T18:04:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1-ketogenesis-and-bp.2380/post-37186>

I've been checking my BP 3-4x/week over the past 9 months. Prior to paleo/leptin etc. I was averaging 130/84. This month I've averaged 100/60 . In fact, I'm now supplementing with NaCL and KCL now to keep the dizzies away.

The following study focuses on ketosis and T2D, but the BP results were quite pronounced as well: The effect of a low-carbohydrate, ketogenic diet versus a low-glycemic index diet on glycemic control in type 2 diabetes mellitus [http://www.nutritionandmetabolism.com/content/5/1/36/](http://http://www.nutritionandmetabolism.com/content/5/1/36/)

### ealachan — 2012-07-12T18:17:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1-ketogenesis-and-bp.2380/post-37188>

Seconded. My BP has been consistantly lower since I started low-carbing. I'm still on a fluid pill that I've been on for a few years, but I was averaging BP of 135/85 with the med before going low-carb, and now I average around 115/80. I'd like to ditch the fluid pill, but I'm a little scared of what it might do to me.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-12T18:24:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1-ketogenesis-and-bp.2380/post-37192>

I had a fast drop in blood pressure upon going VLC high animal fat, in just a couple weeks I had halved my BP medicine, and a couple weeks later I dropped it entirely. You have to be careful, with all the sodium being flushed, quite a bit potassium is also lost. At least at the beginning.

I remember being quite angry to learn that there was such a simple quick solution to something the medical establishment claims is a life-long problem to be carefully managed. Maybe everyone can't get perfect blood pressure in a month, but lots of us can.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-12T18:29:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1-ketogenesis-and-bp.2380/post-37194>

Nanchalant I feel just like you! I tell everyone that they can lower their meds or possibly even get off of them if they just eliminate grains and starchy carbs. My BP went down enough for me to completely go off meds within a month. I monitor the BP several times a day because of the T3 I take so I know it is always within normal range. After 10+ years I am doing a happy dance for my BP!

### Dan in Utah — 2012-07-12T18:30:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1-ketogenesis-and-bp.2380/post-37195>

I was nervous about adding salt to everything, despite the fact that I was dizzy without it. Then I read this interesting blog post from Peter Attia,M.D. [http://eatingacademy.com/nutrition/what-i-actually-eat](http://http://eatingacademy.com/nutrition/what-i-actually-eat) He adds 2 to 6 grams of salt daily, a couple of teaspoons basically. Along with less dizziness, I've found I have less muscle cramping with this, too. Salt is not a bad idea for adrenal fatigue issues either.

### bigknitwit — 2012-07-12T20:38:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1-ketogenesis-and-bp.2380/post-37226>

I'm very interested in your experience. Something doesn't seem to be adding up though. If you are experiencing dizziness alleviated by salt intake, that (at least in my mind) points to adrenal insufficiency, especially given the lower blood pressure. What is your heart rate like with the lower blood pressure? If it's adrenal related, I'm thinking your would be seeing higher heartrates, to compensate for the lower blood pressure (especially during the while feeling dizzy). Do you take thyroid hormone? I get like this when my thyroid levels are too low (like now, I have been struggling with this for the past several months).

### Dan in Utah — 2012-07-12T22:37:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1-ketogenesis-and-bp.2380/post-37254>

> **bigknitwit;37393 wrote:** I'm very interested in your experience. Something doesn't seem to be adding up though. If you are experiencing dizziness alleviated by salt intake, that (at least in my mind) points to adrenal insufficiency, especially given the lower blood pressure. What is your heart rate like with the lower blood pressure? If it's adrenal related, I'm thinking your would be seeing higher heartrates, to compensate for the lower blood pressure (especially during the while feeling dizzy). Do you take thyroid hormone? I get like this when my thyroid levels are too low (like now, I have been struggling with this for the past several months).

My resting HR is 50. I'm not dizzy all the time, mostly just from changing positions, like standing up after pulling weeds in my garden. My cramps were mostly in my lower legs, always in the early morning, prior to getting up.

I suspect my adrenal axis struggles due to 20 years of sleep deprivation and stress. I don't have cortisol numbers to back that up (yet), but I did feel better on DHEA, but I've stopped taking it for a few months to see what my endogenous production is like. A few months back, testing my testosterone was borderline low, SHBG high, so I imagine the ole adrenals are struggling a bit. I guess I could check salivary cortisol, aldactone, pregneneolone, etc. If been interested to see if my T improved with sprinting, weights, and CT.

My last thyroid levels were okay other than mildly elevated rT3. More on my little saga is here: [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?322-Hack-my-labs-LDL-and-Total-Cholesterol-have-gone-psycho-on-Paleo-Leptin-CT&highlight=nutso](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?322-Hack-my-labs-LDL-and-Total-Cholesterol-have-gone-psycho-on-Paleo-Leptin-CT&highlight=nutso) and here [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?750-Do-I-have-a-thryroid-problem-Hack-my-thryoid-panel&highlight=utah](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?750-Do-I-have-a-thryroid-problem-Hack-my-thryoid-panel&highlight=utah)

I'll post my 24 hour cortisol when I do it. Full labs (blood) drawn this am.

Thanks for chiming in.

# Thread: Paleo Blog About Disordered Eating
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleo-blog-about-disordered-eating.2381/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1

### indigogirl — 2012-07-12T18:37:46+0300

# Thread: Can't make ice fast enough
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cant-make-ice-fast-enough.2382/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cowboy — 2012-07-12T19:22:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cant-make-ice-fast-enough.2382/post-37204>

This summer in the rockies has been record breaking hot. In the mid to high 90's in the daytime and at night instead of the normal 55-60 it is 65-70 at night day after day. My tub water is getting hotter and hotter with my tap water also getting hotter and hotter. Runoff from the mountains is minimal at this point and the high lakes that feed my towns drinking supply are also warming fast and higher than most normal years.

Can't seem to get under 60 degrees for my tub,unless I use more than 50 lbs of ice, and I can't make it fast enough. I now have 1/4 of my freezer relegated to ice making.

I know other must be having the same difficulties, and I would be curious as to the possible solutions. Aquarium coolers were mentioned, but have heard nothing in any detail. Also read about a homemade system with cooling coils from a dehumidifier, but it was outside my technical skills. I am just looking for any practical solution to getting the water colder without buying ice daily and without investing more than $500 on some type of cooler.

It also seems that the CT section is getting low activity. Could others be having the same difficulties with getting water cold enough? or is there just nothing more to say about CT?

### MartiD — 2012-07-12T19:33:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cant-make-ice-fast-enough.2382/post-37206>

cowboy, I understand your frustrations. I have same issues in Mid-Missouri...tap water is about 75 so it is really hard to get it down to even 55. I do freeze 2 ltr bottles with water and put in the tub and let them sit for a while and that helps. I also bag my own ice from ice maker but I don't have the funds available to spend $4 on a large bags of ice that will do down the drain in an hour. guess it will be nice when fall arrives and water temps drop!

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-12T20:11:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cant-make-ice-fast-enough.2382/post-37219>

I don't know cowboy... Don't u live higher up? I'm I'm the boulder creek as I'm writing this and it's 60 degrees in town I bet it's nice and cold a bit higher up

60 is good if it's free and comes with the nice view

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### JimG — 2012-07-12T20:54:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cant-make-ice-fast-enough.2382/post-37232>

I have the same issue in cooling the water, so how much down does the water temp go with how many many freezed bottles, just curious

### PrimalPam — 2012-07-12T22:47:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cant-make-ice-fast-enough.2382/post-37259>

I'm in Houston, where the tap is over 80F. So hard to get it into the low 50's!

I have a side-by-side refrigerator/freezer, and about 70% of my freezer space is now devoted to ice.

I use large Rubbermaid containers and large plastic containers of all types, plus the throw-away kinds you get for drinks, etc. I stack them all up and it takes about 24 hours for all of them to freeze.

I'm single, so I don't need a lot of freezer space, but I'm also not using the ice from my icemaker, which I could. I don't want to overwork the icemaker, plus I like to have that ice for drinks. But I could definitely empty that one out for the baths.

I think the CT area is getting lower traffic right now due to summer vacations, etc. Plus, I think a lot of us have gotten our questions answered, so I think we're all busy in the tub!

### popcornsally — 2012-07-12T23:52:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cant-make-ice-fast-enough.2382/post-37288>

I live in Phoenix and my tap water has been coming out between 85 and 90 degrees! I found if I fill the tub with water and let it set there overnight, it gets down to about 72 degrees since our air conditioner is set to 73-80 degrees throughout a 24 hour period.

Today, I added about 3 lb. of ice cubes, 2 half gallon zip top bags of solid ice, a couple of smaller food containers with frozen ice a a couple of gel pacs to the water. It went from 71.5 to 65.5 in about 10 minutes. I'm sure it could go lower by adding more ice cubes. I'm still just starting, so I was happy with 65.5.

Anyway, my tip was to put the water in the tub many hours ahead of time if you have really warm tap water like I do.

### PrimalPam — 2012-07-13T05:06:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cant-make-ice-fast-enough.2382/post-37402>

> **popcornsally;37455 wrote:** I live in Phoenix and my tap water has been coming out between 85 and 90 degrees! I found if I fill the tub with water and let it set there overnight, it gets down to about 72 degrees since our air conditioner is set to 73-80 degrees throughout a 24 hour period.

O.M.G.!! This is one of the best tips I've seen in a long time! I'm doing it tonight for tomorrow night's bath. Thanks!

### Cowboy — 2012-07-13T16:03:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cant-make-ice-fast-enough.2382/post-37470>

I got out my accurate thermometer to see what exactly my water temps were. Started out in my stock tank yesterday at 69 degrees at 3PM and with all my ice was able to drop it only 2 degrees to 67 degrees. It is now 7 AM and the low last night was 60 degrees outside. 150 gal tubs cool down slowly and warm up slowly. We have a cheap ice supplier in my town. I am going to see what $10.00 worth will do.

I did stay in for a full 60 minutes yesterday and it did feel good. I do wonder if it is really worth it to bother with the cold (relative) soaks at such high temps.

# Thread: CT and Thyroid
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-thyroid.2384/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### youthfuliz — 2012-07-12T21:46:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-thyroid.2384/post-37247>

I'd like to use this thread to share information on the effect of CT on thyroid.

I just found that I have benign thyroid nodules. I don't have hyper or hypo symptoms. I started CT in March and have been doing it regularly (5 times a week, with some breaks). The lowest temperature I have done is 52F, usually taking 40 minutes bath, followed by a cold shower. I warm up very easily and I enjoy the cold water. It stimulates me. Actually I feel better when it's colder.

Question for all those CT-ers with thyroid issues:

Do you think CT improves your thyroid function? (I think AKman has a thread on this)

Can it be too much for your thyroid, so you have to go slower with CT?

Do you take extra supplements for your thyroid?

Can CT reduce benign thyroid nodules?

Any suggestions for CT-ers who have thyroid problem?

Dr. Kruse if you could write a blog on Thyroid that'll be great! Or do a webinar on it!

### AKMan — 2012-07-13T20:20:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-thyroid.2384/post-37542>

I don't think CT in any form has any impact directly on your thyroid. I think CT, in conjunction with a healthy diet--be it full-fledged ketogenic sea-food or a strict paleo diet--will ensure a healthy hormonal state in the body which in turn ensures the thyroid is doing the best it can.

It seems to me that proper thyroid function only comes about when the entire endocrine system is working properly.

To answer your questions (I'm no expert):

Do you think CT improves your thyroid function? Yes, in conjunction with diet.

Can it be too much for your thyroid, so you have to go slower with CT? No, go harder.

Do you take extra supplements for your thyroid? I take 2 or 3 150mcg kelp pills on days I don't eat seaweed. I eat 2-3 brazil nuts every day.

Can CT reduce benign thyroid nodules? No

Any suggestions for CT-ers who have thyroid problem? Get a TSH, T4, T3, and rT3 test every 3 months and medicate accordingly. If you have auto-immune condition (ie. hashimotos) that must be dealt with above all. If you have a slow thyroid w/o AI, CT and diet will fix it. I think you are better off with slightly out-of-range TSH/T3/T4 on NO meds than perfect TSH/T3/T4 ON meds. Taking thyroid medicine (Synthroid, Armour, Cytomel, etc....) basically shuts down your thyroid since the feedback loop in your brain sees the T3 as normal, preventing your thyroid from producing it's own T1, T2, T3, and T4--the doctor will tell you this is fine and dandy, but don't believe him!

Just my thoughts from battling conventional wisdom.

### youthfuliz — 2012-07-14T16:37:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-thyroid.2384/post-37747>

Akman, thanks! I have a lot to learn about thyroid hormones. I'm working on my iodine deficiency issue and then I'm sure CT will help with improving thyroid function. Will definitely want to have a full panel thyroid blood test as soon as I find a good doctor that understands iodine deficiency. Will report back.

### jkt2 — 2012-07-23T09:50:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-thyroid.2384/post-40365>

Does anyone know what's recommended to "fix" Hashimoto's? Dr. K. suspects I may have it, but I haven't done the labs yet...

### Claudia — 2012-07-24T23:45:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-thyroid.2384/post-40857>

I just got diagnosed hypo and was put on Cytomel, very low dose 5mcg once a day. I've been fighting the hypo symptoms for years now, didn't get tested to find out, hoping my symptoms would get better with diet. I've been Paleo for years and use a lot of coconut oil in my diet (said to be good for thyroid). I just wasn't feeling better and decided to try the Cytomel (which is t3 only). I'm looking at your post AKMan and wondering if I should be on it. Or can I stay on it short term? Not sure if I have Hashi's. I had very little antibodies on the test,

### youthfuliz — 2012-07-24T23:51:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-thyroid.2384/post-40863>

> **jkt2;40553 wrote:** Does anyone know what's recommended to "fix" Hashimoto's? Dr. K. suspects I may have it, but I haven't done the labs yet...

I suggest that you do a search on hashimoto I think you'll find posts on it... I remember seeing people talking about it here.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-24T23:57:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-thyroid.2384/post-40866>

Claudia - did they put you on T3 only because of a rT3 problem or another reason? I'm just wondering out loud why you didn't get a natural dessicated thyroid. 5 mcg is a very small dose so if you start to feel like you are loosing energy you will probably need to raise it a bit. I was on 70 mcg and now I'm on 56 mcg. Most people are at 70-100 mcg for feeling the best. Some people take it for life, many people in fact. I don't know about the thyroid completely shutting down but I have read that once you are on meds for a while (a year or two) with Hashis you most likely will be on meds for life. You couldn't pay me to go back to being hypo and living like that...I'll take the meds thank you! I think most severely hypo people would agree.

Your antibodies question is something that seems to have differing opinions. Some say any presence of AB mean Hashis and some say you have to be outside the range as our bodies can always produce a little ABs. I don't know which is right. You can always "act as if" and eat the autoimmune Epi-paleo diet and make sure your thyroid isn't getting destroyed.

I usually take 200 mg selenium and for a time went to 400 (max anyone should take) for a few weeks just to build up the load.

You probably already have but go to [www.stopthethyroidmadness.com](http://http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com) for lots of info on thyroid dysfunction. It's good basic info about the little gland. But keep in mind she doesn't do a whole lot of connecting the dots with hormones, etc, although she talks a lot about adrenals.

# Thread: internal trembling -started at same time as Leptin RX- will it stop?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/internal-trembling-started-at-same-time-as-leptin-rx-will-it-stop.2386/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### 2bls — 2012-07-12T23:28:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/internal-trembling-started-at-same-time-as-leptin-rx-will-it-stop.2386/post-37279>

I did a short reply on another post about "pins and needles" saying that I am experiencing something similar since starting LR. I seem to have a strong humming/vibration feeling throughout my body and even some mild head tremors when really tired or emotionally upset or angry. Would this be some kind of detox reaction to the LR? I'm planning to have a Naturopath do a complete workup but can't afford it right now. I am currently using Holy Basil and Relora moring and evening to reduce the vibration feeling and the internal trembling but would be interested in any similar experiences since starting LR.

### 2bls — 2012-07-13T19:00:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/internal-trembling-started-at-same-time-as-leptin-rx-will-it-stop.2386/post-37510>

so I had an emergency appt with my Chiropractor this am. She feels the internal trembling and head tremors are temporary nerve damage that attacked the nerve endings during a terrible respiratory virus I had 3 months ago. She did some active release therapy on my neck region and will reasses on Monday but she says as the neck issue settles down, it should reduce the head tremors as it seems clear to her that it's directly connected to the neck/virus problem. I do see that in the few hours since my appt, the internal trembling and head tremors have reduced but not completely gone, but I have hope this issue will completely resolve itself.

I think I was giving myself anxiety attacks about it thinking I was having neurological issues. She seems very sure that it is actually structural in nature so that makes me feel so much less worried! I am still going to keep up with the Holy Basil and Relora as I am feeling less emotional and sleep much better with a couple of these per day.

### TerrierMom — 2012-07-21T01:56:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/internal-trembling-started-at-same-time-as-leptin-rx-will-it-stop.2386/post-39854>

Glad it seems to be resolving! Just keep questioning  and good luck on the reset!

# Thread: frost bite
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frost-bite.2388/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Souldanzer — 2012-07-13T01:26:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frost-bite.2388/post-37315>

I got frost bite a week and a half ago on my tummy  It went from bright red, painful and swollen to looking like a bruise and itchy, and now it still looks a bit bruised but doesn't hurt anymore. Today I noticed the skin has started to blister with clear fluid and it looks like it is going to peel.

Is this how it's supposed to heal or am I in for a date with my PCP?

### jihad4o — 2012-07-13T09:08:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frost-bite.2388/post-37438>

Did you continue icing after you got burned?

\-10 degree ice(straight out of the freezer) can burn sometimes I guess(or wrong O6/O3?), but once you do it - you stop. Coconut oil, face dunks, and after a couple of days you continue with "warmer" ice.

I think that blisters are a higher degree frostbite.

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Frostbite](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Frostbite)

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-13T10:40:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frost-bite.2388/post-37445>

uh, not good. It didn't blister until a week and a half after the initial exposure though. I did stop icing after it happened. I grabbed the wrong bag of ice I think is what happened. I had a gallon ziplock full of water and one that had alcohol added. I use the one with alcohol for baths but somehow it ended up on my tummy that night.

It's discolored still, sorta blackish in spots. I hope this is going to go away??

### MonteD — 2012-07-13T18:19:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frost-bite.2388/post-37490>

On the upside the area may not be sensitive to cold anymore.

In any case skin is very regenerative and I doubt you have full blown level two frostbite. Sounds like level 1.5......

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-13T18:26:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frost-bite.2388/post-37493>

Great

It's starting to peel today but no more blisters right now. I just don't want scars really. Got enough of them and it's in a rather inconvenient spot if I still want to wear a bathing suit and not look like I got some sort of dangerous disease.

### WannaBeOptimal — 2012-07-13T19:02:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frost-bite.2388/post-37511>

I use bags mixed to 1 part alcohol 4 parts water. Can't keep them on bare skin for more than about 15 minutes, without getting those redish splotches all over my skin. I get good results (skin down to 0-5 degrees celsius), with these packs over a compresson shirt for an hour or more.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-16T11:25:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frost-bite.2388/post-38317>

Looks like the blackish stuff is starting to peel off and underneath is nicer skin. The spots are starting to fade a bit but still quite visible. Not sure yet if I permanently damaged my cold receptors...

# Thread: Not sure what it all means- thyroid
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-it-all-means-thyroid.2397/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sem — 2012-07-13T06:07:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-it-all-means-thyroid.2397/post-37421>

I posted my cholesterol numbers in another thread. My doctor wants me to start taking Crestor. I've always had cholesterol (total) under 200 and now the total is 222. My LDL is 142. HDL 62 and triglycerides 89. (all of this was not fasting)

What I am wondering about is my thyroid panel. I've always suspected a thyroid problem but everyone tells me it's fine.

TSH 1.5 mcIU/mL .3-47

Free T3 Index 83 78-162

T-Uptake 1.2 TBI .9-143

T3, Total 99 ng/dL 85-145

Free T4 Index 4.8 4.5-10.5

T4, Total 5.8 mcg/dL 4.9-11.4

T-Uptake 1.2 TBI .9-1.3

I'm on progesterone right now for ten days to try to get my period so I can take a hormone panel on day 18.

P.S. I am a 42 year old woman.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-13T07:28:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-it-all-means-thyroid.2397/post-37429>

Sem, good to know you did get a thyroid panel. I'd say both your T3 and T4 are low. Free T3 is what is available to you to use and you're at the very bottom of the range. In the webinar, I believe Dr Kruse said he wants us in the top quartile. This gives more credence to Nonchalant's idea that you're not converting your LDL properly. I don't know how it works with the taking P before getting a hormone panel (I'd assume you wouldn't take any hormones before taking a hormone panel... you don't have to be clycling to have hormones tested) but this looks to be interlocking with hormones. There's also the sleep-cortisol-melatonin-stress thing. How's your sleep? I'm guessing not perfect.

Again, I'd be wanting an hsCRP and Vitamin D, along with that hormone panel.

The fact that you've 'suspected' a problem says you're having symptoms and those symptoms can't be ignored. You definitely look to be hypothyroid. You could also get tested for Thyroid antibodies to rule out/rule in Hashimoto's.

If you haven't listened to the webinar I think you'd find it really helpful.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-13T07:43:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-it-all-means-thyroid.2397/post-37433>

PS If you haven't read it, read this blog (will help you with the big picture re: hormones and cholesterol as well as the need for hsCRP).

[http://jackkruse.com/hormone-cascade-101/](http://http://jackkruse.com/hormone-cascade-101/)

### Sem — 2012-07-13T08:08:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-it-all-means-thyroid.2397/post-37435>

She said she can't test my hormones correctly unless she knows where I am in my cycle. (she being my GYN). So she wants me to take the progesterone, get my period and test on day 18. My vitamin D is supposedly in the "optimum range" at 46.

I don't like that my ANA is 1:320. That is supposed to be 1:40 - 1:80.

### Sem — 2012-07-13T08:13:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-it-all-means-thyroid.2397/post-37436>

P.S. thanks so much for responding. I am trying to get on Dr. K's schedule for tomorrow. Do you happen to have a copy of the disclosure?

### Sem — 2012-07-13T08:14:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-it-all-means-thyroid.2397/post-37437>

PPS (that's so second grade) my sleep sucks and I have a LOT of stress.

### vkiernan — 2012-07-13T14:46:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-it-all-means-thyroid.2397/post-37458>

> **Sem;37602 wrote:** She said she can't test my hormones correctly unless she knows where I am in my cycle. (she being my GYN). So she wants me to take the progesterone, get my period and test on day 18. My vitamin D is supposedly in the "optimum range" at 46.
>
>
>
> I don't like that my ANA is 1:320. That is supposed to be 1:40 - 1:80.

D is NOT optimum for sure. Do what you have to do to get it up there and don't argue with them unless you can prove yourself. D is easy to get and test even if on your own. If your sleep sucks, you might have low dhea. Test w/saliva the first time and then test the dheas with blood after that. The progesterone might help the sleep issue too.

I agree w/Lauren, your FT3 is at the bottom, needs to be at the top. You got lot's going on to create that. All in good time you will fix it as you figure out all your ratios. I found since I don't retain a lot I had to pick apart the blog Lauren is talking about. Jack writes backwards for me and I had to put it down on paper in a picture to understand the what, why and wheres. It is a great blog if you can decipher it.

### kathylu — 2012-07-13T15:19:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-it-all-means-thyroid.2397/post-37463>

Sem, your ANA needs to be negative. Anything else is an indication of an autoimmune problem.

### colleencoble — 2012-07-13T17:33:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-it-all-means-thyroid.2397/post-37484>

And that autoimmune problem could very well be Hashi's. your thyroid is much too low. I'd get your antibodies tested too.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-13T19:37:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-it-all-means-thyroid.2397/post-37528>

I second everything said here and strongly suggest the thyroid antibody panel: TPO and TgAb to test for Hashis. That will surely contribute to your LDL number.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-13T22:34:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-it-all-means-thyroid.2397/post-37593>

I just want to say a huge thanks of gratitude to all of you... people like Colleen and VKiernan, PattyCakes and Kathylu are why I could respond to you, Sem, and have a clue what your labs meant. There is so much knowledge here and people share it willingly and I have personally benefited SO MUCH!

### colleencoble — 2012-07-13T22:57:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-it-all-means-thyroid.2397/post-37599>

Yes low thyroid can cause elevated cholesterol. BUT yours isn't elevated, Sem. It's actually very good. I wouldn't let the doctor scare you. I was THRILLED when mine went a little over 200 after being at 130 for years.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-14T00:26:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-it-all-means-thyroid.2397/post-37615>

> **Sem;37604 wrote:** PPS (that's so second grade) my sleep sucks and I have a LOT of stress.

One last comment, in case you haven't made this link. If you have adrenal issues and your stress/cortisol/melatonin balance isn't working properly, you won't be able to sort the other things sufficiently. If you listen to that bit in the webinar, you'll hear Dr K talking about this in detail. You didn't mention if you're paying attention (close attention!) to light cycles, but I think that helps majorly. The hormone panel will also help, though I'm still confused about taking P before testing it... seems like it would make P artifically high when it's not your body producing it. Maybe Colleen knows about this?

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-14T02:34:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-it-all-means-thyroid.2397/post-37637>

Look at this thread (re: not needing to cycle and still being able to draw a hormone panel). I'm worried your doc doesn't have a clue.

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2486-Hormone-test-timing](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?2486-Hormone-test-timing)

### Sem — 2012-07-14T02:51:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-it-all-means-thyroid.2397/post-37642>

Thank you so much for all your help! You have all been more support than you can imagine. I just did a brief consult with Dr. K and he agreed that I need to figure out whether I have Hashi's ASAP. He also raised some red flags and suggested some testing for my kids, which is not shocking to me. I'm going to try to get these labs done ASAP and then do a more in depth consult.

Thanks again! Have a great weekend.

### Sem — 2012-07-25T07:18:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-what-it-all-means-thyroid.2397/post-40947>

# Thread: Buffalo heart
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buffalo-heart.2401/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jmathisen — 2012-07-13T11:30:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buffalo-heart.2401/post-37447>

A cousin (inherited in the divorce, ) is giving me a bunch of organ meat that they don't eat. Elk liver, deer liver, moose liver, some various bear parts, (I drew the line at kidneys...) and some other stuff, but tonight I stopped by and picked up a buffalo heart.

Any ideas on how to prepare it? Quite frankly, it looks like a medium-sized ham. Sucker is big...

I'm thinking bacon has to be involved in the process, but outside of slice and fry, no real good ideas are coming to mind. I did enjoy the chicken hearts, but now that "fowl is foul" from BG4, I'm all depressed, as I liked the hearts and the liver, well, with time, I can see it happening.

### vkiernan — 2012-07-13T14:39:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buffalo-heart.2401/post-37457>

> **jmathisen;37614 wrote:** A cousin (inherited in the divorce, ) is giving me a bunch of organ meat that they don't eat. Elk liver, deer liver, moose liver, some various bear parts, (I drew the line at kidneys...) and some other stuff, but tonight I stopped by and picked up a buffalo heart.
>
>
>
> Any ideas on how to prepare it? Quite frankly, it looks like a medium-sized ham. Sucker is big...
>
>
>
> I'm thinking bacon has to be involved in the process, but outside of slice and fry, no real good ideas are coming to mind. I did enjoy the chicken hearts, but now that "fowl is foul" from BG4, I'm all depressed, as I liked the hearts and the liver, well, with time, I can see it happening.

Lucky you. I am not sure the organs are foul from fowl. Good question for Jack. The meat is but I would further research before you give up on the organs.

### tweety — 2012-07-13T15:51:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buffalo-heart.2401/post-37469>

I still eat the chicken livers and hearts. I figured if they are properly raised they still have important nutrients. I haven't worked up the courage to down the beef hearts and livers. Plus every time we buy a quarter side of beef they discard the organs, despite my requests to leave them in!

As to the original poster, I would grind it up and add it to my ground meat. But I like Souldanzer's plan to make jerky! I wish we had a good source of jerky here- that would be awesome!

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-13T16:26:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buffalo-heart.2401/post-37475>

I've go heart defrosting in my fridge right now as we speak - I'm planning on making jerky - but there's a part of me that wants DH to throw it on the grill for me tonight

### Tizawee — 2012-07-13T16:47:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buffalo-heart.2401/post-37476>

Heart is a working muscle and tends to be tough. You could braise it (check any recipe site for braising instructions; it's basically cooking in liquid). Or you could cube it & make stew.

### johnnyb — 2012-07-13T16:52:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buffalo-heart.2401/post-37478>

The beef heart I have is scheduled to go into the grinder and become one of the meats in a meatloaf.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-13T19:34:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buffalo-heart.2401/post-37525>

I had my cow heart ground up with the liver and I add the mix to ground beef to make burgers. They have been my breakfast staple since January. I love the mix and so easy to get your organ meat into the diet.

I would try what some of the others have suggested and if you cannot stand the taste you can always try this idea as I don't hardly taste the liver in my burgers at all.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-14T03:42:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buffalo-heart.2401/post-37662>

We regularly get pastured ox and lamb hearts. I do what Jonnyb does and grind them up and add them to ground beef (our burgers are usually 1/3 heart, 1/3 liver/kidney and 1/3 ground beef.)

### SeaHorse — 2012-07-14T04:06:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buffalo-heart.2401/post-37667>

You can also stuff a heart with herbs and veggies and braise in a dutch oven with broth and wine (if that works for you).

# Thread: Hormone test timing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-test-timing.2405/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MartiD — 2012-07-13T17:16:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-test-timing.2405/post-37481>

I have seen alot of people mention when they do their hormone labs in relationship to their cycle. I am hopefully getting a full thyroid and hormone panel done in next month but I had a partial hysterectomy 3 years ago so I assume it doesn't matter what part of the month I do the testing? Does having an hysterectomy effect hormone levels differently from others? I do still have ovaries and am 42 with no pre-meni signs.

Also, should I stop all supps a week before the test so it doesn't mess up results? My main concerns on supps vit D, Iron, and 7 Keto. Thanks for any help!

### JanSz — 2012-07-13T19:16:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-test-timing.2405/post-37512>

> **MartiD;37648 wrote: I have seen alot of people mention when they do their hormone labs in relationship to their cycle.** I am hopefully getting a full thyroid and hormone panel done in next month but I had a partial hysterectomy 3 years ago so I assume it doesn't matter what part of the month I do the testing? Does having an hysterectomy effect hormone levels differently from others? I do still have ovaries and am 42 with no pre-meni signs.
>
>
>
> Also, should I stop all supps a week before the test so it doesn't mess up results? My main concerns on supps vit D, Iron, and 7 Keto. Thanks for any help!

This timing is mostly about testing hormones that change daily during menstrual cycle.

LH

FSH

Progesterone

Estradiol

Since it is not practical to draw blood daily for the whole cycle, days when min and max are expected are chosen for testing.

I prefer venous blood testing at LabCorp(I am not a doctor).

Genova Diagnostics offers saliva test, it takes 11 samples over cycle.

..........

When attempting Steroid Hormone Optimization, I suggest to start project optimizing day 2-4 of menstrual cycle.

.

### MartiD — 2012-07-13T23:35:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-test-timing.2405/post-37609>

So since I don't have a cycle it won't matter what day I draw?

### JanSz — 2012-07-13T23:56:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-test-timing.2405/post-37611>

> **MartiD;37776 wrote:** So since I don't have a cycle it won't matter what day I draw?

yes

....

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-14T03:07:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-test-timing.2405/post-37649>

if you still have your ovaries you do have a cycle.... just no period I would say.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-14T03:14:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-test-timing.2405/post-37652>

Do you cycle if your ovaries are no longer producing eggs?

### MartiD — 2012-07-14T15:39:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hormone-test-timing.2405/post-37725>

If I cycle I have indication of it anymore...no breakouts, no irritability, etc

... they seem to be quite good - or what do you think?

Cortisol 6,13ng/ml (1,8 - 14,5ng/ml)

DHEA 0,75ng/ml (0,09 -0,46ng/ml) ?

Estradiol 0,003ng/ml < 0,0032ng/ml

Progesteron 0,004ng/ml 0,0010 - 0,0050ng/ml

E2/Prog ratio 0,074 ???

Testosteron 0,105ng/ml (0,005 - 0,049ng/ml)

....need help understanding ....

>
>
>
>

> WTH does that mean when his Testosteron is off the chart
>

Repeat these (and more) tests at LabCorp.

There is a lots of offers to do home testing, hormone, fatty acids and other via saliva or blood spots.

They should be avoided.

My opinion, I am not a doctor.

..

Only saliva cortisol testing is acceptable from those outfits.

.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-the-whole-shrimp.2410/post-37556>

So I really want to figure out ways to prepare and eat the whole shrimp - shell and all. Are you trying anything like that? Recipes or techniques?

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-13T21:18:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-the-whole-shrimp.2410/post-37563>

you can simmer the shells to make broth and then puree and eat. good for chondroitin and glucosamine Dr K style.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-13T21:42:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-the-whole-shrimp.2410/post-37573>

I just bought some shrimp and had them cleaned. I can't believe I forgot to ask them to also pkg up the shells for a broth w/ ground up exoskeleton! Was really looking forward to trying that. Seriously......

### BJK77 — 2012-07-14T02:05:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-the-whole-shrimp.2410/post-37631>

Has anyone made broth from the shells like Dr K suggested? I cooked shells from a pound of shrimp in my crockpot for 24 hours and then blended everything up in my Vitamix, but the shells were still quite evident! The broth was tasty, but gritty. Blech!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-14T03:45:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-the-whole-shrimp.2410/post-37663>

Just cook and eat them whole. It's pretty easy. We throw the entire shrimp into a pan with CO and garlic/onions and cook with spices (often of the curry sort) and then squirt some lemon juice in and, voila, eat. I just have to shut my eyes when I eat the heads because they still have those beady little eyes looking at me (The only bit I have trouble with is the tails - the shells are pretty easy to eat and usually well flavoured... and that's with big shrimp/prawns that are 4-5 inches long).

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-14T04:30:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-the-whole-shrimp.2410/post-37677>

Holy shrimp, Kiwi. Maybe that will be my next challenge on the road to optimal.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-14T04:37:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-the-whole-shrimp.2410/post-37680>

> **BJK77;37798 wrote:** Has anyone made broth from the shells like Dr K suggested? I cooked shells from a pound of shrimp in my crockpot for 24 hours and then blended everything up in my Vitamix, but the shells were still quite evident! The broth was tasty, but gritty. Blech!

Yea, that was my experience as well - with no industrial grinder getting them powdery seemed impossible.

### kathylu — 2012-07-14T05:40:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-the-whole-shrimp.2410/post-37692>

Some chinese restaurants (authentic, not americanized) make a dish called "salty shrimp". Whole, in the shell, just pick them up and eat! Delicious...maybe a good way to try shrimp in the shell for the first time.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-14T05:44:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-the-whole-shrimp.2410/post-37695>

I want to try the salty shrimp, the AI way of course. I guess the trick is in frying the shrimp with the shell on with lots of salt and get the whole thing crunchy. It looks yummy for sure.

### kathylu — 2012-07-14T05:48:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-the-whole-shrimp.2410/post-37696>

> **kathylu;37859 wrote:** Some chinese restaurants (authentic, not americanized) make a dish called "salty shrimp". Whole, in the shell, just pick them up and eat! Delicious...maybe a good way to try shrimp in the shell for the first time.

[http://www.myrecipes.com/recipe/crunchy-salt-shrimp-with-ginger-sauce-10000000600528/](http://http://www.myrecipes.com/recipe/crunchy-salt-shrimp-with-ginger-sauce-10000000600528/)

### JanSz — 2012-07-17T22:05:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-eat-the-whole-shrimp.2410/post-38822>

In Brazil I eat whole large shrimp fried on a stick.

At first I did not recognized the animal, usually I see just shrimp tails.

Well, that was in 1980, I have not heard of eating whole shirimps then.

Latter, back in USA, I was going for lunches with Philipinos,

some of them eat little shrimps, cooked, just tails complete with shells.

On my last trip to Korean store I got tiny whole shrimps and anchovies, they are wild caught a complete, dried.

First batch, I soaked them up with 3 parts of water per part of shrimps & anchovies, hoping that they would re-hydrate.

Then added half a stick of butter, garlic, hot pepper, lemon and salt.

Heated couple minutes, just to kill smell of garlic.

It was ok to eat it, but not really, they were not really re-hydrated, overly crunchy.

Now I weight 1.5oz shrimp + 1.5oz anchovies + (1.5 + 1.5)\*3=9oz water put in Vitamix blender (Total 12oz)

sea salt, hot pepper, 5-6 garlic cloves

mix, liquefy

empty into narrow tall pot, covered, heat until boil (exhaust fan overhead, lots of pungent smell)

add 4 tbsp Palm Oil, mix

eat

Tastes real good (or possibly I am just used to it).

To make it more eye appealing, garnish with some green, onion or parsley.

\--------------

### janagram — 2012-07-13T22:15:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-take-a-full-spectrum-enzyme-supp-hours-after-eating.2414/post-37584>

my question is just that: can u take a full spectrum enzyme hrs after eating? sometimes at night I feel like I could use some digestive help....(mine has oxbile, etc.)

### vkiernan — 2012-07-14T00:49:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-take-a-full-spectrum-enzyme-supp-hours-after-eating.2414/post-37619>

> **janagram;37751 wrote:** my question is just that: can u take a full spectrum enzyme hrs after eating? sometimes at night I feel like I could use some digestive help....(mine has oxbile, etc.)

My last doc told me to take them before BAB, mid-morn, lunch, mid-afternoon, supper, before bed. Hows that! She told me you need them in between, not just when eating.

### SeaHorse — 2012-07-14T04:13:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-take-a-full-spectrum-enzyme-supp-hours-after-eating.2414/post-37671>

My understanding is that they have a strong anti-inflammatory effect when they are not breaking down food, so in-between meals is great just like vkiernan said.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-14T04:17:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-take-a-full-spectrum-enzyme-supp-hours-after-eating.2414/post-37672>

I've heard the same thing on the inbetween doses. My BF sometimes takes them before bed to help with things other than digestion. Not sure about the ox bile though. If not an amount like 100 mg I would think it would be OK and maybe still OK...just not sure.

### janagram — 2012-07-15T04:15:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-you-take-a-full-spectrum-enzyme-supp-hours-after-eating.2414/post-37936>

thanks...good to know!

# Thread: Mushrooms  Candida
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mushrooms-candida.2418/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-14T03:06:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mushrooms-candida.2418/post-37648>

I love mushrooms but since they are a fungus figured they shouldn't be eaten when trying to get rid of candida. Sure would like to add them into my food supply. Does anyone know if eating mushrooms exacerbates a candida problem?

### chocolate — 2012-07-14T04:07:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mushrooms-candida.2418/post-37668>

[http://www.thecandidadiet.com/foodstoavoid.htm](http://http://www.thecandidadiet.com/foodstoavoid.htm)

> MUSHROOMS / MOLDS
>
>
>
>
>
> Mushrooms
>
>
>
> Truffles
>
>
>
>
>
> Eating some fungi can cause an inflammatory reaction if you are already suffering from Candida.
>
> Mushrooms also often have mold on their exterior.
>
> **On the other hand, some medicinal mushrooms are actually OK on the diet and have strong immune-boosting properties. Good examples are Reishi and Maitake**

I like mushrooms, too.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-07-14T04:10:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mushrooms-candida.2418/post-37669>

I read all the other stuff you posted on the Candida thread. It seems mushrooms are either good or bad and I don't seem to get a clear picture of them. Now if there is mold on them that is a whole other piece that I don't want to ingest!

Unfortunately I don't think medicinal mushrooms are as tasty (if tasty at all) when making something like cream of mushroom soup!

doi: 10.1017/S1751731107657796

‘Mealthy’ food: meat as a healthy and valuable source

of micronutrients

D. Nohr† and H. K. Biesalski

Department of Biological Chemistry and Nutrition, University of Hohenheim, Garbenstrasse 30, 70593 Stuttgart, Germany
